Weather permitting, this is followed by a laser-guided tour of the night sky, where you’ll explore constellations, planets, and celestial stories above Stonehenge Aotearoa.

Please note: Tours are subject to weather and confirmation. After making your booking, contact us directly to confirm your session.

events.id: Thirty-Six-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Thirty-Six
events.venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Jenny and David meet on a tinder date. She wants a one-night stand, and he wants to go through the 36 questions that are meant to lead to love… What unfolds is a night of daring and divulging. Of holding back and holding on. It’s not about happily ever after, it’s about all the people we meet along the way.
Starring Fern Sutherland (Brokenwood Mysteries) Niwa Whatuira (M3gan 2.0) and Anya Tate-Manning (PSA).
Inspired by one of the most esteemed Modern Love articles from The New York Times “The 36 Questions That Lead to Love”. Winner of Best New Play in Bay Area Will Glickman Award 2025.
“A rom-com with an irreverent streak, Thirty-Six is a fun, flirty, occasionally dirty romp. Maybe bring a date. You’ll definitely learn something about each other by the end of the evening” — KQED

events.id: E tū Musicians' Union town hall-57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: E tū Musicians' Union town hall
events.venue: 57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Calling all musicians to the E tū Musicians' Union town hall.
Come hear about what we’ve been working on and why this is the union for all musicians to join. Whether you're a solo artist, DJ, producer, band member, or orchestral player - nau mai haere mai!
We'll be discussing the supports we have for members and key campaigns we’ve been working on. We’ll also share our long-term strategy to win real, material gains for musicians with a particular focus on streaming revenue, artists’ income support, and how we can improve the live music industry.
There are powerful examples from around the world of what musicians can achieve when we come together in a union. Together we can win the conditions to make art while earning our fair share, but we need more of you to make that possible.
There will be kai, beer and non-alc drinks provided. Tell a friend to tell a friend. See you there. Hei reira e hoa mā!
Monday 30 March, 5:30pm to 7:30pm at two/fiftyseven. Access at 70 Victoria Street (by old JB Hifi).
Join the movement now:
https://etu.nz/musicians-union/

events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces.

It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime.
Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour.

events.name: New Zealand Symphony Orchestra 2026 Season
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The NZSO’s reputation for artistic excellence attracts many of the world’s leading musicians, conductors, and soloists. In 2026, some of the music-world luminaries include violinists María Dueñas and James Ehnes, trombonist Jörgen van Rijen, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason, and New Zealand piano prodigy Shan Liu.   
Wellington shows are hosted in the Michael Fowler Centre, home of the NZSO. The touring programme will see the orchestra perform over 100 concerts in over 30 communities. Be among over 100,000 people across the motu who experience live orchestra music.

events.id: New Zealand Cricket Home International Season--Wellington NZ
events.name: New Zealand Cricket Home International Season
events.long_description:
It looks like New Zealand Cricket (NZC) has slogged it for six. The 2025-26 international home season of cricket promises over 40 days of action across the country. Wellington has scored five major opportunities to see the BLACKCAPS and WHITE FERNS in action. Four formidable opponents are travelling to New Zealand shores. Australia, England, the West Indies, and South Africa will compete across multiple formats. The live T20I action at Hnry Stadium and Test matches at Cello Basin Reserve will have Wellington buzzing.
The 2024-25 season saw bumper crowds, so NZC are hoping to build on this exciting momentum. As rising WHITE FERNS star Georgia Plimmer says, “The atmosphere in Wellington — sitting on the bank, having a drink and watching some great cricket — that’s what you want on a summer’s day in New Zealand.”

events.id: Kizomba Ginga-60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Kizomba Ginga
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
In Kizomba/Semba, Ginga refers to the natural movement of the body that gives the dance its softness, rhythm, and life. Rather than being only about steps, Ginga is the subtle motion of the torso, hips, and weight transfer that allows partners to move smoothly together.
It creates the signature feeling of Kizomba—grounded, relaxed, and connected. Without Ginga, dancing can feel rigid or mechanical; with it, movement becomes fluid, musical, and expressive.


Closely related is the concept of Banga, which refers to the leader’s attitude, presence, and confidence while dancing. Banga is not about arrogance but about character and style—the relaxed swagger that comes from comfort in one’s body and control of movement. A leader with Banga moves with intention and calmness, creating clarity and trust for the follower. Together, Ginga and Banga form an important part of the authentic feeling of Angolan partner dancing.


This class will focus specifically on hip mobility and movement, which is one of the foundations of Ginga. Through guided exercises, we will explore how the hips initiate movement, transfer weight efficiently, and create smoother, more natural leading and following. Improving hip mobility not only enhances the look of the dance but also improves balance, comfort, and connection with your partner.
The goal is to develop a body that moves more freely so that the dance feels effortless and expressive rather than forced.

events.id: Practice As a Yogi - 6 Week Daily Yoga Morning Practice-Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Practice As a Yogi - 6 Week Daily Yoga Morning Practice
events.venue: Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The challenging opportunity to practice every day as a yogi in the morning and experience how it can change your life. We come to know our body and ourselves better through the regular daily practice, practising with full awareness and experiencing the effect of yoga on our body, mind and soul.
Going earlier to bed and not eating too much in the evening are the prerequisites to not giving up after a few days/weeks, though

According to the ancient yoga scriptures, yoga needs to be practised during the period of sunrise, when an immense amount of Prana (cosmic energy) enters our system, nourishing our body, mind, intellect, and soul. Daily practice is crucial if we want to master any skill. You will notice an increased level of energy and positive change in your mood, as well as an improvement in your attention span and concentration. You will experience more inspiration, joy, and love in your daily life.

The Beginners Course teaches the “Sarva Hitta Asanas” from Level 1 - postures that are good for everyone. The Course covers all the foundations you need for an ongoing yoga practice. Loosening your body, the joints, and your mind, the exercises are designed for the complete balance and awakening of energy in body, mind and soul. Experience yoga postures, breath techniques, meditation and relaxation, along with knowledge about basic yoga philosophy and a healthy lifestyle. A wonderful course for new beginners and those looking to deepen their practical experience of yoga.
Some of the many benefits of yoga include: improvement in posture and breathing, increased strength and flexibility, a greater ability to cope with stress, and a feeling of peace and calm.

Monday - Friday 6.30 - 7.30am

Cost: 6 weeks| $171
For this course we require booking. 04-801 7012 or info@yogaindailylife.org.nz

There is no discount for this course. Possibility to join the course any time. We will practice each week a different part from the Level 1. If you could make just one or two weeks, you might like to buy a week pass for $54. Or feel free to contact us.

events.id: PADI Open Water Diver Course-New Zealand Sea Adventures, 9 Marina View, Porirua - Mana-Event Finder
events.name: PADI Open Water Diver Course
events.venue: New Zealand Sea Adventures, 9 Marina View, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
PADI is by far the largest and most popular Scuba diver certifying system in the world and for good reason. Courses are fun, safe, relevant, and packed with excitement! No matter what country you are in, your PADI qualification will be recognised.

The practical part of your Open Water Scuba Diver course takes just four days! During the first weekend, we make it very easy and convenient for you with our on-site dive pool (the only one in the Wellington Region). Our pool is where you learn to dive, so we can go at your pace, no matter how long it takes. During the first weekend, you learn and practice all the necessary dive skills. Once completed, you will be ready for the sea dives.

Once you are a certified diver, you will receive an eCard on your PADI App showing your qualification – and any PADI Dive Centre will be able to confirm your certification on-line. You will also be qualified to attend the PADI Advanced Open Water Diver course, and our regular Dive Days.

We want to make your diving safe and enjoyable. Welcome to the exciting world of Scuba diving!

If you have any questions please call, email or stop by our Dive Store.

Kind regards,
Tony Howell
PADI Course Director
Tel: 04 233-8238 Email: nzsa@scubadiving.co.nz Web: www.scubadiving.co.nz
9 Marina View, Mana, Porirua 5026


PADI Open Water Diver course at a glance:
•Digital theory: Open Water Diver eLearning Course (allow 8-14 hours)
•Practical: One weekend pool training, one weekend with four sea dives
•Options: One-on-one or unscheduled dates – additional costs apply.
•Start Time: Please be at NZ Sea Adventures by 8.30am daily.

Location:
We are located at 9 Marina View, Mana, Porirua; just north of the Paremata bridge on SH1. Look for the big yellow building. Check our website for location.

Prerequisites:
•Be at least 10 years old (special conditions apply for 10 and 11-year-olds)
•Be able to swim 300m (no time limit) and swim/float for 10 minutes.
•Be medically fit to dive. Check the Diver Medical Participant Questionnaire. If you answer YES to any of the questions or have any doubts, a visit to your doctor is required.
•Elearning must be completed before your pool training can begin.
•Complete the RDP Tables Quiz and Exam if you don’t have a dive computer.

What to Bring:
•Completed Diver Medical Participant Questionnaire.
•If Under 18 years – bring a Parent or Legal Guardian to sign your forms.
•A pen, swimming costume, towel and a snack for the breaks.
•Any personal dive equipment you wish to use.
•The $450.00 balance of your course fee.

Cost:
•Scheduled Open Water Diver Course Fee: total is $750.00; deposit is $300.00.
•Includes: PADI eLearning Course, Dive Gear Rental, Pool Sessions, and Sea Dives. Rental wetsuits are available in stock sizes only. Other sizes are available for purchase. Made-to-measure options can be ordered and purchased
•10% off all in-store purchases during your course. Does not include packages.
•$20 off your course fee for every person you get to sign on with you.

When:
Our PADI Open Water Diver Courses are scheduled year-round. See our online calendar for scheduled course dates or ask in store. Booking early guarantees your place and price.
Looking for different dates or smaller classes? Ask about our one-off course options. Let us know what you need and we will do our best to help (conditions may apply).

Booking your course:
•Course bookings can be made in-store, via email, online or over the phone
•The deposit of $300.00 secures your place on a course and purchases your eLearning course.
•Deposits are transferable to another person but are not refundable.
•Elearning courses, once redeemed, cannot be transferred.
•Before we can send you the eLearning link, we require your full name, date of birth, a valid personal email address and phone number.
•Your course balance is to be paid before we can begin your pool training.

events.id: Eat Your Art Out: High Tea-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Eat Your Art Out: High Tea
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Eat Your Art Out brings a creative twist to high tea, inspired by the bold and brilliant works of Toi Art.

Enjoy a menu of classic treats with an artistic edge: from the Sweet William’s Dish, Golden Ratio Scone, Madeleine Moment, and the Mint to Be mousse. Each dish is a playful nod to the world of art, clever, colourful, and made to be shared.

Your high tea includes your choice of tea or coffee. Alcoholic beverages are available for purchase, and dietary options are available on request.

Every purchase helps to support Aotearoa New Zealand’s national museum.

------

Set Menu
(Includes one of each item per person)

Cheery Cherry Curator
A silky milk chocolate crémeux layered with vibrant cherry glaze and topped with a dark chocolate disc creates a perfectly balanced blend of tart and sweet

Mint to Be
Cool mint and sweet strawberry mousse gently resting on a white chocolate base, combining vibrant flavours that are meant to be together

Madeleine Moment
A citrus-infused madeleine paired with silky sambuca namelaka, vibrant green apple gel, and an elegant chocolate garnish delivers golden flavours with a playful twist

Sweet William’s Dish
Mānuka honey ganache nestled in a crisp vanilla tart shell, topped with a chocolate ‘The Dish’ by William De Morgan. A refined take on New Zealand’s liquid gold

The Golden Ratio
A perfectly proportioned cheese scone layered with sundried tomatoes, olives, and fresh basil brings Mediterranean flavours in perfect balance

The Pecking Order
Tender horopito-spiced chicken layered with creamy Tatua mayo and peppery watercress on fresh focaccia fingers, with native pepper leaf adding a bold, warming kick

Still Life with Eggplant
Tangy eggplant and native kawakawa caponata with buffalo bocconcini and fragrant basil pesto on artisanal brioche. A bold sandwich blending native botanicals and classic flavours.

Alcoholic beverages are available for purchase, and dietary options are available on request.
Every purchase helps to support Aotearoa New Zealand’s national museum.

events.id: E tū Musicians' Union town hall-Two/Fiftyseven, Level 2, 57 Willis Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: E tū Musicians' Union town hall
events.venue: Two/Fiftyseven, Level 2, 57 Willis Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Calling all musicians to the E tū Musicians' Union town hall.

Come hear about what we’ve been working on and why this is the union for all musicians to join. Whether you're a solo artist, DJ, producer, band member, or orchestral player - nau mai haere mai!

We'll be discussing the supports we have for members and key campaigns we’ve been working on. We’ll also share our long-term strategy to win real, material gains for musicians with a particular focus on streaming revenue, artists’ income support, and how we can improve the live music industry.

There are powerful examples from around the world of what musicians can achieve when we come together in a union. Together we can win the conditions to make art while earning our fair share, but we need more of you to make that possible.

There will be kai, beer and non-alc drinks provided. Tell a friend to tell a friend. See you there. Hei reira e hoa mā!

Monday 30 March, 5:30pm to 7:30pm at two/fiftyseven. Access at 70 Victoria Street (by old JB Hifi).

events.long_description:
Join us for the 7th year of Yoga in the Park at Waikanae Park on Park Ave!

Our classes focus on balance and stability, incorporating breathing exercises, longer holds, and plenty of options to modify poses based on your fitness level. We’ve had kids join us in past years, and if you consider yourself "older," you’re more than welcome, too. This is your practice, so if you’d rather spend the hour in Child’s Pose, soaking in the sounds of the birds, that’s perfectly fine!

events.long_description:

The previous excercise have increased the flexibility of the spine, improved the body's physical condition and heightened awareness to the importance of relaxation. Ideal for those who would like to learn the levels of the System YIDL.

events.id: Emotional and Mental Wellbeing workshop - Understanding Change, Loss and Grief Seminar-Khandallah Town Hall, 11 Ganges Rd, Khandallah, Wellington 6035, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Emotional and Mental Wellbeing workshop - Understanding Change, Loss and Grief Seminar
events.venue: Khandallah Town Hall, 11 Ganges Rd, Khandallah, Wellington 6035, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Emotional and Mental Wellbeing Workshop is made available through a collaboration between Age Concern Wellington Region and the Catholic Social Services, using Seasons for Growth resources.
The aim of the workshop is to strengthen the emotional and mental wellbeing of adults who are adapting to experiences of change, loss and grief in their lives, such as death, separation and divorce. It’s a small group programme that combines psychology and education with peer support, within a person centered learning approach.
Suggested donation: $15 to help cover materials. Spaces are limited so bookings are essential.
Please book via our website: https://www.acwellington.org.nz/events/

events.long_description:
Paekākāriki Hill Area Pest Control Group
Kia ora koutou.
E tūmanako kei te ora koutou.
It’s my pleasure to invite you to a meet at Battle Hill Farm Park Woolshed at 10am this Tuesday.
The mission of this hui is to enable collaborative & effective animal pest animal control in our catchment, which in turn will assist native regeneration, improve land stability, reduce erosion & support biodiversity.
Apologies for the late notice, I realise the initial hui won’t suit everyone so will happily arrange another hui on Tuesday &/or Wednesday evening also if required.
Please rsvp by phone or email (below).
Include notes on your preferences for how you would like to see collaborative pest animal control operations proceed. (communications, access, timing, methods, safety, outcomes etc).
I look forward to meeting & working with you to help indigenous forest & fauna thrive in the years ahead.
Noho ora mai.
Gerry Dysart,
1007 Paekākāriki Hill Road.
ph 021855857
email: gerry@mosaicaotearoa.com
Ps. Please forward this message on to interested parties. Ngā mihi.

events.id: Moonday Quiz hosted by Young Gho$t-Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Moonday Quiz hosted by Young Gho$t
events.venue: Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mondays don’t have to be a drag - come flex your brain at Moon with quizmaster Young Gho$t. Expect two music rounds, plus general knowledge, pop culture, geography and whatever else we feel like throwing at you.

It’s $2 to play, with every dollar going to a different charity each month. Teams are capped at 8, so round up your smartest mates and grab a table.

The quiz kicks off at 7 pm, but come early for food - we’ve got pizza and drink deals to keep your neurons firing. Prizes include Moon show tickets and more!

Book your table at newtownmoon.co.nz

This month's quiz proceeds will be going to Kaicycle!

Kaicycle is an urban farm & composting initiative in Newtown that has been operating for over 10 years. They're a climate justice project at their core, aiming to develop a resilient food system for Te Whanganui-a-Tara and divert food scraps from landfill. Kaicycle connect people of all ages with the whenua, sharing their knowledge on organic & regenerative growing practices, collecting & composting food scraps from local businesses and households on e-bikes, and donating the kai they grow to local food programmes.

events.id: SGCNZ VUW Wellington Regional Shakespeare Festival-Wellington East Girls' College, Austin Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: SGCNZ VUW Wellington Regional Shakespeare Festival
events.venue: Wellington East Girls' College, Austin Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Shakespeare Globe Centre New Zealand Regional Shakespeare Festival Wellington is one of 24 similar regional events across New Zealand. These Regional Festivals involve more than half the secondary schools, home schoolers and community youth drama groups in the country. During these Festivals, students perform their reimagining of Shakespeare's plays, in their 5-minute and 15-minute scenes, between late March and the end of Term 1. A Group from both categories from each region is selected from these to go on to participate in the National Festival over King's Birthday weekend in Wellington.

The Festival provides a wonderfully eclectic mélange of the creativity of the imaginative rangatahi of New Zealand. A large percentage of the scenes are student-directed. Come and see the way they relish explore and present the Bard's works!

events.id: THE NORTH Q&A Screening with director Bart Schrijver-Light House Cuba Cinema, 29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: THE NORTH Q&A Screening with director Bart Schrijver
events.venue: Light House Cuba Cinema, 29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Director Bart Schrijver joins us for a special Q&A screening of his film THE NORTH!
Monday 30th March, 7:00pm at Light House Cuba.
Watch the trailer here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0N1WvlLhx7A&t
...
Prepare to be transported to the striking beauty of the Scottish wilderness in THE NORTH, a visceral journey through sheets of rain, craggy cliffs and lonesome beaches.
A decade after being best friends and roommates, Chris and Lluis set out on a 600 kilometre hike through the Scottish Highlands. Following the West Highland Way and The Cape Wrath Trail, they spend 30 days together in nature - hoping to rekindle their once-powerful friendship.
But while Chris remains preoccupied with work and life back at home, Lluis is determined to finish the trail to prove he can do it. The solitude and silence of the Highlands forces them to confront harsh truths about themselves, their friendship, and what it truly means to stand still and listen.
Set against the pace and demands of long-distance hiking, THE NORTH depicts the intricacies and challenges of both friendship and internal human struggles against the backdrop of the beautifully stark landscapes of the Highlands.

events.id: Soweto Gospel Choir-St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Soweto Gospel Choir
events.venue: St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
With their new concert, the multi-Grammy® and Emmy Award®-winning SOWETO GOSPEL CHOIR return to Aotearoa 2026 to perform a joyful program of South African freedom songs, traditional spirituals and some classics.

On Saturday March 19, Soweto Gospel Choir will be visiting the Waikato Regional Theatre in Kirikiriroa Hamilton before heading to the Kiri Te Kanawa Theatre in Tāmaki Makaurau Auckland on Sunday March 29, then on to Pōneke Wellington at the St James Theatre on Tuesday March 31 before ending the Aotearoa tour in Ōtautahi Christchurch at the Christchurch Town Hall on Wednesday April 1, 2026.

The new concert by 3-time Grammy®-winning SOWETO GOSPEL CHOIR; celebrates songs and anthems of Nelson Mandela’s Freedom Movement in South Africa and the Civil Rights Movement of Dr Martin Luther King’s America.

Opening with a rousing program of South African freedom songs, the repertoire moves down through time to the USA, with beautiful renditions of the protest music of the Civil Rights Movement and more contemporary classics, including works by legendary artists Elvis Presley, Sam Cooke, Cynthia Erivo, Stevie Wonder and the one-and-only Aretha Franklin.

Formed to celebrate the unique and inspirational power of South African Gospel music, SOWETO GOSPEL CHOIR is dedicated to sharing the joy of faith through music with audiences around the world, receiving critical acclaim and audience adoration for their powerful renditions of African American spirituals, gospel and classics.

events.id: Soweto Gospel Choir-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Soweto Gospel Choir
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The sound of close harmony singing from South Africa is set to ring out across the St James Theatre in March. The Soweto Gospel Choir is a 30-member ensemble that blends African gospel, spirituals, reggae and American popular music into rousing concerts. For its Wellington concert, the choir will present songs and anthems from Nelson Mandela’s Freedom Movement in South Africa and the Civil Rights Movement of Dr Martin Luther King’s America. There will also be contemporary classics. Hear songs by legendary artists Elvis Presley, Sam Cooke, Cynthia Erivo, Stevie Wonder, and Aretha Franklin. 
Since it formed in 2002, the Soweto Gospel Choir has performed around the world. The choir is dedicated to celebrating the unique and inspirational power of South African Gospel music. It’s won numerous awards, including three Grammy Awards for Best Traditional World Music Album.
events.description:
The sound of close harmony singing from South Africa is set to ring out across the St James Theatre in March.
The Soweto Gospel Choir is a 30-member ensemble that blends African gospel, spirituals, reggae and American popular music into rousing concerts.
There will also be contemporary classics.
Hear songs by legendary artists Elvis Presley, Sam Cooke, Cynthia Erivo, Stevie Wonder, and Aretha Franklin.
The choir is dedicated to celebrating the unique and inspirational power of South African Gospel music.

events.name: Brazilian Zouk - Improvers - Balão Apagado
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come and join us for Improvers Zouk Block B (5 weeks).
We have some great new content for you this year and will be building on foundational movements.
The complete Improvers course consists of 6 different Blocks (A-F).
Each block focuses on a different aspect and technique of Brazilian Zouk and so they can be completed independently of each other.
Our recommendation is to complete all 6 Blocks before progressing onto the Brazilian Zouk Deep Dive classes.
In Improvers Block B we will introduce you to the Balão Apagado head movement, as well as some exercises on Musicality & building a dance.
===Prices and payment:
- Prepaid 5 week Block cost is $90 waged or $75 for unwaged/students
- Ad hoc cost per class is $20 waged or $18 unwaged/students
Online payments can be made to Adam Polaczuk
Acct: 03-0531-0559348-25
Details: Your name and ZoukImpB
===Location:
Classes are in Footnote dance studio, 60 Dixon Street
===Housekeeping:
- You should have completed the 4 beginner Class blocks before joining Improvers classes
- Drop in or new students, please get in contact before the start of class to let us know you are interested so we can assess your level and organise a private class if necessary to catch you up. If you just show up on the night, you are welcome to watch the class but will not be able to join in (this is to respect the learning of our regular students as the classes are progressive)

events.id: From Miramar to Pandora - Industry Talk with the Avatar Fire and Ash Sound Team-11 Mount St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: From Miramar to Pandora - Industry Talk with the Avatar Fire and Ash Sound Team
events.venue: 11 Mount St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join the The Screen Music and Sound Guild (SMSG) for a special industry talk exploring the craft of sound on one of the world’s most ambitious film productions, Avatar Fire and Ash.
Hosted at Massey University Cinema, this event brings together three Wellington-based members of the film’s sound team: Supervising Sound Editor Brent Burge, Sound Designer Hayden Collow, and Supervising Dialogue Editor Martin Kwok.
In conversation with SMSG, the panel will share insights from their work on the latest film in the Avatar franchise and reflect on the creative and technical challenges of working at this scale. From early collaboration and evolving workflows to the realities of post-production on a major international film, the discussion will offer a rare glimpse into how world-class sound teams operate.
The evening will include moderated discussion and selected scene breakdowns, offering practical insight into the creative decisions and processes that shape cinematic sound. Whether you’re a composer, sound professional, filmmaker, or student, this is a valuable opportunity to hear directly from leading practitioners working at the forefront of the industry.


Doors open 6pm
Panel Discussion begins 6.30pm


Drinks and Snacks available for purchase.


Free for SMSG members.
Spaces are limited, so secure your ticket early.

events.id: Brazilian Zouk Dance Classes - Beginners block B-60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Brazilian Zouk Dance Classes - Beginners block B
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Connect with other like minded people that have a love of dance.
Brazilian Zouk was developed in Rio de Janeiro in the mid 90s, and is now becoming more and more popular
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DfhRHnEAbak
..............
This is stunning to watch and even more fun to dance! It is based on the original Lambada dance but adapted to more modern music with a slow-quick-quick beat. It can be danced to a wide variety of music including R&B, Rock and Pop.
In this block of 5 classes you will discover the fundamental techniques, movements and sequences of Brazilian Zouk to get you up and running on the dance floor.
This includes:
Week 1: Passo basico
Week 2: Lateral
Week 3: Ioio
Week 4: Bonus
Week 5: Combination
They are designed so you can start this block as a complete newbie, or you can continue on as an existing student from the last block.
There are 4 blocks of classes (A, B, C, D) in the complete Fundamentals course. Once these are done, you can progress to the Improvers classes.
Prices and payment:
- Prepaid 5 week term cost is $90 waged or $75 for unwaged/students.
- Ad hoc cost is $20 waged or $18 unwaged/students
Online payments can be made to Adam Polaczuk
Acct: 03-0531-0559348-25

events.id: Astronomy on Tap-40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Astronomy on Tap
events.venue: 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Experience the wonders of the Universe with Astronomy on Tap, Space Place’s after‑hours planetarium series, where great science meets great drinks.
Begin your journey with a drink on arrival and some first‑class in-flight snacks. Then settle into our digital full-dome planetarium as we travel from Pōneke into the vast depths of space. Complete your trip with a visit to the cash bar and our interactive exhibitions.
Whether you’re bringing someone special, catching up with a friend, or exploring solo, raise your glass to the night sky and enjoy a stellar ride.
Tuesday 31 March: Tour of the Solar System (and Beyond!)
Have you ever looked up and wondered what’s really out there? This month, we’re taking you on a grand tour of our cosmic neighbourhood, and then pushing far beyond it.
We’ll begin right here in Pōneke, exploring the night sky and discovering where Earth sits in the vastness of space. Then buckle up for a whistle‑stop journey around our Solar System: planets, moons, oddballs, and everything in between. Finally, we’ll launch thousands of light-years outward into deep space to meet some of the galaxy’s most extraordinary sights.
Come see the night sky as you’ve never seen it before.
Tuesday 28 April: Scale of the Universe
How big is big? How small is small? And where exactly do we fit?
April’s Astronomy on Tap dives into the mind‑bending scales of the cosmos, from our skies over Pōneke to the edge of the observable Universe. After orienting ourselves in the night sky, we’ll journey outward past planets, moons, star‑forming nebulae, and even black holes, until we reach the faint glow of the Cosmic Microwave Background nearly 93 billion light-years away.
It’s a fast, fun, and perspective-shifting ride through space and time, with drinks and nibbles for the trip.
Thursday 28 May: Planets, Exoplanets & Moons
Our Solar System alone is packed with wonders, icy moons, volcanic worlds, and Saturn’s hundreds of orbiting companions. But beyond our Sun lies an even larger menagerie of strange and spectacular exoplanets.
In May, join us for a journey through the planets, exoplanets, and moons both near and far. We’ll visit some of the Solar System’s most dramatic worlds, then leap to distant star systems to explore alien planets that are hotter, colder, larger, smaller, weirder, and wilder than anything we’ve ever seen up close.
What might we discover out there? Come along and find out.
Thursday 25 June: Matatiki
Ka puta Matariki, ka rere Whānui, ko te tohu o te tau.
Matariki appears as Whānui flees; this is the sign of the new year.
The Matariki star cluster appears in our morning sky in midwinter, signaling the transition to spring and eventually to summer. This collection of stars, along with Puanga, te Marama, and other aspects of tō tātou taiao – our environment – are just some of the tohu or signs that tell us we are approaching the New Year. Haere mai, join us for this month’s Astronomy on Tap to learn how to read the signs and celebrate the significance of this time of year.
$45pp | 8PM–10PM | R18 | Space Place

events.id: Beehive to Business with Hon. David Seymour-Wellington Chamber Of Commerce,NTT Tower, Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Beehive to Business with Hon. David Seymour
events.venue: Wellington Chamber Of Commerce,NTT Tower, Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Beehive to Business with Hon. David Seymour
Beehive to Business Breakfast with Hon. David Seymour

About this Event

Election years have real consequences for business.

Join us on March 31 as we kick off our election series of Beehive to Business, with ACT Leader David Seymour, Deputy Prime Minister, Minister for Regulation and Associate Minister of Finance, Health, Education & Justice.

As leader of ACT and a central figure in the Coalition Government, Minister Seymour is shaping some of the most significant policy reforms affecting New Zealand businesses. In an election year, the direction of travel matters more than ever.

Through his work as Minister for Regulation and alongside his Associate Finance, Health, Education and Justice portfolios, he is involved in decisions that shape regulatory settings, public spending, and policy frameworks that affect business confidence, investment and day-to-day operations across New Zealand.

This event offers the opportunity to hear directly from a senior Coalition leader about the Government’s reform programme, ACT’s priorities heading into the election, and what they could mean for the business community.
In an election year, conversations like this matter.

Seats are limited — we encourage early registration.

Agenda:
7:15am- Standing breakfast, networking
7:45am- Address
8:10am- Q&A
8:30am- Event concludes

Can't make it? Click here to register for the free livestream. Sign up and watch at a time convenient to you.

Part of the ongoing Beehive to Business series, this event is proudly made possible through our partnership with Wellington-based consulting firm Allen + Clarke.

events.id: Astronomy on Tap-40 Salamanca Road, Kelburn, New Zealand 6140,40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Astronomy on Tap
events.venue: 40 Salamanca Road, Kelburn, New Zealand 6140,40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Astronomy on Tap
Experience the wonders of the Universe with Astronomy on Tap, Space Place’s after‑hours planetarium series, where great science meets great drinks.

Begin your journey with a drink on arrival and some first‑class in-flight snacks. Then settle into our digital full-dome planetarium as we travel from Pōneke into the vast depths of space. Complete your trip with a visit to the cash bar and our interactive exhibitions.

Whether you’re bringing someone special, catching up with a friend, or exploring solo, raise your glass to the night sky and enjoy a stellar ride.

Tuesday 31 March: Tour of the Solar System (and Beyond!)

Have you ever looked up and wondered what’s really out there? This month, we’re taking you on a grand tour of our cosmic neighbourhood, and then pushing far beyond it.

We’ll begin right here in Pōneke, exploring the night sky and discovering where Earth sits in the vastness of space. Then buckle up for a whistle‑stop journey around our Solar System: planets, moons, oddballs, and everything in between. Finally, we’ll launch thousands of light-years outward into deep space to meet some of the galaxy’s most extraordinary sights.

Come see the night sky as you’ve never seen it before.

Tuesday 28 April: Scale of the Universe

How big is big? How small is small? And where exactly do we fit?

April’s Astronomy on Tap dives into the mind‑bending scales of the cosmos, from our skies over Pōneke to the edge of the observable Universe. After orienting ourselves in the night sky, we’ll journey outward past planets, moons, star‑forming nebulae, and even black holes, until we reach the faint glow of the Cosmic Microwave Background nearly 93 billion light-years away.

It’s a fast, fun, and perspective-shifting ride through space and time, with drinks and nibbles for the trip.

Thursday 28 May: Planets, Exoplanets & Moons

Our Solar System alone is packed with wonders, icy moons, volcanic worlds, and Saturn’s hundreds of orbiting companions. But beyond our Sun lies an even larger menagerie of strange and spectacular exoplanets.

In May, join us for a journey through the planets, exoplanets, and moons both near and far. We’ll visit some of the Solar System’s most dramatic worlds, then leap to distant star systems to explore alien planets that are hotter, colder, larger, smaller, weirder, and wilder than anything we’ve ever seen up close.

What might we discover out there? Come along and find out.

Thursday 25 June: Matatiki

Ka puta Matariki, ka rere Whānui, ko te tohu o te tau.
Matariki appears as Whānui flees; this is the sign of the new year.

The Matariki star cluster appears in our morning sky in midwinter, signaling the transition to spring and eventually to summer. This collection of stars, along with Puanga, te Marama, and other aspects of tō tātou taiao – our environment – are just some of the tohu or signs that tell us we are approaching the New Year. Haere mai, join us for this month’s Astronomy on Tap to learn how to read the signs and celebrate the significance of this time of year.

$45pp | 8PM–10PM | R18 | Space Place
events.description:
Whether you’re bringing someone special, catching up with a friend, or exploring solo, raise your glass to the night sky and enjoy a stellar ride.
Tuesday 31 March: Tour of the Solar System (and Beyond!)
We’ll begin right here in Pōneke, exploring the night sky and discovering where Earth sits in the vastness of space.
April’s Astronomy on Tap dives into the mind‑bending scales of the cosmos, from our skies over Pōneke to the edge of the observable Universe.
Haere mai, join us for this month’s Astronomy on Tap to learn how to read the signs and celebrate the significance of this time of year.

events.id: Karori Friendship Club-St Ninian's Uniting Church, 208 Karori Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Karori Friendship Club
events.venue: St Ninian's Uniting Church, 208 Karori Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Tuesday mornings just got better!

If you enjoy a good chat, a decent cuppa, and the kind of laughter that makes you forget the time, the Karori Friendship Club is for you. We meet at St Ninian’s, 208 Karori Road, Karori, from 10 am to 12 pm, and for a koha you’ll be treated to morning tea and genuinely friendly company.

It’s a wonderful fit for seniors, people new to Karori, or any ESOL learners who would like a relaxed place to practise their language skills and settle into the community. Some weeks, there’s a board game or a puzzle on the go. Most weeks, it’s simply good conversation, shared stories, and the kind of connection that brightens your whole day.

No pressure, no fuss — just a warm welcome and a seat at the table. Come and see what makes this little Tuesday gathering such a special part of our community. You don't have to live in Karori to come; we have members from all over Wellington.

Ph: 476 7137 for more info or check out our website.

events.id: Book Group: Smith's Dream by C.K. Stead-The Library, 1/53 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Book Group: Smith's Dream by C.K. Stead
events.venue: The Library, 1/53 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Do you keep meaning to read more of the great classics of literature? At Wellington Classic Literature Club, we read novels that have stood the test of time, whether they were written in 1780, 1880 or even 1980. We meet on the first Tuesday of the month.

NOTE: this ‘April’ meeting is on Tuesday 31 March as the following Tuesday is right after Easter.

Our group reads one Kiwi classic each year; this year we read Smith's Dream...

New Zealand in economic and political crisis. Oil shocks. Industrial disputes. Isolation. The government declares martial law, while opponents of the overnight police state arm themselves and take to the bush... One man, Smith, who thought he was getting away to the Coromandel to escape his personal troubles, is unwillingly dragged into the struggle.

C.K. Stead's short 1971 novel became a prescribed text for thousands of school students and later that decade a major feature film. Sleeping Dogs (1977) was the first New Zealand film released in the United States and stars Sam Neill in his breakout role.

RSVP
Bookings essential. Get your free ticket by clicking 'Register' above. Limited places.

If you've signed up, but things change and you're not going to be able to make it along, please email classiclitwgtn@gmail.com as we may be able to make more spaces available for others.

VENUE
We meet in the ‘Games Room’, The Library bar's private area overlooking Courtenay Place. After you enter the bar, head to your right (up the step) and through a smaller space with booths to the large carpeted space beyond.

ABOUT US
Wellington Classic Literature Club has been running for more than a decade - since the start of 2013 - and we have a great bunch of regulars as well as readers who join us for particular books or simply when it suits.

Oil shocks.

Wellington Classic Literature Club has been running for more than a decade - since the start of 2013 - and we have a great bunch of regulars as well as readers who join us for particular books or simply when it suits.

events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.id: Curator’s talk — ‘Future Proof: Paintings in Perpetuity’ exhibition-Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Curator’s talk — ‘Future Proof: Paintings in Perpetuity’ exhibition
events.venue: Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come along to this Curator’s talk about the exhibition ‘Future Proof: Paintings in Perpetuity’ and discover how and why these significant oil paintings have been conserved for future generations.
Behind the scenes of the exhibition
Join Paper Conservator Jennifer Cauchi as she discusses her work co-curating the exhibition Future Proof: Paintings in Perpetuity.
This exhibition asks, ‘What does it take to preserve an artwork in perpetuity?’ A mandate to preserve can take many forms and sometimes we need extra help to not simply preserve these taonga, but to make them shine into the future.
The Paintings in Perpetuity programme
Future Proof: Paintings in Perpetuity showcases paintings that have had conservation treatment through the support of the Turnbull Endowment Trust and the Paintings in Perpetuity programme.
The programme was created to fund external conservation treatment for artworks in need of special care, so that they are available for generations to come to appreciate.
The funds provided by the Trust and other generous donors have allowed us to treat these precious paintings, but there is much more to do to future-proof the Library’s large painting collections.
Jennifer will share insights into why these paintings were selected for conservation, what the preservation treatments were and some of the stories behind the paintings.
Paintings in Perpetuity: https://natlib.govt.nz/blog/posts/paintings-in-perpetuity
About the speaker
Jennifer Cauchi is Team Leader, Conservation at the National Library, and Archives New Zealand. A trained paper conservator, she studied art conservation in Canada and has worked in Canada, the United States, and at Te Papa in Wellington. While her speciality is works of art on paper, she also has extensive experience with a wide range of paper-based materials and photographic collections.

Come along to this Curator’s talk about the exhibition ‘Future Proof: Paintings in Perpetuity’ and discover how and why these significant oil paintings have been conserved for future generations.

The programme was created to fund external conservation treatment for artworks in need of special care, so that they are available for generations to come to appreciate.
Jennifer will share insights into why these paintings were selected for conservation, what the preservation treatments were and some of the stories behind the paintings.
While her speciality is works of art on paper, she also has extensive experience with a wide range of paper-based materials and photographic collections.

events.id: Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop ~ Wednesday 1st April-Wild Botanica Raumati South,10 Leinster Ave, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop ~ Wednesday 1st April
events.venue: Wild Botanica Raumati South,10 Leinster Ave, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop ~ Wednesday 1st April
Join expert forager Felicity Joy of Wild Botanica for a comprehensive Wild Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop in her wild & private garden in Raumati South.

This inspiring experience will open your eyes to the abundance of edible wild plants growing right under your nose.

We’ll unlock the secrets of foraging, connecting with the land, and turning humble "weeds" into delicious, nourishing food. You’ll gain confidence in identifying common edible plants, learn their botanical names, and understand their medicinal and nutritional benefits. We’ll also explore ethical foraging practices, how to spot toxic lookalikes, and share simple, practical ways to incorporate wild foods into your daily meals.

This isn’t just about food: it’s about sustainability, resilience, and reconnecting with the environment in a meaningful way. You'll leave with a deeper appreciation for the land, new skills to support local food security, and the knowledge to make your own simple meals from wild ingredients.

Class sizes are limited, so book early to secure your spot.
For more details and bookings, visit

www.wildbotanica.co.nz
follow @wild.botanica.nz on social media.

For questions, email .

Join my email newsletter to hear about upcoming foraging workshops: https://www.wildbotanica.co.nz/pages/newsletter

events.id: The Big Bike Film Night 2026 - Wellington-205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Big Bike Film Night 2026 - Wellington
events.venue: 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Let the good times ROLL! The screening sensation known as The Big Bike Film Night is back in '26 for your bike films fix!!!
Sunday 22nd March 5:00pm SOLD OUT
Wednesday 25th March 6:30pm SOLD OUT
Wednesday 1st April 6:30pm
Thursday 7th May 6:15pm
Thursday 7th May 7:00pm ENCORE
What's it all about??? The Big Bike Film Night is on a mission- bringing the best cycling short films from around the world together for you.
Showcasing 2.5 hours of riveting bike films that have everything a cycle-centric audience could want - action, drama, humour, and plenty of inspiration; the evening is unashamedly and utterly, utterly, utterly designed and devised to delight the two-wheel devotee!
Our Promo Trailer
https://bit.ly/Big_Bike_Film_Night_2026_Promo_Trailer
The 2026 treasure chest of films includes:
• a dazzling mountain bike ride exploring the breathtaking landscapes of Norway, embracing the magic of the midnight sun
• a life-changing experience for two adventurous women as they embark to cycle to all the ski-fields in New Zealand in one gigantic attempt
• an inspiring film that captures the wettest, most miserable riding conditions imaginable
• a film that playfully delves into the world of those taking cycling to new heights - celebrating fun, silliness, and creativity that comes with pushing boundaries on two wheels
• a compelling story about perseverance, showcasing the challenges and triumphs of navigating daily life following a life-altering injury, and how adaptive design has transformed and enriched the journey forward
• a moving movie that follows a father and son’s journey as they take on one of life’s most meaningful milestones- riding a bike together as a family
• a bold and uplifting story of an adventure activist, attempting to break the 24-hour Guinness World Record in downhill mountain biking- all to raise funds for reforesting foothills around Queenstown, New Zealand. Her relentless pursuit not only pushes the limits of endurance but also becomes a powerful symbol of resilience and environmental commitment
• and crowning the night off… we all love an underdog tale. This charming, funny, heartfelt film inspires us to believe that we all have the capacity to be unlikely heroes in our own stories. The audience is transported back to the nostalgic 1990s, to the slopes of the punishing Mont Ventoux, in Southern France, to witness the drama of a battle between two giants
ALL in the mix!
These short films are a great reminder of why we love to ride our bike; with stories that captivate us, stories that make us think, and most importantly of all- inspiring us to get out and ride. All celebrate the fun, adventure, and joy that cycling enables.
The New Zealand
󠁿
2026 National Tour is brought to you by our PRESENTING PARTNERS
CYCLE JOURNEYS, and Destination Queenstown | Lake Wānaka Tourism
NATIONAL COMPETITION*
When you buy a ticket to attend a Big Bike Film Night New Zealand you can enter our National Competition to win some incredible prizes
Thanks to our Presenting Partners, Cycle Journeys, you could WIN a day ride for two on the new Kawatiri Coastal Trail, the prize includes 1 night's accomodation in Westport + breakfast
Website https://cyclejourneys.co.nz/kawatiri/
Thanks to our Presenting Partners, Destination Queenstown and Lake Wānaka Tourism you could WIN a biking holiday for two in Queenstown and Wānaka - New Zealand’s ultimate mountain biking and cycling destination.
Website https://www.queenstownnz.co.nz
Website https://www.wanaka.co.nz
Thanks to our Supporters, Ground Effect Cycle Clothing, you could WIN some awesome CYCLING KIT and 6 x $100 gift vouchers
Website https://www.groundeffect.co.nz
*Terms and Conditions apply

PREVIOUS ATTENDEE’S COMMENTS:-
“One of the most inspiring events of the year"
“Wow! If it comes to your town do NOT miss it. Something for everyone.”
“The Big Bike Film Night is always a wonderful, eclectic, wacky mix of films and I love it!”
“Inspiring | Breathtaking | Heartwarming | Quirky | Bonkers | Funny as F##k!"
HEALTH WARNING: Watching this collection may cause you to want to ride for no less than 1 second and no more than 24 hours per day. Potential side effects could also include buying a bike, bike gear, bike clothing, bike travel books, etc., etc., and longing to get lost with your bicycle. In addition to being entertaining, this event will inspire and lead to activities that improve your physical and mental well-being.
FILM CLASSIFICATION: THE BIG BIKE FILM NIGHT is exempt from classification, but the films are suitable for the entire family, and would obtain PG ratings.
Reserving seats on these nights is always tricky so we cannot guarantee specific spots. If you have a strong preference, TEXT 027 590 2117 to reserve your seats ONLY after you have paid for them.
So...listen to the voice inside you; it’s telling you to go. Grab your mates, grab your tickets, GO ON… grab your bike and come along for the ride!!

events.id: Roxy's 15th Birthday | Feast Your Eyes | King Kong (2005)-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Roxy's 15th Birthday | Feast Your Eyes | King Kong (2005)
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come celebrate The Roxy's 15th Birthday as, once again, we show the first film to ever grace our big beautiful screen!
Directed by Peter Jackson
Starring Andy Serkis, Jack Black, Kyle Chandler, Jamie Bell, Naomi Watts, Adrien Brody, Colin Hanks
--- Peter Jackson's expansive remake of the 1933 classic follows director Carl Denham (Jack Black) and his crew on a journey from New York City to the ominous Skull Island to film a new movie. Accompanying him are playwright Jack Driscoll (Adrien Brody) and actress Ann Darrow (Naomi Watts), who is whisked away by the monstrous ape, Kong, after they reach the island. The crew encounters dinosaurs and other creatures as they race to rescue Ann, while the actress forms a bond with her simian captor. ---
Upgrade your ticket to the FEAST YOUR EYES (FYE) experience which includes themed drink and dessert.
At Feast Your Eyes, cinephiles and foodies will be treated to a movie and food experience in the cinema at the perfect after dinner event!
Admit only tickets are $15, but you can upgrade to a Feast Your Eyes tickets for $45. All Feast Your Eyes tickets include a themed dessert* and cocktail to enjoy during the film.
The upstairs bar will open at 6:30pm for cocktail and dessert collection. The pre-show kicks off at 6:55pm and the feature begins at 7:05pm.
*No dietaries catered for.

events.id: Bret McKenzie Freak Out City Tour | Wellington SECOND SHOW-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bret McKenzie Freak Out City Tour | Wellington SECOND SHOW
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Grammy and Academy Award–winning songwriter, composer and performer Bret McKenzie has announced two highly anticipated dates in his homeland of New Zealand in support of his latest album, Freak Out City. Touching down in Wellington and Auckland this April, the shows follow a run of sold-out international concerts and mark McKenzie’s long-awaited return to New Zealand stages. Best known as one half of Flight of the Conchords, Bret McKenzie has built an acclaimed solo career defined by sharp wit, impeccable musicianship and a deep love of classic songwriting.
Following his solo debut Songs Without Jokes, Bret released his second project, Freak Out City, in August 2025 via Sub Pop Records. Co-produced with longtime collaborator Mickey Petralia, the collection was shaped on the road with his eight-piece band, The State Highway Wonders. The influence of his musical heroes Harry Nilsson and Randy Newman resonates again on this record, where Bret playfully weaves humour and heart into his songs. Hailed as “upbeat, well-made and refreshingly smart” (Daily Mirror) and “superbly crafted with a nod to the greats (God is in the TV), the record highlights Bret’s evolving artistry, blending sharp lyricism, lush arrangements and his unmistakable offbeat charm.
The upcoming shows will feature newly-released songs alongside old favorites, plus hilarious recountings of key moments from his colorful career, songs written on the fly using audience members’ real-life stories, and various other onstage antics.
Following sold-out performances overseas, these intimate New Zealand dates offer a rare opportunity to experience Freak Out City live in two of Aotearoa’s most beloved venues.
Tickets go on sale Friday 16 January at 9am via ticketek.co.nz.
Artist presale begins Wednesday January 14 at 10am local time. Sign up for access via the mailing list at bretmckenzie.os.fan

Tickets go on sale Friday 16 January at 9am via ticketek.co.nz.

events.id: Eyegum (free) Wednesdays: Cruelly, Half/Angel + Charmort-171 Cuba Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Eyegum (free) Wednesdays: Cruelly, Half/Angel + Charmort
events.venue: 171 Cuba Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Eyegum free Wednesdays are BACK for 2026! And we're kicking off with a hard-hitting line-up you don't wanna miss!
8:30 Half/Angel
9:15 Cruelly
10:00 Charmort
Cruelly, the Grungegaze/Noise-Rock quad from Te Whanganui-a-Tara carry a juxtaposition of screeching feedback to harmonic mellows with all the anger in-between on their sleeves.
Half/Angel drifts between dream and distortion, weaving ethereal melodies with raw, untamed energy. Their sound is a shifting landscape—delicate one moment, thunderous the next, always unpredictable.
You love dancing. You loving moving. You love charmort.
Artist links:
Half/Angel: https://www.instagram.com/halfangel.wav/?hl=en
Cruelly: https://cruelly.bandcamp.com/.../cruelly-live-at-san-fran...
https://www.instagram.com/cruelly.band/?hl=en
Charmort: https://www.instagram.com/charmort4u/
Pōneke is home to an incredible diversity of artists and music lovers.
We want Eyegum Wednesdays to be a space that everyone can enjoy with care and respect.
We have no room for transphobia or racism, sexism, homophobia, and disregarding consent.

events.id: Gong Bath-Sound Meditation-Aro Valley Community Centre, 48 Aro St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Gong Bath-Sound Meditation
events.venue: Aro Valley Community Centre, 48 Aro St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join Nick and his weekly Gong Bath-Sound Meditations on Wednesdays at 5.30 - 6.30 pm to unwind from the stresses of your day and help reset your nervous system before dinner! Having played gongs around the Wellington region for over two years in various yoga studios and local music festivals, these events are a pivot towards offering the local community a break from modern-day stressors in an accesible part of the city.

If you haven't been to an event like this before, some of the benefits come from the complex sounds of the instruments in assisting the brain to reach deeper, more restful brainwave states, while the steady drones invite the nervous system to relax into the rest & digest mode. The sounds themselves are unlike anything else really, sounding like some kind of rich, cosmic, electronic music. Nick uses three medium-large Paiste gongs, full moon Tibetan singing bowls, monochord, zither, percussion, and a singing pyramid, and builds the music within a three-stage, therapeutically informed structure.

Nick has been producing and playing with sound since his early teens, having graduated from The New Zealand School of Music, and later with a University qualification, international trainings, and work experience in experiential Psychology.

Please bring along anything that'll help you feel more comfortable (yoga mat, pillow, eye mask, blanket, or earplugs if you live with tinnitus).

If you live with any of these, please talk to your Doctor first, to make sure the event will be suitable:
-have a pacemaker or other implanted medical device
-live with epilepsy or a sound-sensitive condition
-are in the first trimester of pregnancy
-are experiencing severe or unmanaged mental health challenges (such as psychosis, schizophrenia, or PTSD)

Looking forward to seeing you there!

events.id: Sunset Guided Tour-Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Sunset Guided Tour
events.venue: Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton
events.long_description:
Experience Stonehenge Aotearoa at its most breathtaking — as the sun dips below the Wairarapa horizon.
Our Sunset Guided Tour blends astronomy, storytelling, and the natural beauty of twilight for an evening you will never forget.

Led by one of our knowledgeable guides, this 90-minute experience explores how ancient cultures used the Sun to track time and mark the turning of the seasons. You’ll discover the scientific and cultural thinking behind the Henge’s unique Southern Hemisphere design before watching the sunset alignment from inside the stone circle.

Weather permitting, you’ll witness how the setting Sun interacts with the structure — a striking moment that links ancient knowledge with the rhythms of the natural world.

Duration and Schedule
• Allow: Approximately 90 minutes
• Start Time: Begins one hour before sunset
• Availability: Offered any day with advance booking

events.id: Italian Short Film Night-Light House Cuba Cinema, 29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Italian Short Film Night
events.venue: Light House Cuba Cinema, 29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Enjoy six bold new Italian short films that explore life, love and memory. Passionate feelings, delicious cuisine, the romance of nature and her people burst onto screen.

The 10th annual Italian Short Film Night is presented by Show Me Shorts in partnership with the Embassy of Italy. Book now as tickets will sell out.

- 'Il Giardino Delee Delizie (The Garden of Delights)', a documentary set in a prison culinary school, directed by Simone Spampinato
- 'Il Velo (The Veil)', a drama about a lovelorn nun, directed by Cristian Patanè
- 'The Boy's Gaze', a romance about love and blindness, directed by Niccolò Salvato - WORLD PREMIERE
- 'The Eggregore's Theory', an AI science fiction reverie, directed by Andrea Gatopoulos
- 'Il Memorato di Novello (Novel of Lost Memories)', a mockumentary set in a town where people have the longest memories, directed by Bruno Ugioli, Domenico Bruzzese, Mauro Mola
- 'Ronzio (Hum)', a drama about a beekeeper with ADHD, directed by Niccolò Donatini

Language: Italian, with English subtitles
Total run time: 73 minutes
Rating: to be advised

Season Pass
Save money and join us for our entire winter season for only $50, to watch the following programmes*, only available in Auckland and Wellington. Book via Humanitix:

Italian Short Film Night – 31 March AKL / 1 April WLG
German Short Film Night - 28 April AKL / 29 April WLG
Turkish African Short Film Night - 26 May AKL / 27 May WLG
Spanish Short Film Night - 23 June AKL / 1 July WLG
Cambodian Short Film Night - 21 July AKL / 22 July WLG

*Dates and programmes subject to change.

events.id: The Big Bike Film Night 2026-Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Big Bike Film Night 2026
events.venue: Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Big Bike Film Night is on a mission - bringing the best short cycling films from around the world together for you.

Showcasing 2.5 hours of dazzling bike films that have everything a cycle-centric audience could want – action, drama, humour, and plenty of inspiration. The evening is unashamedly and utterly, utterly, utterly designed and devised to delight the two-wheel devotee!

These short films are a great reminder of why we love to ride our bike; with stories that captivate us, stories that move us, and most importantly of all – inspiring us to get out and ride. All celebrate the fun, adventure, and joy that cycling enables.

PREVIOUS ATTENDEE’S COMMENTS:
“One of the most inspiring events of the year"
“The Big Bike Film Night is always a wonderful, eclectic, wacky mix of films and I love it!”
“If you haven’t experienced the cinematic excellence of The Big Bike Film Night – then YOU MUST!!!”

So...listen to the voice inside you; it’s telling you to go. Grab your mates, GO ON… grab your bike and come along for the ride!!

FILM CLASSIFICATION: THE BIG BIKE FILM NIGHT is exempt from classification, but the films are suitable for the entire family, and would obtain PG ratings.

events.id: King Kong-The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Roxy Cinema
events.name: King Kong
events.venue: The Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Details
Directed by Peter Jackson
Starring Andy Serkis, Jack Black, Kyle Chandler, Jamie Bell, Naomi Watts, Adrien Brody, Colin Hanks
Come celebrate The Roxy's 15th Birthday as screen again the first film to ever grace our beautiful big screen!

--- Peter Jackson's expansive remake of the 1933 classic follows director Carl Denham (Jack Black) and his crew on a journey from New York City to the ominous Skull Island to film a new movie. Accompanying him are playwright Jack Driscoll (Adrien Brody) and
actress Ann Darrow (Naomi Watts), who is whisked away by the monstrous ape, Kong, after they reach the island. The crew encounters dinosaurs and other creatures as they race to rescue Ann, while the actress forms a bond with her simian captor. ---

Upgrade your ticket to the FEAST YOUR EYES (FYE) experience which includes themed drink and dessert.

At Feast Your Eyes, cinephiles and foodies will be treated to a movie and food experience in the cinema at the perfect after dinner event!

Admit only tickets are $15, but you can upgrade to a Feast Your Eyes tickets for $45. All Feast Your Eyes tickets include a themed dessert* and cocktail to enjoy during the film.

The upstairs bar will open at 6:30pm for cocktail and dessert collection. The pre-show kicks off at 6:55pm and the feature begins at 7:05pm.

*No dietaries catered for.

events.id: MELT - The Listening Party-Valhalla, 154 Vivian Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: MELT - The Listening Party
events.venue: Valhalla, 154 Vivian Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
"We have minutes of our lives before the sun and earth collide..."

STAFFROOM presents the release of his brand new electronica album, MELT!
After three years in development, Staffroom is finally ready to share his entirely self produced work which cleverly explores climate, culture and the mind. The album blends sounds of the past and present through a rich, sample heavy palette, all set to danceable beats.

'MELT - The Listening Party' will be more than just your average gig. Staffroom's live singing will be accompanied by art installations made in collaboration with local artists. Merchandise and CDs will be available at the door, and some sick new tracks will be available for your ears!

"Dream it all away..." and enjoy our celebration of music, dance and art!

events.id: Bic Runga & The NZSO-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Bic Runga & The NZSO
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
More than 60 classical musicians will collaborate with Bic Runga and her band for a special concert in Wellington. Bic Runga & the NZSO is a concert of songs from Bic’s much-anticipated new album ‘Red Sunset’. The performance will also feature a selection of hits and best-loved songs spanning the artist’s extraordinary 31-year career.   
This is the only leg of the ‘Red Sunset’ tour featuring orchestra, so expect the songs to be transformed by the powerful accompaniment. Also involved in the show is Bic’s partner, multi-instrumentalist Kody Nielson of The Mint Chicks and Unknown Mortal Orchestra fame.   
Bic Runga (Ngāti Kahungunu) is a multi-award-winning singer-songwriter. The youngest inductee into the New Zealand Music Hall of Fame, she shot to fame in 1996 with the single ‘Drive’. Her 1997 debut album ‘Drive’, including the hit ‘Sway’, won the Tūī for Album of the Year. Over her career, she has won 20 Tūī awards — the most for an Aotearoa solo artist, including Best Female Vocalist four times. 
A New Zealand Order of Merit recipient for services to music. Bic is also acclaimed for her collaborations and live performances with other music icons, such as Tim and Neil Finn, Sir Dave Dobbyn and Anika Moa. She’s also inspired and influenced a new generation of New Zealand musicians. Lorde described her as “New Zealand music royalty” when she performed on stage with Bic in 2019.
events.description:
Bic Runga & the NZSO is a concert of songs from Bic’s much-anticipated new album ‘Red Sunset’.
The youngest inductee into the New Zealand Music Hall of Fame, she shot to fame in 1996 with the single ‘Drive’.
Her 1997 debut album ‘Drive’, including the hit ‘Sway’, won the Tūī for Album of the Year.
She’s also inspired and influenced a new generation of New Zealand musicians.
Lorde described her as “New Zealand music royalty” when she performed on stage with Bic in 2019.

events.id: Adrian Cox Trio - Live in Wellington-G20 Apartments, 20 Garrett St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Adrian Cox Trio - Live in Wellington
events.venue: G20 Apartments, 20 Garrett St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
April 2nd - Adrian Cox Trio
Bedlam & Squalor, Wellington
Doors 7pm Concert 8pm
Internationally acclaimed clarinetist Adrian Cox and his trio return to Aotearoa for a series of performances promoting their recently released album 'Club Croco'.
Adrian is a leading authority on New Orleans music, bringing an authentic and electrifying voice to the idiom. As a celebrated exponent of the style, Cox has dedicated his career to preserving and pushing the boundaries of early jazz, earning praise for his masterful technique, deep historical knowledge, and infectious energy on stage. His unrivalled feel and virtuosity for the music he plays have led to concerts with the biggest names in jazz including two concerts with Wynton Marsalis and the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra both in London and New York. Adrian is currently touring with his exceptional Trio featuring the New Zealand born guitarist Honey Boulton and New York based bassist Alex Gilson, a true world class power house that leaves audiences spellbound.
There will be Vinyl, CDs and a super special fanzine (w/ EP download) available for purchase.

events.name: Hunt for the Wilderpeople | Q&A
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Directed by Taika Waititi
Starring Julian Dennison, Rachel House, Rhys Darby, Rima Te Wiata, Sam Neill, Cohen Holloway
To celebrate 'Hunt for the Wilderpeople''s 10th year anniversary, we will be hosting a Q&A with Rima Te Wiata, Rachel House, Cohen Holloway, Tioreore Ngatai-Melbourne and Moniker.
Moniker will be playing live after the show upstairs in the Grand Lobby with bar specials including the limited release of Garage Project's 'Majestical Lager'.
--- Ricky (Julian Dennison) is a defiant young city kid who finds himself on the run with his grumpy foster uncle (Sam Neill) in the wild New Zealand bush. A national manhunt ensues and the two are forced to put aside their differences and work together to survive in this hilarious and heart-felt adventure. ---
All tickets are $21

events.id: Simon Donald-21 Blair St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Simon Donald
events.venue: 21 Blair St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Simon Donald made a serious impression when he opened for Ash Grunwald at Meow Nui last year and his follow‑up show at The Underworld confirmed it — this is a musician who knows exactly how to hold an audience.
If you missed him then, here’s your chance to put that right.
If you caught him before, you already know why you’ll want to be back.
Simon is a master of acoustic blues: clean, expressive playing, a warm stage presence, and a set that moves effortlessly between his own originals, classic blues, and some slyly brilliant reworkings of rock tunes, including a few Creedence Clearwater Revival favourites.
And this time he’s playing for us at Lynott’s — the freshly rebranded Thunder Road at 21 Blair St, just off Courtenay Place.
Come hear one of the most engaging blues players on the circuit right now.
$20 or $10 for Capital Blues Inc members.

ANI SAAFA, an indie four piece birthed from the Ōtepoti scene in 2022, are making the trip round the country to raise money for their DEBUT ALBUM set to release in mid 2026.

Emily Alice (band)

events.long_description:
Hutt City Night Market - 2 April 2026
Experience the sights, smells, and flavors of a traditional night market right in the heart of Lower Hutt this autumn. 🍂

The Hutt City Night Market is an international celebration of food, bringing together a delicious mix of cuisines from around the world.

Stroll through bustling stalls, grab a cozy bite (or three), bring your friends and family, stay for a picnic, and enjoy the crisp autumn air in a vibrant community setting.

🕓 4pm – 8pm
🌧️ Every Thursday night, rain or shine (unless there is a thunderstorm or severe weather, in which case the market will be cancelled)
📍 Dowse Square, 45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt (opposite HCC building and next to Dowse Museum)

The food trucks attending each week will be announced by Tuesday, so keep an eye out to see if your favorite is rolling in!

👉 Like and follow Wellington Street Eats on Facebook & Instagram for all updates:
@wellingtonstreeteats

See you there under the autumn skies! 🍁

events.id: Bret McKenzie Freak Out City Tour | Wellington [SOLD OUT]-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011-All Events In
events.name: Bret McKenzie Freak Out City Tour | Wellington [SOLD OUT]
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
events.long_description:
Bret McKenzie Freak Out City Tour | Wellington [SOLD OUT]
Grammy and Academy Award–winning songwriter, composer and performer Bret McKenzie has announced two highly anticipated dates in his homeland of New Zealand in support of his latest album, Freak Out City. Touching down in Wellington and Auckland this April, the shows follow a run of sold-out international concerts and mark McKenzie’s long-awaited return to New Zealand stages. Best known as one half of Flight of the Conchords, Bret McKenzie has built an acclaimed solo career defined by sharp wit, impeccable musicianship and a deep love of classic songwriting.

Following his solo debut Songs Without Jokes, Bret released his second project, Freak Out City, in August 2025 via Sub Pop Records. Co-produced with longtime collaborator Mickey Petralia, the collection was shaped on the road with his eight-piece band, The State Highway Wonders. The influence of his musical heroes Harry Nilsson and Randy Newman resonates again on this record, where Bret playfully weaves humour and heart into his songs. Hailed as “upbeat, well-made and refreshingly smart” (Daily Mirror) and “superbly crafted with a nod to the greats (God is in the TV), the record highlights Bret’s evolving artistry, blending sharp lyricism, lush arrangements and his unmistakable offbeat charm.

The upcoming shows will feature newly-released songs alongside old favorites, plus hilarious recountings of key moments from his colorful career, songs written on the fly using audience members’ real-life stories, and various other onstage antics.

Following sold-out performances overseas, these intimate New Zealand dates offer a rare opportunity to experience Freak Out City live in two of Aotearoa’s most beloved venues.

Tickets go on sale Friday 16 January at 9am via ticketek.co.nz.

Artist presale begins Wednesday January 14 at 10am local time. Sign up for access via the mailing list at bretmckenzie.os.fan
