Pipi, the Kororā/Little Blue Penguin from Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo, has woken up to a bit of an Easter emergency… her eggs have gone missing from her nest!
The Welly wind has been at it again… sweeping Pipi’s eggs along the hill of the Wellington Cable Car.
She’s called in the Easter Bunny for backup, and now they need a team of sharp-eyed little helpers (that’s you!) to track them down.
Hop your way to the Wellington Cable Car this Easter and join the hunt.
Here’s how it works:
Pick up your egg hunt sheet from the ticket box
Keep count of all the hidden eggs you can spot in the terminals and along the track from inside the cars
Don’t forget to BYO pen!
Hand in your best guess to our ticket-box
If you correctly spot the number of Pipi’s missing eggs, you’ll be rewarded with a small Easter treat (while stocks last).
Bring your whānau and your best egg-spotting skills to help Pipi bring her eggs safely home.

Hop your way to the Wellington Cable Car this Easter and join the hunt.

events.long_description:
Join our Italian conversation classes and boost your fluency!
Small groups, all levels welcome, and plenty of fun with native Italian speakers.

In addition to classes, we offer a range of extra activities to immerse you in Italian language and culture, including:

Italian film screenings

Visits to art galleries with discussions in Italian

Lecture series on Italian art, history, and literature

Italian book clubs

Experience Italian language in a dynamic, cultural, and interactive way!

Our courses run throughout the year, divided into four terms, so you can start at any term that works best for you.

For more details and info, feel free to get in touch with Antonella:
evans.sarti@gmail.com

events.name: Massage (By appointment only)
events.venue: 12 King Street, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Oasis Centre offers Massage by appointment. During your spiritual Massage session you will feel your body, mind and soul nurtured in the most spiritual, unwinding and relaxing way. Mindful healing touch and care is given so that you can receive the full benefit of this experience.
The benefits of Massage Therapy are many and varied, and include:
•Body realignment
•Clarity for the mind
•Motivation
•Pain relief
•Rejuvenation
•Relaxation
•Relief of stress and tension
•Sense of calmness
•Therapeutic tension release
Massage is an excellent therapy for relieving pain and stiffness from the muscles of the face, scalp, neck, body, legs, upper back to the shoulders. We often use hot stones to promote deeper relaxation and muscle release.
Massage/Spiritual Healing is also an effective method to bring about a deep relaxing, enjoyable and harmonious healing. In some cases it will deal with the emotional release that is required at the time.
Come and enjoy the healing atmosphere. Have a Massage session and experience the benefits it gives to your mind, body, soul and spirit.
Each Massage session usually takes about an hour. Bookings are required as spaces are limited.
*Bookings Only*
Cost: Unfortunately we have had to set a $25 minimum on the Koha/Donation.
Phone Anne on 027 6404968

events.venue: 7A Rintoul St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Newtown Crafty Mums (Bring Your Baby)
A creative morning for mothers who need a moment for themselves.
Join me on Fridays for a weekly get-together where you can craft, chat and connect while your little one plays nearby.
Bring your own craft project or choose something from our shared craft table (painting, crochet, beads, embroidery…), all crafts are welcome.
Morning tea will be available (tea/coffee/juice and some snacks) and I will aim to cover all dietary needs.
A play space will be set up for babies, with treasure baskets, toys and books to explore independently while you craft nearby. Please bring along any snacks your little one may need.
This is a koha event with a suggested $5 to $10 contribution to help me cover cost of supplies and morning tea. The lovely people at the Newtown Community Centre are offering me 4 weeks free of charge to get the word out
Together, let’s create a warm, welcoming space to slow down, make something with our hands, and build our village.
I can’t wait to meet you �
Fridays
9:30am – 11:30am
Newtown Community Centre
Koha (booking mandatory due to limited space)

events.id: Stations of the Cross-Sacred Heart Cathedral, 40 Hill St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Stations of the Cross
events.venue: Sacred Heart Cathedral, 40 Hill St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description: The object of the Stations is to help the faithful to make in spirit, as it were, a pilgrimage to the chief scenes of Christ's sufferings and death, and this has become one of the most popular of Catholic devotions. It is carried out by passing from Station to Station, with certain prayers at each and devout meditation on the various incidents in turn. It is very usual, when the devotion is performed publicly, to sing a stanza of the "Stabat Mater" while passing from one Station to the next. All are welcome.

events.id: Easter Pole Weekend 2026-55 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Easter Pole Weekend 2026
events.venue: 55 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Easter Pole Weekend – WELLINGTON, New Zealand
Friday 3rd April – Monday 6th April
16 workshops – Pole Showcase – Pole Photoshoots
*
This Easter we have an exciting four-day Pole Weekend planned at Body Electric Pole Dance Studio!
On Friday 3rd, Saturday 4th and Sunday 5th April we run our workshops. We have invited Body Electric instructors plus guests to create something special for you – there is A LOT of variety to choose from: from Pole Tricks and Flow classes to Choreography and Twerk workshops. You can choose one workshop if you want! Or we would recommend jumping in the deep end and doing several! We have a package of 6 workshops for those of you wanting to be extra hardcore. We also have a special deal of 2 workshops plus a pole show ticket for $95!
*
On Saturday night The Pole Room Easter-themed showcase “Let There Be Light” will impress and entertain with workshop instructor performances plus special guests. $30 tickets from www.eventfinda.co.nz
Monday 6th April, is devoted to pole photoshoots! Book a session with talented pole photographer Dave Giles and get pics of your favourite moves. This shoot is suitable for pole students of all levels. Maree & Giada will be there to help you with poses. $120 for 20 mins (2x 10min sessions) 4 edited images.
*
PRICES
1 workshop - $40
2 workshops, plus a ticket to the pole show - $95
6 Workshops - $210
Pole Show Tickets - $30 through Eventfinda
Pole Photo Shoot with Dave Giles - $120 for 20 min shoot (2x10 min, with room between for a costume change). 4 edited images with the option to purchase more. Help will be given by Giada and Maree for posing.
Please email us to book: info@bodyelectricpole.co.nz
WORKSHOPS (Scroll down for more detailed descriptions of workshops)
*
Friday 3rd April
10.30am Pole Flow, Leg Rotations & Flexible Hips (Ana)
12 noon Belly Dance: Making It Slinky (Pip E-Lysaah)
1.30pm Sensual Awakening: Unleash your Inner Siren (Vixen Temple)
3.00pm Nail Your Inverts (Elisa Puccioni)
4.30pm Feeling the Floor (Karina Shann)
6.00pm Choreography Kickstarter (Maree & Giada)
Saturday 4th April
10.30am Chair Tricks & Choreo (Billie Jean)
12 noon Creative Practices For Freestyle (Rachael)
1.30pm Stripper Flow (Maddie May)
3.00pm Fonji 101 (Terumi Isogai)
4.30pm Tumbles & Drops (Terumi Isogai)
Saturday 4th April, 7.30pm, The Fringe Bar, Wellington
Pole Show featuring Teachers & Guests – “Let There Be Light”
Sunday 5th April
10.30am Booty Electric: Twerkshop (Emma Hopkins)
12 noon Bendy Bunnies: Stretch (Judith Dillon)
1.30pm Hula Hoop 101 (Dylan Daisy)
3.00pm Perfect Pirouettes (Gabrielle McElwee)
4.30pm Handstands with Julia (Julia Bromley)
Workshop details
Friday 3rd April
10.30am Pole Flow, Leg Rotations & Flexible Hips (Ana)
Leg rotations can create different shapes, lines and feeling to our movement. They can elevate our pole flow and are foundational to many transitions and moves. Leg rotations start from the hips. We can make them more comfortable and access more dramatic shapes by conditioning our hip flexors and increasing our range of motion. In this workshop we will learn:
•Conditioning exercises for our hip flexors that you can add to your regular practice
•The foundational movement of leg rotations and how they can elevate and create variety in our pole moves
•A variety of dance elements that use leg rotations, that you can add to your pole flow or routines
(All levels)
12 noon Belly Dance: Making It Slinky (Pip E-Lysaah)
The magic isn’t in the big tricks. It’s in the pause before them.
In Making It Slinky, we’ll dive into the slow moments that define a performance, the entrance, the exit, and every delicious transition in between. This workshop is all about how you arrive, how you linger, and how you leave them slightly unwell. We’ll explore controlled undulations, weighted steps, breath-led movement, and the art of stretching time so the audience forgets to blink. Less rushing. More reigning.
This is signature muscle isolation and control, the kind that makes a simple belly roll wave feel scandalous. We’ll break down my structured approach to belly rolls, arm waves, and wraps ... not just how to execute them, but how to layer and thread them so nothing ever “stops.” Everything melts, coils, and unwraps with intention. You’ll learn how to build tension through transitions, turning the space between movements into something almost indecent. If you’ve ever been told to “do more,” this workshop is about doing less… slower… and making it hit harder.
(All levels)
1.30pm Sensual Awakening: Unleash your Inner Siren (Vixen Temple)
Get out of your head and into your body with Vixen Temple! In this empowering workshop, you'll explore techniques to tap into your inner sensuality and trust your body. Harness your erotic energy to move with seductive fluidity and hypnotic flow.
(All levels)
3.00pm Nail Your Inverts (Elisa Puccioni)
Elisa will guide you into a series of exercises, precise techniques, and insider tips to help you finally master those elusive inverts and leg hangs. Whether it's refining your form, building strength, or gaining more aerial control, we've got you covered! This workshop is ideal for beginners who are starting with their inverts up to intermediate students that want to polish their inverting techniques. Don't forget to BYH (bring your heels!) for an extra challenge and added flair!
(All levels)
4.30pm Feeling the Floor (Karina Shann)
In this workshop Karina will utilize fundamental floorwork moves such as shoulder stands, rolls, duck dives and body lines to guide you through ways that you can accentuate parts of your body, move with intention and captivate the audience. She’ll teach transitions and combos incorporating the fundamentals that you can take away to elevate your floor game. Heels optional but recommended. Socks, leg warmers optional, knee pads necessary.
(Prerequisite – Backwards & forwards roll)
6.00pm Choreography Kickstarter (Maree & Giada)
This workshop is perfect to get you started on your performance journey.
• Practical exercises to unlock your creativity, start building combos for your routines, and practice transitions between moves.
• Talk through how to structure a routine, performance tips and tricks, how to deal with nerves and much, much more!
This workshop is suitable for students of all levels. If you are currently working on a dance for a show, we can help with that. But you don't have to have a performance in mind to get heaps out of this session!
Saturday 4th April
10.30am Chair Tricks & Choreo (Billie Jean)
A burlesque classic – Express! Let’s get our groove on to this banger. We will learn some cute chair tricks and transitions to sass our way through this choreography. Beginner friendly, this dance is suitable for all levels! Plus, progressions for those who want to get upside down.
Wear comfortable clothes, socks or dance heels.
(All levels)
12 noon Creative Practices for Freestyle (Rachael)
Freestyling is a fantastic way to develop your movement style. This workshop will cover various concepts like tempo, sensation, texture, intention. You’ll learn new approaches for practicing freestyling and have fun while gaining a new appreciation for the practice.
(All levels)
1.30pm Stripper Flow (Maddie May)
Learn how to slink around the pole in your own authentic way, focusing on embellishing movements, fluid transitions and musicality!
(All levels)
3.00pm Fonji 101 (Terumi Isogai)
This is an introduction course for fonji related moves. Starting with preparation exercises to get used to the movement. Learn the techniques to execute fonji safely and effectively!
(Prerequisite: Shoulder mount, preferably aerial)
4.30pm Tumbles & Drops (Terumi Isogai)
You climb up the pole, and now what? Learn fun ways to come down the pole!
(Prerequisite: Aerial invert. Leg hang)

7.30pm Pole Show – “Let There Be Light”
Sunday 5th April
10.30am Booty Electric: Twerkshop (Emma Hopkins)
Come along to Emma’s beginner Twerkshop where you will learn basic twerk moves from the floor to on the pole. We will cover different styles of twerking and then put them together for a sexy, bootiful routine. We need to protect our knees while we are popping it like it's hot so please bring kneepads. Sneakers are also recommended.
(All Levels)
12 noon Bendy Bunnies: Stretch (Judith Dillon)
Level up your flexibility this Easter with fun and creative ways to move your body. Whether you've got a dedicated practice or you've never tried stretching before, all levels are welcome - Judith will adapt poses for every level and ability.
In this workshop we will focus on partner assisted stretches and stretching with props (blocks/wall) We will work on breathing into deep stretches, relaxing into passive holds, as well as muscle activation for some fun duo poses.
(All levels)
1.30pm Hula Hoop 101 (Dylan Daisy)
Learn the basics of hula hooping. Start the spin, maintain your core tension and you’re off! Using different body parts to flow and transition make hula hooping a fun and dynamic art form.
After a lifetime of commanding stages and perfecting her craft, Dylan is a fantastic and skilled hoop performer who has become a favourite in the Wellington performing arts scene. Bring a hoop if you have one (We will supply some).
(All levels)
3.00pm Perfect Pirouettes (Gabrielle McElwee)
Keen to learn some smooth ways to turn, flow and transition around the pole?
Want to improve your dance fluidity?
Then this workshop is for you! Pirouettes are a MUST HAVE for any pole dancer, so this workshop will cover pirouette technique, a variety of fun pirouette patterns & transitions which build in level, ending with a short flow sequence. Socks are recommended.
(All levels)
4.30pm Handstands with Julia (Julia Bromley)
Come and get upside down with Julia! This class workshop will focus on the basic foundations of handstands and will give you skills and drills to continue to work on towards your upside-down goals. You will be guided through exercises and drills to maximise strength and technique needed to begin or extend your handstand journey. Julia has been teaching workshops and private lessons for over 7 years in both circus, pole and dance studios and loves seeing students gain the body awareness and technique to attain their goals!
(All levels)
*
Prices:
Single Workshop session: $40 per person
2 workshops, plus a ticket to the pole show - $95
Group package: 6 Workshops - $210

events.name: Simon Donald
events.venue: Lynott's Irish Bar, 21 Blair Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Simon was a hit when he supported Ash Grunwald at Meow Nui last year. As a result, we booked him at The Underworld, and he was sensational. So, for those who couldn’t get to see him before, he will be playing for us at Lynott’s.

Lynott’s is the rebrand of Thunder Road at 21 Blair St, just off Courtenay Place.

Those who did see him before, I’m sure you’ll be back.

Simon plays skilful acoustic blues. He has some originals and also his interpretations of some classic blues tunes. He also does some clever reworkings of rock songs, including some Creedence Clearwater Revival.

Come and check him out.

$20 or $10 for Capital Blues Inc members.
events.description:
Simon was a hit when he supported Ash Grunwald at Meow Nui last year.
As a result, we booked him at The Underworld, and he was sensational.
So, for those who couldn’t get to see him before, he will be playing for us at Lynott’s.
Lynott’s is the rebrand of Thunder Road at 21 Blair St, just off Courtenay Place.
Simon plays skilful acoustic blues.

events.id: Adrian Cox Trio Club Croco Album Tour-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Adrian Cox Trio Club Croco Album Tour
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Internationally acclaimed clarinetist Adrian Cox and his trio return to Aotearoa for a series of performances promoting their recently released album 'Club Croco'.

Adrian is a leading authority on New Orleans music, bringing an authentic and electrifying voice to the idiom. As a celebrated exponent of the style, Cox has dedicated his career to preserving and pushing the boundaries of early jazz, earning praise for his masterful technique, deep historical knowledge, and infectious energy on stage. His unrivalled feel and virtuosity for the music he plays have led to concerts with the biggest names in jazz including two concerts with Wynton Marsalis and the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra both in London and New York. Adrian is currently touring with his exceptional Trio featuring the New Zealand born guitarist Honey Boulton and New York based bassist Alex Gilson, a true world class power house that leaves audiences spellbound.

There will be Vinyl, CDs and a super special fanzine (w/ EP download) available for purchase.

events.id: Service of Shadows-Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Service of Shadows
events.venue: Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A moving and powerful act of worship at the foot of the Cross, sung by the Cathedral Choir.
This service, which dates back to the very early days of the Church, is sometimes called Tenebrae. We kneel or sit as we listen in the readings describing the shadows which descended on our Lord during his last hours of life on earth.
After each reading, the candles are extinguished and the shadows fall.
The Choir takes up the most ancient of hymns, Kyrie Eleison – Lord have mercy, Christ have mercy, Lord have mercy, sung in Greek, the original language of the Church. We pray and watch in silence.
After the concluding prayer, the bell will be tolled 33 times, one for each year of our Lord's life.
Then like the first disciples, we leave in silence and sadness, but know that this is not the end of the story.

events.id: 2 Day Easter Extravaganza + Stockcar King of the Arena-85 Cuba Street, New Zealand 4410,85 Cuba St, Palmerston North 4410, New Zealand, Palmerston North-All Events In
events.name: 2 Day Easter Extravaganza + Stockcar King of the Arena
events.venue: 85 Cuba Street, New Zealand 4410,85 Cuba St, Palmerston North 4410, New Zealand, Palmerston North
events.long_description:
2 Day Easter Extravaganza + Stockcar King of the Arena
A massive double-header Easter weekend at the Robertson Prestige International Speedway will see the most sought-after Stockcar title in the Manawatu decided, as the 'King of the Arena' is crowned. Add in Midgets for the open-wheel fans, plus all your favourite classes racing, and you have two epic nights of speedway entertainment!

Kicking off on Good Friday is qualifying for the Manawatu Speedway Club Stockcar King of the Arena Championship, along with the Lucas Oil Superstock Series finale, Lucas Oil Super Saloon Series finale, ENZED Midget 30 and the Manawatu Streetstock Championship.

Good Friday promises to be an outstanding night as qualifying gets underway for the Manawatu Speedway Club Stockcar King of the Arena. Local drivers remain among the favourites to take the ultimate title and the massive $3,000 in prize money. With stirrers’ money and championship pride on the line, expect some of the hardest Stockcar racing seen this season.

Current Travel Managers Season Points leader 172p Daniel Burmeister looks a likely podium contender, alongside 48p Josh Mulcahy, 134p Rebecca Barr and 76p Cameron Renwick, who are all at the pointy end of the standings and in with a real shot at the podium. The battle for this title will be intense - tactics will come into play, and only the best will rise to the top. A hard-fought contest will determine the top 32 Stockcars for the championship.

The familiar roar of Superstocks hitting the dirt is an institution in Palmerston North, and a quality field of these fearless drivers is ready to race in the Lucas Oil Superstock Series finale. Four races will feature in the programme - three heats and a final - with $1,000 on offer for the winner. Local drivers will be at the forefront, while visiting competitors aim to spoil their chances.

The Manawatu Streetstock Championship will have fans reminiscing about the glory days of Valiants, Commodores, and Falcons racing side-by-side for prestigious honours. With drivers from across the North Island, this title is always hotly contested and delivers spectacular limited-contact racing.

Super Saloons also join the packed programme for the Lucas Oil Super Saloon Series finale. With wide tyres, V8 engines, and distinctive low sportscar bodies, these machines deliver impressive wheel-to-wheel racing every lap, thrilling both drivers and spectators alike.

Two nights of sensational Midget racing are not to be missed. The weekend begins with the ENZED Midget 30, featuring competitors from across the North Island converging to contest the title. Local hard chargers 93p Karl McGill and 41p Shane Dewar will be doing their utmost to keep the honours at home.

Racing is starting at the earlier time of 5pm!

Manawatu Speedway Club Stockcar King of the Arena Format & Prize Money Schedule, please follow this link;

https://jeremystorage1.blob.core.windows.net/piranha-pnspeedway/b4c0e1a0-f048-4dbb-b84e-8e3d79306d9a-2025-26_Stockcar_King_of_the_Arena.pdf

Classes racing are: Stockcars, SuperStocks, Super Saloons, Midgets, Streetstocks

Drivers please register your intention to race using the following link for Good Friday –

https://www.pnspeedway.co.nz/forms/03-04-2026

With qualifying now complete, Saturday night kicks off with a Stockcar repechage race, along with the exciting addition of promoter/club choice drivers to complete the final 32-car field. The Manawatu Speedway Club Stockcar King of the Arena Champion will be crowned.

After Friday night’s qualifying action, there is no doubt that Saturday night’s Stockcar racing will have you on the edge of your seat. With a massive $3,000 in prize money and added incentives for drivers to stir up the championship, it’s set to be the best Stockcar event of the season.

Robertson Prestige International Speedway is home to a number of highly competitive Stockcar drivers, many of whom are genuine title contenders. Will team tactics come into play, or will it come down to individual brilliance to stand atop the podium? Make sure you are trackside to witness this prestigious championship being decided.

Consolation Stockcar races will also feature in the programme for drivers who didn’t make the top 32-car championship field.

The non-stop action continues with sensational Midget racing, going wheel-to-wheel as they compete for the Dewtec Manawatu Midget Championship. With a quality field of drivers who have dialled in their setups from the previous night, this championship is sure to come down to the wire and deliver edge-of-your-seat action. Two massive nights of the best Midget racing the lower North Island has seen in recent times - these speed demons mean business!

Adding further support to what promises to be a sensational night of speedway action are the impressive Minisprints, the future stars in the Youth Ministocks (PN Registered Only), and the Historic Stockcars, which will take you back to the good old days of stockcar racing.

Racing is starting at the earlier time of 5pm!

Classes racing are: Stockcars, Midgets, Minisprints, Youth Ministocks – PN Registered Cars only, Historic Stockcars

Drivers, please register your intention to race using the following link for Easter Saturday –

https://www.pnspeedway.co.nz/forms/04-04-2026

Admission prices per night are $30 for an Adult (16 years and over), $10 for Children aged 8-15, and $70 for a Family pass of 2 adults and up to four children. Kids aged 7 and under are FREE.

Tickets are available to pre-purchase online from https://www.eventfinda.co.nz , (tickets purchased incur an additional service fee) or on race day from Gate 1 (Waldegrave Street), Gate 2 (Cuba Street - Bridge) and Gate 3 (Cook Street) from 3.30pm both days.

Racing starts at the earlier time of 5pm both nights.

Click here to view a full list of the Arena terms and conditions of entry https://venuespn.co.nz/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/Terms-and-Conditions-of-Entry-2023.pdf

Consolation Stockcar races will also feature in the programme for drivers who didn’t make the top 32-car championship field.
With a quality field of drivers who have dialled in their setups from the previous night, this championship is sure to come down to the wire and deliver edge-of-your-seat action.

events.id: APRIL FULL MOON SONG AND SOUND CEREMONY-Paraparaumu Memorial Hall-All Events In
events.name: APRIL FULL MOON SONG AND SOUND CEREMONY
events.venue: Paraparaumu Memorial Hall
events.long_description:
APRIL FULL MOON SONG AND SOUND CEREMONY
Come and Sing, Sound and Meditate!

Want to mark the important time of the April Full Moon!!

Come and join with us on Friday 3rd April for a group ceremonial gathering in Paraparaumu.

We will explore sacred conscious sounding practices to balance and align the energy system, sing healing chants and songs from different traditions, and explore guided vocal improvisation for freedom, upliftment and joy!

There will be guided journal prompts around the energy of the April Full Moon and the whole session will finish with a full sound healing.

Everyone is welcome and there is no need for any particular preparation or pre existing skill.

Please get in touch with any questions or to book. Come and enjoy Song, Chant, Sound and Healing!!

So looking forward to gathering for the Full Moon. Come and join with us to invoke and celebrate the energies of balance, creation, abundance and celebration at this profound time of year.

This session will explore singing, chanting, sounding and conscious voice empowerment practices. We will also explore energy practices like Qigong and meditation. All within a ceremonial framework.

Everyone is welcome. Limited spaces. Get in touch soon

You are warmly invited to book to join this powerful sound and voice ceremony which will work with Spirit, the forces of Nature and Bryony's skill and expertise in creating sound for growth and transformation.

This is a special opportunity to tune into the sacred energies and potentialities of this time: opening us up to balance, healing, nourishment, new growth and positive change.

Bryony will invoke and work with the healing power of the supportive energies of Spirit, ancestors and our highest Selves. Bryony's has an extensive background experience with Spirit, the Earth forces and sacred lands; these forces are central to her practice and Self.

She will lead you firstly in a process of meditation, conscious sounding, toning and singing healing chants.

This use of our voices to co-create a sacred group sound is uplifting and transformative and will support you in your process of healing, releasing and visioning.

There will be time for guided journaling and intention setting.

The second section of the workshop will be a focused and dedicated sound bath which will bring balance and deep relaxation, into a sense of stillness and peace. There can be a sense of gratitude and appreciation.

Both the conscious use of the voice and the sound healing have many benefits, which are verified by science and which can effect us positively on the physical, mental, emotional, energetic and spiritual levels. A document which has more information about these benefits can be provided on request.

All are welcome:
I look forward to sharing with you and supporting you in this ceremony.

Investment for full 2.5 hours

$77 - full price - https://buy.stripe.com/bIY9Ep67A00xdwYcND

$59 - concession - https://buy.stripe.com/8wMaItcvY6oV0Kc7ti

Please contact me if finances are a challenge for you and we can make it work for you to join.

events.name: Easter Church Services
events.venue: St Ninian's Uniting Church, 208 Karori Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Something is stirring in Karori this Easter! St Ninian's and St Anselm's are joining together this Easter to celebrate the events that changed everything — and we'd love you to join us.

*Palm Sunday — 29 March, 10am
A king arrives, not as the world expected. Come and see.
St Ninian's, 208 Karori Road, Karori

*Good Friday — 3 April, 10am
The day the world went dark. A service of quiet reflection for all who have known loss, grief, or the long wait for things to be made right.
St Ninian's, 208 Karori Road, Karori

*Easter Sunday — 5 April, 10am
The stone is rolled away. Come and celebrate the most surprising morning in history — with music, community, and genuine joy.
St Anselm's, 30 Makara Road, Karori

Please make note of the venue for each service, we'd hate for you to go to the wrong place.

events.id: Game Review hosted by Armageddon Expo-Hnry Stadium, 105 Waterloo Quay, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Game Review hosted by Armageddon Expo
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, 105 Waterloo Quay, Wellington
events.long_description:
It's the 2nd ever Wellington Game Review!! This time, we're being hosted by Armageddon Expo. Try out games for free and support your local dev with feedback.

You can play these games:
- Dream Team Supreme = You're a scientist, I'm a scientist, and together we pilot a giant robot against a monster invasion in this 2P co-op roguelike deckbuilder. Two heads are better than one, so bring a friend!
- Coastal Postal = Soar across the sunlit Archipelago in your trusty delivery seaplane. Explore diverse islands, meet charming locals, and deliver packages by sea and sky in this short cozy adventure.
- Cooking with MA! = Go on an adventure through the spirit world to save your grandma from an evil sea serpent using the power of cooking! Cook dishes for different characters, exploring Korean cuisine in this story about identity, diaspora, and family.
- Fledgling = Fledgling is a 2D pixel-art game set in an original fantasy world. Its two core aspects, deckbuilding combat and branching narrative are deeply intertwined, putting player agency at the heart of the experience.
- NoteWorthy = NoteWorthy uses the language of games to teach the language of music, providing a low friction experience for learners as they practice reading notes on the stave, as well as reinforcing other basic music theory concepts through play.1

Tell your friends about it. Stop by with the family. Enjoy all the entertainment Armageddon has to offer and take a break by playing games. We're easy and we'll be gaming and chatting all Easter Weekend.

The Wellington Game Review is an effort by local game devs that band together to get feedback from regular, everyday gamers. The five games are being developed in town and each have something specific that the devs made for this playtest. Let's keep the fun going with some games made right down your street!

This time, we're being hosted by Armageddon Expo.

= Go on an adventure through the spirit world to save your grandma from an evil sea serpent using the power of cooking!
Stop by with the family.
The Wellington Game Review is an effort by local game devs that band together to get feedback from regular, everyday gamers.

events.name: COFFEE & CLAY - Hand made platters
events.venue: Sophie Florence Ceramics,100 Tinakori Road, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
COFFEE & CLAY - Hand made platters
Join us for a beautiful morning enjoying coffee and learning pottery techniques, to create your own selection of usable platters and dishes!

About this Event

Bubbles & Clay - Hand made platters
Come join us at Sophie Florence Ceramics for a relaxed morning creating your own ceramic platters and plates, ready for the perfect cheese baord. No proir clay experience is necessary - you'll be guided through the handbuilding techniques by award winning ceramicist, Sophie. With plenty of decorating and texture options to make them your own. Pick out a glaze, then collect your fired ready to use pieces 4 weeks later.
Get ready to make some beautiful pieces, in a working pottery studio, designed by Mary Daish Architects. If the Wellington weather permits, we will take our workshop into the beautiful summer garden.
Havana Coffe and home baked monrging tea are provided.
Come with your friends, or come alone to meet some like minded people

events.id: Junior Intro to Sculpting Workshop-Wētā Workshop Group Space, 129 Park Road, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Junior Intro to Sculpting Workshop
events.venue: Wētā Workshop Group Space, 129 Park Road, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get ready to sculpt a creature of your own! Step into the magical hands-on world of Wētā Workshop, where creatures, characters and crazy-cool creations come to life.

Looking for a fun, screen-free activity to spark their imagination these school holidays? Our daily Junior Intro to Sculpting Workshop is the perfect way for young creators to explore the world of sculpture.

In this 60-minute session, children will learn how to shape and sculpt their very own three-dimensional creature using everyday materials like tinfoil and modelling clay. Young makers will create their own imaginative creature from scratch and take home at the end to add any special finishing touches they want. They'll gain new creative skills they can keep exploring long after the workshop ends.

Workshops are kept small to ensure lots of individual attention and support. Whether a budding artist or someone who just loves to make things with their hands, this is a fun and confidence-building creative experience.

Ideal for ages 6 to 11. All materials provided.

Children must be accompanied by an adult. Parents and guardians are FREE.

events.id: The garden is a ‘pharmacy’ - Guided Walk-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The garden is a ‘pharmacy’ - Guided Walk
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Plants, both native and exotic, in the Wellington Botanic Garden and surrounding areas are or have been the basis for medicines that can enhance human health and well-being. This one-hour downhill walk will touch plant-based treatments for a range of afflictions from the ‘everyday’ to more serious.
Meet at the Cable Car Entrance Lookout, Upland Road, Kelburn.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)
This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.
Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.id: Kroměříž: From the Archive-6 Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Kroměříž: From the Archive
events.venue: 6 Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us in the informal setting of the Harbourside function venue on Wellington's waterfront for an unforgettable afternoon of music from the Habsburg Empire. Performed in The Queen's Closet's signature historical performance style, based as always on the latest scholarship and cutting-edge performance practice. You can expect our usual mix of the small and intimate works, offset by more grand music featuring our strings, winds, voices, and percussion.
We've been busy mining the Kroměříž archive for music never heard in Aotearoa New Zealand, and possibly not heard anywhere for over three-hundred years. Long-forgotten gems and masterpieces from the likes of Biber, Vejvanovský, and Rittler, alongside lesser-known names like Brückner (no, not that Bruckner, but a glorious Habsburg mass complete with natural trumpets and winds, by the lesser-known Jindřich Alois Brückner from seventeenth century Habsburg).

events.id: Easter Valley Egg Hunt 2026-1747 Main Road North Kaitoke,361A Main Rd N, Timberlea, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Easter Valley Egg Hunt 2026
events.venue: 1747 Main Road North Kaitoke,361A Main Rd N, Timberlea, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Easter Valley Egg Hunt 2026
🌼 Easter Valley Egg Hunt at Aston Norwood 🌼

Experience a relaxed, family‑friendly Easter adventure across 14 acres of autumn gardens.

What’s Included

Easter Egg Hunt

Explore the gardens at your booked session time and search for painted Easter tokens hidden throughout the grounds.

Adorable Hoppers Zone (Ages 1–3)

A gentle, dedicated area for our youngest hunters with easy‑to‑find tokens.

Egg Swap

Trade up to 5 tokens for Easter treats at the Easter Bunny’s Burrow.

(If you’ve collected more, you may need to return a few.)

The Easter Bunny’s Burrow

This year, the Burrow will be transformed into a cosy “bunny home” — a warm, playful space for photos, treats, coffee, and simple family games. A perfect spot to unwind after the hunt.

The Burrow is located at the far end of the gardens, and reaching it is part of the Easter Valley adventure. Families follow a short, scenic walk through the autumn trees, guided by gentle wayfinding and playful bunny‑themed markers. It’s designed to encourage exploration, movement, and time together outdoors.

Optional Add‑Ons

A seasonal High Tea will be available in the café over Easter weekend. Details and menus will be released closer to the event.

Bookings: www.astonnorwood.co.nz

What to Expect

Enjoy the gardens at their autumn best — Japanese Maples, crisp colours, and plenty of space for families to explore together. Hidden throughout the grounds are Easter‑themed painted stones, including special gold, silver, and bronze tokens.

A Note on the Walk to the Burrow

The Easter Bunny’s Burrow sits on the opposite side of the gardens and is reached by a gentle walk along our garden paths. This is an intentional part of the experience, encouraging families to enjoy the scenery and take their time as they make their way to the collection point.

Pram‑friendly routes are available, and families with little ones are welcome to move at their own pace — the Burrow remains open throughout your session.

Rain or Shine

The event runs in all weather.

A portion of ticket sales supports 4 A Better City, helping local children in need.

Disclaimer: No bunnies were harmed in the making of this event. 🐇✨

Please arrive as close to your session start time as possible and follow any instructions given by parking wardens upon arrival. Once you have parked make your way through the cafe to the ticket booth where your tickets can be scanned.

We cater to dairy and gluten free with a limited amount of Nomo, Jellybean, Marshmellow, and home baking Easter treats. Unfortunately we cannot Guarantee celiac suitable options

events.id: Hannah Simpson Album Release Party-Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Hannah Simpson Album Release Party
events.venue: Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Undercurrent presents Hannah Simpson!
Hannah Simpson is a life-long performer who wows her audiences with her soaring vocals, unexpected tumbling melodies and unique, soulful lyrics weaving love and politics together. With a full band she will be bringing her new album ‘Uncharted Waters’ to life featuring Hannah on vocals, piano, guitar, and flute; Vorn Colgan on the bass, trombone, accordion, violin; Linda Joy on vocals and trumpet; and Sam Tattersfield on something.
Hannah was the shining star of Cherry's Gemstones punkedalic group who released and sold out a six track EP 'Love in the Dreamscape Matrix Reality' in 2006. They toured the nation twice, opened at the Wellington town hall with raving teen and adult fans and featured at numerous events. More highlights of Hannah's musical career include featuring in a More FM event, leading 'High Resonators' as a house electronic duo, singing for the Capital Gospel band in 2015, and playing with the Avalanches in Paraparaumu, to mention a few! In England she performed at The Arts House, The Duke, Left Bank and Golden Lion venues in Bristol. She also had a residency at the Elbow Room, the Boston Tea Party Café and did a stint as a trapeze artist! The amazing range of colourful projects Hannah has been involved in over the years bear witness to her talent, versatility and dedication to music.
Saturday Apr 4
Doors 6pm
Music 6:30pm
$25 ($15 unwaged/$40 with CD)
Tickets available online and on the door!
https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/.../Hannah-Simpson-Album...
Find her album online: https://hannahsimpson.bandcamp.com/album/uncharted-waters
events.description:
The Undercurrent presents Hannah Simpson!
Hannah Simpson is a life-long performer who wows her audiences with her soaring vocals, unexpected tumbling melodies and unique, soulful lyrics weaving love and politics together.
With a full band she will be bringing her new album ‘Uncharted Waters’ to life featuring Hannah on vocals, piano, guitar, and flute; Vorn Colgan on the bass, trombone, accordion, violin; Linda Joy on vocals and trumpet; and Sam Tattersfield on something.
Hannah was the shining star of Cherry's Gemstones punkedalic group who released and sold out a six track EP 'Love in the Dreamscape Matrix Reality' in 2006.
They toured the nation twice, opened at the Wellington town hall with raving teen and adult fans and featured at numerous events.

events.id: Hobnail - The Three Part Tour comes to Vogelmorn!-93 Mornington Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Hobnail - The Three Part Tour comes to Vogelmorn!
events.venue: 93 Mornington Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hobnail released their 8th album Three Part in 2025, and in 2026 continue to tour NZ in support.
With “Jo Moir's barnstorming fiddle”, great songs and spectacular harmonies, Hobnail's live show will make your feet move and your heart sing.
Hobnail formed in 1994 with little ambition beyond playing a few shows and drinking a few beers. 32 years later and they have travelled from Porirua to Pittsburgh, New Plymouth to Norfolk Island, Auckland to Amsterdam, Germany to Gore, the Canadian Prairies to the Canterbury Plains. They have released 8 studio albums and a “Best Of” collection, played over 1,000 shows and made at least 100 trips across Cook Strait. There have been hundreds of flights, and somewhere north of 250,000 km’s in the tour van. They are 3 time finalists at the NZ Music Awards. In 2025 Hobnail released their 8th album “Three Part” and toured throughout New Zealand. They don’t plan to slow down any time soon.
Refreshingly down-to-earth and lovely with vocals so tightly knit they could be family
Mike Alexander NZ Herald.

“Their music has been described as a fusion of bluegrass, Celtic, folk and rock'n'roll. That description lists some of their influences, but doesn't do them justice. Their influences include jazz and poetry, and the songwriting reflects their personal experiences. They moved me across a broad spectrum. They invoke joy and sadness, spirit and laughter. They play music worth playing, sing lyrics worth hearing. And they play and sing with beauty and clarity. I know that concert will stay in my memory alongside the very best I have seen. A world-class act in our small corner of the world. Thank you, Hobnail!”
events.description:
Hobnail released their 8th album Three Part in 2025, and in 2026 continue to tour NZ in support.
Hobnail formed in 1994 with little ambition beyond playing a few shows and drinking a few beers.
There have been hundreds of flights, and somewhere north of 250,000 km’s in the tour van.
They don’t plan to slow down any time soon.
And they play and sing with beauty and clarity.

events.long_description:
Circus BBQ Party - Dowse Square
🎪 Come down to the Dowse Square for a family circus show these school holidays! 🎪

𝟒𝐭𝐡 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝟓𝐭𝐡 𝐀𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐥 @ 𝟐𝐩𝐦 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝟔𝐩𝐦

This show features three of Aotearoa's leading circus artists. Come be blown away by world class aerials , equilibristics, uniquely kiwi comedy and bizarre party tricks. All set on and around the humble BBQ.

𝐇"𝐀𝐰𝐞-𝐢𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠, 𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐨𝐮𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐚𝐛𝐬𝐨𝐥𝐮𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐲 𝐭𝐨𝐩-𝐧𝐨𝐭𝐜𝐡" - Erin Banks, CDC

Not to mention… the finale of this show is the marriage of a wild idea, bespoke engineered equipment and specialist skills which make it a world first!

𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐢𝐬 𝐚 𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐬𝐡𝐨𝐰! 𝐒𝐨 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐚𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐢𝐟 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐚 𝐠𝐨𝐨𝐝 𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞 𝐩𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐞 𝐩𝐨𝐩 𝐬𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐢𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐭!

events.name: Live Music - Cammy
events.venue: Plimmerton Boating Club,66 Moana Road, Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Live Music - Cammy
Cammy is a dynamic pop and R&B performer known for his soulful vocals, smooth guitar work, and magnetic stage presence. Based in Kāpiti, he’s become a favourite across bars, restaurants, and weddings nationwide, delivering cover sets that blend the best pop hits and crowd favourites from every era.
With rich vocal tones, a lush falsetto, and an impressive range, Cammy brings a fresh energy to each performance—whether it’s a chilled afternoon session or a packed night of singalongs. His sets are built around connection, rhythm, and feel-good moments, ensuring every audience leaves with a smile and a song in their head.
Alongside his extensive cover repertoire, Cammy also performs original material, showcasing his artistry and versatility beyond the stage.

events.id: The Boy With Wings-Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Boy With Wings
events.venue: Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
A comic, touching, and environmental story using the ageless wonder of puppetry, live music and exquisite hand-crafted sets that magically unfold out of boxes, telling an epic fable of one young man’s life-altering journey set against the backdrop of the miracle of the great migration of Kuaka (Bar-tailed Godwits). As these fragile birds set out on their extraordinary journey from New Zealand to Alaska, watching them take to the sky in awe is Jack-the-Everyman, who is contemplating a journey of his own to seek the ‘treasure’ that will save his dying orchard. Award-winning theatre company ‘Birdlife Productions’ promise laughter, tears and thrilling adventure, enticing us to ask the question, “how do we trust our inner map - how do we find our own wings?” Suitable for ages 5 to 95!

Performers: Bridget and Roger Sanders

events.id: The garden is a ‘pharmacy’ - Guided Walk-Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The garden is a ‘pharmacy’ - Guided Walk
events.venue: Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Plants, both native and exotic, in the Wellington Botanic Garden and surrounding areas are or have been the basis for medicines that can enhance human health and well-being. This one-hour downhill walk will touch plant-based treatments for a range of afflictions from the ‘everyday’ to more serious.

Meet at the Cable Car Entrance Lookout, Upland Road, Kelburn.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)

This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.

Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.id: TTRPG Live Show - Crepuscule at the Convention-Westpac Stadium, Waterloo Quay, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: TTRPG Live Show - Crepuscule at the Convention
events.venue: Westpac Stadium, Waterloo Quay, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Saving the world, one convention at a time.

A legally distinct Monster of the Week adventure where fictional fan favourites come to life at sundown, protecting the world from terrifying threats from other legally distinct pop culture dimensions.
Join the cast of Avatar: the Last Airbender as they dive into a TTRPG world not too different from our own, guided by Questbook Storyteller Ruby Kemp. Tackling the monstrous and mundane alike as the protectors of the Apocalypse Convention Centre, watch the adventure unfold with almost familiar characters like Professor How, The Bulk, and That One Lady From That Show In The '90s. Come laugh, gasp, and maybe even have the chance to break through the fourth wall and help our heroes save the day!
Monster of the Week is an iconic New Zealand-created TTRPG from game designer Michael Sands.

This is a free live TTRPG show at Armageddon, featuring a cast of Armageddon’s special guests from across the nerdy universe. Some may be seasoned adventurers, others might be rolling their very first dice, but either way, it’s going to be chaotic, unpredictable, and an absolute blast.
____________________________________________________________________
If you haven’t already, make sure to buy your ticket to Armageddon Expo below. As this these games will only be available once you are inside the show.
https://www.armageddonexpo.com

events.id: The Greatest Story Ever Told | Tea Time Talkies-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Greatest Story Ever Told | Tea Time Talkies
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Directed by George Stevens
Starring Max von Sydow, Charlton Heston, Dorothy McGuire
--- From his birth in Bethlehem to his death and eventual resurrection, the life of Jesus Christ (Max von Sydow) is given the all-star treatment in this epic retelling. Major aspects of Christ's life are touched upon, including the execution of all the newborn
males in Egypt by King Herod (Claude Rains) ; Christ's baptism by John the Baptist (Charlton Heston) ; and the betrayal by Judas after the Last Supper that eventually leads to Christ's crucifixion and miraculous return. ---
This is a TEA TIME TALKIE screening where a delicious Devonshire Tea* awaits for upgraded tickets holders (a cup of tea and traditional homemade scones with cream and jam). This is served upstairs in the Grand Lobby before the film commences.
Admit only tickets are available for $15 or you can upgrade to include a Devonshire Tea for $24.
*No dietaries catered for.

events.id: Gongs at Old St Paul's: A Vigil for Peace-Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Gongs at Old St Paul's: A Vigil for Peace
events.venue: Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
$40 waged
$30 unwaged
Old St Paul's Cathedral
2:30pm to 4:00pm
Sunday 5th April
Gongs at Old St Paul’s: A Vigil for Peace is a 90-minute immersive gong bath held in the warm afternoon light of Old St Paul's.
Musicians and gong players Nick Hyder and Erika Grant will offer a spacious, deep and relaxing sound journey designed to support rest, reflection, and a shared intention for peace. As the layered tones of the gongs move through the body and the resonant wooden interior of the building, participants are invited to slow down, listen inwardly, and settle into stillness.
This is a quiet gathering – open to all – to pause together and rest in sound.

*For this sound journey, we'll be lying on the carpet near the altar. Please bring a yoga mat, pillow, eye mask and blanket – or whatever it is you need to feel most warm and comfortable for this*

Scientific studies have highlighted the benefits of sound therapy, particularly gong baths, in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The rhythmic patterns and overtones of the gongs can help entrain brainwaves, leading to a meditative state and improved mental clarity.
Beyond the immediate effects, many participants report experiencing lasting benefits such as improved sleep, reduced anxiety, and enhanced mood following a gong bath session. By immersing yourself in the healing vibrations of the gongs, you can tap into a deeper sense of relaxation and rejuvenation.
THIS EVENT IS RESTRICTED TO 20 PEOPLE ONLY
About Us
Nick's background is in music production, sound art, and experiential therapies. His long-term pursuit of well-being is currently being explored through live events that apply concepts from nervous system science with the mysterious and immersive sounds of contemporary gongs.

Erika is a musician and astrologer based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. She has 35+ years musical experience. Erika has an interest in the healing properties of sound for mental and physical wellbeing, which she currently explores through her gong bathing practice.
Disclaimer
Gong baths are safe and supportive for most people. However, please consult your doctor or mental health professional before attending if you:
-have a pacemaker or other implanted medical device
-live with epilepsy or a sound-sensitive condition
-are in the first trimester of pregnancy
-are experiencing severe or unmanaged mental health challenges (such as psychosis, schizophrenia, or PTSD)
If you have recent injuries, surgeries, or other health concerns, let us know so we can make sure you’re comfortable.
Everyone responds differently to sound. If at any point during the session you feel overwhelmed or uncomfortable, you are welcome to sit up, move gently, or step outside. Your wellbeing always comes first.

events.venue: 15 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Our Out in the Pool sessions are regular monthly sessions for all queer, transgender, gender diverse and non-binary folks
We invite our rainbow communities and allies to dive in and swim in a safe and welcoming environment at Keith Spry Pool. These sessions are held on the first Sunday of every month and run 5pm-7pm, starting slightly later in February with our first event on Sunday 8 February 2026.
Out in the Pool evenings at Keith Spry have:
Gender neutral changing rooms and bathrooms, as well as our Men's and Women's changing facilities.
Recreation lanes with inflatables, as well as the Dive board open (as staffing allows).
Lane space will be available to swim some lengths or aqua jog, and the teaching pool will be open as staffing allows.
Spa and Sauna available
Music, swimming and good vibes only.
There is no additional cost for these sessions, simply turn up and pay our regular pool entry fees. There is no need to register, just come on down!

events.venue: Sky Stadium,Westpac Stadium, Waterloo Quay, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
TTRPG Live Show - Crepuscule at the Convention
Saving the world, one convention at a time.


A legally distinct Monster of the Week adventure where fictional fan favourites come to life at sundown, protecting the world from terrifying threats from other legally distinct pop culture dimensions.

Join the cast of Avatar: the Last Airbender as they dive into a TTRPG world not too different from our own, guided by Questbook Storyteller Ruby Kemp. Tackling the monstrous and mundane alike as the protectors of the Apocalypse Convention Centre, watch the adventure unfold with almost familiar characters like Professor How, The Bulk, and That One Lady From That Show In The '90s. Come laugh, gasp, and maybe even have the chance to break through the fourth wall and help our heroes save the day!

Monster of the Week is an iconic New Zealand-created TTRPG from game designer Michael Sands.


This is a free live TTRPG show at Armageddon, featuring a cast of Armageddon’s special guests from across the nerdy universe. Some may be seasoned adventurers, others might be rolling their very first dice, but either way, it’s going to be chaotic, unpredictable, and an absolute blast.
____________________________________________________________________

If you haven’t already, make sure to buy your ticket to Armageddon Expo below. As this these games will only be available once you are inside the show.

https://www.armageddonexpo.com

events.long_description:
Join us EVERY Sunday for a 90 minute Heated Flow for koha entry (ie, give what you can)! Proceeds are donated to a different charitable organisation each month that's committed to supporting the wellbeing of Aotearoa.

This weekly class is open to everyone, regardless of yoga experience - haere mai! BYO mat or borrow one from us. Towel hire $3 if needed, and make sure to bring a water bottle!

To book: Download the AWHI app to view the timetable, or simply show up in good time on Sunday!

WHAT IS 'KOHA'?
Koha loosely translates to 'gift' or exchange. In other words, pay what you can for the class!

For more information about our studio, visit our website at awhiyoga.co.nz

events.long_description:
The Upper Hutt Hunting Competition
This easter sees competitive hunting return to Upper Hutt, with Brewtown’s first annual Upper Hutt Hunting Competition!

With 55 hours of hunting culminating in the final weigh-in taking place in the heart of Brewtown, this is a chance for the region’s best to test their mettle and prove themselves the ultimate Pig, Deer or Goat hunter.

⚖️Weigh-in day
Sunday 5th April
11am – 6pm

Join us for a full-blown celebration of the hunt, for both hunters and the public alike.

Delicious eats from Welly’s best foodies, the Brewtown bar serving up its finest brews, live music, market stalls, educational stands on hunting and conservation, activities for the kids and of course the all-important weigh-in and prize-giving!

Entry to the Sunday event is completely FREE!

Keen to enter the comp?
Follow the link to the Brewtown website for all the category info, rules and registration.
https://www.brewtown.co.nz/event/hunt/

events.id: The Fabulous Carmen Walk Tour: Tea, Toasties and Trade-Ilott Green, Cnr Jervois Quay & Harris St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Fabulous Carmen Walk Tour: Tea, Toasties and Trade
events.venue: Ilott Green, Cnr Jervois Quay & Harris St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Entrepreneur, entertainer, madam and mayoral candidate Carmen Rupe was based in Wellington for less than 15-years but has left us a rich legacy of memories and inspiring moments.

Join us on this free 90-minute community walk-tour as we trace Carmen's Wellington years (1967-1980). We'll visit some of the remaining inner-city business locations and imagine some of those that are no longer here: Le Balcon, Carmen's International Coffee Lounge, The Peacock, Cleopatra's Coffee Lounge, Carmen's Bazaar, The Gazebo massage parlour, The Cottage brothel and Carmen's Curio Shop.

Learn about Carmen's landmark 1966 court case, her race to become Mayor of Wellington in the 1970s, and how she supported and offered a safe harbour to Wellington's Takatāpui LGBTI Rainbow communities.

“I would more or less hypnotise all my guests like a black widow spider and trap my clients caught in my web. As people left my coffee lounge at 6am or earlier I would splash them with Carmen’s exotic juice which meant that they would be back another night for more. The scent would guide them to me. Some would hear my voice calling like an eerie whisper from the depths of darkness.” [from Carmen’s autobiography]

Accessibility information: The tour is on flat public footpaths. Tours are limited to around 20 walkers.

Time/Date: 1pm, Sunday 5 April, 2026
Starts: starts at the rugby sculpture, Jack Ilott Green, corner of Harris Street & Jervois Quay (just by Civic Square)
Ends: Upper Cuba Street
Duration: 90 minutes
Cost / Bookings: the tour is free but you do need to register

PLEASE REGISTER (if there are no registrations the tour won't go ahead).
events.description:
Entrepreneur, entertainer, madam and mayoral candidate Carmen Rupe was based in Wellington for less than 15-years but has left us a rich legacy of memories and inspiring moments.

Learn about Carmen's landmark 1966 court case, her race to become Mayor of Wellington in the 1970s, and how she supported and offered a safe harbour to Wellington's Takatāpui LGBTI Rainbow communities.
As people left my coffee lounge at 6am or earlier I would splash them with Carmen’s exotic juice which meant that they would be back another night for more.
Some would hear my voice calling like an eerie whisper from the depths of darkness.” [from Carmen’s autobiography] Accessibility information: The tour is on flat public footpaths.

events.id: Vintage cake masterclass-Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Vintage cake masterclass
events.venue: Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Step into the charm of classic pâtisserie and learn the art of timeless cake design in our Vintage Cake Masterclass.

In this guided, hands-on session, each guest will receive a 10cm mini chocolate cake — beautifully prepared and ready for decorating. You’ll begin with a live demonstration by Chef Shuchi Bhardwaj, who will walk you through the techniques and piping styles that define elegant vintage cakes.

Then, with expert guidance, you’ll recreate the design on your own cake, icing and piping step by step to achieve a stunning, nostalgic finish.

All materials are thoughtfully provided, including:

• Freshly baked mini chocolate cake
• Buttercream icing
• Decorations and piping tools
• Cake box to take your creation home

Whether you’re a beginner or a baking enthusiast, this interactive class is designed to be relaxed, creative, and enjoyable.

Gluten-free options are available.
$70 per person, including a hot beverage of your choice.

Come for the experience, leave with new skills — and a cake that looks straight out of a vintage bakery window.

events.id: Gongs at Old St Paul’s: A Vigil for Peace-Old St Paul's, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Gongs at Old St Paul’s: A Vigil for Peace
events.venue: Old St Paul's, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington
events.long_description:
Old St Paul's Cathedral
2:30pm to 4:00pm
Sunday 5th April

Gongs at Old St Paul’s: A Vigil for Peace is a 90-minute immersive gong bath held in the warm afternoon light of Old St Paul's.

Musicians and gong players Nick Hyder and Erika Grant will offer a spacious, deep and relaxing sound journey designed to support rest, reflection, and a shared intention for peace. As the layered tones of the gongs move through the body and the resonant wooden interior of the building, participants are invited to slow down, listen inwardly, and settle into stillness.

This is a quiet gathering – open to all – to pause together and rest in sound.

*For this sound journey, we'll be lying on the carpet near the altar. Please bring a yoga mat, pillow, eye mask and blanket – or whatever it is you need to feel most warm and comfortable for this*

Scientific studies have highlighted the benefits of sound therapy, particularly gong baths, in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The rhythmic patterns and overtones of the gongs can help entrain brainwaves, leading to a meditative state and improved mental clarity.

Beyond the immediate effects, many participants report experiencing lasting benefits such as improved sleep, reduced anxiety, and enhanced mood following a gong bath session. By immersing yourself in the healing vibrations of the gongs, you can tap into a deeper sense of relaxation and rejuvenation.

THIS EVENT IS RESTRICTED TO 20 PEOPLE ONLY

About Us
Nick's background is in music production, sound art, and experiential therapies. His long-term pursuit of well-being is currently being explored through live events that apply concepts from nervous system science with the mysterious and immersive sounds of contemporary gongs.

Erika is a musician and astrologer based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. She has 35+ years musical experience. Erika has an interest in the healing properties of sound for mental and physical wellbeing, which she currently explores through her gong bathing practice.

Disclaimer
Gong baths are safe and supportive for most people. However, please consult your doctor or mental health professional before attending if you:
-have a pacemaker or other implanted medical device
-live with epilepsy or a sound-sensitive condition
-are in the first trimester of pregnancy
-are experiencing severe or unmanaged mental health challenges (such as psychosis, schizophrenia, or PTSD)
If you have recent injuries, surgeries, or other health concerns, let us know so we can make sure you’re comfortable.
Everyone responds differently to sound. If at any point during the session you feel overwhelmed or uncomfortable, you are welcome to sit up, move gently, or step outside. Your wellbeing always comes first.

events.id: KRAANIUM - Wellington - ONE NZ SHOW ONLY-154 Vivian Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: KRAANIUM - Wellington - ONE NZ SHOW ONLY
events.venue: 154 Vivian Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
KRAANIUM – AUSTRALIA & AOTEAROA TOUR MMXXVI
with SLAUGHTERCULT (NSW)
For over two decades, KRAANIUM have ruled the slam world with a blood-slick crown, dragging their twisted, groove-soaked brutality across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. Born in Norway in 2001, the band helped define slamming brutal death metal turning obscene speed and perverse groove into an international cult. Six albums deep, signed to Unique Leader Records, and still filthier than ever, Kraanium have shared the stage with everyone from Abominable Putridity to Vulvodynia, leaving nothing but broken necks and grinning corpses in their wake.
Now, for the first time ever, Kraanium are bringing their global infection down under set to unleash Slamchosis across Australia & Aotearoa this April. Expect bone-grinding riffs, relentless blast beats, and a live show so vile it should come with a biohazard warning.
This isn’t a tour, its an autopsy in motion.
The pits will open.
The riffs will hit like hammers.
April 2026. Aotearoa.
KRAANIUM IS COMING.
Mon 6th April 2026
Valhalla - Wellington/Te Whanganui-a-Tara
TICKETS ON SALE NOW AT UTR.CO.NZ

events.id: Flight Club - School Holiday Science Workshop-40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Flight Club - School Holiday Science Workshop
events.venue: 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
3… 2.. 1… Blast off! Tamariki learn all about the forces of flight at Space Place’s 4-hour school holiday workshop featuring rocket experiments, vortex cannons and beyond.
From making everyday objects fly with a leaf-blower, to observing what happens when you put a marshtronaut (marshmallow-astronaut) into a vacuum chamber, tamariki will come home brimming with new knowledge about how Gravity, Lift, Resistance and Thrust can be used to make cool things happen!
Suitable for ages 8 – 12, the workshop rounds off with a planetarium film.
Tamariki can be dropped-off in the morning and picked-up afterwards; caregivers are not required to stay for the duration of the workshop. Tamariki should bring a packed lunch.
7, 8, 14, 15 April | 9am–1pm | Space Place | Ages 8-12

events.id: Jewellery-making: Tuesday Beginner’s 8-week block-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Jewellery-making: Tuesday Beginner’s 8-week block
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
This beginner-level class is designed for students who are new to jewellery making, with everyone following the same guided project.
The course starts Tue 7 April and runs for 8 weeks till Tue 26 May, 5.30pm - 8pm.
Our popular weeknight classes are a welcoming and hands-on way to get started, offering a lively, friendly studio environment and plenty of support along the way.
The stone setting process teaches you many fundamental techniques in one project. You’ll begin by learning basic ring-making and stone-setting skills in copper or brass, including measuring, texturing, shaping, cutting and soldering.
From there, you’ll make a practice ring set with a cabochon stone (a stone with a flat base and smooth, domed top) in a classic bezel setting. Once you’re confident, you can choose to progress to working in silver to create a second ring using a stone you love, or continue developing a more ambitious design in copper and brass.
The class fee includes copper, brass and two gemstones. Silver is available for purchase in the studio if you’d like to work with precious metal for your second stone set ring.
You’ll have a beautiful selection of beginner-friendly stones to choose from, in a wide range of colours.
Course fee $415, early bird $374 till 15 March.

events.name: Matt Lenart Solo exhibition & book launch
events.venue: 148 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
You are welcome to attend the opening of Matt Lenart's solo exhibition of paintings & poem's on the 7th of April, at 5:30 pm on Tuesday.
Matt will also be having his book launch during the opening as well.
Vincents gallery : Willis St Village
5/148 Willis St
Wellington
Nau mai haere mai
Kia Ora
My name is Matt Lenart
Welcome to my Exhibition
Out of the box
A whimsical selection and comical reflection of artwork and poetry
Sometimes dark sometimes comical
Merely a reflection of people and nature we engage with
And try and make sense of usual stimulus
As reflective opinions by Matt Lenart AKA Mash
Not to be taken too seriously
Matt Lenart also known as Mash
Is a self taught and practicing Artist, Poet, Writer and Actor
Having been in practice of these mediums
for around 40 years since beginning as a child
He has worked on many projects over many years
Having worked on the Lord of the Rings and The Hobbit
And a few short films while studying at Film School
And has also worked on the Cuba Street Carnival at the beginning of its inception
This exhibition Out Of The Box
is a small collection of some of his art and poetry from over the years
Currently he is working as a gardener and doing artworks and writing part time
Being Creative and a Nature Lover are the two main things I am Passionate about
I hope you enjoy my Exhibition and Book

events.id: The Overthinking Mind – Tap to Break Free from Mental Loops-175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Overthinking Mind – Tap to Break Free from Mental Loops
events.venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars
The Overthinking Mind - Tap to Break Free from Mental Loops
This workshop explores why our thoughts get stuck in loops of worry, doubt, and replay. Drawing on the insights of Carl Jung, we’ll uncover how the unconscious mind, shadow, and how unresolved inner conflicts fuel over-thinking. You’ll also learn how the body and nervous system become conditioned to repeat these mental patterns, creating the familiar cycle of racing thoughts and restless emotions.
Through a guided exercise, you’ll experience practical tools from FasterEFT and the science of neuroplasticity—methods that help rewire the brain and bring the mind back to calm clarity.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll not only understand the deeper roots of over-thinking, but you’ll also walk away with simple, repeatable techniques to interrupt mental loops, ease the nervous system, and create a lasting sense of inner peace.
“This is an interactive workshop where you’ll connect with others and see that many of us face the same challenges beneath the surface—making growth and healing easier when we explore them together
“This is an interactive workshop that offers the chance to connect with others on a similar journey. It’s powerful to know, that beneath the surface, we often share the same challenges and struggles—and that healing becomes easier when we explore them together.”
Meet your host, Krishnaloka
Introducing Krishnaloka, who is driven to help others overcome their issues within their mind and pyschology. As a mind technician trained in eutaptics (Faster EFT), she employs the fastest and most powerful method to create lasting change by addressing the root causes of issues rather than just the symptoms. Her warm and caring nature makes every interaction with her both comforting and transformative.
End the session on a high note with a delicious plant-based dinner, dessert, and good conversation.
Tickets available here~ bit.ly/eventTickets
Bhakti Lounge 1/175 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington. http://www.bhaktilounge.org.nz

events.long_description:
A story about the goddess and the dark side of the moon.
A young girl is watching the moon landings on repeat, entranced by those astronaut’s weightlessness.
A bunch of teenagers are swimming under a lunar eclipse, lost in what might happen next.
A mis-matched couple are watching a horror film at a drive in cinema, about to step into all the next stuff.
SELENE is a radical explosion of an ancient myth.
It’s about how we grow up defined by our bodies.
It’s about the light sides of us and the dark sides of us.
And it’s about the stuff inside us. All the wild stuff inside of us.
A new sibling show to the hugely acclaimed HELIOS, the UK's "masters of storytelling" (ThreeWeeks) Wright&Grainger return to Aotearoa with SELENE - an intimate theatre experience with cinematic score and striking storytelling. Told by and made with Megan Drury.
Presented by Wright&Grainger with Theatre@41 Monkgate. NZ tour produced by A Mulled Whine Productions.

events.id: Flight Club - School Holiday Science Workshop-Space Place at Carter Observatory, 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Flight Club - School Holiday Science Workshop
events.venue: Space Place at Carter Observatory, 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
3… 2.. 1… Blast off! Tamariki learn all about the forces of flight at Space Place’s 4-hour school holiday workshop featuring rocket experiments, vortex cannons and beyond.

From making everyday objects fly with a leaf-blower, to observing what happens when you put a marshtronaut (marshmallow-astronaut) into a vacuum chamber, tamariki will come home brimming with new knowledge about how Gravity, Lift, Resistance and Thrust can be used to make cool things happen!

Suitable for ages 8 – 12, the workshop rounds off with a planetarium film.

Tamariki can be dropped-off in the morning and picked-up afterwards; caregivers are not required to stay for the duration of the workshop. Tamariki should bring a packed lunch.

7, 8, 14, 15 April | 9am – 1pm | Space Place | Ages 8-12

events.id: Hindi Language Classes-Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Hindi Language Classes
events.venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
events.long_description:
Learn Hindi language in 8 to 12 weeks. Travelling to India? Hindi will help you explore and give you invaluable experience of incredible India.

The lesson is approximately 90 mins long one on one teaching, and I teach anyone according to your ability. Can you please let me know how much do you know about the Hindi language and why do you want to learn.

Example: are you planning to travel to India? Or just interested in learning a new language?
We teach basic writing, reading and speaking in 6 sessions, and also teach more advanced levels if anyone wants to learn more. Classes are available everyday. Day and time can be arranged according to our availability.

If the time and cost of these lessons are prohibiting you from learning the language then please let me know and I can definitely work something out for keen Hindi learners.

Email: hindiclass@xtra.co.nz
Txt: 02102697038

events.id: Intro to 2D Traditional Animation-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Intro to 2D Traditional Animation
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
This is a beginner's course in 2D animation where tweens & teens will learn the time-honoured principles of hand-drawn animation and how to apply them to their own animation project. Delivered over two morning sessions from 10 am - 1 pm on Tuesday & Wednesday, 7-8 April. Students may work on a project incorporating their own ideas, or if you'd rather, be assigned a project from your tutor.

This is a bring your own device course, and any device, iPad or laptop you already own (except a mobile – it's really not big enough to do good animation on) can be used. The school does not provide any digital equipment. It's important that the app/programme is installed on the student's device before the course begins, and that they have had a play on it to get used to how it works. This is so that the student is at least a little familiar with their chosen software so that it's enough to begin learning animation techniques on day one. Recommended 2D Animation software, including free options, is included in the course listing on our website.

events.id: School Holiday Workshop: Monster Me-The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: School Holiday Workshop: Monster Me
events.venue: The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
School Holiday Workshop: Monster Me
Create wild, monstrous self-portraits inspired by Jason Greig—ink, print, and transform yourself into fierce, funny, or furry creatures!

About this Event

Drop-off your kids for a fun school holiday workshop.

Inspired by the exhibition Blood is Thicker by Jason Greig, in this printmaking workshop we will create wild, expressive self-portraits with a monstrous twist. Drawing on Greig’s werewolf family wall, we’ll transform ourselves into strange, spooky, or silly creatures. Using printmaking techniques, we’ll ink and print bold portraits full of personality, experimenting with exaggerated features to become something completely different. Are you fierce, furry, fanged, or funny? Turn yourself into a monster and bring your alter ego to life on paper!

Create something special these school holidays at Paraparaumu Library! Tamariki are invited to draw portraits of the people (or pets!) they love – from whānau and friends to teachers and furry companions.

Tamariki can take part in fun, guided art workshops (registration required), or drop into our Makerspace craft station to create at their own pace – no booking needed. Caregivers are warmly welcome to join in too and create alongside their tamariki.
