events.id: HELP! A Monster Ate My Story-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: HELP! A Monster Ate My Story
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
The delightful words and pictures of award-winning Wellington author Ruth Paul (Lion Guards the Cake, Stomp, Anahera, I Am Jellyfish) have thrilled young readers for many years. Now all the fun is coming to the stage in a brand- new play for children.

With your HELP! Ruth and favourite characters LION, JELLYFISH, T-REX and KIWI will join forces against the MISH-MASH MONSTER to stop it eating their stories!

Romping through the April school holidays, these fun-loving characters sing songs by Ruth Paul and Charlotte Yates, with costumes by WoW veteran Fifi Colston and stage direction by Jacqueline Coats (The Adventures of Tahi & Kowhai, Party Pigs!) in a celebration of creativity, imagination and collaboration.

Tues 7 – Sun 19 April (no shows Friday 10 April)
Tues – Sat 11.30am & 1.30pm, Sunday 12 & 19 April 1.30pm & 3.30pm
Q&A with Ruth and the cast after the 1.30pm show Thursday 9 April
$20pp, $70 for a family pass (4 tickets), aged 2 and under free
Running time: 50 minutes

events.id: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This beginner level class is designed for students who are new to jewellery making, with everyone following the same guided project. It starts on Tue 7 April and runs for 8 weeks till Tue 26 May, 5.30pm - 8pm.

Our popular weeknight classes are a welcoming and hands-on way to get started, offering a lively, friendly studio environment and plenty of support along the way.

The stone setting process teaches you many fundamental techniques in one project. You’ll begin by learning basic ring-making and stone-setting skills in copper or brass, including measuring, texturing, shaping, cutting and soldering.

From there, you’ll make a practice ring set with a cabochon stone (a stone with a flat base and smooth, domed top) in a classic bezel setting. Once you’re confident, you can choose to progress to working in silver to create a second ring using a stone you love, or continue developing a more ambitious design in copper and brass.

The class fee includes copper, brass and two gemstones. Silver is available for purchase in the studio if you’d like to work with precious metal for your second stone set ring.

You’ll have a beautiful selection of beginner-friendly stones to choose from, in a wide range of colours.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time, please email us to arrange this ;)
events.description:
It starts on Tue 7 April and runs for 8 weeks till Tue 26 May, 5.30pm - 8pm.
The stone setting process teaches you many fundamental techniques in one project.
From there, you’ll make a practice ring set with a cabochon stone (a stone with a flat base and smooth, domed top) in a classic bezel setting.
Silver is available for purchase in the studio if you’d like to work with precious metal for your second stone set ring.
You’ll have a beautiful selection of beginner-friendly stones to choose from, in a wide range of colours.

events.venue: 29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Be captivated these school holidays the gorgeous puppet show “Once in a full moon” performed by Anna Bailey of String Bean Puppets.
Deep in the bush a lonely kiwi looks up at the sky and mistakes the full moon for a giant egg. Can kiwis fly? Can moons fall from the sky? Can wishes come true? This magical string puppet adventure that introduces the audience to an intimate encounter with a few of Aotearoa New Zealand's special native creatures.
This show will be held in the Visitor Centre Foyer and is suitable for all ages, normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street during the busy school holiday period.

events.id: Champion Brass — New Zealand Brass Band Championships 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Champion Brass — New Zealand Brass Band Championships 2026
events.long_description:
More than 1000 competitors blow into Wellington in April to take part in one of the oldest music competitions in the world. The annual National Brass Band Championships attract competing bands from across Aotearoa, Australia, and beyond. Together, they represent the spirit of community music-making at its pinnacle. This year, 40 bands, including one travelling from Tonga, will compete across four grades. 
The Championships are more than just a competition. They are an annual institution with a huge sense of camaraderie between competing musicians. It is a celebration of everything that is great about live music and therefore attracts a wide audience of supporters. 
The highlight of the week is a gala performance from the National Band of New Zealand. The 12 April concert features top musicians selected from competing bands plus guest soloists. Other events are free and open to the public. This includes solo, chamber, and full-band performances, the Champion of Champions concert, and a masterclass with the principal trombonist of the Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra, Jorgen Van Rijen.
events.description:
More than 1000 competitors blow into Wellington in April to take part in one of the oldest music competitions in the world.
The annual National Brass Band Championships attract competing bands from across Aotearoa, Australia, and beyond.
Together, they represent the spirit of community music-making at its pinnacle.
The Championships are more than just a competition.
The highlight of the week is a gala performance from the National Band of New Zealand.

events.id: The Pogues — Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Pogues — Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s been 35 years since The Pogues were in New Zealand, and 40 years since the release of their most famous album. Now is your chance to hear them live singing songs from their iconic 1985 album ‘Rum, Sodomy & the Lash’. Not only will you get the full album, but there will also be other much-loved favourites. Hear A Pair of Brown Eyes, Dirty Old Town, A Rainy Night in Soho, and plenty more. 
Helping bring this re-creation to life are original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer and Spider Stacy. There is also a special lineup of acclaimed guest vocalists, Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn. Together they’ll present a soul-stirring celebration of one of modern music’s most enduring legacies. 
The Pogues formed in 1982 and fused punk with traditional Irish music. They originally called themselves Pogue Mahone. This phrase is an anglicisation of the Irish for póg mo thóin, meaning “kiss my arse”.
events.description:

Helping bring this re-creation to life are original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer and Spider Stacy.

events.id: Absolute Beginners' Social Dancing Introductory Class-110 Hutt Rd, Kaiwharawhara, Wellington 6035, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Absolute Beginners' Social Dancing Introductory Class
events.venue: 110 Hutt Rd, Kaiwharawhara, Wellington 6035, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Ever wanted to dance like Ginger Rogers and Fred Astaire, or maybe just be the couple who get up on the floor and look like they know what they're doing? Perhaps you've thought that learning social dancing might be fun but not certain of committing to a whole term of classes? Well here is a one-hour class to give it all a try and it is totally FREE!
The class will cover two simple building blocks, which form the basis of five dances: Waltz, Foxtrot, Rumba, Cha Cha, and East Coast Swing. Partners always welcome but not a requirement to enrol.
DATE: Wednesday, 8 April 2026
TIME: 6-7pm
PLACE: Level 2, 110 Hutt Rd, Kaiwharawhara, Wellington
COST: FREE!
This Introductory Class will be followed by a seven-week course to develop these building blocks and practice the five named dances. While we hope you will sign up to the course there is no obligation, whatsoever.
Come and experience the relaxed and fun atmosphere of social dancing, enjoying the music and company, and learn a new skill that will have all the people watching wishing they could dance like you!
Come give it a try!

events.id: NZAC Wellington: Colin Monteath - The Expeditions Archives-Tararua Tramping Club Inc., 4 Moncrieff St, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: NZAC Wellington: Colin Monteath - The Expeditions Archives
events.venue: Tararua Tramping Club Inc., 4 Moncrieff St, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Please note we are running this section night on Wednesday the 8th due to Easter weekend.
For our next section night, we're very excited to welcome Polar and Mountain Nomad, Colin Monteath.
Dusting off his expeditions archives, Colin Monteath will take us on a journey through his expedition career spanning 33 seasons in Antarctica, 4 in the Arctic and some 20 expeditions throughout Asia and beyond. The lecture will focus on his freelance photography and writing career that started by documenting mountaineering expeditions but gradually morphed into magazine and book publishing featuring a series of pilgrimages to sacred mountains, or legendary traverse routes often in the footsteps of pioneers from yesteryear.
Check out Colin's exceptional bio here: https://www.colinmonteath.nz/about
All are welcome to this talk, and as ever, there will be pizza!
See you there!
Image source: provided by Colin Monteath

events.id: THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington-83 Courtenay Pl, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington
events.venue: 83 Courtenay Pl, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Pogues make their long-awaited return to NZ stages with a special 40th-anniversary celebration of their classic album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash!
The tour brings together original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer, and Spider Stacy, alongside a stellar lineup of guest vocalists Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn. They’re joined by musicians Holly Mullineaux, Jordan O’Leary, Fiachra Meek, Jim Sclavunos, Pete Fraser, Daniel Hayes, and Ian Williamson, performing the album in full plus other fan favourites.
Released in 1985, Rum, Sodomy & the Lash remains a cornerstone of alternative music — fusing traditional Irish folk with punk and poetic storytelling. The album features some of The Pogues’ most celebrated songs, including A Pair of Brown Eyes, Dirty Old Town, and A Rainy Night in Soho.
Returning for their first NZ tour in over 35 years, The Pogues promise an unforgettable night honouring one of the most influential albums in modern music.

events.id: First World War Memorial Walk-Bolton Street Cemetery, Bolton Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: First World War Memorial Walk
events.venue: Bolton Street Cemetery, Bolton Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
In honour of Anzac Day, embark on a self-guided journey around the memorials dedicated to the 18 servicemen who valiantly fought in the Great War, many of whom never returned home. Discover their touching stories and take a moment to pause, read, reflect, and honour their memory.

This self-guided activity is available from dawn to dusk. Simply pick up a map and locate the white crosses next to their graves to uncover each soldier's unique narrative.

Maps are available at both entrances to the Bolton Street Cemetery, inside the Bolton Street Cemetery Museum, or you can download a digital version by copying and pasting this link: https://wellingtongardens.nz/assets/PDF-Brochures/J018060-WorldWarOneMemorial-Brochure.pdf

events.id: Health and Safety Essentials for Small Business-Business Central,Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Health and Safety Essentials for Small Business
events.venue: Business Central,Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Health and Safety Essentials for Small Business
This course will give you the oversight you need to ensure that your business health and safety systems and processes are up to date.

About this Event

The purpose of this course is to give you the oversight you need to ensure your business health and safety systems and processes reflect a best-practice approach. Each session comes with practical templates for you to use in your business to comply with the Health & Safety at Work Act 2015. It also supports your understanding of the core elements of a health and safety system.


Key Audience:
This course is suitable for people who own a small business and want to ensure they comply with health and safety best practice requirements. It is also good for those businesses without a health and safety committee and anyone who is required to be involved in health and safety in a small business.


Learning outcomes
Understand the core elements of a health and safety system.
Understand the key duty holders and their respective duties under the Act.
Understand essential health and safety components and how they relate to your legal duties.
Understand practical ways to increase the effectiveness of your health and safety system.

If you would like to register with Business Central directly and receive an invoice for payment, please contact our training team via .

Places are limited, so register today.


Course details
Duration: One day, in person.
Time: 9am - 4pm


Course includes
Workbook, arrival tea or coffee, morning tea, lunch and afternoon tea.

To view our Terms and Conditions click here.

events.id: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - MIRAMAR-Miramar Central School, 38 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - MIRAMAR
events.venue: Miramar Central School, 38 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Introducing our new updated “Ancient Runes Puzzle Box”. Keep your treasures safe in the coolest way possible. Only you will know the special code to release the catch hidden under the mechanical Iris! This new improved version has 2 code wheels that you can use to set your own unique code, as well as 2 code pins that are stored on the box but have to be inserted correctly to activate the mechanism. If everything is set a hidden push button will then activate a solenoid that releases the catch.

Powered by 4 AAA batteries (provided), you’ll get to solder the components, screw and glue all the intricate pieces together and unwrap the puzzle to learn how it works. And, of course, you'll get to take it home! No previous experience is required and you’re welcome to bring a grown up if you want to.

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $135 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.id: String Bean Puppets: Once In a Full Moon-ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: String Bean Puppets: Once In a Full Moon
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Be captivated these school holidays by the gorgeous puppet show, Once in a Full Moon, performed by Anna Bailey of String Bean Puppets.

Deep in the bush, a lonely kiwi looks up at the sky and mistakes the full moon for a giant egg. Can kiwis fly? Can moons fall from the sky? Can wishes come true? This magical string puppet adventure introduces the audience to an intimate encounter with a few of Aotearoa New Zealand's special native creatures.

This show will be held in the Visitor Centre Foyer and is suitable for all ages; normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street.

events.id: Otis Prescott-Mason, solo piano-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Otis Prescott-Mason, solo piano
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Programme:

Mozart - Sonata in D major, K.311

Liszt - Étude in Db major, ‘Harmonie du soir’, S.139, No.11

Chopin - Barcarolle, Op.60

Biography:

Otis Prescott-Mason is one of New Zealand’s leading young pianists. He currently studies in Wellington under Dr Jian Liu and, in the United States, under Professor Boris Berman.

In 2025 Otis was awarded 3rd prize at the National Concerto Competition in Christchurch, performing Prokofiev’s 3rd concerto with the Christchurch Symphony. In 2022 he was awarded 1st prize, Best Performance of a Classical Sonata, and the Audience Choice Award at the Lewis Eady National Piano Competition. In the same year he was awarded 1st prize at the PACANZ National Piano Competition. Otis was also awarded 1st prize at the 2020 New Zealand Junior Piano Competition.

Now finished with undergraduate studies at the New Zealand School of Music in Wellington, Otis plans to pursue graduate study abroad in the near future.

events.id: Plant-Based Craft Club-Toi Poneke Arts Centre,Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Plant-Based Craft Club
events.venue: Toi Poneke Arts Centre,Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Plant-Based Craft Club
Haere mai planty weavers all across the spectrum of craft! This club is for plant craft lovers who want to make alongside other plant craft lovers. BYO project, some kai to share if you would like (not expected), and your lovely plant enthusiastic self.

This is a space for you if you want to develop your skills in community through weaving together with any plants and techniques you please - think cordage/baskets/mats/containers in Harakeke, Ti Kouka, Willow, Ivy, etc etc! This space will be facilitated by MicroMill tutors who are keen to help you think through your projects/practices and weave threads of connection and collaboration across craft spaces. And who knows - maybe we can have some informal wee foraging outings between club sessions too? The sky is the limit.

Appropriate tīkanga for working with and around various plants will be shared at the beginning of each session. By attending you have a responsibility to respect this tīkanga while in the space.

There will not be any formal instruction, club costs are intended to cover the venue and create a supported space for us to learn through doing, together.

Sign up on micromill.org

events.long_description:
The Thistle Inn presents: Wednesdays with Munro

Join us for Wednesdays with Munro, a four-month live music series celebrating a rich mix of genres, moods, and musical traditions.
Held on the first Wednesday of each month at 7pm, this intimate program showcases the Michael Munro Band in a different themed performance for each night:


Wednesday 8th April – Michael Munro Band performing ‘Retro-Rock Turned Lounge’. A 5-piece combo to kick off the ‘1st Wednesday of the month’ program.

Wednesday 6th May – Michael Munro Band performing an evening of ‘Latino and Bossas’.

Wednesday 3rd June – Michael Munro Band performing a French music-evening ‘Bon Soir'

Wednesday 1st July – Michael Munro Band performing ‘King of Swing'.


Relax, enjoy great food and drinks, and experience a new musical journey each month at the Thistle Inn!

events.id: Whānau Print Making-29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Whānau Print Making
events.venue: 29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us at Zealandia these school holidays for a free, nature inspired craft event!
Local artist Tayla Hartemink has designed a series of unique stamps featuring beautiful native manu and ika, perfect for decorating cards, and printing fabric and clothing.
A limited amount of materials will be provided, but we encourage you to bring your own T-shirts, tote bags, tea towels, or other fabric items to personalise with these stunning designs.
Tayla Hartemink (Tūhoe) is a multi-media artist, illustrator and creative workshop facilitator based in Te-Whanganui-ā-Tara. Tayla’s work is rooted in a deep connection to whakapapa and indigenous storytelling. Her work incorporates graphic design, painting, print-making and traditional motifs. Through her artwork she hopes to inspire and empower people to connect with Te Ao Māori.
Suitable for all ages— drop in, get hands-on, and make something colourful to take home!
This event will be held on the 9th and 10th of April, 10am-3pm in the Zealandia Visitor Centre Foyer. Normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street.

events.id: Kauri Theatre Presents: Catch Me If You Can the Musical-Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Kauri Theatre Presents: Catch Me If You Can the Musical
events.venue: Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Seeking fame and fortune, precocious teenager Frank Abagnale, Jr., runs away from home to begin an unforgettable adventure. With nothing more than his boyish charm, a big imagination and millions of dollars in forged checks, Frank successfully poses as a pilot, a doctor and a lawyer – living the high life and winning the girl of his dreams. When Frank's lies catch the attention of FBI agent Carl Hanratty, Carl pursues Frank across the country to make him pay for his crimes.

Based on the incredible true story and hit film, Catch Me If You Can is the high-flying musical comedy about chasing your dreams and not getting caught. Nominated for four Tony awards, including Best Musical, this delightfully entertaining show was created by a Tony Award-winning "dream team," with a book by Terrence McNally (The Full Monty, Ragtime) and a swinging score by Marc Shaiman and Scott Wittman (Hairspray).

Content: Catch Me If You Can includes depictions of alcoholism, includes prop guns, and loud noises (i.e., jet engines and gunshots).

events.name: Quiet Days
events.venue: Home of Compassion, 2 Rhine St, Island Bay, Wellington 6023, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The theme of this Quiet Day is Joy!
Eastertide provides us with the opportunity to reflect on the life of Jesus and celebrate the resurrection.
Where is God turning our grief into joy? What does it mean to live into the joy of the resurrection? How might we embody joy in our lives? We will spend time gently reflecting on the joy of the resurrection and the hope of Eastertide.
10:30-2.30pm at Chapel.

events.id: THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington-St. James Theatre,83 Courtenay Pl, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington
events.venue: St. James Theatre,83 Courtenay Pl, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
THE POGUES | St James Theatre, Wellington
The Pogues make their long-awaited return to NZ stages with a special 40th-anniversary celebration of their classic album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash!

The tour brings together original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer, and Spider Stacy, alongside a stellar lineup of guest vocalists Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn. They’re joined by musicians Holly Mullineaux, Jordan O’Leary, Fiachra Meek, Jim Sclavunos, Pete Fraser, Daniel Hayes, and Ian Williamson, performing the album in full plus other fan favourites.

Released in 1985, Rum, Sodomy & the Lash remains a cornerstone of alternative music — fusing traditional Irish folk with punk and poetic storytelling. The album features some of The Pogues’ most celebrated songs, including A Pair of Brown Eyes, Dirty Old Town, and A Rainy Night in Soho.

Returning for their first NZ tour in over 35 years, The Pogues promise an unforgettable night honouring one of the most influential albums in modern music.

events.id: The Pogues - Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary-St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Pogues - Rum, Sodomy & the Lash 40th Anniversary
events.venue: St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bringing the spirit of Bluesfest to stages across New Zealand, Bluesfest Tours are proud to present one of next year’s most anticipated Bluesfest artists - The Pogues, who will perform three New Zealand sideshows nationwide, celebrating 40 years of their iconic album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash.

All tickets through Ticketmaster | Access all show tickets from Bluesfesttours.com.au

Now, for the first time in 35 years, The Pogues return to New Zealand to celebrate the 40th Anniversary of their seminal album Rum, Sodomy & the Lash — performed in full alongside beloved favourites.

Featuring original members James Fearnley, Jem Finer and Spider Stacy, this special line-up unites acclaimed guest vocalists Daragh Lynch, Iona Zajac, and John Francis Flynn, bringing one of folk-punk’s most influential albums back to life. Ranked among Rolling Stone’s 500 Greatest Albums of All Time, the 1985 classic features ‘A Pair of Brown Eyes’, ‘Dirty Old Town’, and ‘A Rainy Night in Soho’.

The Pogues are just one of the many world-class artists performing as part of Australia’s Bluesfest 2026 line-up, who have collectively sold tens of millions of albums, earned multiple GRAMMY Awards and Rock & Roll Hall of Fame honours, and defined generations of live music.

The Pogues’ New Zealand tour and Bluesfest return promise a rousing, soul-stirring celebration of one of modern music’s most enduring legacies.

events.id: Daniel Sloss — Bitter-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Daniel Sloss — Bitter
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description: He’s one of the biggest global names in live stand-up comedy. Daniel Sloss is heading to The Opera House to regale a Wellington audience with his edgy brand of comedy. ‘Bitter’ is an opportunity to experience Daniel’s brilliant and devastatingly hilarious delivery. His impact on the comedy scene has been impressive. Now 35, Daniel has been peddling his dark sense of humour for over half his life. At the age of 19, he was the youngest comedian to perform a solo season in London’s West End. Now, on to solo show number 13, he is one of the top ticket-selling comedians on the planet. His extensive tours have taken him to 55 countries and counting.
events.description:

events.id: Book Launch | Night, Ma-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Book Launch | Night, Ma
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us to launch a stunning new memoir by Elizabeth Knox, in Ngā Pou Ruahine, within the newly opened central library Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui. The book will be launched by Emily Perkins.
Thursday 9 April, 6pm.
Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington.
Books will be for sale thanks to Unity Wellington.
Free entry – all welcome!
For three and a half years, calamities hit Elizabeth Knox and family in rapid succession. Her sister suffered a psychotic break and was hospitalised against her will, her husband’s brother died by violence, and her mother was diagnosed with motor neurone disease.
In time, she was able to write about it.
Night, Ma is a book about the net of family which people are held by, but also slip through. About the actual daily work of love; the physical and cognitive work love requires.
Knox is a beloved storyteller who has given us other worlds; now she invites us into her own. With characteristic generosity and transcendence, she guides us through time, illness, loss, and the loneliness of unutterable experiences.
Night, Ma offers the gift of seeing as Elizabeth sees.
'Absolutely brilliant. This radiant, radically honest memoir pulls the pin on a sequence of domestic grenades, from the perils of semi-feral childhood to a cruelly compacted series of family crises that, like shock waves, sweep all before. Armed with inimitable wit, the consolation of cats and a forensic gaze that spares no one, least of all herself, Knox interrogates the act of caring; the ties that burn and bind, that we somehow survive.' —Diana Wichtel
'An unforgettable record of love and pain, as wide and deep as the ocean and as mighty. There is such life in this, such wit and goodness. Telling the truth of how we are, all of us, trembling on the edge of a great and terrible mystery.' —Noelle McCarthy
Elizabeth Knox is the bestselling author of fourteen novels, most recently the young adult novel Kings of this World, three autobiographical novellas, and a collection of personal essays, The Love School. Her best-known books are The Vintner’s Luck; YA novels Dreamhunter, Dreamquake, and Mortal Fire; and The Absolute Book. Elizabeth was made a Companion of the New Zealand Order of Merit (CNZM) in 2020.
Cover: Todd Atticus

events.id: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey-175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
events.venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
“Āio” speaks to peace and inner calm. In this immersive evening, pūrākau, taonga pūoro and waiata are woven together with the healing vibrations of crystal singing bowls to create a space of deep listening and restoration.
The organic tones of traditional Māori instruments — including kōauau, pūtōrino, porotiti and pūtātara — blend with the resonance of crystal bowls, chimes and ukulele, inviting you to slow down, soften, and reconnect with your wairua and your body. Through story, sound and vibration, this is a gentle journey inward.
After the sound journey, share in a nourishing plant-based dinner and dessert, included in your ticket — a warm way to close the evening together.

What to bring:
Yoga mats, blankets and cushions will be provided but you are welcome to bring extra for comfort!


Meet your hosts & musicians: Smita, Peter, and Alexandra
Smita (Ngāi Te Rangi) was born and raised in Aotearoa. From a young age she was drawn to music as a means for reflection and self-expression. During her music studies at university, she was introduced to taonga pūoro, and this sparked a personal journey of reconnection to te ao Māori and her own whakapapa. Following university, she lived in an ashram for several years where she was introduced to the wonderful practice of bhakti yoga. She now lives with her husband in the Wellington region, where they regularly participate in kirtan (mantra music meditation) at Bhakti Lounge.
Peter is Pākehā, with grandparents from England and Ireland, and has been on a four-year journey learning the art of taonga pūoro — the traditional Māori musical instruments of Aotearoa. Guided by the wisdom and generosity of expert makers and players, he has learned both to craft and to play these unique instruments. For Peter, taonga pūoro offer a way to connect deeply with sound, story, and the natural world, and he is excited to share their voices as part of this sound bath experience.
Alexandra is a sound healing practitioner devoted to nurturing healing in both mind and body. She believes that sound vibration holds the power to expand consciousness and restore harmony and balance in our lives. Her journey into sound healing began with the realization of how deeply sound vibration was affecting her psyche—making her more mindful of the music she listens to and the intention it carries. Along the way, she has drawn inspiration from artists such as Deva Premal, The Flying Mystics, Stevin McNamara, and the Bhakti Lounge Kirtan. Their music has profoundly influenced her own sound healing practice, inspiring her to explore sound as a tool for transformation, connection, and inner peace.
................................................................................................
Booking for Thursday is recommended!
For more details and to book please go here--
https://bit.ly/eventTickets
...............................................................................................
Make Thursdays your night to connect and take a break from your week. Events range from creative workshops, wellness courses to entertaining experiences. All within a down-to-earth and welcoming space. Each event wraps up with a plant-based dinner and dessert.
...............................................................................................

1/175 Vivian Street

events.id: Resonance - Wellington-111 Wakefield St, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Resonance - Wellington
events.venue: 111 Wakefield St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Conductor André de Ridder returns for a concert featuring Shostakovich’s poignant reflection on the tragedy of war, and a world premiere performance featuring trombonist Jörgen van Rijen.
Programme:
Ravel Pavane pour une infante défunte
Andrew Norman Trombone Concerto (World Premiere)
Shostakovich Symphony No. 8
André de Ridder appears with generous support from The Diessl Family.
Ravel’s Pavane pour une infante défunte, (Pavane for a Dead Princess), despite its title, is not a funereal procession but rather a sublime and delicate portrait.
It evokes the luminescent paintings of Diego Velázquez, with music that glitters.
From such an exquisite miniature, we launch the world premiere of a new trombone concerto from the brilliant American composer Andrew Norman. Featuring one of the world’s very best trombone players Jörgen van Rijen, the vast architectures of sound and light within Norman’s music will blow you away.
Shostakovich described his Eighth Symphony as an “attempt to reflect the terrible tragedy of war”. Composed in 1943, it is amongst his most complex, meaningful and personal works. An immense symphony that speaks of the despair of the individual against industrialised violence, the final movement finds its way towards the light and remains a clarion call for peace.

events.id: YAWNING MAN + The Rubber Snake Charmers-17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: YAWNING MAN + The Rubber Snake Charmers
events.venue: 17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Formed in the sun-baked deserts of Southern California, Yawning Man are true pioneers of the desert rock sound. Emerging from the legendary Palm Desert generator parties, their hypnotic instrumental music blends surf-tinged psychedelia, loose improvisation, and expansive, rolling grooves that helped shape generations of heavy and experimental music.
Joining them on this tour are Mario Lalli & The Rubber Snake Charmers (feat. Sean Wheeler), delivering a raw and soulful extension of the original desert scene. Expect deep grooves, cosmic textures, and an unfiltered connection to one of underground rock’s most influential movements.

events.id: Book binding - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for teens & adults-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Book binding - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for teens & adults
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this workshop for ages 12+ you will be guided in assembling your chosen materials for your book, create decorative covers and learn Coptic binding - an elegant and simple binding that is unlimited in versatility.

No special tools or materials are needed and yet a wide spectrum of creative results can be created, from a notebook, sketch book and album to a highly crafted artwork. The books themselves can be made from found items, recycled material or quality art papers and card. You will produce one bound book and one bound album. Once you have Coptic binding in your skill set you can produce your choice of books in any shape or form, for any purpose.

All basic materials supplied.

events.name: Whānau Print Making
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us at Zealandia these school holidays for a free, nature-inspired craft event!

Local artist Tayla Hartemink has designed a series of unique stamps featuring beautiful native manu and ika, perfect for decorating cards, and printing fabric and clothing. A limited amount of materials will be provided, but we encourage you to bring your own T-shirts, tote bags, tea towels, or other fabric items to personalise with these stunning designs.

Tayla Hartemink (Tūhoe) is a multi-media artist, illustrator and creative workshop facilitator based in Te-Whanganui-ā-Tara. Tayla’s work is rooted in a deep connection to whakapapa and indigenous storytelling. Her work incorporates graphic design, painting, print-making and traditional motifs. Through her artwork, she hopes to inspire and empower people to connect with Te Ao Māori.

Suitable for all ages— drop in, get hands-on, and make something colourful to take home!

This show event will be held in the Visitor Centre Foyer and is suitable for all ages; normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street.

events.id: Rise & Shine: Start before you’re ready, back yourself and take the leap.-1 Whitmore Street-All Events In
events.name: Rise & Shine: Start before you’re ready, back yourself and take the leap.
events.venue: 1 Whitmore Street
events.long_description:
Rise & Shine: Start before you’re ready, back yourself and take the leap.
Kick-Arse Meetup for Wellington Business Women Hosted by Patrice Green, with Viv Conway. Powered by Wellington Beds and BNZ.

About this Event

Rise & Shine: Kick-Arse Meetup for Wellington’s Women in Business powered by Wellington Beds and BNZ.

Join us for on Thursday 9th of April, at 6:00pm for an exciting in-person event designed for Wellington Women in Business.
Step into the dynamic and driven world of Rise and Shine, where the spirit of Wellington’s businesswomen comes alive with energy, ambition, and enterprise.
Hosted by Patrice Green and powered by Wellington Beds, this series is dedicated to supporting women who are building, leading and creating change in business.
We’re excited to connect you with Viv Conway, co-founder of Girls Get Off, at the beautiful BNZ Wellington Partners Centre, Level 1, 1 Whitmore Street. The entrance is on the corner of Featherston and Ballance Street.

When you know what you want and need, everything changes.

Join Patrice as she hosts Viv Conway this November to take the chaos out of 2026 and design a year that feels as good as it looks.
Viv is part of a new generation of founders building bold, high-growth businesses and challenging traditional ideas about women’s products and markets.
In this conversation with Patrice, Viv will share the story behind Girls Get Off and what it has taken to grow a brand in the women’s sexual wellness space. She will talk about the realities of launching and scaling a business in a category that has often been misunderstood or overlooked, and the resilience required to keep moving forward when the path is not always straightforward.
Her journey is a powerful example of what can happen when women identify gaps in the market, back their ideas, and build communities around products that truly serve women.
Expect an honest conversation about entrepreneurship, resilience, community building and what it takes to create something new in a space where the rules are still being written.

What to expect:
6.00pm - Arrival
✨ 30 - minute keynote with Viv Conway
🔥 15 - minute fireside chat with Patrice Green - a candid conversation about entrepreneurship, leadership, and building a bold brand
🎤 15 - minute interactive Q&A - your chance to ask Viv your questions
🥂 7.30 - 8pm - Networking drinks and nibbles - connect with like-minded women and expand your network

This is a women-only event for ambitious, curious, and courageous women. Whether you are in corporate, running your own business, or planning your next move, this is the room you want to be in.
Expect to leave with insights, encouragement, and new connections.
Bring a friend or come solo. We’ll make sure you don’t sip your drink alone.
Seats are limited, so secure your spot and join us for another inspiring Rise and Shine event.

About our events:
Each event is a vibrant venue for visionary growth, where trailblazing speakers share their paths and insights, paving the way for others to follow. It’s not just a meeting, it’s a movement fueled by ambition and the collective power of women who are ready to lead.
These sessions are designed to inspire, challenge, and equip you with the tools to take your business and personal growth to new heights. With a focus on forging lasting connections that grow into business opportunities, ‘Rise and Shine’ is a kick-arse community ready to welcome the next trailblazer - YOU. Get ready to be inspired, to inspire, and to turn every interaction into a stepping stone for success.
Beyond networking, this is a platform for vibrant, interactive conversations. Each event spotlights a trailblazing guest, introduced and conversed with by none other than Patrice herself. But here’s the twist: the floor opens to you, offering a chance to engage directly and ask questions.
At this meetup, you'll have the opportunity to have networking drinks and nibbles while sharing your experiences, ideas, and challenges with fellow businesswomen. It's a perfect occasion to gain valuable insights, learn from each other's successes, and build meaningful connections.
This Kick-Arse Meetup, powered by Wellington Beds and BNZ who are all about celebrating the achievements of women in business and fostering a supportive community.

events.id: Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh-Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh
Join Sri Ganesh for an inspiring Ayurveda talk on how food & senses shape health. Learn 6 tastes, conscious eating, balancing your dominant

About this Event



Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh

What you feed your body and mind today shapes your health tomorrow.
Join us for an inspiring and eye-opening lecture with renowned Ayurvedic teacher and bestselling author Sri Ganesh, as he guides us through the powerful connection between physical and mental food—and how they shape our well-being.
In this talk, you’ll discover:
The two types of food we consume: what we eat with our mouths, and what we absorb through our senses.
How to make choices that support both body and mind—using Ayurveda’s six tastes as your guide.
Practical tools to shop wisely, cook from the heart, and eat consciously.
How to align your food and lifestyle with your presently dominant element (PDE) to prevent or heal imbalances at any age.
Whether you’re curious about Ayurveda, seeking to improve your health, or looking to deepen your relationship with food—this lecture will leave you empowered, informed, and inspired.
Sri Ganesh is the author of nine books, including the cookbooks 'Food is my medicine' and 'Food from my village’.




Event followed by a plant-based dinner and dessert, all included in your ticket!



Meet Your Host: Sri Ganesh

Sri Ganesh, the founder of Vaidya’s Ayurveda Village in Kerala, India, is an internationally recognised Ayurveda practitioner, inspirational speaker, and the author of eight widely read health books published in nine languages across the United States, Europe, and India.
Born into a traditional family in South India, Sri Ganesh began his education in Ayurveda in the early years of his life with his grandmother, a well-respected Ayurveda practitioner and poison therapist in their village. He later expanded his knowledge in the subjects of Mana-shastra (psychology), Vedanta (philosophy), Dhyana (meditation), Kalari Marma Chikitsa (Kerala martial art therapy), and Yoga Chikitsa (yoga therapy) through traditional schools in South Indian villages known as gurukulams. Sri Ganesh also holds a bachelor’s degree from Mahatma Gandhi University and a master’s degree in Human Resource Development (HRD).
Drawing on his traditional knowledge and over two decades of experience working as an Ayurveda practitioner around the world, Sri Ganesh has travelled extensively throughout Europe and the United States as an ambassador of Ayurveda, sharing his ancestors’ wisdom through talks, columns, and books. Over the years, Ganesh has delivered hundreds of inspirational talks and has written eight books in the field of health, which have been translated and published in Germany, Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the Netherlands, the United States, India, and Brazil.



Agenda


🕑: 06:00 PM - 06:15 PM

🕑: 06:15 PM - 07:15 PM
Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh
Host: Sri Ganesh

🕑: 07:15 PM - 08:15 PM
Plant-based dinner and dessert!
events.description:
Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh Join Sri Ganesh for an inspiring Ayurveda talk on how food & senses shape health.
Learn 6 tastes, conscious eating, balancing your dominant About this Event Food is My Medicine: A Transformative Evening with Sri Ganesh What you feed your body and mind today shapes your health tomorrow.
Meet Your Host: Sri Ganesh Sri Ganesh, the founder of Vaidya’s Ayurveda Village in Kerala, India, is an internationally recognised Ayurveda practitioner, inspirational speaker, and the author of eight widely read health books published in nine languages across the United States, Europe, and India.
Born into a traditional family in South India, Sri Ganesh began his education in Ayurveda in the early years of his life with his grandmother, a well-respected Ayurveda practitioner and poison therapist in their village.
Drawing on his traditional knowledge and over two decades of experience working as an Ayurveda practitioner around the world, Sri Ganesh has travelled extensively throughout Europe and the United States as an ambassador of Ayurveda, sharing his ancestors’ wisdom through talks, columns, and books.

events.id: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey-175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
events.venue: 175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey

“Āio” speaks to peace and inner calm. In this immersive evening, pūrākau, taonga pūoro and waiata are woven together with the healing vibrations of crystal singing bowls to create a space of deep listening and restoration.

The organic tones of traditional Māori instruments — including kōauau, pūtōrino, porotiti and pūtātara — blend with the resonance of crystal bowls, chimes and ukulele, inviting you to slow down, soften, and reconnect with your wairua and your body. Through story, sound and vibration, this is a gentle journey inward.

After the sound journey, share in a nourishing plant-based dinner and dessert, included in your ticket — a warm way to close the evening together.


What to bring:

Yoga mats, blankets and cushions will be provided but you are welcome to bring extra for comfort!



Meet your hosts & musicians: Smita, Peter, and Alexandra

Smita (Ngāi Te Rangi) was born and raised in Aotearoa. From a young age she was drawn to music as a means for reflection and self-expression. During her music studies at university, she was introduced to taonga pūoro, and this sparked a personal journey of reconnection to te ao Māori and her own whakapapa. Following university, she lived in an ashram for several years where she was introduced to the wonderful practice of bhakti yoga. She now lives with her husband in the Wellington region, where they regularly participate in kirtan (mantra music meditation) at Bhakti Lounge.

Peter is Pākehā, with grandparents from England and Ireland, and has been on a four-year journey learning the art of taonga pūoro — the traditional Māori musical instruments of Aotearoa. Guided by the wisdom and generosity of expert makers and players, he has learned both to craft and to play these unique instruments. For Peter, taonga pūoro offer a way to connect deeply with sound, story, and the natural world, and he is excited to share their voices as part of this sound bath experience.

Alexandra is a sound healing practitioner devoted to nurturing healing in both mind and body. She believes that sound vibration holds the power to expand consciousness and restore harmony and balance in our lives. Her journey into sound healing began with the realization of how deeply sound vibration was affecting her psyche—making her more mindful of the music she listens to and the intention it carries. Along the way, she has drawn inspiration from artists such as Deva Premal, The Flying Mystics, Stevin McNamara, and the Bhakti Lounge Kirtan. Their music has profoundly influenced her own sound healing practice, inspiring her to explore sound as a tool for transformation, connection, and inner peace.

Ravel Pavane pour une infante défunte
Andrew Norman Slip: Concerto for Trombone and Orchestra
Shostakovich Symphony No. 8

André de Ridder appears with generous support from The Diessl Family.

From the ethereal glow of Ravel’s luminous sound world to the towering emotional force of Shostakovich, this is music that confronts darkness and insists on the triumph of light. At its heart is a world premiere showcasing the extraordinary artistry of trombone virtuoso Jörgen van Rijen.

events.long_description:
Come join us for the fortnightly ritual on a Thursday night at Parrotdog Bar with legendary quizmaster Sam, where you and your crew will negotiate five rounds of amazing trivia with Gee Quiz.

Your team's street cred, life smarts and common sense will go a long way to ensuring success on the night.

events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Les Tripes (French for 'The Guts') Present:
The Music of Django Reinhardt

Dynamic woodwind virtuoso Oscar Lavën joins forces with swing guitarist specialists Adrian Jensen & James Quick, and double bass raconteur Daniel Yeabsley to present a homage to the unique music of guitarist Django Reinhardt and violinist Stephane Grapelli. Together they will create hard-working interpretations of the infectious, foot-stomping, film noir music known in 1940s Paris as Hot Club.

Thursday April 9th.

9pm Downbeat. Free Entry, Koha Encouraged.

Poster art by the marvelous Justine.

events.long_description:
ULTRA NEW ZEALAND - PHASE ONE LINEUP

A-Z
THE CHAINSMOKERS
DJ SNAKE
DARREN STYLES
MARLO
MISS MONIQUE
NICO MORENO
OLIVER HELDENS
ZEDD

PHASE TWO LINEUP DUE EARLY 2026

ULTRA Worldwide has just announced the inaugural ULTRA New Zealand, which will take place at Wellington Waterfront in Wellington on Friday, April 10, 2026. In celebration of this historic debut edition, ULTRA New Zealand will transform the country’s capital into a multi-stage experience featuring world-class electronic music talent. ULTRA New Zealand will be held at an idyllic waterfront location, promising to channel the signature spirit of ULTRA’s flagship Miami festival and deliver a landmark moment for both ULTRA Worldwide and New Zealand’s live music landscape.

ULTRA Worldwide’s arrival on the Wellington Waterfront brings a sense of grand spectacle to the capital - an event launch that will create an unprecedented atmosphere for New Zealand. Set against the harbor’s sweeping backdrop, ULTRA New Zealand will be an electronic music playground that elevates Wellington City onto the global stage of dance music culture.

ULTRA New Zealand is part of the ULTRA Worldwide family, which continues to cement its status as the world’s most international music festival brand. Boasting active events on all six inhabited continents, including the flagship Ultra Music Festival in Miami, the global festival brand is heralded for delivering an unparalleled combination of top-tier electronic talent, cutting-edge technology and large-scale productions.

FINAL TICKETS AVAILABLE AT -> https://ultranewzealand.com/

Stay tuned for additional lin

events.id: ULTRA New Zealand-Wellington Waterfront-Wellington NZ
events.name: ULTRA New Zealand
events.venue: Wellington Waterfront
events.long_description:
It’s entertained fans across six continents. Now, for the first time, ULTRA Music Festival is coming to New Zealand. Arguably one of the most recognisable brands in electronic dance music, ULTRA is coming to Wellington for an exclusive stop-off. Known for delivering an epic combination of electronic music talent and impressive stage productions, the festival will take place along the capital’s picturesque waterfront on Friday, 10 April 2026. 
The one-day festival will span three world-class stages, with a fourth dedicated to local drum and bass artists. Along with Wellington’s calm autumn weather, ULTRA is expected to attract up to 20,000 people from across the country, as well as Australia and beyond. 
There are some major international artists confirmed including Alison Wonderland, NGHTMRE, Pendulum and Ray Volpe. You can also expect to see The Chainsmokers, DJ Snake, Zedd, Darren Styles, Nico Moreno, Oliver Heldens, Marlo, & Miss Monique.

events.id: Ringlets EU/UK Fundraiser at San Fran, Wellington-171 Cuba Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Ringlets EU/UK Fundraiser at San Fran, Wellington
events.venue: 171 Cuba Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ringlets are soon heading off. March & April allows them just enough time to pack up an international driving permit, some muesli bars, and a set of guitar strings (can also be used as floss if you don’t mind a little blood) into a polka-dot bindle. May will then see them embark on a five week long tour of Europe & the United Kingdom. They also have a number of bookings later into the year, so they think they’ll stay on.
You can help them out: these two final headline shows – a shameless shake of the tip jar – in Wellington on April 10th, and Auckland on April 11th will be a chance for you to send them off in style. In the bands hometown of Auckland there will also be a garage sale (anything that didn’t fit into the bindle will be flogged off) alongside some actually high-quality merch and small trinkets exclusive to these fundraiser shows (Merch available in both cities).
Presented by Junk Mail & 100% Good
Ringlets live at San Fran
Friday 10th April - R18
With special guests Dropper, Cruelly & Girls Factory
Tickets from ringlets.co.nz

events.id: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition-11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition
events.venue: 11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for another fun night of social dancing with DJ Rafa, playing the grooviest Mambo, Salsa, and Bachata tracks! Emphasis on the MAMBO
Tonight we celebrate the Palladium era of Latin dance! Known as “the home of the Mambo,” the Palladium Ballroom was America’s most important venue for the development of Latin music and dance throughout the 1950s.
Mambo music and dance were co-created at the Palladium where Puerto Rican, Cuban, Dominican, Italian, Jewish, Irish, and African American patrons danced nightly to music from the “Big Three” Latin orchestras: Tito Puente, Tito Rodríguez, and Machito.
You can expect lots of Mambo and all-around good vibes!
$12 entry
DJ Rafa
Something classy or flashy (totally optional)
BYO drinks (but please keep them off the dance floor)
Salsa Magic's Friday Fiestas are held on the second Friday of every month and are a staple in the Wellington Latin dance community. All levels of experience and styles are welcome!

events.id: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - CROFTON-Crofton Downs Primary School, Chartwell Drive, Crofton Downs, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - Build Your Own Ancient Runes Puzzle Box - CROFTON
events.venue: Crofton Downs Primary School, Chartwell Drive, Crofton Downs, Wellington
events.long_description:
Introducing our new updated “Ancient Runes Puzzle Box”. Keep your treasures safe in the coolest way possible. Only you will know the special code to release the catch hidden under the mechanical Iris! This new improved version has 2 code wheels that you can use to set your own unique code, as well as 2 code pins that are stored on the box but have to be inserted correctly to activate the mechanism. If everything is set a hidden push button will then activate a solenoid that releases the catch.

Powered by 4 AAA batteries (provided), you’ll get to solder the components, screw and glue all the intricate pieces together and unwrap the puzzle to learn how it works. And, of course, you'll get to take it home! No previous experience is required and you’re welcome to bring a grown up if you want to.

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $135 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.id: Printing With Chine Colle - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Teens-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Printing With Chine Colle - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Teens
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Welcome to mixed media collage, the printmaker's way. Chine Colle is a printing process in which a thin sheet of paper is printed on and, at the same time, mounted on a sturdier sheet, creating a subtle, delicate backdrop to the printed image. Expanded on, this can include contrasting papers, graphic images, select colour shapes and fabric. The mission is just to have fun creating together in the fabulous Inverlochy print studio.

In this one-day workshop for ages 12+, students will:

- Practice traditional and individual Chinese collage techniques
- Explore the use of delicate papers more responsive to ink and hand printing
- Test and practice mixed media chine colle with images, papers and fabric
- How effective Chinese Collage is as a method of adding colour to prints
- Making and using a damp pack
- Recipes for traditional glues

Bring a water bottle and a packed lunch to enjoy in the historic Inverlochy house during break time.

events.id: Goya Live at The Dowse with RadioActive.FM-The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Goya Live at The Dowse with RadioActive.FM
events.venue: The Dowse Art Museum,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Goya Live at The Dowse with RadioActive.FM
Join us in the gallery for a live performance by Goya, which will be broadcast live by RadioActive.fm.

About this Event

Wellington noise-punks Goya take their name from 19th century Spanish painter, Francisco Goya, whose dark imaginative work spanned romanticism, madness and the horrors of war. Goya’s lyrics take aim at the inherent social-pathology of our consumerist lives in the final days of a dying a capitalist system.
pairs the heavy metal-inspired works of rocker artist Jason Greig with the darkly sublime works of old masters, including several pieces by Francisco Goya. Creating the effect of a séance across time, with themes of art, music, social criticisms, darkness and chaos, this exhibition provides the perfect backdrop for a live performance in the gallery by Goya.
Join us to hear from curators Chelsea Nichols and Aaron Lister, followed by the live performance by Goya, which will be broadcast live by RadioActive.fm.

PHASE TWO LINEUP DUE EARLY 2026
ULTRA Worldwide has just announced the inaugural ULTRA New Zealand, which will take place at Wellington Waterfront in Wellington on Friday, April 10, 2026. In celebration of this historic debut edition, ULTRA New Zealand will transform the country’s capital into a multi-stage experience featuring world-class electronic music talent. ULTRA New Zealand will be held at an idyllic waterfront location, promising to channel the signature spirit of ULTRA’s flagship Miami festival and deliver a landmark moment for both ULTRA Worldwide and New Zealand’s live music landscape.
ULTRA Worldwide’s arrival on the Wellington Waterfront brings a sense of grand spectacle to the capital - an event launch that will create an unprecedented atmosphere for New Zealand. Set against the harbor’s sweeping backdrop, ULTRA New Zealand will be an electronic music playground that elevates Wellington City onto the global stage of dance music culture.
ULTRA New Zealand is part of the ULTRA Worldwide family, which continues to cement its status as the world’s most international music festival brand. Boasting active events on all six inhabited continents, including the flagship Ultra Music Festival in Miami, the global festival brand is heralded for delivering an unparalleled combination of top-tier electronic talent, cutting-edge technology and large-scale productions.
FINAL TICKETS AVAILABLE AT -> https://ultranewzealand.com/

events.name: The Gumboot Day Express
events.venue: Paekakariki
events.long_description:
�� We’re excited to announce a special collaboration between Steam Incorporated and Feilding and District Steam Rail Society for 2026 — introducing The Gumboot Day Express!
Join us as we steam our way to Taihape, the Gumboot Capital of the World, for one of the most iconic and entertaining days on the Kiwi calendar. Saturday 11th April Only!
Tickets available at www.steaminc.org.nz/book-train-trip/tgde/
Hauled by Ab 608 “Passchendaele”, this heritage journey takes in the spectacular scenery of the North Island Main Trunk — including the stunning Rangitīkei viaducts — before arriving right in the heart of the Gumboot Day festivities.
Expect a fantastic day of small-town charm, live entertainment, markets, and of course, the famous gumboot throwing competition — all wrapped around a classic steam-hauled rail experience.
This is a joint venture between two passionate heritage rail organisations, and we’re proud to be working together to bring something special to the region.
Seats are now available, and we’d love to have you onboard!
Find out more and book via Steam Incorporated's website!
www.steaminc.org.nz/book-train-trip/tgde/

events.id: Family Shiny - Akatarawa Forest-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Family Shiny - Akatarawa Forest
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Registrations open 20th March 2026
What to expect
This is a “Family Shiny” trip into the Akatarawa Forest.
Non members are welcome to join us but must register to obtain details of start time and location.
Vehicles must have a low ratio gearbox and must not be a 4wd car, station wagon or van, and if a soft top must have a roll cage.
Visitor Registration- $20.00 per driver
There is no trip fee for this trip however non-members pay a “Day Membership Fee” of $20. This is payable in Cash to the Trip Leader on the day.
The trip should be relatively easy as it needs to cater for people just starting out and all types of 4wd vehicles – but a good way to see if you want to get into 4wding and a chance to meet some of our club members (if you are unsure if your vehicle is suitable just ask).
No Dogs Allowed sorry
Once you have completed 3 Family Shinys you can join the club but can only participate in Family Shiny or Touring Class trips. Once you have also done a Training Day and had your vehicle inspected by one of our approved inspectors you can come along on our other more adventurous trips. To find out more on how to join our club go to https://ccvcwellington.wildapricot.org/page-1725861.
Like our Facebook page to find out about more trips and club nights.

events.id: Lions Premier League 2026-Liardet Street Park, Berhampore, Wellington 6023, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Lions Premier League 2026
events.venue: Liardet Street Park, Berhampore, Wellington 6023, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Lions Premier League 2026 is set to become the flagship event of the SLSC calendar — a high-energy, franchise-style T20 tournament inspired by the biggest leagues, but grounded in club pride, community, and competition.
Six franchises. Six owners. One champion.
This tournament brings together the best of our club — past, present, and emerging talent — in a format designed to entertain, challenge, and elevate Lions cricket to the next level.
---
The Format
• 6 franchise teams
• T20 format
• Each team must field a minimum of 8 SLSC players, with up to 3 contracted non-club players
• Played in a festival-style atmosphere with crowds, food, energy, and atmosphere
---
The Franchises
• Ella Beauties
• Mirissa Mission
• Rajarata Rambos
• Yala Night Chasers
• Colombo Seven XI
• Sigiriya Fresh Bros
Each franchise will be led by an owner who builds their squad through the Lions Premier League Auction.
---
Team Ownership & Auction
Each team is owned by an individual or group for $500, which includes:
• $250 franchise ownership fee
• $250 in auction credits to recruit players
---
Lions Premier League Auction Night:
6 March 2026
A marquee evening where strategy, nerves, and big calls shape the destiny of each franchise.
---
Tournament Dates
April 3 - Round Robin
April 11 - Finals Day
April 12 - Reserve Day
---
Prizes & Awards
•
Champions: $1,500
•
Runners-Up: $1,000
•
Most Valuable Player: $200
•
Best Batter: $150
•
Best Bowler: $150
---
More Than Cricket
The Lions Premier League is about:
• Bringing the cricket community together
• Creating opportunities for players to shine
• Uncovering future Premier talent
• Delivering a professional, entertaining cricket & community event
Expect a fantastic couple of days with joyful crowds, great food, and an atmosphere to cherish.
---
Powered by SLSC

events.description:
The Lions Premier League 2026 is set to become the flagship event of the SLSC calendar — a high-energy, franchise-style T20 tournament inspired by the biggest leagues, but grounded in club pride, community, and competition.
Six owners.
This tournament brings together the best of our club — past, present, and emerging talent — in a format designed to entertain, challenge, and elevate Lions cricket to the next level.
--- The Format • 6 franchise teams • T20 format • Each team must field a minimum of 8 SLSC players, with up to 3 contracted non-club players • Played in a festival-style atmosphere with crowds, food, energy, and atmosphere --- The Franchises • Ella Beauties • Mirissa Mission • Rajarata Rambos • Yala Night Chasers • Colombo Seven XI • Sigiriya Fresh Bros Each franchise will be led by an owner who builds their squad through the Lions Premier League Auction.
--- Tournament Dates April 3 - Round Robin April 11 - Finals Day April 12 - Reserve Day --- Prizes & Awards • Champions: $1,500 • Runners-Up: $1,000 • Most Valuable Player: $200 • Best Batter: $150 • Best Bowler: $150 --- More Than Cricket The Lions Premier League is about: • Bringing the cricket community together • Creating opportunities for players to shine • Uncovering future Premier talent • Delivering a professional, entertaining cricket & community event Expect a fantastic couple of days with joyful crowds, great food, and an atmosphere to cherish.

events.id: Kaicycle Community Open Day Āperira (April)-7 Hospital Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Kaicycle Community Open Day Āperira (April)
events.venue: 7 Hospital Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Have you been looking for a chance to visit Kaicycle Urban Farm? Come along our Community Open Day in Āperira!
9:30am - 12.30pm COMMUNITY WORKING BEE - Registrations required.
Support our local food by helping to grow it! Many hands make light work - come and get your hands in the māra, learn some skills, and meet some cool people. Please register at www.kaicycle.org.nz/volunteer
12:30PM - 1:30pm, KAI BREAK
Join us for a shared lunch and a chilled hang out in the māra. Please bring some kai to share if you can.
1:00PM - FARM TOUR
If you haven't been to our urban farm before, this is a great opportunity to find out what we're all about and how our farm and community composting works.
1:30pm - 3.oopm KOHA WORKSHOP: INTRODUCTION TO SEED SAVING - Registration require - email community@kaicycle.org.nz to register.
We know many of you couldn’t make it to our seed saving workshop in March, so we’re going to run it again for April! Seed saving is an ancient practice and an essential tool in creating a circular and resilient food system and enabling kai sovereignty. It also allows us to select for seed that will thrive in our micro-climates, strengthening crops year by year. We'll wānanga about the importance of saving seed, understanding the whakapapa of seeds and plant reproduction, and what you need to know to start saving seed yourself. We'll put our learnings into practice at the farm, saving and processing seed from our summer crops. We will have an area for swapping seed, so please bring along any saved seed you would like to swap. This is a koha workshop and we ask you to pay what you can afford. If that’s a gold coin that is completely fine! If you can afford more we really appreciate it - we are a charity and rely on donations to be able to carry out our mahi. Koha can be paid in cash or by bank transfer.
Registrations required - email community@kaicycle.org.nz to sign up.
****
Any pātai? Email community@kaicycle.org.nz

events.long_description:
Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo is turning 120 years old this April, and we want to celebrate this milestone with all our Te Whanganui-a-Tara Wellington whanau!
Our official birthday celebration weekend will be held on 11-12 April, and you’re all invited!
Pre-book your tickets to our birthday weekend now and go in the draw to win an amazing prize pack, including:
- 1x family Zoo Crew membership for one year (2x adults and up to 3x kids)
- 1x Close Encounter for 4x people
- $50 Rex Tremendous voucher (can be redeemed at Banana Bar too)
- 2 x tickets to do Wellington Chocolate Factory's Original Chocolatier Experience
- A retail prize pack from Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo and Iko Iko
Zoo Crew members, since you already have a membership, you don't need tickets. Just make sure you scan your pass when you arrive at the Zoo to go in the draw!

events.id: Cantina Comedy-Sunday Cantina,31 Poplar Avenue, Raumati South, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Cantina Comedy
events.venue: Sunday Cantina,31 Poplar Avenue, Raumati South, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Cantina Comedy
Get ready for a Good Friday… the fun kind.

Join us at Sunday Cantina in Raumati on Friday 10th April from 7pm for a stand-up showcase that’s bringing the laughs like the Easter Bunny brings chocolate — generously and slightly unhinged.

Twelve comedians. Five minutes each. Rapid-fire punchlines, bold new material, and just enough chaos to keep things interesting. If one joke doesn’t land, don’t worry — another comic is hopping up right behind them.

Expect everything from sharp one-liners to awkward confessions and the kind of stories that should probably have stayed in the group chat. It’s fast, it’s fun, and it’s the perfect way to kick off your Easter weekend.

Grab a margarita, order some tacos, bring your mates, and settle in.

Tickets are just $10 — cheaper than a supermarket Easter egg and way more satisfying.

events.id: Elsewhere in the Building - Improv with WIT-Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Elsewhere in the Building - Improv with WIT
events.venue: Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Elsewhere in the Building is a friendly improv format that happens in different places within an overarching location suggested by the audience, creating a world of multiple scenes.

This is the first of a series of monthly shows WIT will be holding at Thistle Hall, the next will be Friday 1 May (Micetro), then on 5 June and 3 July (shows tba).

The Wellington Improvisation Troupe, aka WIT, is a member-based community theatre group and registered charity, teaching and performing in Wellington since 2003. Our theatre is made up on the spot, usually inspired by suggestions from the audience and each other. WIT players share a love of storytelling and work together as a team to inspire the audience, we’re sometimes serious, often hilarious and always totally unpredictable.

Pay-what-you-can tickets from $5, and door sales. Entrance to the Upstairs Hall is in Arthur St, or through the exhibition space entrance in Cuba Street.

events.id: FIW26: Feldenkrais Intensive Wellington-Te Whaea, 11 Hutchison Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: FIW26: Feldenkrais Intensive Wellington
events.venue: Te Whaea, 11 Hutchison Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for the popular 11th annual Feldenkrais® Intensive in Wellington.
It's a special retreat-style weekend of somatic, mindful movement and awareness that helps you discover easier, more comfortable ways to move and feel.
Over two days you’ll experience a series of 8 guided movement lessons from 4 experienced Feldenkrais Practitioners where small, curious explorations can lead to surprisingly big changes.
You will:
Feel more at home and comfortable in their body
Move with less effort and less tension
Improve balance, coordination and flexibility
Experience greater body awareness and mindful movement
Find a sense of calm, curiosity and discovery
Enjoy movement that feels lighter, smoother and more enjoyable
Doing the lessons close together has a powerful accumulative benefit. Each lesson builds on the last, helping new patterns settle in while old habits fade into the background.
Who is it for?
People completely new to the Feldenkrais Method® who are curious to experience it for the first time
Those who already love Feldenkrais® and want a deeper immersion
Anyone wanting to move with more ease in everyday life, sport, dance, music, martial arts, yoga or simply feel better in their body
What to expect
Gentle guided lessons in somatic and mindful movement
The chance to explore the Feldenkrais Method more deeply
A welcoming community of like-minded people
Plenty of space to rest, reflect and integrate
Delicious morning and afternoon tea included
Beautiful venue with lots of free parking
Please note: This year’s Intensive is a 2-day event (instead of the usual 3 days) on Saturday and Sunday, 11 and 12 April 2026 from 10.00am to 4.30pm daily.
Cost
$190 for the two days ($150 for earlybird registrations by 15 March)
To book
Please contact Lois at team@fiw.nz or 027 601 1858.
Come spend a weekend learning, moving, connecting and feeling more at home in your body.

events.id: Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Weekend: Our Ancestors panel talk-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Weekend: Our Ancestors panel talk
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Over the weekend of 11 and 12 of April we will be staging a very special series of exploration events to celebrate the recent reopening of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui. As part of this series, we're very excited to be hosting Tusiata Avia, Gareth Watkins and Flora Feltham for a panel discussion on the theme of 'Our Ancestors' on Saturday 11 April, 11am - 12pm.
This event will be held in the beautiful Ngā Pou Ruahine space on the second floor. This is a free event and all are welcome. We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited, so please arrive early to ensure a seat.
Meet our esteemed panellists...
Tusiata Avia:
Tusiata Avia MNZM is a celebrated New Zealand poet and children's author. Among her many awards, her 2021 collection 'The Savage Coloniser' won the Mary and Peter Biggs Award for Poetry at the Ockham New Zealand Book Awards. She was honoured in the 2020 Queen’s Birthday list.
Gareth Watkins:
Gareth Watkins is a hugely respected LGBTQIA+ historian and a prominent voice and recorder of LGBTQIA+ history, especially that of the Wellington area.

We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited, so please arrive early to ensure a seat.

events.id: NZ & OZ Rock Experience - Live at Abandoned Taproom Petone-Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: NZ & OZ Rock Experience - Live at Abandoned Taproom Petone
events.venue: Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
NZ & OZ Rock Experience - Live at Abandoned Taproom Petone
The NZ/Oz Rock Experience is the sound of Downunder!

Aussie. Kiwi. Absolute classics.

The NZ/Oz Experience is a high-energy celebration of the songs that shaped Australia and New Zealand’s rock history — performed live by charting NZ and Australian musicians who know how to bring a crowd with them.

From the hooks of The Mockers and The Exponents, to the pub-rock punch of IThe Angels and Australian Crawl, through to favourites from Dragon, INXS and Th' Dudes — this is the soundtrack of stadiums, summer festivals and late-night dancefloors on both sides of the ditch.

It’s loud in the right places, melodic where it matters, and built for singing along. Whether you were there the first time around or just love great live rock, this is a night of nostalgia, energy and songs everyone knows.

If you know the words… you’re coming.
