events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ringlets are soon heading off. March & April allows them just enough time to pack up an international driving permit, some muesli bars, and a set of guitar strings (can also be used as floss if you don’t mind a little blood) into a polka-dot bindle. May will then see them embark on a five week long tour of Europe & the United Kingdom. They also have a number of bookings later into the year, so they think they’ll stay on.   
You can help them out: these two final headline shows  – a shameless shake of the tip jar – in Wellington on April 10th, and Auckland on April 11th will be a chance for you to send them off in style. In the bands hometown of Auckland there will also be a garage sale (anything that didn’t fit into the bindle will be flogged off) alongside some actually high-quality merch and small trinkets exclusive to these fundraiser shows (Merch available in both cities).

“Incredible band” - Deb Grant, BBC Radio 6

“Ringlets are the most exciting post-bunk band to emerge from the New Zealand music scene in years.” -  Rolling Stone Magazine 

Presented by Junk Mail & 100% Good
Ringlets live at San Fran
Friday 10th April  - R18
With special guests Dropper, Cruelly & Girls Factory
Tickets from ringlets.co.nz

events.name: THE BLUE SUMMER EP RELEASE W/ SERENNA
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
After slaving away at a leisurely pace for a year in Lyall Bay, dynamic Wellington rock five-piece The Blue Summer finally has something to show for their toil. Engineered by scene veteran Peter Jamieson and mixed by their own Alan Beere, The Blue Summer delivers five searing rock tracks with wisps of the gothic, the chill of post-punk and the lingering hint of funk. Witness them perform the EP along with older and newer material at their spiritual home of Valhalla on Vivian Street on Friday the tenth of April.
Joining them is Serenna - a gothic rock band recently resurrected after a fifteen-year hiatus, and now complete with some luminaries of the darker local scene.
Doors open at 8pm. Punctuality is encourated. Tickets on UTR for $10 + BF.

events.id: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition-Dancetopia - Dance Studio,11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition
events.venue: Dancetopia - Dance Studio,11 Torrens Ter, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Salsa Magic’s Friday Fiesta - Palladium Edition
Join us for another fun night of social dancing with DJ Rafa, playing the grooviest Mambo, Salsa, and Bachata tracks! Emphasis on the MAMBO 💃

Tonight we celebrate the Palladium era of Latin dance! Known as “the home of the Mambo,” the Palladium Ballroom was America’s most important venue for the development of Latin music and dance throughout the 1950s.

Mambo music and dance were co-created at the Palladium where Puerto Rican, Cuban, Dominican, Italian, Jewish, Irish, and African American patrons danced nightly to music from the “Big Three” Latin orchestras: Tito Puente, Tito Rodríguez, and Machito.

You can expect lots of Mambo and all-around good vibes!

💰 $12 entry
🎧 DJ Rafa
👗 Something classy or flashy (totally optional)
🍹 BYO drinks (but please keep them off the dance floor)

Salsa Magic's Friday Fiestas are held on the second Friday of every month and are a staple in the Wellington Latin dance community. All levels of experience and styles are welcome! 🙌

events.id: Historic Postcard Walk: Before and After - Guided Walk-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Historic Postcard Walk: Before and After - Guided Walk
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join the Friends of the Garden for an easy 60-minute walk in the Main Garden to reflect on the past hundred years of the Botanic Garden through the views of 20th century postcards. Donal Duthie, a head gardener in the Wellington Botanic Garden in the 60s and 70s, collected many valuable postcards of the Garden.
Meet at the Founders Entrance, Glenmore Street.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)
This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.
Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.long_description:
Slam! The Ultimate After Party feat. David Chouman ( Aus), Ashwynz & Halo
Signature Socials Proudly Present's


Wellington… are you ready to finish your night with BIG energy vibes? 🔥


Signature Socials is back with another absolute stunner — and this one is built for the true after-hours crew.


🔊 Featuring


David Chouman (AUS) | Ashwynz| Halo


From pounding techno grooves to euphoric trance lifts and full-power psy drops — this is the after party that keeps the pulse racing till the lights come on.


📍 Valhalla – Wellington
📅 FRIDAY, 10 April
🎶 Underground vibes. Heavy bass. No holding back.


If you know, you know.
If you don’t… it’s time to find out.


Get ready to SLAM. 💥


About our headliner, David Chouman:


Hailing from Melbourne, David Chouman is a rising force in the modern dance scene, known for explosive productions and performances. With a release catalog spanning Reaching Altitude, Nocturnal Knights, and CLUBWRK, his sound has been championed by global icons like MaRLo, Giuseppe Ottaviani, and Aly & Fila.


Fresh off a massive performance at Dreamstate Melbourne and the successful launch of his own 'Chew On This Live' brand, David is now taking his high-octane sound across to New Zealand. Known for creating moments that make the dancefloor feel truly alive, David Chouman arrives in Wellington to prove why he is one of the most exciting names in the new generation of Trance and Techno.




R18 | ID Complulsory

events.id: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
UPGRADED TO MEOW NUI DUE TO OVERWHELMING DEMAND
Handsome Tours are proud to present The Mountain Goats!
Veteran indie-rock storytellers The Mountain Goats will make their long-awaited return to Australia and New Zealand in April 2026, with performances in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth, Auckland and Wellington. This eagerly-anticipated tour represents the band's first visit to Australia in 9 years (2017) and their first trip to New Zealand in 16 years (2010).
Fronted by prolific songwriter and novelist John Darnielle, The Mountain Goats are revered the world over for their sprawling catalogue of twenty-three studio albums released between 1994 and 2023. This tour announcement coincides with the band today unveiling album number twenty-four, 'Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan' - to be released 7th November 2025 on Cadmean Dawn Records (the band’s own label).
Produced by the Mountain Goats’ multi-instrumentalist Matt Douglas, Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan is a full-on musical that stands as the most conceptually detailed and musically elaborate project in the band’s ever-expanding catalogue. It features appearances from The Replacements’ Tommy Stinson, harpist Mikaela Davis, musical theatre royalty Lin-Manuel Miranda (Tony Award-winning ‘Hamilton’, Disney’s ‘Moana’ & ‘Encanto’), and the band’s new bassist Cameron Ralston.
Don’t miss one of indie rock’s most enduring and beloved bands as they make their long-overdue return to our shores!
events.description:
Handsome Tours are proud to present The Mountain Goats!
Veteran indie-rock storytellers The Mountain Goats will make their long-awaited return to Australia and New Zealand in April 2026, with performances in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth, Auckland and Wellington.
This eagerly-anticipated tour represents the band's first visit to Australia in 9 years (2017) and their first trip to New Zealand in 16 years (2010).
Fronted by prolific songwriter and novelist John Darnielle, The Mountain Goats are revered the world over for their sprawling catalogue of twenty-three studio albums released between 1994 and 2023.
This tour announcement coincides with the band today unveiling album number twenty-four, 'Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan' - to be released 7th November 2025 on Cadmean Dawn Records (the band’s own label).

events.id: Ride the Cyclone-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Ride the Cyclone
events.venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Darkly funny, wildly imaginative, and surprisingly heartfelt, Ride the Cyclone is the viral musical phenomenon that has taken the world by storm.
In this hilarious and exhilarating story, the lives of six teenagers of the St. Cassian Chamber Choir are changed forever after they board a doomed rollercoaster ride.
Thrown into the abyss, they awake to find an ominous mechanical fortune teller that offers them the chance to compete for the prize … to return home in one piece.
Now confronted with their darkest desires, shameful secrets and each other, this “supremely witty … and just plain delightful” (New York Times) musical makes a uniquely uplifting and outrageous case for what makes a life worth living.

events.id: The Old Cold Hearts Club-12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: The Old Cold Hearts Club
events.venue: 12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Everyone has a secret to reveal… and Detective Raven McAllister is determined to uncover the truth.
Set over one unforgettable night at a local karaoke bar, The Old Cold Hearts Club follows Detective McAllister as he observes a wild and intriguing cast of characters. As the songs roll on and secrets begin to surface, the detective inches closer to a revelation that will throw everyone - including himself - for a loop.
Presented by Silver Noodle Soup, this original production is created and performed by young disabled artists from Te Whanganui-a-Tara. SNS is a Wellington-based charitable trust where disabled people aged approximately 16–35 are fully included in the leadership and direction of all aspects of theatrical and cinematic creativity - from development and production to promotion and performance.
Guided by experienced theatre-makers Stevie Hancox-Monk, Madeline McNamara and Hannah Kelly, the ensemble brings humour, heart and mystery to the stage in a celebration of inclusive theatre-making.
Silver Noodle Soup has been creating original performance and film work across Wellington for several years, including opening Disability Pride Week in 2019 with a performance and film showcase at BATS Theatre.
Join us at Hannah Playhouse for a night of karaoke, secrets and surprises - where nothing is quite what it seems.
Cold Noodle Soup is dedicated to making this show accessible to all audience members, if you would like to contact them directly with questions or to discuss the show please do so at silvernoodlesoup@gmail.com.
For more details about access at The Hannah please visit their website here.

events.id: William Jackman - Album Launch Party @Vogelmorn Upstairs-93 Mornington Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: William Jackman - Album Launch Party @Vogelmorn Upstairs
events.venue: 93 Mornington Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
William Jackman is a singer songwriter born n raised in Te Whanganui-a-Tara, Wellington.
Performing his way through high-school on the streets and stage, William helped form the group The Shadow Blasters, who released two albums, one here in Aotearoa and one while living in the UK. Showcasing a myriad of genres, from Hard Rock to the psychedelic, Alternative Country to soul based ballads and blues.
William takes to the stage this autumn to celebrate his two upcoming solo releases, which include a string of original compositions. The albums build upon some of his earlier works resonating with older fans, while showcasing his natural aptitude for poetic discourse.
He’ll be preforming a set solo and then backed by the duo “The New Thing” bringing both sides of the coin.
You can find his music on the web and across all the regular platforms.
Tickets can be found @ https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/.../William-Jackman...
7pm Doors - 8pm Music Starts
$20
-------------------------------------------------------------------------
"Vogelmorn Upstairs is not a wheelchair accessible building. There is an accessible bathroom in the space.
This is an all ages venue but those under the age of 16 must be accompanied by a designated adult after 8pm.
The Vogelmorn Precinct is a community space located in a residential area.
With that in mind, the team ask that you be kind to others' and that when you leave the gig, you do so quietly to avoid disturbing our neighbors.”
“Please note that our restaurant is currently closed.

events.name: LNKF #63: Mal and Mog
events.venue: 1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Late Night Knife Fight is the monthly improv battle where three teams face off in a fight for glory, honour, and the audience's favour. The winner returns in the headline spot next month; the losers wail and gnash their teeth.
We'll bring the knives; you bring the judgement.
Headlined by:
MAL AND MOG (Megan Connolly and Malcolm Morrison)
Contested by:
THE HISTORY BOY: WILD PLANET (Matt Powell)
ALPHA? BET! (Steven Youngblood, Guanny Liu-Prosee and Annabel)
TBC
The first rule of Knife Fight is... BOOK EARLY
Monthly (ish) at BATS Theatre
Tix $25 Waged / $15 Unwaged
Upcoming teams, full title history and more at http://knifefight.nz
~~~
ACCESSIBILITY
The Stage is located on the ground floor. The ground floor at BATS is fully wheelchair accessible with a ramp at the north end of the building, which provides direct access to the foyer and bar area, and a wheelchair accessible Box Office counter and bathroom on the ground floor.
If you have accessibility questions or requirements please contact BATS directly on book@bats.co.nz or call 04 802 4175.
The venue's bar Lumen is open from 5pm, doors open 10 minutes before showtime. Seating is not allocated. Latecomers will be admitted but you have to go across the stage - we recommend being on time
~~~
Late Night Knife Fight (presented by Locomotive) is a joyful proving ground for improvisors creating new work and ensembles, offering development, coaching, and collaboration across companies and styles. Seize the opportunity to catch the next big thing in improv before it takes flight!

events.id: Rts / Dj Thank U So Much / Geist-272 Taranaki St, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Rts / Dj Thank U So Much / Geist
events.venue: 272 Taranaki St, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
RTS is the analogue soundscape project of Harry Lilley and Fraser Williams, otherwise known for playing in Te Papaioea-based noise rock band First Move. De-quantizing the minimal angularism of their ottheher project, RTS sees the duo orient themselves within opaque sonic spaces that exist near the intersection of acoustic resonance, electromagnetism and amplification. Building on shows and recording experiments over the last year, this tour will coincide with the release of their first full-length offering.
--
DJ Thank U So Much cut her teeth on 100 birthdays and 100 house parties for 100 people, and is now ready to deftly crawl onto the opposite end of the scene unprepared & hungry for consideration. Her specialties are weird edits and mangled 21st century classics. Her setup doesn't make a lot of sense, but this end product might: every dance pop song you heard from 2000-2014 forcefully turned into something that is no longer dance music. Be prepared to sit down and raise an eyebrow at least twice. TYSM!
--
Geist is the duo of Laurence Diack (electronics, coding) and Helena Erickson (theremin) who make experimental noise & drone sets often incorporating multimedia and unconventional elements.

$15 presales via UTR
$20 door sales ($10 unwaged)
Special thanks to CNZ for supporting Pyramid Club's programme

events.long_description:
Unleash your inner dark side at URGE BLACK. The pulsating rhythms of liberation meet the sultry allure of leather-clad revelry. This isn’t just a dance party; it’s a sensorial odyssey that transcends age and convention. Prepare to be captivated, challenged, and ultimately liberated in an atmosphere where every shimmy and shake is an act of personal revolution.
URGE doesn’t just throw parties; we craft transformative experiences that celebrate the raw, unbridled essence of gay culture. Leather, fetish, uniform, gear, sports kit, etc.
Let’s dance and celebrate our authentic selves!
URGE tips:
Doors open at 9:00 PM.
Door sales are limited and $60 (cash only) if the event has not sold out prior.
Peak times: Queues can form if everyone arrives at once. We work to get you in quickly, so please be patient and courteous to our staff.
Dark Room: Located out back. Explore it if you’re feeling adventurous!
Your DJs:
Winner Mick
DJ K-OTIK
Everett Wayne
Event Rules:
This is a safe space: have fun, be kind, spread love, and look out for each other.
Follow instructions: Listen to staff and volunteers—they’re there to keep everyone safe. Ignoring their directions may result in removal from the event.
ZERO tolerance for harassment: This includes racism, transphobia, homophobia, sexism, misgendering, xenophobia, ableism, and fatphobia.
CONSENT IS KEY: Consent must be explicit and enthusiastic and can be withdrawn at any time.
- - - - SEE YOU ON THE DANCE FLOOR - - - -

events.venue: Ākau Tangi Sports Centre, 72 Kemp Street,72 Kemp St, Kilbirnie, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
1st Filipino Pickleball Fundraising Tournament
Kia ora, mga kababayan!


We’re excited to share that Pistang Pilipino, our biggest annual Filipino cultural celebration in Aotearoa is returning this year! Even more special, Wellington will be hosting Pistang Pilipino for the first time in 10 years through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, bringing together Filipino families, players and communities from all over New Zealand.

And this year, we’re adding something NEW and HISTORIC…
The FIRST‑EVER Filipino Pickleball Tournament in Pistang Pilipino history! A fun, competitive, and community‑driven event.

Calling all Filipino Pickleball Players!

This 11th April of 2026, we’re starting things off with the First Filipino Pickleball Fundraising Tournament! All proceeds will go towards supporting Pistang Pilipino 2026 through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, helping us make this celebration bigger and better for everyone.

Tournament Categories:

Singles
• Men’s Intermediate
• Men’s Advance
• Women’s Intermediate
• Women’s Advance

Doubles
• Men’s Intermediate
• Men’s Advance
• Women’s Intermediate
• Women’s Advance

Mixed Doubles
• Intermediate
• Advance

Entry Fees

$40 – Base fee (includes your first category)
+$20 – For each additional category you join
Play more, enjoy more, support more!

Scan the QR code on the poster or click the registration link to secure your spot. Limited slots per category!


Let’s make history together, support Pistang Pilipino, and celebrate our Filipino pride on and off the court.

Salamat po!

events.id: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
About 1 in 5 New Zealand adults experience a common mental illness each year. Understanding how to talk about mental health and help someone in need are important skills. This certified Mental Health First Aid course, will teach you the skills and knowledge to recognise and respond to someone experiencing mental health challenges. Course fees: If you are a NZ citizen/Permanent Resident Visa the course fee is $199 (evidence of eligibility required). Others pay $499.

Description
Mental Health First Aid is an evidence-based workshop which gives people the skills and confidence to have supportive conversations with their whānau, co-workers and community and help guide them to professional help if needed.

You will learn to recognise common mental health challenges, how to provide initial help to someone experiencing a mental health challenge using a practical, evidence-based action plan, and how to respond in a crisis situation. During the workshop you will have the opportunity to practice new skills in a safe environment.

You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the reduced course fee. A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Mental health challenges covered in the course include:

Depression
Anxiety
Psychosis
Substance use problems
Mental Health Crisis Covered:

Suicidal thoughts and behaviours
Non- Suicidal self-injury
Panic attack
Traumatic events
Severe effects from Alcohol or other substance use
Aggressive behaviours
People who attend Mental Health First Aid training can complete a short online assessment to be accredited as a Mental Health First Aider. You must attend the full two days to be able to do the online assessment for accreditation. The accreditation is valid for three years.

If you choose not to be accredited, you will receive a certificate of participation if you attend the full course. You will also receive a workbook as part of your course fee.

A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Prerequisites
You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the reduced course fee.

A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

events.name: Leaf Library
events.venue: Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington
events.long_description:
Create art from leaves and lightboxes these April School Holidays with artist Dr Gabby O’Connor.

To accompany her exhibition Rau Whārangi at Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, local artist Dr Gabby O’Connor invites tamariki and whānau to take part in a hands-on, drop-in art and ecology experience during the April school holidays.

Tamariki can create leaves from recycled items and use lightboxes to explore the art of shadows.

Digital images of the artworks created during the session may be included in the Rau Whārangi exhibition video, which plays alongside Gabby O’Connor’s installation at Te Matapihi.

Suitable for all ages. Free, donations appreciated.

Held at Nōku te Ao Capital E, Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central.

events.id: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Kua raranga tahi tātou he whariki mō āpōpō
Together we weave the mat for future generations

Nau mai haere mai, all are welcome to attend Ōtari Raranga wānanga (classes).

These classes run approximately fortnightly.

No experience required and all materials provided. You will walk away with woven taonga at the end of every class.

During the first 2 classes, beginner students will complete a variety of putiputi (flowers).

We provide harakeke for you if it is your FIRST class and we will teach you how to harvest for yourself during this time and what to harvest for your next class.

After that, if you would like us to harvest for you, purchase the add-on - "Please harvest for me" for an additional fee.

Moving forward, you will start making kōnae (2 corner kete - basket), kono (4-sided kete), tapawhāroa and more.
You will need to harvest 8-10 rau for yourself and start building your own tool kit.

After completing 6 or more beginner classes (close together), you may be ready to move up to our intermediate classes. Please talk with our kaiako and kaiāwhina if you are interested.

With regular attendance, you will:
- Learn tikanga (protocols) and karakia for hauhake (harvesting) and preparing harakeke
- Build your mātauranga (knowledge)
- Use kupu Māori appropriate to raranga practice
- Develop your pūkenga (practical skills)
- Create taonga (treasures) for your whānau
- Build hononga (connections) within our raranga rōpū (group)
- Have the skills to practice at home.

What’s included in your beginner's ticket?
- Three-hour weaving class with experienced kaiako
- Harakeke is provided for your first class, and then you should hauhake (harvest) for yourself or click the "please harvest for me" option in addition to your ticket at least two days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share

Led by Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera) , Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri), and/or Anna Lees (Te Āti Awa, Kāi Tahu).
On occasion our raranga rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), will teach and we often have support from our kaiāwhina.


Important information
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia
- For tikanga purposes, beginner ticket sales close two days before wānanga, giving us time to hauhake (harvest) for you
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets
- We welcome children aged 10-14, but they should be accompanied by an adult with a ticket

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers
For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit our website: www.otariraranga.co.nz

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with the Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits, please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com or visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information

events.venue: Level 1, 66 Tory Street, New Zealand 6011,Lone Star, 66 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
🇳🇿🇩🇪Kiwi Kölsch Service!🇩🇪🇳🇿
Kölsch Service is BACK!! But this time with a kiwi twist to celebrate FRESH HOP SEASON!! Frischë Dösen Kölsch is made with fresh Wakatū hops, straight off the bine in Nelson, which we'll be serving the traditional way on Saturday 11th of April - just like in Köln.

What's Kölsch service?
It's kinda like beer yum cha but there's only one thing on the menu - 200ml glasses of Kölsch bier! 🍺
We keep track of your tab by marking the edge of your coaster and when your glass is empty - voilà, another glass appears! 🪄 If you're done, just pop your coaster on top of your glass to indicate no more.
You can order food and non-alcoholics the same way, just take a seat and everything will come to you. 🪑

🇳🇿Special KIWI Menu!🇳🇿
We'll be serving up distinctively kiwi snacks - like classic kiwi dip with Proper Crisps, Marmite & Cheese Garlic Flatbreads plus our usual offerings of Cheese Rolls and Cheerios and delicioius pizzas.

To make sure you get a table, book here: https://www.meandoses.co.nz/bookings


We'll be running Kölsch service from 12pm - 5pm on Saturday April 11th, come along and get the Köln experience! 🍻🥨🇩🇪

To make sure you get a table, book here: https://www.meandoses.co.nz/bookings We'll be running Kölsch service from 12pm - 5pm on Saturday April 11th, come along and get the Köln experience!

events.id: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Waenganui (Intermediate)-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Waenganui (Intermediate)
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ma te mōhio ka roa, ma te ora ka mōhio
Through learning there is life, through life there is learning

Nau mai haere mai, welcome to Ōtari Raranga Wānanga - Waenganui

Our kaiako will have made it clear to you when you can advance to this level from beginners.

If you are new to Ōtari but have experience please reach out before committing.

Projects are planned in advance of each wānanga to help us move forward together as a rōpū and awhi one another while working on similar taonga.
Please check your emails beforehand so you know what to bring.

Waenganui Wānanga are an important step toward joining our Tuakana Rōpū, who will often be attending these wānanga.
While they work on their own taonga projects, they are a valuable source of support for learners—happy to kōrero, share advice, and offer inspiration along the way.

Projects may include new closes for your kete timata, kete waikawa, puritanga, pīkau and pōtae.
We will teach you preparation, preservation and storage of harakeke - important especially over winter months. Additionally, we will induct you to the Ōtari Pā Harakeke when ready.

With regular attendance, you will deepen the mātauranga gained from beginner classes and share your learnings with others. (Refer to below for a reminder)

What’s included in your intermediate ticket?
- Three+ hours weaving class with our tuakana weavers and raranga rangatira.
- Limited access to tools, as you are expected to have your own kete mahi (tool kit).
- You are expected to bring your own harakeke.
- We encourage you to harvest for yourself, but if you are unable to harvest for class, we have ‘Please harvest for me’ options in addition to your ticket at least three days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share


Led by our raranga Rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), with Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri) and / or Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera)


Important information:
- Waenganui Rōpū are expected to harvest for themselves, but if something unexpected pops up we have add-on services available during the ticketing process. A minimum of 3 days notice is required.
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia.
- At our discretion, tickets are fully refundable or transferable.
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class.
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets.

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit www.otariraranga.co.nz

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community.
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- All donations over $5 made to The Trust are tax-deductible
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com
- Visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information

events.venue: Newtown Community Centre Theatre, Columbo Street and Rintoul Street, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
FIESTA! - Latin American Show & Flavours is a vibrant cultural event happening on Saturday 11 April 2026, from 6:00 to 9:00 PM, at the Newtown Community Centre in Wellington, bringing together live music, dance, comedy, and Latin American food to celebrate the richness, energy, and diversity of Latin culture in one unforgettable evening. Fiesta is a Family Friendly Event.

Get ready for a high-energy night with Cumbiasonica, whose infectious cumbia grooves and Latin rhythms will have everyone singing, dancing, and moving together all night long. Their live shows are known for big energy, catchy choruses, and an atmosphere that invites the whole audience to join in.

The evening also features the hilarious Teti Queen, delivering bold comedy and Latin charm, plus vibrant performances and a short salsa class with Salsa Therapy, perfect for both beginners and dance lovers. Special guests: Estudio de Samba Wellington and Wellicomedia.

Complete the experience by tasting Latin America with Argentine, Peruvian, and Brazilian flavours, alongside traditional Latin snacks and drinks provided by Latino Foods NZ.

Tickets:

Early Bird $20 (till12th March)

Standard $25 (till 10th April)

Find us!

https://www.facebook.com/LatinEventsWelly

https://www.instagram.com/latineventswelly

#WellingtonEvents #WhatsOnWellington #CulturalEventsNZ #LiveMusicWellington #LatinCultureNZ

events.id: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011-All Events In
events.name: Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
events.long_description:
Mountain Goats | Meow Nui, Wellington - VENUE UPGRADED!
UPGRADED TO MEOW NUI DUE TO OVERWHELMING DEMAND

Handsome Tours are proud to present The Mountain Goats!

Veteran indie-rock storytellers The Mountain Goats will make their long-awaited return to Australia and New Zealand in April 2026, with performances in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth, Auckland and Wellington. This eagerly-anticipated tour represents the band's first visit to Australia in 9 years (2017) and their first trip to New Zealand in 16 years (2010).

Fronted by prolific songwriter and novelist John Darnielle, The Mountain Goats are revered the world over for their sprawling catalogue of twenty-three studio albums released between 1994 and 2023. This tour announcement coincides with the band today unveiling album number twenty-four, 'Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan' - to be released 7th November 2025 on Cadmean Dawn Records (the band’s own label).

Produced by the Mountain Goats’ multi-instrumentalist Matt Douglas, Through This Fire Across From Peter Balkan is a full-on musical that stands as the most conceptually detailed and musically elaborate project in the band’s ever-expanding catalogue. It features appearances from The Replacements’ Tommy Stinson, harpist Mikaela Davis, musical theatre royalty Lin-Manuel Miranda (Tony Award-winning ‘Hamilton’, Disney’s ‘Moana’ & ‘Encanto’), and the band’s new bassist Cameron Ralston.

Don’t miss one of indie rock’s most enduring and beloved bands as they make their long-overdue return to our shores!

events.id: Ride the Cyclone-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ride the Cyclone
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Darkly funny, wildly imaginative, and surprisingly heartfelt, Ride the Cyclone is the viral musical phenomenon that has taken the world by storm. Buckle up for the ride of a lifetime, as Ride the Cyclone crashes into Wellington to make its Aotearoa New Zealand premiere at Circa Theatre next April.

In this hilarious and exhilarating story, the lives of six teenagers of the St. Cassian Chamber Choir are changed forever after they board a doomed rollercoaster ride.

Thrown into the abyss, they awake to find an ominous mechanical fortune teller that offers them the chance to compete for the prize … to return home in one piece.

Now confronted with their darkest desires, shameful secrets and each other, this “supremely witty … and just plain delightful” (New York Times) musical makes a uniquely uplifting and outrageous case for what makes a life worth living.

Featuring a deliriously funny, genre-defying score, Ride the Cyclone introduces some of the most remarkable characters in modern musical theatre – brought to life by six of Wellington’s finest musical theatre performers.

★★★★★ "...utterly charming, utterly bonkers, and utterly wonderful.” – ArtsHub Australia

★★★★ “Dark yet camp, nihilistic yet strangely life-affirming, this show is full of surprises and just as brilliantly bonkers as it sounds. This darkly comedic viral musical sensation is a wild ride” – Time Out AU

events.id: Waypeople Album Release Tour - Wellington-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Waypeople Album Release Tour - Wellington
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Wellington-based saxophonist and composer Jake Baxendale brings together a remarkable collective of Aotearoa’s leading jazz and contemporary musicians for Waypeople, a luminous new album inspired by Ursula K. Le Guin’s English-language version of the Tao Te Ching. Drawing from jazz, Chinese classical music and improvisation, the project fuses lyrical storytelling with meditative textures and powerful ensemble interplay.

Featuring Chelsea Prastiti (voice), Jia Ling “Jessie” (guzheng), Callum Passells (saxophones), Daniel Hayles (piano), Seth Boy (double bass), and Rick Cranson (drums), Waypeople transforms the timeless Taoist philosophy of harmony, balance, and paradox into sound. The result is a work of shimmering beauty — grounded in jazz but alive with the rhythm of ancient poetry and the pulse of contemporary life.

Tickets $20-30+BF https://events.humanitix.com/waypeople-album-release-tour-wellington/tickets

events.long_description:
The Miramar Maupuia Community Centre (MMCC) warmly welcomes weavers from across Te Motu Kairangi to our monthly weaving space in 2026.
When: Second Sunday of each month
Where: Miramar Maupuia Community Centre
Koha appreciated (on the day or via bank transfer—email for details: temotukairangi.huihui@gmail.com)
Teina (beginner) Weaving
11:00am – 1:30pm
A gentle, supportive space for those new to weaving or wanting to strengthen their foundations.
Please bring:
• 5–10 rau (blades) of harakeke
• Sharp scissors
• An old towel
• A bag to take home leftover harakeke
If you need harakeke, a TMK weaver can harvest it for you: $10 for 10 rau. Please make your request at least 5 days in advance.
Tuakana Kūmara Weavers
1:00pm – 5:00pm (Pack up from 4:45pm)
A shared working space for experienced weavers to weave, wānanga, and support others.
Tūakana weavers are expected to source their own harakeke if needed.
What to Expect
• A welcoming, inclusive weaving space
• Shared support and guidance
• Strengthening connections to Te Motu Kairangi through mahi raranga
2026 Dates (Second Sundays)
• 8 February • No weaving in March • 12 April • 10 May
• 14 June • 12 July • 9 August • 13 September • 11 October
• 8 November • 13 December TBA – Christmas finish event
Health & Safety / Tikanga
• Please handle tools with care and supervise tamariki at all times
• Harvesting of harakeke follows tikanga—please do not harvest without permission
• All leftover harakeke must be taken home
• Stay home if unwell and respect the shared space and each other
Contact:
Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/TeMotuKai

Email: temotukairangi.huihui@gmail.com
Nau mai, haere mai — all are welcome, even if you’re just popping in for a cuppa.

events.id: Helmet 2026 | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Helmet 2026 | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
"Helmet stripped away the excess, dialled in the precision, and redefined what heaviness could sound like without succumbing to clichés." Pitchfork

NYC Noise Rock Instigators Helmet return to Aotearoa / New Zealand for two shows in April 2026. Playing Wellington’s San Fran on Saturday 11 April, followed by The Powerstation in Auckland on Sunday 12 April. 
Tickets on sale Thursday 2 October 12pm from helmetmusic.com
Helmet has built a reputation as one of the most commanding forces in the underground scene. Born at the dawn of the rage-filled 90’s, Helmet fiercely holds space for multiple generations of alternative fans seeking the vital energy of hostile riffs and inventive metronomic groove. With a refusal to fit neatly into a genre, Helmet’s music creates an atmosphere best known live; part industrial, part hip-hop, part existential sermon. 
Formed in 1989 in NYC by Page Hamilton, Helmet has been crushing stages for over three decades. In that time they have only come to Aotearoa / NZ a handful of times, most recently in 2023 during their sold out Best of Helmet 1989-2023 tour, with 13th Floor declaring “a complete evening of essential music from one of heavy music’s most essential bands.” With 35 years of rocking behind them, Helmet are at the top of their game. Don’t miss Helmet in New Zealand next April. Tickets on sale Thursday 2 October, 12pm 

HELMET 
New Zealand April ‘26
Presented by Eccles Entertainment, 95bFM, Under The Radar

Sat 11 April 
San Fran | Wellington
helmetmusic.com

Sun 12 April 
Powerstation | Auckland
helmetmusic.com

events.id: URGE BLACK-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: URGE BLACK
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Unleash your inner dark side at URGE BLACK. The pulsating rhythms of liberation meet the sultry allure of leather-clad revelry. This isn’t just a dance party; it’s a sensorial odyssey that transcends age and convention. Prepare to be captivated, challenged, and ultimately liberated in an atmosphere where every shimmy and shake is an act of personal revolution.
URGE doesn’t just throw parties; we craft transformative experiences that celebrate the raw, unbridled essence of gay culture. Leather, fetish, uniform, gear, sports kit, etc.
Let’s dance and celebrate our authentic selves!
URGE tips:
Doors open at 9:00 PM.
Door sales are limited and $60 (cash only) if the event has not sold out prior.
Peak times: Queues can form if everyone arrives at once. We work to get you in quickly, so please be patient and courteous to our staff.
Dark Room: Located out back. Explore it if you’re feeling adventurous!
Your DJs:
Winner Mick
DJ K-OTIK
Everett Wayne
Event Rules:
This is a safe space: have fun, be kind, spread love, and look out for each other.
Follow instructions: Listen to staff and volunteers—they’re there to keep everyone safe. Ignoring their directions may result in removal from the event.
ZERO tolerance for harassment: This includes racism, transphobia, homophobia, sexism, misgendering, xenophobia, ableism, and fatphobia.
CONSENT IS KEY: Consent must be explicit and enthusiastic and can be withdrawn at any time.
- - - - SEE YOU ON THE DANCE FLOOR - - - -
Tickets here

events.id: Wellington Burner PICNIC - at CENTRAL PARK PLAY AREA-Brooklyn Rd, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Wellington Burner PICNIC - at CENTRAL PARK PLAY AREA
events.venue: Brooklyn Rd, Wellington
events.long_description:
Kiwiburn Community Picnic @ Central Park (Bi-Monthly Hang)
We’re kicking off our bi-monthly community picnics, and you’re warmly invited to come hang out. Bring yourself, bring a friend, bring a blanket, and kai for low-key good times with burners in the (mild) wild
Weather backup: if the weather turns feral and you’ve got a space where our crew could gather instead, sing out! Community hosts always appreciated.
Where: Central Park Play Area (child-friendly spot)
When: We’ll go from 1pm until we naturally peter out
Bring along:
- BYO picnic (no cooking facilities available at this location)
- Instruments if you’ve got them. Guitars, small drums, anything easy to carry for some light tunes
- Good vibes and curiosity
A few practical bits:
Dogs welcome, but must be on-leash at this spot.
On-street parking available, with easy access to/from SH1.
Public transport: No. 7 bus from Wellington Station with the stop right outside at the park gate.
Central Park tracks are nearby if anyone feels like a short bushwalk side quest (some tracks may be closed, so keep an eye out).
This one’s all about simple connection. Meet new folks, catch up with old ones, lounge in the grass, maybe wander the bush, maybe make a little music.
See you there

events.id: Poison - a reading by Andi C. Buchanan-69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Poison - a reading by Andi C. Buchanan
events.venue: 69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us to hear a work-in-progress reading from Andi C. Buchanan. Andi will be reading extracts from their draft Young Adult novel, Poison, which explores interdependence, community, and our expectations of self-reliance, and is filled with plants both poisoned and poisonous. Andi has been working on the novel over the last 8 weeks while on the Toi Pōneke d/Deaf and/or Disabled Artist Development Residency.
For this reading, we will have a NZSL interpreter present for our d/Deaf community members. For more information on venue accessibility, see below.
Accessibility:
NZSL live interpreter
venue:
The upper chamber is on the first floor in the west building. The room will have natural lighting from the windows. For wheelchair users we'd recommend using the ramp entrance at our east building then using the lift. Take the overbridge across to the west building. Staff can help open internal doors which can be heavy. Talk to our reception staff and they will help you. PH. 04 3851929. Any other access needs please e-mail Sascha - sascha.perfect@wcc.govt.nz.
For more info:
https://toiponeke.nz/.../poison-a-reading-by-andi-c-buchanan

events.id: Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Weekend: Oceans of the Mind Discussion Panel-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Explore Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Weekend: Oceans of the Mind Discussion Panel
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Over the weekend of 11 and 12 of April we will be staging a very special series of exploration events to celebrate the recent reopening of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui. The panel topic for our Sunday 12 April 2pm panel is: 'Oceans of the Mind'.
This event will be held in the beautiful Ngā Pou Ruahine space on the second floor. This is a free event and all are welcome. We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited, so please arrive early to ensure a seat.
Meet our esteemed panellists...
Grant Nalder:
Grant Nadler is our beloved Wellington Harbourmaster. He has devoted most of his working life to the sea and has a treasure trove of fabulous nautical true-life tales to draw on.
Dr Nicole Miller:
A keen explorer of the underwater world, Nicole uses citizen science, visual storytelling, and art to connect people of all ages with their local marine environment. As the founder of Explore Your Coast (https://exploreyourcoast.co.nz/), she is passionate about bringing the underwater world to shore - sparking curiosity, a deeper sense of connection and care, and action to protect and restore marine ecosystems and biodiversity across New Zealand.
The short documentary 'Seaweed, A Love Story', featuring Nicole’s work, is now screening at venues across New Zealand.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.id: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)-39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
events.venue: 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Please note: the event is free but it can only continue if we support the Southern Cross by buying food or drinks

Wellington’s favourite monthly Bachata Latin dance party

Join us for an awesome mix of bachata, focusing on the best sensual tracks and remixes, along with salsa and a smattering of kizomba and zouk. We welcome everyone, from the absolute beginner to the most experienced dancer. We’re all about the joy of social dancing together.
And what makes a free party with great people even better? A free beginners’ bachata lesson with some of Wellington‘s best Latin dance teachers. If you’ve never danced bachata, Latin dance or even any other dance style before then this is the perfect lesson for you. We will have you dancing like a pro in no time!
Bailamos Bachata is hosted free of charge by the Southern Cross. They love the atmosphere we bring but we also need to support them in return. We’re asking everyone attending to purchase soft drinks, food or alcohol from the bar. It’s vital we show our gratitude.
1800-1830 social
1830-1900 free lesson
1900-2100 social
Q&A
Q. What is bachata?
A. Bachata is a popular and fun Latin couples dance. It started in the Dominican Republic, then travelled to Europe and the rest of the world. It now includes a wide range of music styles and dance influences and is still changing and evolving.
Q. I haven’t danced before, is that a problem?
A. Not at all! That’s exactly what the lesson is for – everyone has to start somewhere and we’re a very welcoming crowd for beginners.
Q. Is bachata a difficult dance to learn?
A. Its origins as a party/community dance mean the basics can be learnt quite quickly, so you’ll be enjoying yourself in no time.
Q. Who are the capital bachata collective?
A. We are the combined Wellington dance schools and people teaching bachata and we really want to share this dance with everyone. We love bachata, we love our community and we want to share it with you
￼

events.id: Suena Oro - Te Heke Tō Taura Kōrari - Valhalla-154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Suena Oro - Te Heke Tō Taura Kōrari - Valhalla
events.venue: 154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Suena Oro, a te reo Māori/Spanish fusion band from Whakatū/Nelson are bringing their psychedelic sonic experience to Valhalla on their ‘Te Heke Tō Taura Kōrari’ tour.
Whakatu’s fertile music scene has been ignited by Suena Oro’s hypnotic extended jams that touch on folk, reggae, drone-music and other broad genres, all the while keeping experimentation, improvisation and taonga pūoro at the core.
Suena Oro’s original songs draw inspiration from tangata whenuatanga, a rich depth of Spanish heritage and many years of experience at the coalface of various musical forms. The songs are vehicles for political and humanist perspectives riding dance-orientated grooves. Kanikani mai!
Suena Oro will be supported at Valhalla by Whakatū-based live electronic artists Droneeater and Alvaro Moreno. Droneeater - aka Chris Mulholland - performs live te reo Māori dance orientated music. His 2025 single ‘Tō Wairua’ reached number #1 on Radioactive’s Top 11 Charts. Alvaro Moreno, alongside running the record label ‘Nelson Techno Collective’ that released Droneeater’s 2025 EP ‘Ngā Oro Rōria’, performs live techno-inspired music.
Tickets: $10 presale (link forthcoming) or $15 at the door.

Timings TBC

events.long_description:
Directed by Frank Perry
Starring Faye Dunaway, Diana Scarwid, Mara Hobel, Steve Forrest, Howard De Silva
--- In this biographical film, glamorous yet lonely star Joan Crawford takes in two orphans, and at first their unconventional family seems happy. But after Joan's attempts at romantic fulfillment go sour, and she is fired from her contract with MGM studios, her callous and abusive behavior towards her daughter Christina becomes even more pronounced. Christina leaves home and takes her first acting role, only to find her mother's presence still overshadowing her. ---

events.name: Te Omanga Hospice Charity Motorcycle Ride 2026
events.venue: 136 Woburn Rd, Woburn, Lower Hutt 5010
events.long_description:
Te Omanga Hospice Charity Motorcycle Ride 2026
Whoooey!

events.long_description:

Our vision at Artisan Craft Market is to make the market accessible to those who appreciate shopping locally. We aim to provide a platform that supports local artisans, allowing them to showcase their meticulously crafted, high-quality products. By bringing the market directly to the people, we hope to celebrate and promote the unique talents of our community’s skilled artisans.

Come support local at our monthly market in Wellington City on the Waterfront at Wharewaka O Poneke venue.

events.id: Autumn Textured Pendants/Earrings-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Autumn Textured Pendants/Earrings
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This quick, creative taster class is perfect for a fun outing with friends, a thoughtful gift-making session, or a treat just for you.

In just three hours, you’ll learn to craft a stunning pair of textured metal earrings from scratch. You’ll be guided step-by-step through heating and texturing the metal, then cutting and shaping it with traditional jewellery tools. By the end, you’ll have a beautiful pair of earrings to wear proudly or gift to someone you care about!

You will practice on smaller texture samples to work out what texture and what metal you want to go with, and then move on to creating your earrings in warm copper or yellow brass.

Your class materials pack includes everything you need for the class to make a super cool pair of shiny gorgeous earrings.
Using copper and brass as our main material allows us to have more creative fun and create larger earrings with less expense.

We do have a lovely range of sterling silver sheets that you can purchase as extra if you are committed to working in silver!

We use quality pre-made sterling silver and stainless steel earring hooks and hoops for these taster classes so that you can focus on making the main part of the earrings and know that your findings will be just perfect.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany anyone under the age of 16.

events.venue: The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
The National Brass Band of New Zealand is set to light up Wellington Opera House this April with a world-class Gala Concert you won’t want to miss.
Following the National Contest, the National Band's performance features phenomenal guest artists: Harmen Vanhoorne, Celine Wu, Helen and Glyn Williams, alongside the band’s principal trombone, Aishah Leitner.
The programme includes brass band classics including Paganini Variations, The Kingdom Triumphant, and more outstanding works.
Join us at 2pm on Sunday, April 12 at the Wellington Opera House for an afternoon of music-making at a the highest level.

events.id: Amici (Saxophone Quintet)-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Amici (Saxophone Quintet)
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Long-established Amici Ensemble performs each year with varying combinations of instruments. In 2026 this is a quintet with the saxophone adding richness to a string quartet. The performers are Donald Armstrong – violin, Anna van der Zee – violin, Nicholas Hancox – viola, Andrew Joyce – cello, and Simon Brew – saxophone. The string players are members of the NZSO, joined by Simon Brew who is much in demand as a saxophonist.

Since Adolf Sax invented the saxophone in the 1840s, composers have been enchanted with its sonorities, combining the saxophone with other instruments in many idioms. Composers such as Debussy and Ravel incorporated its beautiful sweetness in their music, influencing more recent composers such as Ellen Zwilich and Russell Peterson.

This unusual lineup of saxophone and string quartet, while embracing their classical origins, plunge headlong into the evocative sounds, catchy rhythms and soaring melodies that this combination lends itself to.

Russell Peterson, Quintet for Alto Saxophone and Strings
Max Richter, On the Nature of Daylight (string quartet)
Ellen Taaffe Zwilich, Quintet for Alto Saxophone and Strings
Wolfgang A Mozart, String Quartet K590
Astor Piazzolla, The Four Seasons: Winter and Spring

events.id: Sunday Jazz - James Wylie-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Sunday Jazz - James Wylie
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer, hangs, and jazz, on a Sunday, perfect!

This week featuring local saxophonist, back in town briefly from Greece, James Wylie

A modern look at the saxophone-organ trio tradition through the prism of original compositions by James Wylie and Miles Crayford. Joining them will be long-time collaborator, drummer Mike Jensen.

Miles Crayford and James Wylie have been musical brothers since their teenage years and their influence on each other has been central to their artistic worldview. Since that time, their individual paths have diverged, been enriched and new musical identities have been forged. This meeting will be the first time playing together in more than ten years (and Wylie's first appearance in Wellington in as many years) and will feature a range of newly composed orginal music by both musicians. For this performance Crayford will be performing on Hammond organ and Wylie on tenor saxophone - a nod to their childhood trio which was active in and around Wellington in which Mike Jensen held down the drum chair. This is a rare opportunity to hear this group due to James Wylie being based in Europe for the last 15 years. Not to be missed!

Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY

events.name: Monk and Munchies
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Monk and Munchies
Monk & Munchies is where plant-based eats meet meaningful conversation - with guest speaker, author and monk, Devamrita Swami.

About this Event


Monk & Munchies is where plant-based eats meet meaningful conversation.

This time we’re joined by Devamrita Swami a monk, author, and speaker who travels the globe meeting people and sharing practical wisdom that actually fits into everyday life. Come meet him in person and see what you take from the conversation.
We’ll warm things up with mantra music meditation, live music, voices rising together, and that familiar Bhakti Lounge buzz building in the room.
Stick around later as the mantra picks up and the dancing begins!

Then it’s time to eat.
Think:
BBQ Cauliflower Pizza
Crumbed Zucchini Fries with Yum Yum Sauce
Fresh Salad with Lemon Maple Vinaigrette
Brownie with whipped coconut cream & berry sauce
Sangria mocktail


Good food, live mantra music, a great social scene, and insights that might stay with you long after the night ends.

Pull up, meet some good sorts, and see what happens. 🎶





Agenda


🕑: 05:45 PM - 06:00 PM
Doors Open at Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:00 PM
Mantra Music ~Kirtan

🕑: 06:30 PM - 07:05 PM
Talk with a Monk

🕑: 07:05 PM - 07:30 PM
Mantra Music~Kirtan and Dance

🕑: 07:30 PM - 08:00 PM
Plant-based dinner (the best part of the night!)

🕑: 08:00 PM - 09:00 PM
After Dinner Mantra Music & Dance

events.id: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)-Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant,39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
events.venue: Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant,39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
🔥 Please note: the event is free but it can only continue if we support the Southern Cross by buying food or drinks 🔥


🌟 Wellington’s favourite monthly Bachata Latin dance party 🌟


Join us for an awesome mix of bachata, focusing on the best sensual tracks and remixes, along with salsa and a smattering of kizomba and zouk. We welcome everyone, from the absolute beginner to the most experienced dancer. We’re all about the joy of social dancing together.

And what makes a free party with great people even better? A free beginners’ bachata lesson with some of Wellington‘s best Latin dance teachers. If you’ve never danced bachata, Latin dance or even any other dance style before then this is the perfect lesson for you. We will have you dancing like a pro in no time!

Bailamos Bachata is hosted free of charge by the Southern Cross. They love the atmosphere we bring but we also need to support them in return. We’re asking everyone attending to purchase soft drinks, food or alcohol from the bar. It’s vital we show our gratitude.

1800-1830 social
1830-1900 free lesson
1900-2100 social

Q&A
Q. What is bachata?
A. Bachata is a popular and fun Latin couples dance. It started in the Dominican Republic, then travelled to Europe and the rest of the world. It now includes a wide range of music styles and dance influences and is still changing and evolving.

Q. I haven’t danced before, is that a problem?
A. Not at all! That’s exactly what the lesson is for – everyone has to start somewhere and we’re a very welcoming crowd for beginners.

Q. Is bachata a difficult dance to learn?
A. Its origins as a party/community dance mean the basics can be learnt quite quickly, so you’ll be enjoying yourself in no time.

Q. Who are the capital bachata collective?
A. We are the combined Wellington dance schools and people teaching bachata and we really want to share this dance with everyone. We love bachata, we love our community and we want to share it with you
￼

events.id: SUENA ORO - TE HEKE TŌ TAURA KŌRARI-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: SUENA ORO - TE HEKE TŌ TAURA KŌRARI
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Suena Oro, a te reo Māori/Spanish fusion band from Whakatū/Nelson are bringing their psychedelic sonic experience to Valhalla on their ‘Te Heke Tō Taura Kōrari’ tour.
Whakatu’s fertile music scene has been ignited by Suena Oro’s hypnotic extended jams that touch on folk, reggae, drone-music and other broad genres, all the while keeping experimentation, improvisation and taonga pūoro at the core.
Suena Oro’s original songs draw inspiration from tangata whenuatanga, a rich depth of Spanish heritage and many years of experience at the coalface of various musical forms. The songs are vehicles for political and humanist perspectives riding dance-orientated grooves. Kanikani mai!
Suena Oro will be supported at Valhalla by Whakatū-based live electronic artists Droneeater and Alvaro Moreno. Droneeater - aka Chris Mulholland - performs live te reo Māori dance orientated music. His 2025 single ‘Tō Wairua’ reached number #1 on Radioactive’s Top 11 Charts. Alvaro Moreno, alongside running the record label ‘Nelson Techno Collective’ that released Droneeater’s 2025 EP ‘Ngā Oro Rōria’, performs live techno-inspired music.
Tickets: $10 presale via UTR or $15 at the door.

events.id: Georgia Knight-San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Georgia Knight
events.venue: San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way. After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band.

Naarm-born and sometimes Aotearoa-based, Knight crafts otherworldly pop from autoharp, dusty loops, synth textures, and avant-garde edges. Her 2023 EP Hell on Bent Street established her as a rising force, with appearances at Dark Mofo, Rising, Camp A Low Hum, and shows alongside Blonde Redhead and Mogwai.

It’s been a big 12 months for Georgia. Her independently released debut album Beanpole, described by KEXP as "an evocative set of dimly-lit, hypnotic, psych-tinted pop", earned praise from Sharon Van Etten, Arab Strap, and BBC Radio 6 Music. Critics highlighted her "shadowy, seductive trip-hop textures" (KCRW), "extraordinary vocal range and graveside poems" (The 13th Floor), and "goosebumps"-inducing live performances (BBC Radio 6).

Supporting the album, she was booked for a string of highly coveted festivals including The Great Escape (UK), Golden Plains (AU), Phoenix Central Park (AU), The Others Way (NZ), and Port Noise (NZ).

Now touring her 2025 album Beanpole, recorded with Andrew ‘Idge’ Hehir, Georgia returns with a fuller, immersive live show, intimate, cinematic, and impossible to look away from.

Banished Music proudly welcomes Georgia Knight back to Aotearoa, following her standout solo performance at The Others Way.
After captivating audiences with a spellbinding set that lingered long after the final note, Georgia brings her singular world of sound back to New Zealand stages, expanded, immersive, and more powerful than ever, this time with her full live band.
Naarm-born and sometimes Aotearoa-based, Knight crafts otherworldly pop from autoharp, dusty loops, synth textures, and avant-garde edges.
Her 2023 EP Hell on Bent Street established her as a rising force, with appearances at Dark Mofo, Rising, Camp A Low Hum, and shows alongside Blonde Redhead and Mogwai.
It’s been a big 12 months for Georgia.

events.long_description:
Porirua Grand Traverse - 2026
Established in 2006, the Porirua Grand Traverse showcases the region's awesome outdoor resource and active lifestyles. From the Arena 9k Fun Run/Walk, and FS Cycles 13k Fun Bike, through to the 56k Multisport and 48k Duathlon events, to simple Paddle, Mtn Bike and Mtn Run options, this vibrant festival of fitness has something for everyone.

It is a non-profit event organised locally by the Porirua Grand Traverse charitable trust and since day one our goals has remained the same...

All Ages: We're about participation for everyone from retirees to kids!
All Abilities: Options for rookies to experienced endurance junkies!
All Amazing: Showcasing Porirua's hills & harbour, bush & beaches!

events.id: Held in Motion - Somatic Movement Series-1 Left Bank, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Held in Motion - Somatic Movement Series
events.venue: 1 Left Bank, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Held in Motion
A 5-Week Evening Somatic Movement Series for Women
April 13 – May 11
(Monday evenings)
6pm - 7.15 pm

Held in Motion is a five-week somatic movement series for women who want to deepen their relationship with body, expression, and emotion …especially if you feel disconnected, overwhelm, or unsure how to stay in contact with what you’re feeling
At its core, somatic movement is movement guided by internal experience rather than external form.
It is movement led by sensation, impulse, breath, rhythm, and nervous system cues, rather than choreography, performance, or aesthetics.
This space is about listening.
~ Listening to impulse.
~ Listening to instinct.
~ Listening to sensation before it becomes story.
~ Listening to breath, to boundary, to rhythm.
~ Listening to the subtle yes.
~ Listening to the quiet no.
~ Listening to the places that want to soften.
~ Listening to the places that want to move.
Through free movement (without performance), somatic exploration, nervous system awareness, and guided reflection, we explore how to remain in relationship with ourselves while moving.
Each evening is intentionally paced and thoughtfully facilitated, weaving together:
~ Nervous system literacy, gently and accessibly held
~ Nervous system regulation practices integrated throughout
~ Guided somatic movement and free exploration
~ Music that fuels expression
~ Journaling and reflection
~ Moments of softness and moments of wildness
~ Joy. Depth. Integration.

Together we will explore how to:
~ Strengthen internal resourcing
~ Reconnect to sensation
~ Expand capacity for feeling without overwhelm
~ Access playful aliveness
~ Strengthen embodied choice and agency
~ Integrate emotional experience through the body
---
This is for you if…
~ You want to feel more at home in your body.
~ You sense there is more expression inside you than you currently allow.
~ You feel caught in thinking and long for deeper connection with your whole body.
~ You are curious about movement as a pathway to emotional integration.
~ You are ready to listen to your body in a new way.
~~~
REGISTER
Please fill out this form to register: https://forms.gle/vvGR4XzbynNBHEoa7
Spaces are limited to allow for a deeper, more intimate experience.
Cost: $30–$40 per session ($120–$160 for the 4-week series)
Choose the rate that feels right for you. Sliding scale pricing is to make this space more accessible while supporting the container we’re creating together.
***If finances are an issue for you to attend this space, please email me directly at freedomofsoma@gmail.com so we talk further

~~~

Four weeks.
Evenings devoted to the body.
If you are curious about using movement as a way back to yourself, without pressure, without needing to be a “dancer,” without needing to be anything other than human, this space may be for you.

See you soon!
Fiona x

~~
About me, your facilitator:
Hi, I am Fiona
I am an Integrative Somatic Facilitator™, Somatic Dance Facilitator, and Therapeutic Yoga Teacher devoted to helping people come home to their bodies. Drawing on somatics, nervous system literacy, and embodied movement, I hold spaces that support regulation, expression, and deeper self-connection.
Alongside my facilitation work, I collaborate with SomaPsych, supporting the expansion of trauma-informed and somatic education into community-centred trainings for vulnerable populations, minority communities, and professionals in demanding, client-facing roles. I am passionate and commitmed to collective care and to making embodied practice accessible beyond traditional wellness spaces.

events.name: Stitch and Butch @ Blondini's
events.venue: 10 Kent Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Stitch & Butch @ Blondini’s!
A casual meet up of crafters within the rainbow community
New members welcome.
Bring your knitting/crochet/cross stitch/embroidery/crafting/art or just come and have a chat.
Feel free to come partway through the time or leave early as needed.
Stitch and butch is a playful twist on the stitch and b!tch name to reflect the queerness of the group. Attendees do not need to be in any particular subset of the queer community to be welcome.
Usually gathered around one of the tables in the lounge area upstairs. Often near the walls for the brighter lights to better see our stitches.
There is a lift as well as stairs and Courtenay place bus stops across the road.
Held on the 2nd and 4th Mondays of the month with an end of year break.

events.id: Pottery wheel throwing & hand building - creative arts Holiday program-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Pottery wheel throwing & hand building - creative arts Holiday program
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore the techniques of pottery making including creating a mug using handbuilding processes and making a small bowl using throwing techniques on a pottery wheel. Students will be shown how to centre clay and make a small bowl on the wheel and trim it. Cups and bowls will be glazed by the tutor and ready for pickup approximately 3-4 weeks later depending on drying conditions.

This workshop is limited to three students only to ensure each person has individualised attention.

Suitable for ages 12+.

events.id: Musicking Together: Capoeira for Music therapy week-Evaro, Level 1, James Smith Corner, 65 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Musicking Together: Capoeira for Music therapy week
events.venue: Evaro, Level 1, James Smith Corner, 65 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Musicking Together is a community music circle. It's free, accessible, open to anyone and instruments are provided.

It is run by Sophie Sabri, a registered music therapist and in collaboration with different local musicians each month.
This month we will host Michael Person for a night of capoeira rhythms, songs and dance.
It's is also MUSIC THERAPY WEEK and the theme this year is about Weaving Connections, Te Poutama Hononga.

Come along to meet new people and play music without pressure.
"Musicking Together" is a space to create music together, to dance, to sing or simply listen to others. As long as we are in space, we are musicking.

How to find us? Evaro is located in the James Smith building, at the corner between Manners and Cuba Street. Enter the building and pass Rebel Sports to find the stairs. Go up to level 1 and follow the corridor on the left. You'll find Evaro to your left.

Directed by Steven Spielberg
Starring Jeff Goldblum, Samuel L. Jackson, Joseph Mazzello, Laura Dern, Sam Neill, Richard Attenborough, Wayne Knight, Ariana Richards, BD Wong
Designed to showcase the incredibly creative and ever-changing menu of our playful, fine dining establishment CoCo At The Roxy. EAT THE FILM provides film and food fans with a cult classic film and delicious seasonal and thematic dishes* designed to arrive in-theatre, perfectly timed to specific scenes.

All tickets include several themed dishes and drinks brought to you in the theatre, an experience with more than enough to sate your eyes and appetite.

While the park's mastermind, billionaire John Hammond (Richard Attenborough), assures everyone that the facility is safe, they find out otherwise when various ferocious predators break free and go on the hunt. ---

--- In Steven Spielberg's massive blockbuster, paleontologists Alan Grant (Sam Neill) and Ellie Sattler (Laura Dern) and mathematician Ian Malcolm (Jeff Goldblum) are among a select group chosen to tour an island theme park populated by dinosaurs created from prehistoric DNA.

events.name: Paint and Wine Night - Mohua in Autumn
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop Bar, 166 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.long_description:
Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening (JBLZE) is coming to The Opera House for a night of rock and roll classics. The landmark tour will let fans experience hits such as ‘Kashmir’ and ‘The Wanton Song’ live alongside deeper cuts like ‘In The Light’ and ‘Boogie With Stu.’ Along with playing the iconic album in its entirety, other Led Zeppelin favourites, including ‘Good Times Bad Times,’ ‘Whole Lotta Love”, and of course ‘Stairway To Heaven’, find their way into the setlist.

events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Of all the print-making methods, mono printing is the most spontaneous, responsive and versatile, being the medium for everything from abstract expressionism to hyper photographic realism. Simple and direct with the minimum of equipment and media, mono printing is approachable for any age or ability level, yet the creative potential is unlimited.

Bring a water bottle and a packed lunch to enjoy in the historic Inverlochy house during break time.

Two-day workshop on Tuesday & Wednesday, 14-15 April, 10 am - 2 pm daily.

All materials included. Recommended age 12 and above.

Drop-off your kids to create their own art inspired by the sculptural work of George Agius.

events.long_description:
Explore Government House gardens with the BARK garden team (90 minute tour)

Government House was opened in Newtown in 1910, and its extensive gardens have been in continuous development since then. As part of the Spring Festival, the Government House garden team is offering guided walks through our gardens. Tours will go ahead regardless of weather. Tours will not enter the main house.

Bookings are essential for the free tour. Places are limited: https://gg.govt.nz/government-house/government-house-wellington/government-house-tours/upcoming

Some parts of our grounds are not mobility friendly. Please contact the Visitor Centre direct if you have any questions regarding accessibility.

Groups wanting to organise a garden tour or house tour can email our Visitor Centre team direct: bookings@govthouse.govt.nz

If you have any questions or need more information, please email us at bookings@govthouse.govt.nz, or call us at 04-382-0837. You will receive a reminder email 24 hours before your tour.
