events.name: Draw It Out Teens Workshop
events.venue: Drama Studio House, 93 Kelburn Parade, Wellington
events.long_description:
Draw It Out Teens Workshop

Theme: Learn a tool that helps when feeling stuck
Teens 11-17 get to learn a new tool that can help when working through hard stuff and helps to listen to their own inner voice to move forward.

This single workshop helps teens to use drawing to access their inner resources for when they feel stuck, either with hard feelings or moving forward, because often they have the guidance within, and just need help to bring it out.

No art skills are needed! The tool is called Interactive Drawing Therapy, and uses simple crayons to learn a tool they can take home.

Workshops are held at the Draw It Out studio - Level 2, 105 High street.

Liability disclaimer:
Draw It Out offers creativity for wellbeing. It is not intended as therapy nor does it replace counselling, therapy, or any other medical or clinical professional help. Participants understand it is their own responsibility to manage their mental and physical health during sessions, to opt in and out of activities as they see fit, and make the facilitator aware if further support is required.

events.id: Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington-Opera House-All Events In
events.name: Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington
events.venue: Opera House
events.long_description:
Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington
Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening Announces New Zealand as part of the World Tour Celebrating the 50th Anniversary of Physical Graffiti this April 2026

Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening/JBLZE is announcing the final dates to Celebrate the 50th Anniversary of the Led Zeppelin classic, Physical Graffiti and what better way to wrap up the World Tour than with dates down under! The landmark tour will let fans experience classics such as “Kashmir” and “The Wanton Song” live alongside deeper cuts like “In The Light” and “Boogie With Stu.” Along with playing the iconic album in its entirety, other Led Zeppelin favourites including “Good Times Bad Times,” “Whole Lotta Love” and of course “Stairway To Heaven” find their way into the setlist.

“For me, this isn’t just an album—it’s the album,” declares Jason Bonham. “Physical Graffiti has always been my ultimate Led Zeppelin record, and I set out to honour its 50th Anniversary with 50 unforgettable shows. That vision now looks more like 80 shows! My last tour of Australia and New Zealand was nothing short of electrifying, and I know the April tour will raise the bar even higher. I’m counting down the days until JBLZE share our final powerful, celebration of Physical Graffiti together.”

events.id: Cameraless Photography & Artist's Book - Creative Arts Holiday Program-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cameraless Photography & Artist's Book - Creative Arts Holiday Program
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore the historic photographic process of the cyanotype, and stitch your prints into a unique, handmade artist’s notebook.

In this three-day workshop, you’ll explore the beautiful 19th-century photographic process of the cyanotype (often called a blueprint), known for its striking Prussian blue tones. In its earliest form, the cyanotype was used to record botanical specimens. On day one of our workshop, you’ll collect plants or foliage from the garden (or bring your own) to create delicate contact prints on Japanese rice paper and watercolour paper. On day two, we’ll plan your book structure and complete your cyanotypes. On day three, we’ll trim your prints and stitch or construct them into a handmade notebook of your own design.

Course dates are Wed-Fri, 15-17 April, 10 am - 2 pm daily. All materials provided.

Recommended age for this course is tweens & teens ages 10 and above.

events.name: Ronald McDonald House Variety Concert
events.venue: Southward Car Museum,235 Main Rd, Otaihanga, Paraparaumu 5036, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Ronald McDonald House Variety Concert
Back by popular demand! The Kāpiti Variety Concert returns to Southward Theatre on Wednesday 15 April 2026, with shows at 2.30 pm and 7.00 pm.

This lively showcase of local talent will raise vital funds for Ronald McDonald House Charities, which supports families when their child is in a hospital away from home.

The line-up features legendary New Zealand performer Tina Cross ONZM with Australian singer-guitarist John Bettison, plus crowd favourite Steve McDonald. Also appearing are Unity Wara, Andrew London, Sam Manzanza, Unity Wara, Steve Poulton and Carylann Martin, Jess Deacon, and the Big Little Uke Band. The programme also features performances by students from Kāpiti College, Paraparaumu College, Ōtaki College, as well as from Sweet Studios and Step Out Dance Studio (Wellington).

Before the 2.30 pm matinee, the grounds at Southward Car Museum will come alive with children’s activities on the lawn and a ‘world-first’ relay contest between teams from local schools. Kids will also be able to ride in vintage cars.

All proceeds from the event will support Ronald McDonald House Charities.

Tickets are on sale through Eventfinda — be quick and plan some fun for the whole family these school holidays.

events.id: Botanical Insights talk - Women in Botany-Treehouse Visitor Centre, 101 Glenmore St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Botanical Insights talk - Women in Botany
events.venue: Treehouse Visitor Centre, 101 Glenmore St, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this talk the accomplishments of a selection of brilliant, forcefully independent-minded, women botanists of the 18th, 19th and 20th centuries will be reviewed. Their work had far-reaching benefits on, amongst other things, the economic development of their countries, conservation management and higher education. One of the ‘Sisterhood’ received the Nobel Prize. Others gained high office in various learned societies and institutions. Several New Zealand botanists will be highlighted.

Located in the Treehouse Seminar Room, Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā.
Free of charge. You’re welcome to bring along your own ‘brown bag’ lunch to enjoy during the talk.

The speaker is Reg Harris, a volunteer guide [botany] at Wellington Botanic Garden.
Reg delivers botany lectures for diverse audiences, delving into plant interactions, adaptive radiation, tropisms, and more. As a dedicated volunteer guide at Wellington Botanic Garden (WBG), he pioneered collaborations between WBG and Victoria University, organising annual field labs for biology students.

events.id: NZ Tunnelling Company 110th Commemorations-Pukeahu National War Memorial Park, Buckle St, Mt Cook, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: NZ Tunnelling Company 110th Commemorations
events.venue: Pukeahu National War Memorial Park, Buckle St, Mt Cook, Wellington
events.long_description:
Members of the pubic are warmly invited to attend a special commemorative event as descendants of the First World War New Zealand Tunnelling Company are gathering at Pukeahu National War Memorial Park above the Arras Tunnel. 2026 is the 110th anniversary since the Company arrived on the Westen Front. The Tunnelling Company was the first New Zealand unit to arrive on the Front. Their duties included working underground at Arras in northern France. Today, the Arras Tunnel in Wellington is a visible reminder of the story of those who served underground.

We are gathering in the nation’s capital, in a space of national significance, to pay appropriate respect to a group of men whose service and contribution have often not been understood. This will be a very special moment as descendants and supporters gather along with others who share their stories. We are honoured to have RNZE providing practical support and assistance on the day. This event has been made possible by partial funding from Veterans Affairs and many volunteers assisting at their own expense. Descendants are also helping with ongoing costs.

The commemoration is shaping up to be a special cultural blend of Aotearoa New Zealand, including Tangata Whenua, the Seyip Association representing a Tunneller of Chinese descent, as well as Cook Islands, French and Tahitian influences. They all share the Tunnelling Company story, with representatives from these groups participating. Over 50 descendants representing more than 20 Tunnellers’ families will be joining us, travelling from as far afield as Kaitaia and Invercargill.

We are honoured to have the Mayor of Wellington His Worship Andrew Little welcome us to the capital, followed by our keynote speaker Sir Jerry Mateparae GNZM, QSO, KStJ. The French Ambassador to New Zealand H.E. Laurence Beau and RNZE: Colonel Commandant Col. Paul Curry will also speak.

A special guest at the commemoration is Hon. Chris Finlayson KC. Chris was instrumental in the naming of the Arras Tunnel in 2014. Other distinguished guests who will join us include official representatives from the Cook Islands, the United Kingdom, Australia and France. Also attending will be past and present military representatives including Masterton and Wellington RSAs, the Sappers Association NZ, with five historical military vehicles also taking part to help us honour the ASC who were attached to the Tunnellers. Along with the Wellington Mayor, one other Mayor from the North Island and one from the South Island who share the Tunnellers’ story are also joining us. We will have two Tunnelling Company Chaplains with us on the day. Descendant Mons. Bernard Kiely and Waihi Tunnelling Company Chaplain Rev. Christina Syratt.

An important part of this commemoration is the reading out of a Roll of Honour by descendants and supporters. A descendant will also read the names on the Roll of Remembrance which recognises Tunnellers who took their own lives after the war. As far as we are aware, this will be the first time a list like this has been read out at Pukeahu.

The service proper begins at 1.00pm. Those not participating in the march are requested to be seated by 12.45pm. At 12.45pm descendants and supporters will march from Buckle Street to Anzac Square, led in by supporting RNZE personnel, a piper, flag bearer and three historic military vehicles. People who have a relationship to the Tunnelling Company story are invited to join descendants in this short march.
events.description:
Members of the pubic are warmly invited to attend a special commemorative event as descendants of the First World War New Zealand Tunnelling Company are gathering at Pukeahu National War Memorial Park above the Arras Tunnel.
Along with the Wellington Mayor, one other Mayor from the North Island and one from the South Island who share the Tunnellers’ story are also joining us.
We will have two Tunnelling Company Chaplains with us on the day.
An important part of this commemoration is the reading out of a Roll of Honour by descendants and supporters.
People who have a relationship to the Tunnelling Company story are invited to join descendants in this short march.

events.long_description:
A Wine & Food Pairing Night at Cuba St Tavern

Join us at Cuba St Tavern for Perfect Pairs - a special evening celebrating great wine, good food and the people around the table.

For one night only, we’ll be pouring five standout wines from around the world, each paired with a dish designed by our kitchen to bring out the best in every glass.

Guiding the evening is Brandon Nash, from Importer and Distributor Dhall & Nash Fine Wines, who will introduce each wine throughout the night and share a little about the regions, producers and stories behind the pours.

From English sparkling to Central Otago Pinot Noir, this lineup takes guests across some of the world’s most exciting wine regions - all enjoyed in the relaxed, social atmosphere of the Tavern.

Expect a night of great wine, thoughtful plates and good company in the heart of Cuba Street.

Cuba St Tavern

Five wines paired with five dishes

Tickets

events.id: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
Two titans of the turntables. One night only.

Three-time DMC World Champion DJ QBert is widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential DJs of all time. His dominance at the DMC World Championships in the early ’90s redefined what was possible with two turntables and he is widely credited with transforming the turntable from a device used to play records into a respected, expressive musical instrument. Decades later, his innovation, technical mastery and cultural impact remain unmatched. Seeing QBert live is not just a show, it is witnessing the evolution of hip hop craft in real time.

Sharing the bill is Aotearoa’s own 2002 New Zealand DMC Champion Alphabethead, a shining star of experimental hip hop and razor-sharp turntablism. "With a career spanning international tours, acclaimed projects both solo and in bands Bad Taste and The All Seeing Hand, Alphabethead represents the very best of New Zealand DJ culture.

This is a rare meeting of eras, styles and legacies at the highest level of turntablism. Miss it, and you will be hearing about it for years to come.

events.id: SIKA Sound Journey - KAPITI-Raumati South Memorial Hall. Tennis Court Road, Raumati South.,43 Tennis Court Rd, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: SIKA Sound Journey - KAPITI
events.venue: Raumati South Memorial Hall. Tennis Court Road, Raumati South.,43 Tennis Court Rd, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
SIKA Sound Journey - KAPITI
SIKA Sound Journey - 'Between the Worlds'
Something between an ancient ceremony and a movie soundtrack.

Please bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket. Be prepared to drop into a deep space.
Door sales will be available if there is space. Advanced booking is recommended.

Between the Worlds is an evening Sound Journey shaped by over thirty years of deep ceremonial and immersive sound practice. Blending primal rhythm, atmospheric voice, deep didgeridoo, and field recordings, the journey unfolds organically — shifting from dynamic, embodied energy into vast, cinematic stillness.

This is not passive listening.
It is an experience to be entered.

As sound layers, dissolve, and reform, participants are guided into a liminal space where time softens and inner landscapes awaken. The journey becomes a passage — one that listeners don’t just hear, but inhabit.

Guided by the myth of the Wandering Forest Spirit, Between the Worlds invites a reconnection with self, with nature, and with something older than language. Ceremonial, immersive, and deeply evocative, this is an evening to slow down, listen deeply, and cross into the unseen.
It's like a soundtrack that is crafted to go with your life.

Sika's Sound Journeys are truly unique. They are sonic explorations that feature didgeridoos, drums, flutes, voice and orchestral elements. A collection of foraged sounds from objects he has recorded in nature, add even more textures. Many of his sounds are completely unrecognisable and abstract. Immersed in this cocoon of abstract sound, we can find ourselves in the most surprising places in our mind. In this timeless realm, beyond the normal limitations of rational reality, anything is possible.
An epic adventure deep inside yourself.
Journey to worlds within worlds on incredible sound waves.

Sika creates a safe space to relax deeply and connect with our inner guidance. This Sound Journey begins and ends with a short guided visualisation.
There will be two journeys with an interval. Most people choose to lie down, so bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket and get really comfortable.
A journal is also recommended.

SIKA Sound Journey - KAPITI SIKA Sound Journey - 'Between the Worlds' Something between an ancient ceremony and a movie soundtrack.
Between the Worlds is an evening Sound Journey shaped by over thirty years of deep ceremonial and immersive sound practice.
It is an experience to be entered.
A collection of foraged sounds from objects he has recorded in nature, add even more textures.
There will be two journeys with an interval.

events.long_description:
Setting about creating an Aotearoa New Zealand that is built on notions of comprehensive, holistic justice, we need to re-imagine how an Aotearoa with a future would look like. There are concepts emerging internationally and nationally that will help us develop a framework for a society built on equity and justice for all.

with Tayla Forward, Boyd Scirkovich, Marilyn Waring, James Belich, Meihana Durie, Carwyn Jones, Gareth Hughes, Frances Hancock, Pania Newton, Bianca Elkington, Clive Aspin, Heather Came, Fleur Fitzsymonds, Rebecca Kiddle and more.

events.id: Joanne McNally — Pinotphile-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Joanne McNally — Pinotphile
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
She’s a comedian boasting stage, television, and podcast success. Joanne McNally heads to The Opera House in Wellington for a new stage show. Pinotphile displays the Irish comic’s wry wit and sense of the absurd. Hailed by Variety as One to Watch, McNally has been keeping herself busy since her last tour by ruining men’s lives (and her own). For Pinophile, she’s still single, still unfertilised, and laden with irreverent, ridiculous and hilarious war stories. Join Joanne for a wild ride as she navigates situationships, revenge, frenemies and rejection. The Irish Examiner describes her trademark style as ‘joyously nuts’. 
You may have come across Joanne as a co-host of the award-winning podcast My Therapist Ghosted Me. She’s also had riotous appearances on Taskmaster, The Jonathan Ross Show and The Big Fat Quiz of Everything.

events.id: Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development-Maungaraki School,Dowse Drive, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development
events.venue: Maungaraki School,Dowse Drive, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development
Teaching has never been more complex or more important. Every classroom is a dynamic mix of neurodiversity, cultural identity, language, trauma, and rapid societal change. Educators are being asked to do more, with less - often caught between policy pressures and what they know is right for children.

This new national workshop from Longworth Education acknowledges that complexity and offers practical, evidence-informed strategies for navigating it.

This is not a one-size-fits-all model. Instead, we explore what it means to teach in ways that are developmentally responsive, neuro-affirming, trauma-aware and culturally sustaining, all while managing the daily realities of the classroom.

Designed for teachers, leaders, SENCOs, learning support staff and anyone working in education, this workshop will support you to:

Better understand the developmental, neurological and relational needs that shape student behaviour and learning

Recognise and respond to complexity, without becoming overwhelmed by it

Strengthen inclusive, identity-affirming practices that work in diverse, real-world classrooms

Learn practical ways to hold onto both rigour and humanity in your teaching

Leave with tools, frameworks and language you can use the very next day

Whether you're new to this way of thinking or looking to deepen your practice, this workshop will meet you where you are and support you to move forward with confidence, clarity and care.

Join us as we mark ten years of Longworth Education’s commitment to child-centred pedagogy and celebrate our recognition as one of HundrED’s Top 100 Global Education Innovations for 2026 and Aotearoa's leading expert in play pedagogy across the school sector.
events.description:
Real Kids, Real Complexity: Teaching at the Intersection of Neurodiversity, Culture and Development Teaching has never been more complex or more important.
Every classroom is a dynamic mix of neurodiversity, cultural identity, language, trauma, and rapid societal change.
Educators are being asked to do more, with less - often caught between policy pressures and what they know is right for children.
This new national workshop from Longworth Education acknowledges that complexity and offers practical, evidence-informed strategies for navigating it.
This is not a one-size-fits-all model.

Experience the sights, smells, and flavors of a traditional night market right in the heart of Lower Hutt this autumn. 🍂

The Hutt City Night Market is an international celebration of food, bringing together a delicious mix of cuisines from around the world.

events.name: Welcome 2 Reception - Fundraiser For Reception By Unions' Wellington
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Welcome 2 Reception is a dance party fundraiser for Reception featuring the dangerous and eccentric talents of DJ Pinky, Weather Dog, and Dwarfstar. Come down, shake your money maker - and share the money you made - to support our efforts to push forward organic union organising in Wellington. See you there!

Reception, powered by Unions Wellington, is a space for unionists, activists, artists and interested community members to organise and collaborate. Based in the Trades Hall (next to Taste of Home) it is both a reception to Wellington Trades Hall and a reception to the union movement.

The space is entirely independent and relies on the people's solidarity to run. And yet, we still have bills to pay! Your contribution helps us keep the lights on and do our part to build and deepen the union movement. See you there!

For more ways to get involved or support the kaupapa, visit: https://reception.nz/.

See you there!

events.id: Sushi Unfolded: A Vegan Demonstration & Tasting-Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Sushi Unfolded: A Vegan Demonstration & Tasting
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sushi Unfolded: A Vegan Demonstration & Tasting
Enjoy a vegan sushi demonstration with tastings as you learn to craft nigiri, rolls, and inari in a fun, relaxed evening of creativity

About this Event


Sushi Unfolded: A Vegan Demonstration & Tasting

Back by popular demand! An evening of flavour, creativity, and connection as Balarama once again shares the craft of vegan sushi-making. From delicate hand-pressed pieces to beautifully rolled varieties, you’ll watch each step come to life in an engaging demonstration that’s both practical and inspiring.
Balarama’s grounded and approachable style makes the process easy to follow—even if it’s your first time exploring sushi-making. There’ll be space for questions throughout, and plenty of tastings along the way—so come hungry!
On the menu for the demo:
Hand-pressed sushi (Nigiri)
Sushi rolls (Maki-zushi)
Tofu pocket sushi (Inari-zushi)
Afterwards, stay on for a full vegan dinner and dessert, freshly served and enjoyed in good company—a deliciously relaxed way to spend your evening.





Event followed by a plant-based dinner and dessert, all included in your ticket!


Meet Balarama, your chef & host
Say hello to Balarama, our amazing chef who’s always meditating on his next culinary creation. At Bhakti Lounge, he’s known for mastering a range of cuisines—from comforting classics to vibrant international dishes. Lately, he’s been exploring the delicate and artful world of Japanese cooking, crafting plant-based sushi that’s as beautiful as it is flavourful. Balarama’s goal is to make food that feels nourishing, thoughtful, and easy to enjoy—like a home-cooked meal made with heart. With his calm, down-to-earth style, he makes even the most intricate dishes feel approachable.



Agenda


🕑: 06:00 PM - 06:15 PM
Arrive to Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:15 PM - 07:15 PM
Sushi Unfolded: A Vegan Demonstration & Tasting
Host: Balarama

🕑: 07:15 PM - 08:15 PM
Plant-based dinner and dessert!

events.id: Looking for a New Sport? Try Ultimate Frisbee-Ākau Tangi Sports Centre, 72 Kemp Street, Kilbirnie, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Looking for a New Sport? Try Ultimate Frisbee
events.venue: Ākau Tangi Sports Centre, 72 Kemp Street, Kilbirnie, Wellington
events.long_description:
Looking for something active and social to try on a Thursday night?

Come along to our free beginner Ultimate Frisbee taster sessions at Ākau Tangi Recreation Centre. These relaxed games are designed for people who have never played before or want a casual introduction to the sport.

Ultimate frisbee is a fun, fast-moving team sport played with a flying disc. It is easy to pick up, very social, and a great way to meet new people while getting some exercise.

Each Thursday night is a stand-alone session, so you can drop in whenever it suits you. You do not need to have attended earlier weeks, and there is no commitment to come every week.
These sessions focus on beginners and newer players, with guidance available to help people learn the basics, build confidence, and enjoy their first games.

Most people who attend are in their 20s and 30s, but anyone curious to try ultimate frisbee is welcome.

Bring a friend, try something new, and see why so many people love ultimate.

Event Details
What: Beginner Ultimate Frisbee “Taster” Sessions
When: Thursdays, 7:00pm
Dates: 16 April – 18 June
Where: Court 1, Ākau Tangi Recreation Centre, Kilbirnie, Wellington
Cost: Free
Who: Beginners and anyone curious about trying ultimate frisbee
What to bring: Comfortable sports clothes and running shoes

No experience needed. Just show up and join in.

events.id: The Lord of the Rings in 90 Minutes-Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: The Lord of the Rings in 90 Minutes
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
The Lord of the Rings in 90 Minutes
The Lord of the Rings in 90 Minutes is a hilarious spin on Tolkien’s epic trilogy.

Calling all elves, orcs and halflings! Buckle up for a glorious, laugh a minute journey into Middle Earth (and back again), as three actors and a musician attempt the impossible – to retell the famous series in one outrageous live experience!

Complete with battles, LOTR bingo and a banging early noughties soundtrack, expect an evening filled with irreverent delight.

The influential Lord of the Rings films changed the face of tourism in Aotearoa forever. Now it’s time for a version that delivers live music, ridiculous impersonations, and justice for Tom Bombadil!

All ages are welcome, although these shows are best enjoyed by ages 12 years and over.

--

For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email or call 04)9740535 during office hours.

High School Group bookings please email or call 04)9740535 during office hours.

events.id: Joanne McNally - Pinotphile-The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Joanne McNally - Pinotphile
events.venue: The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
After the success of her record-breaking tour The Prosecco Express, Joanne McNally returns with her hotly anticipated new stand-up show Pinotphile. Hailed by Variety as One to Watch, McNally has been keeping herself busy by ruining men's lives (and her own) and now she's back; still single, still unfertilised, and laden with irreverent, ridiculous and hilarious war stories.

Join Joanne for a wild ride as she navigates situationships, revenge, frenemies and rejection in her “terrifyingly funny” (Evening Standard) and “joyously nuts” (Irish Examiner) trademark style.

She's the co-host of award-winning podcast My Therapist Ghosted Me, and with riotous appearances on Taskmaster, The Jonathan Ross Show and The Big Fat Quiz of Everything. Joanne also hosts the hugely successful BBC Sounds series Joanne McNally Investigates: Who Replaced Avril Lavigne and Do Furbys Spy On Us?

“A truly gifted stand up” - Sunday Times

“The most exciting thing to happen to Irish comedy in years” - Irish Times

“Next stop, Netflix” - Irish Daily Star

“Please bring your friends and laugh until you cry” - Edinburgh Reporter

“Masters of no-holds barred craic” - Time Out

events.id: Whānau Printing Creative arts holiday programme for tamariki & adults-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Whānau Printing Creative arts holiday programme for tamariki & adults
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Children and their caregivers are invited to explore printmaking!

Learn simple printmaking techniques with your tamariki in the historic Inverlochy House. In this workshop you and your young person will work together to learn simple print making techniques and create finished artwork to take home. You will experience a low mess creative process with recycled materials and have an awesome opportunity to learn printing techniques that will enable you to recreate the experience using equipment that you are likely to have at home.

This booking is for one adult and one tamariki/teen (minium age 6)

events.id: Poppy Day-RSA Wellington, Level 9, Technology One House 86 Victoria Str, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Poppy Day
events.venue: RSA Wellington, Level 9, Technology One House 86 Victoria Str, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Wellington RSA has commenced planning for the RSA Poppy Day 0n 17 April 2026. To cover the greater Wellington area we are looking for people to be collectors on Friday 17 April.

The allocation of collector positions and times is coordinated by myself, WRSA Office Manager, Zenetta Ganic.

If you are able to assist us with Poppy Day collection please contact Zenetta on zenetta@wrsa.org.nz or phone 04 385 1191

The Poppy Day collection is very important for the WRSA so that we can fund our support for Veterans through our medical scheme, District Support Advisor and support for the Trentham and Linton Hubs.

Your assistance will be appreciated.

Regards, Zenetta

events.long_description:
Paint our feathered friends with artist and educator, Joe McMenamin. See Wellington Museum’s new kororā little blue penguin display and learn about our native manu in the Attic, before learning the basics of painting with Joe.

All tamariki will be able to take home their painting.

Aprons are provided, but tamariki are advised to wear clothes they don’t mind getting messy.

events.id: Whānau Sculpture - Creative arts holiday programme-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Whānau Sculpture - Creative arts holiday programme
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Children and their caregivers are invited to learn how to sculpt with clay!

Our popular whānau workshops are designed to allow tamariki and their caregivers work on their own projects side-by-side and learn basic hand-building techniques from our wonderful tutor Christine Winbush. Sculpting creations change every term so if you've joined us before, you'll be creating something new & different to take home!

The pieces will be fired once, (bisqued) and can be decorated with paint at home. All creatures will be ready for pickup approximately 3 weeks later depending on drying times.

Recommended age for children is 6 and up. The booking is for one child and one adult and an additional child can be added for only $29.

Join us for a behind-the-scenes tour of Wellington's iconic St James Theatre and The Opera House. Discover hidden stories, architectural splendour, and rich theatrical history as you step behind the velvet curtain and explore two of the city's most beloved performance spaces. Led by expert guides, this tour takes you on a journey through over a century of theatre magic. Tread the boards in the footsteps of legendary performers. Uncover the secrets of stagecraft and marvel at the breathtaking details of these heritage-listed buildings. From opulent foyers and ornate auditoriums to backstage corridors brimming with history, you'll see the theatres as you've never seen them before.

events.id: Lost Wax in Three - Hard Wax - Fridays!-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lost Wax in Three - Hard Wax - Fridays!
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Over three sessions, create stunning jewellery using the age-old lost wax technique. This is an excellent opportunity to treat yourself to some creative fun on a Friday night!

In weeks one and two, you will learn how to work with jeweller's hard wax to create beautiful textures in metal that can be used to create amazing rings or objects. Your waxes will be sent away for casting in the metal of your choice.

Then there will be a week's break before the third session, to allow time for everyone's waxes to be cast. The third session covers how to trim, clean and finish your casts, turning them into original jewellery ready to wear!

More advanced students can use these sessions to complete projects in a class setting.

Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.

Not included are the casting and metal costs which vary from student to student depending on what sized ring they want to make and the type of metal you choose. Small castings can cost around $6.65 - $12 and might cost between $7 to $30 in metals depending on if you use red bronze, white bronze or silver.

This class is great for teens and kids over 12 or so. Anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.name: Glow In the Dark Titiwai Tours - New Zealand Glow Worms
events.venue: Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
A fascinating tour into the world of titiwai, New Zealand's glow worms, in Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā. This outdoor night-time tour takes about an hour, and all are welcome to join. If bringing children, we find it most suitable for those aged 7 years and older.

Meet at the Founders’ entrance to Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā on Glenmore Street. Please bring your ticket, a torch, and an umbrella if it’s raining. We'll see you there!
This tour is operated by the Friends of Wellington Botanic Garden.

Glow Worm Tour Ticket Refund Policy

• No Refunds: We do not offer refunds for Glow Worm Tour tickets.

• Ticket Transfers: If you can’t attend your booked tour, with 2 weeks notice we can put you on a future tour, subject to availability.

• Weather Conditions: Tours operate rain or shine. In the event of extreme weather or unsafe conditions, we will reschedule your tour or provide a refund.

For any changes or enquiries, please get in touch with us in advance at Treehouse@wcc.govt.nz or call 04 499 1400.

events.id: Sika Sound Journey-Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sika Sound Journey
events.venue: Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Part of the new SIKA Sound Journey International Tour - 'Between the Worlds'
Something between an ancient ceremony and a movie soundtrack.

Please bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket. Be prepared to drop into a deep space.
Door sales will be available if there is space. Advanced booking is recommended.

Between the Worlds is an evening Sound Journey shaped by over thirty years of deep ceremonial and immersive sound practice. Blending primal rhythm, atmospheric voice, deep didgeridoo, and field recordings, the journey unfolds organically — shifting from dynamic, embodied energy into vast, cinematic stillness.

This is not passive listening.
It is an experience to be entered.

As sound layers, dissolve, and reform, participants are guided into a liminal space where time softens and inner landscapes awaken. The journey becomes a passage — one that listeners don’t just hear, but inhabit.

Guided by the myth of the Wandering Forest Spirit, Between the Worlds invites a reconnection with self, with nature, and with something older than language. Ceremonial, immersive, and deeply evocative, this is an evening to slow down, listen deeply, and cross into the unseen.
It's like a soundtrack that is crafted to go with your life.

Sika's Sound Journeys are truly unique. They are sonic explorations that feature didgeridoos, drums, flutes, voice and orchestral elements. A collection of foraged sounds from objects he has recorded in nature, add even more textures. Many of his sounds are completely unrecognisable and abstract. Immersed in this cocoon of abstract sound, we can find ourselves in the most surprising places in our mind. In this timeless realm, beyond the normal limitations of rational reality, anything is possible.
An epic adventure deep inside yourself.
Journey to worlds within worlds on incredible sound waves.

Sika creates a safe space to relax deeply and connect with our inner guidance. This Sound Journey begins and ends with a short guided visualisation.

There will be two journeys with an interval. Most people choose to lie down, so bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket and get really comfortable.

A journal is also recommended.
events.description:
Part of the new SIKA Sound Journey International Tour - 'Between the Worlds' Something between an ancient ceremony and a movie soundtrack.
Between the Worlds is an evening Sound Journey shaped by over thirty years of deep ceremonial and immersive sound practice.
It is an experience to be entered.
A collection of foraged sounds from objects he has recorded in nature, add even more textures.
There will be two journeys with an interval.

events.id: FOGHORN - A nautical Airhorn encounter-Newtown Community Centre - Te Whare Hapori o Ngā Puna Waiora,7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: FOGHORN - A nautical Airhorn encounter
events.venue: Newtown Community Centre - Te Whare Hapori o Ngā Puna Waiora,7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
FOGHORN - A nautical Airhorn encounter
Before Airhorn, before the Newtown Community Council, before all the wild and woolly chaos of today...there was Foghorn. A town, a people, a comedy show, and a secret that could end it all. Foghorn. A place without time, a comedy without comparison. From the pages of Airhorn comes a bold new adventure. Foghorn. What beckons you, out there in the fog? What is the light that brings you in? The future of comedy is written in scrimshaw on a whale's bone.

Come to Newtown, let the fog envelop you.

events.long_description:
A new club night for Wellington. Nostalgic modernism for retro-futurists.
Alternative sounds of the eighties and beyond to make you move.
Your host is DJ Matai, known for sets spanning vintage goth, new wave and post-punk. Expect to hear the classic and the obscure. Not only a selector, but a genuine alternative music fanatic, he is also known for running Razored Raw and drumming in bands such as No Sector and Maced.
Joined by guests:
DJ Idle Hands- Katie is a vinyl DJ who has been playing around Pōneke for more than a decade. She is the drummer in DÄHTM, works in film sound, and is the former host of the Dolls House radio show on the (now defunct) Fast Radio. Expect 80s punk, cold wave, and some Continental deep cuts.
DJ Boss Dude- It's your ol' mate, thee space vampire of Newtown, thee headhoncho of Creeps record parlour, thee master of many genres, 80s electronique, synthesizer, Italo-disco, and New Wave.
$15 door/ Presales via UTR
8pm-late

events.id: Meow Calling-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Meow Calling
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Two of Wellingtons longest running shows team up to raise money for Head and Neck Cancer Support Aotearoa.
Wazzo Clash present an evening of Classic Clash songs played loud and live along with DJ Bill E (Atomic/24hr Party People) setting the scene with late 70s vibes.
Don't miss this exciting evening in support of people undergoing treatment for HNC.
Head and neck cancer is the 6th most common cancer in the world, although not widely known meaning it is often not diagnosed until an advanced stage, making raising awareness of symptoms and causes essential.
We look forward to seeing you there - let's Rock the Casbah while we do a little good in this world!

events.id: Weekend Laughs with Tony Lyall and Lesa Macleod-Whiting-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Weekend Laughs with Tony Lyall and Lesa Macleod-Whiting
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Weekend Laughs at Fringe Bar is a two hour comedy showcase with some of the country's favourite performers.

Each month's line up will feature a guest MC, plus 6 acts from around the capital and the country, doing their best to bring the big laughs!
With a changing line up each month, there'll be a little of something for everyone.

Tony Lyall is one of New Zealand's most exciting comedians. His mix of high energy storytelling, off the cuff crowd work and general good times vibe shakes up any stage he appears on. Tony is head writer and a reporter on The Project, appeared on the Comedy Gala, writes for 7 Days and was nominated for 'Breakthrough Comedian' at the NZ Comedy Guild Awards.
"Sharp, engaging and consistently funny." - Appetite for the Arts
"A unique and entertaining hour of comedy" - The Speakeasy

And Lesa Macleod-Whiting - 2025 Billy T Award nominee
Lesa is a Wellington-based writer, improviser and comedian. She has been a regular on the Wellington improv scene since 2016 and began doing stand-up in July 2020, going on to nab the Runner Up spot at the Wellington Raw Comedy Quest. She has a penchant for characters, accents and fast-paced silliness.
Winner – Best Newcomer – 2020 Wellington Comedy Awards Nominated – Best Newcomer (Lower North Island) – 2020 NZ Comedy Guild Awards

These shows regularly sell out, we encourage our audience to arrive at least 15min before start time to grab a drink and find a seat.

events.id: Nelson College vs. Wellington College 150th-Wellington College (New Zealand),Dufferin Street, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Nelson College vs. Wellington College 150th
events.venue: Wellington College (New Zealand),Dufferin Street, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Nelson College vs. Wellington College 150th

Our Nelson College Rugby 1st XV are heading to the capital to be part of NZ rugby history when we take on Wellington College on Saturday April 18. The game marks 150 years since our two schools matched up in the first ever inter-collegiate game played in New Zealand. Sky will be televising this significant event, and there will be two days of events leading up to the game with a golf tournament on April 16 and a gala luncheon on April 17 which promises to be entertaining with Marc Ellis and Sir Graham Henry attending.

For more information and to purchase tickets to pre-game events go to: https://www.wellington-college.school.nz/wcrugby150/

#ncrugby Nelson College Old Boys Nelson College Nelson College Sport

events.name: Deva Mahal & Friends tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: 100% GOOD & UTR
Born with the blues in her blood, Deva has forged a rare blend of masterful songwriting, breathtaking vocal talent, and soulful production. Her ability to convey both profound joy and deep sorrow through her lyrical and melodic compositions often leaves audiences moved to tears and overflowing with emotion.
Her career has taken her from iconic stages around the world to the big screen, including a role in David O. Russell’s acclaimed film American Hustle, alongside Christian Bale, Bradley Cooper, Jennifer Lawrence, and Robert De Niro.
Her voice is also featured on the soundtrack of The Experimenter (starring Winona Ryder and Peter Sarsgaard). She has performed at renowned venues and festivals such as the Hollywood Bowl, Carnegie Hall, Bonnaroo, and the Montreal Jazz Festival.
Now New Zealanders will have the chance to be the first in the world to celebrate FUTURE CLASSIC VOL ll: FUTURE with Deva and her band. Don’t miss the chance to catch this powerhouse up-close-and-personal in a city or town near you!

events.id: Mozart Requiem and Christopher Tin’s To Shiver the Sky-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mozart Requiem and Christopher Tin’s To Shiver the Sky
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
From the earliest dreams of flight to one of the greatest choral masterpieces ever written — Wellington is in for a treat in April. Wellington’s Orpheus Choir joins forces with Orchestra Wellington to present a mighty programme. Mozart Requiem and Christopher Tin’s To Shiver the Sky explores five centuries of human imagination and purpose.
Mozart’s Requiem stands as one of the greatest choral works ever composed. Written in the final months of his life, it sets the words to the Catholic requiem mass with blazing dramatic power. The oratorio has a depth of feeling that has captivated audiences for more than two centuries. To Shiver the Sky is an epic choral spectacular from Grammy-winning composer Christopher Tin. It draws on texts from da Vinci, Hildegard von Bingen, Ovid, Jules Verne, Amelia Earhart and John F. Kennedy. The work builds a sweeping musical history of human flight from imagined wings to the Moon landing. Orchestra and choir conductor Brent Stewart will also lead four soloists through the concert. They include soprano Emma Pearson, alto Charlotte Secker, tenor Ridge Ponini, and bass Robert Tucker.

It draws on texts from da Vinci, Hildegard von Bingen, Ovid, Jules Verne, Amelia Earhart and John F. Kennedy.

events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Wellington Women’s Lifestyle Expo brings 150 of the best lifestyle companies to TSB Arena for the ultimate girls’ weekend. 
The expo will have something for everyone. Featuring fashion, beauty, health, wellness, artisan goods, eco-products, gourmet food and beverages, and so much more. It’s the perfect opportunity for a girls’ day out or a leisurely wander between the exhibitors. 
The popular Artisan Zone will include crafters from all over New Zealand. There will be handmade products such as soaps and organic beauty goods. Jewellery, original art, home décor, and clothing. Appreciating that shopping, trying, and testing can be hard work, the expo Taste Zone will be on hand. You’ll find pick-me-ups, tasty treats and bites to keep you going.

events.id: Blue Dragon Book Fair 2026-Ngaio Town Hall, 1 Ottawa Rd, Ngaio, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Blue Dragon Book Fair 2026
events.venue: Ngaio Town Hall, 1 Ottawa Rd, Ngaio, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Blue Dragon Book Fair 2026 will be held in the Ngaio Town Hall, as usual, on the weekend of 18 and 19 April. We are accepting donations of good-quality books, puzzles and board games from Saturday 21 March to 5pm Tuesday 14 April.
In recent years, we have been receiving huge numbers of donations for which we are very grateful. However, the space in the hall is tight, and our capacity to sort through so many donations needs to be managed. For those reasons, the 2026 book fair will NOT accept donations of:
• CDs, DVDs and records
• Travel guides, self-help books, business books and textbooks
• Food books and gardening books before 2020.
This decision is based on the fact that these books and CDs etc don’t sell very well but take up a lot of space.
We have a much-loved section of quality children's books for only $1 each. That's because the trustees all value reading and know how important it is for children to read.
Drop your donations from Saturday 21 March at Kennards Storage, 19 Hutt Road, in the downstairs area on the right-hand side, labelled 'Blue Dragon'. Please, no books after Tuesday 14 April. Kennards Storage is open M-F 8.30am-6pm; Sat 9am-5pm; Sunday 10am-4pm. It is closed on Easter Friday and open on Easter Monday from 10am to 4pm. Check Kennards’ website for opening hours
If you have any questions or want to help, please email us at bluedragonnz@gmail.com

🎪✨ ROLL UP, ROLL UP – THE EXTRAVAGANZA FAIR IS BACK! ✨🎪

🎉 Celebrating 11 epic years of magical mayhem on the road! 🎉

New Zealand’s biggest and most fabulous travelling fair is rolling back into town – and you’re invited!

We’re bringing grassroots charm, vintage flair, and old-school fun to town – a joyride for all ages! Whether you’re 5 or 95, there’s something here to make your heart happy.

Get ready for a weekend packed full of FREE entertainment the whole whānau will love.

Expect a kaleidoscope of colour, creativity, and community vibes with:

🎨 Unique market stalls, arts,crafts & more!
🌮 Yummy street food galore
🎡 Kids rides & games all day
🎭 The fabulous Ashton Family Circus
🐉 Burns the Dragon breathing fun
🧜‍♀️ Enchanting Mermaid Storytime
🎪 Sparkling LuckyStar Zone (as seen at Splore!)

See you there this summer 2025 - 2026!! 💥 🌼

events.id: Autumn Sandcasting Day-Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Autumn Sandcasting Day
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ltd, 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for this wonderful intro to sand casting - it is a great opportunity to make some gorgeous lasting jewellery gifts for yourself or your loved ones!

The class runs from 10am - 4pm and there will be a lunch break.

Students are shown the ancient technique of sand casting, using a wax to make a simple hard model or found objects such as buttons or simple small forms - create your simple 2-part mould and melt and pour sterling silver or shibuichi (a mix of silver and copper) to cast into the sand.

The model you have made or found enables you to make multiple pours possible to create multiple pieces. Best suited to smaller scale items, both to keep the budget down and the amount of weight in a piece this technique can be used to create rings, pendants or small earrings. This is a fun process for those who just want to try their hand at it, or a very useful low-tech process for those who are making jewellery into a career or creative activity. Each student will be given opportunity to cast one or two items, and then time will be included in the day to finish up the pieces so you can wear them away.

This class uses a combination of tutor supervision and also a peer-buddy system so that students are also given opportunity to watch and learn from each other's casts and pours.

The class has a practical health and safety aspect to it as students are introduced to working with an oxy-lpg torch and molten metal.

Course cost does not include silver costs but students can expect to use around $60 - $120 worth of silver or shibuichi.

events.id: Fabric-a-brac 2026-St Anne's Hall, 22 Emmett St, Newtown, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Fabric-a-brac 2026
events.venue: St Anne's Hall, 22 Emmett St, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
Fabric-a-brac is a fun fabric and sewing supplies market where you can shop for vintage and modern sewing and upholstery fabric, patterns, haberdashery and more. It brings the sewing community together to share stashes, stories and delight in our mutual love of textiles.

The first Fabric-a-brac was held in Wellington 15 years ago - we're sew excited to still be here, bring the sewing community together and raising money for Mary Potter Hospice.

Fabric-a-brac is volunteer-run, and is a fundraiser for hospice - in Wellington this is Mary Potter Hospice. All profits from this event are donated to their work.
Selling fabric
If you have a stash you'd like to sell, you can come and take a stall. You pay a stallholder fee (which goes to Hospice) but anything you make on the day is yours to keep. Stalls are allocated via a ballot, which will take place closer to the time. Go to our website to find the form.

If you have fabric, haberdashery or patterns you'd like to move on, but don't want to have a table, you can donate items. This will all go onto the hospice table on the day, and all profits will go to their wonderful work.
Fabric donation points
- Made Marion
- Mary Potter Hospice - you can take donations to the Mary Potter Hospice in Mein St, Newtown

Pop-up cafe
The fabulous cafe is back with all of its gorgeous volunteers and fab food. There will be tea, coffee and sweet treats for sale, so you can sit down and chat over your new projects and fabric purchases. All proceeds from the cafe also go to Hospice.

Buying fabric
Bring cash - most stallholders and the cafe will be selling by cash only.

We look forward to seeing our fabulous fabric and sewing community soon!

*St Anne's Parish Hall, Emmett Street, Newtown, Wellington

events.long_description:
Three-Region Savory Dumplings Combo | Bánh ba Miền
Join us for a hands-on Vietnamese cooking workshop where we’ll prepare a Three Region Savory Dumplings Combo together, a delicious journey through flavours and techniques from different parts of Vietnam.



In this session, you’ll learn how to make:



* Steamed pork rice pyramids

* Clear shrimp and pork dumplings

* Sticky rice dumplings

Guided step by step, we’ll cook side by side, share tips, and enjoy the process in a relaxed, welcoming environment. This workshop is suitable for beginners and those who simply enjoy cooking and eating together as a community.



This class is delivered in collaboration with I Love Viet Food, and made possible thanks to the generous support and sponsorship.

Your participation helps us continue running meaningful, accessible community workshops and creating more opportunities to cook, learn, and connect together.

We look forward to welcoming you into the kitchen.

Ticket Information

• $10 per person – appreciation contribution towards facilitator effort

• Optional koha/donation welcome to support VHNZ Cuisine community programmes

events.id: Wellington Women's Lifestyle Expo 2026-TSB Arena, Queens Wharf, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Wellington Women's Lifestyle Expo 2026
events.venue: TSB Arena, Queens Wharf, Wellington
events.long_description:
Date: Saturday 18 & Sunday 19 April 2026
Venue: TSB Arena, Wellington
Opening hours: Sat & Sun 10am to 4pm

The ultimate girls' day out awaits you, filled with shopping, pampering and mouth-watering indulgences, and the chance to win amazing prizes! Gather your besties, grab your earlybird tickets, and prepare for an unforgettable day at the Wellington Women’s Lifestyle Expo on Saturday 18 & Sunday 19 April at TSB Arena!

150 Companies
The expo will have something for everyone, featuring over 130 of the best lifestyle companies under one roof, including gourmet food and beverages, fashion and beauty, jewellery, health and wellness, artisan goods, unique artwork, homewares, eco-products and much more. It’s the perfect opportunity for a girls’ day out, or to treat yourself to a day leisurely wandering between exhibitors.

Goodie Bags
Our mystery goodie bags are $20 each and usually contain items such as beauty and skincare products, food items, magazines and more. The contents of the goodie bags can vary from bag to bag, adding to the mystery! Goodie bags are available to purchase online with your tickets until the Friday night before the show, and at the event; however we do sell out so our hot tip is to pre-purchase online to guarantee your bag!

Taste Zone
Appreciating that shopping, pampering, trying and testing can be hard work, the expo Taste Zone a mini food show, will be on hand to offer pick-me-ups, tasty treats and bites to keep you going.

Exhibitors
Keen to exhibit at the expo? Find out more at www.womenslifestyleexpo.co.nz/apply, call the friendly NZME Events team on 06 354 0498, or email enquire@events.nzme.co.nz.

events.name: Social Sewing Saturday - April
events.venue: Karori Arts & Crafts,7 Beauchamp Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Social Sewing Saturday - April
Come join us for a fun-filled day of sewing and socializing at Karori Arts & Crafts!

About this Event

Come join us for a fun-filled day of sewing and socializing at Karori Arts & Crafts!
Come join us for a fun-filled day of sewing and socializing at Karori Arts & Crafts! Whether you're a seasoned pro or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone who loves to sew. Bring your latest project or start something new - either way, you'll have a blast chatting with fellow sewing enthusiasts. Bring your current project or get inspired by others' creations at our friendly gathering. Share tips, tricks, and stories while working on our projects together.
Don't forget to pack your sewing machine, fabric, and any other supplies you'll need. We can't wait to see what you create! You will need to bring your own machine and materials. Please bring an extension lead and multipoint box if you have one. (Don't worry, though—we have some in the centre, too!) We'll supply an iron and ironing board to share, as well as a table and relatively comfy chair each. There are also spare cutting-out tables at a taller height. You are welcome to come along and work on hand-sewing projects.
A kitchen is available on site and supermarkets as well as cafes are very close by.Parking is free and there are 3 dedicated spaces for the centre as well as others closeby.
Cost: Free for members of the Karori Arts Centre or $15 per person payable directly to KACC. (Membership of the centre is $105 annually and also gives you reductions on classes and access to other groups)
Please do NOT attend if you are sick (even just a little bit!)

Discover wonder and adventure these school holidays at Wellington Museum with String Bean Puppets - The Penguin and the Sea Monster. events.name: Découvrez La Drôme - Workshop & Tasting
events.venue: Alliance Française Welington - French Cultural Centre,78 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Découvrez La Drôme - Workshop & Tasting
Join us and learn more about this beautiful region!

About this Event

Nestled in the heart of southeastern France, the Drôme is a captivating region of rolling lavender fields, charming hilltop villages and sun-drenched landscapes.
Discover this beautiful region, where Provençal douceur de vivre meets rich history, local markets and delicious regional cuisine.
Join our French teacher Claudie for a fun and immersive workshop (en français!) and explore the authentic atmosphere and flavors of the Drôme.
We’ll wrap up with a tasting of traditional and delicious treats from the region!
🗯️ French A2/B1 level required
🗓️ Saturday, 18 April 2026, 11.30am–1pm
📍 Alliance Française Wellington (78 Victoria Street, Wellington)
🎟️ $30 for AF members & $35 for non-members

events.name: Brewtown Beer Fest
events.venue: 25 Blenheim Street, New Zealand 5014,25 Blenheim St, Maidstone, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Brewtown Beer Fest
Brewtown Beer Fest is Back!
Lining up with Fresh Hop season, we will be bringing the all the incredible beers, outstanding entertainment and delicious kai you expect, with a fresh hop twist!

Save the date and watch this space for more info, coming soon!

Drinks!
It wouldn’t be NZBF without great beer, and this year 20 of Aotearoa’s best breweries will descend on Brewtown to pour every style you could possibly desire, from crisp Lagers, cold IPAs and hoppy Pale Ales, to funky Sours, smoky Porters, rich Pastry Stouts – and everything in between!

Visit Brewtown’s own custom Unimog Bar for 13 beers handpicked by our craft beer experts, but get in quick – we only have 1 keg of each, and they won’t last long!

New for this year! Step into our cocktail bar for some seriously scrumptious spirits! Our mixologists have been hard at work creating some delicious drops just for you.

We’re bringing in some of the best wines the Wairarapa has to offer, so whether you prefer a crisp Sauv, a fruity Pinot or a refreshing Frosé there will be something to enjoy.

Still not enough for you? Then head down to the Everything Else Bar, for NZ crafted ciders, as well as bourbon, rum, gin and vodka alongside a range of low and no alcohol beers, cocktails and soft drinks.

Kai!
The NZBF food court hosts the region’s top foodies, bringing you big, bold, flavour-packed festival eats that’ll satisfy every craving.
Watch this space as we announce our festive foodie lineup!

Entertainment!
Lose yourself in a cabaret of the bizarre, where the performances are as dark as they are dazzling.

Dance the day away at the main stage, as Welly’s top talent smash out tune after tune, all building up to a headline show from Brewtown favourites, The Blueprint.

Grab your mates and get stuck in at the Games Zone, where a bit of friendly rivalry meets festive fun, and the loser buys the next round.

With affordable return coaches from Welly and Petone and great discounts for group tickets it's scarily easy to get involved!
R18

events.id: Bach Choir of Wellington present Sounding Boards-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Bach Choir of Wellington present Sounding Boards
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Echoes of Friends/Spheres of influence

What happens when composers become each other’s teachers, critics, and friends?
Their music begins to resonate — ideas travelling in widening circles, carried forward through time.

Mendelssohn and Schumann shared a productive working relationship in Leipzig, marked by deep professional respect. A generation later, Schumann’s championing of Brahms as the “future of German music” forged one of the most influential artistic bonds of the Romantic era.

Fauré and Saint-Saëns sustained a remarkable sixty-year friendship, documented in more than a hundred letters. Saint-Saëns taught Fauré, mentored him, and opened the doors of Parisian musical life, shaping his early career.

Mozart and Haydn were kindred spirits across a generation, admiring each other’s craft, playing quartets together, and exchanging works. Mozart dedicated his six “Haydn” quartets to his elder friend — a gesture of profound artistic respect.

Bartók and Kodály discovered a shared passion for Hungarian folk music in 1905, sparking a partnership that transformed ethnomusicology. Their fieldwork shaped two distinct musical voices and left a lasting imprint on 20th-century composition.

Copland, Bernstein, and Barber formed a vibrant American circle. Bernstein regarded Copland as his “only real composition teacher,” while Barber shared their artistic milieu; all three worked closely with the Boston Symphony at Tanglewood.

Holst and Vaughan Williams, the self-styled “committee of two,” were lifelong friends and trusted critics. Their shared ideals and collaboration helped define the sound of English music in the early 20th century.

These are the musical friendships that shaped eras and traditions. Join the Bach Choir for Sounding Boards, featuring music shaped by the artistic relationships that defined their time.

events.id: Sounding Boards-St Andrew's on The Terrace,30 The Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Sounding Boards
events.venue: St Andrew's on The Terrace,30 The Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sounding Boards
What happens when composers become each other’s teachers, critics, and friends?

Sounding Boards celebrates the musical friendships that changed history. From Mozart and Haydn trading ideas around a quartet table to Mendelssohn, Schumann, and Brahms inspiring one another across generations, these relationships sparked creativity that continues to resonate today.

Across Europe and beyond, composers shaped each other’s voices through mentorship, collaboration, and shared curiosity. Fauré and Saint‑Saëns sustained a lifelong artistic partnership, Bartók and Kodály transformed folk research into modern composition, and Copland, Bernstein, and Barber forged a distinctly American sound. Holst and Vaughan Williams, trusted companions in craft, helped define English music for a new century.

Join the Bach Choir for Sounding Boards, a concert woven from these remarkable connections.
This programme brings together music born from friendship, respect, and shared artistic vision, inviting audiences to hear how great composers shaped one another and the worlds they lived in.

events.id: Comedy Q&A with Tony Lyall-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Comedy Q&A with Tony Lyall
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wellington Comedy's Q&A sessions continue with this month's guest - Tony Lyall

Having performed up and down the country, Tony's mix of high energy storytelling, off the cuff crowd work and general good times vibe makes him a crowd favourite on any stage he appears on.

Known for his work on 7 Days, The Project, The ACC Does Game of 2 Halves, Radio Hauraki and multiple NZ International Comedy Festival Galas, he has performed 7 solo hours as part of the NZ International Comedy Festival and at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival. He has also interviewed some of the biggest celebrities on the planet, including Jake Gyllenhaal, Keira Knightley, Ryan Reynolds, Melanie Lynskey, Chris Pratt and the cast of Jackass to name a few. As well as doing all that, he still has time to be a part of the much loved Alternative Commentary Collective.

Tony's MC work has taken him to various sports events around the country. He is the regular Match Day MC for the Black Caps and New Zealand Cricket, and has MCed for both Wellington Cricket and the Blues Rugby Club. He's also a sought after corporate MC and performer, and has entertained some of the biggest companies in New Zealand.

Originally from Blenheim in the South Island, Tony now lives in West Auckland with his wife, three kids and his Toyota Aqua.

events.id: Art Flow: Dine & Discover-Doubletree By Hilton, 28 Grey St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Art Flow: Dine & Discover
events.venue: Doubletree By Hilton, 28 Grey St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Following the success of the award-winning 'ArtFlow' programme at DoubleTree by Hilton Wellington, we invite you to join us for the third chapter of 'ArtFlow: Dine & Discover'; an intimate evening where visual art and culinary craft come together in a truly unique setting.

Developed in partnership with the New Zealand Academy of Fine Arts, 'ArtFlow' has recently been recognised with the Innovative Collaboration Award at the 2025 HM Awards, celebrating its distinctive approach to bringing together artists, hospitality, and the wider community. For this chapter of 'ArtFlow', Elder Pinot joins as an associate partner, bringing a curated selection of their wines to complement the evening’s culinary experience.

Learn more about the programme and award here:
https://nzafa.com/art-flow-winners-2025-hm-awards
https://www.cordonbleu.edu/news/participating-in-the-award-winning-art-flow/en

For this special evening we welcome Lynn Blann, whose artistic journey traces back to Auckland’s vibrant creative culture of the 1960s and 1970s. During this influential period, shaped by the Peace, Anti-War, Women’s and Māori rights movements - artists, writers, poets and musicians gathered in galleries such as the renowned Barry Lett Gallery. Surrounded by the emerging works of figures including McCahon, Hanly and Twiss, Lynn developed a deep connection to the artistic energy of the time.

Today, working in acrylics, charcoal and pastels, Lynn continues to translate these influences into contemporary abstract forms on canvas. Her work reflects a lifelong engagement with New Zealand’s creative culture, recently recognised with a Merit Award at the 2025 Academy Prize for Visual Art.

The evening begins at Grazie Italian Restaurant with a welcome drink and an opportunity to meet Lynn and fellow guests. A thoughtfully curated three-course dinner follows, with dessert prepared by Chef Aman Khanna and the team at Grazie Italian Restaurant, celebrating the culinary artistry that complements the visual experience. Selected wines from Elder Pinot will accompany the entrée and main courses in tasting portions, offering guests a refined introduction to the character and craftsmanship of this distinctive New Zealand wine label.

As the evening unfolds, guests will gain insight into Lynn’s artistic journey and inspirations before continuing the experience with a guided tour through the hotel’s 'ArtFlow' exhibition spaces, where the artworks come to life within the hotel’s beautifully restored heritage interiors.

Event Programme:
• Welcome drink on arrival
• Mix & mingle - meet featured artist Lynn Blann
• Three-course dinner at Grazie Italian Restaurant **paired with a range 'Elder Pinot' wines.
• Guided ArtFlow exhibition tour with the artist

**Wine pairings will be served as tasting portions. Guests are welcome to enjoy additional wines from the Elder Pinot range, available for purchase by the glass on the evening.

'ArtFlow: Dine & Discover' offers a rare opportunity to experience art beyond the canvas bringing together artists, cuisine and conversation in an elegant and intimate setting.

events.id: Ōtari Glow Worm Tour-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Glow Worm Tour
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Take a guided night tour through Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush and discover some of the fascinating night-time residents on your way to see the titiwai/glowworms. Enjoy the tranquil beauty of the bush after dark and witness the magical glow of these unique creatures.

This outdoor night-time tour takes just over an hour.
The tour requires a moderate level of fitness; the route includes numerous steps and a steep incline to and from the glowworms.
No dogs, and not suitable for babies and young children.

Meet at the Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road. Wrap up in warm clothes, wear sturdy walking shoes, and bring a torch or head lamp.

Glow Worm Tour Ticket Refund Policy:
• No Refunds: We do not offer refunds for Glow Worm Tour tickets.
• Ticket Transfers: If you can’t attend your booked tour, with 2 weeks notice we can put you on a future tour, subject to availability.
• Weather Conditions: Tours operate rain or shine. In the event of extreme weather or unsafe conditions, we will reschedule your tour or provide a refund.

For any changes or enquiries, please get in touch with us in advance at Treehouse@wcc.govt.nz or call 04 499 1400.

This tour is operated by the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust. Join the Trust from only $20 a year and the glow worm tour is free, plus you’ll be supporting the amazing work the Trust does. Join today at owbt.nz/membership-donations/

events.id: Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band-St Michaels Community Hall, Kelburn,81 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band
events.venue: St Michaels Community Hall, Kelburn,81 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band
Does Irish music get your feet tapping? This is your opportunity to get up and dance to some cracking tunes at our evening Céilí on Saturday 18th of April.

Come to St Michael’s Hall in Kelburn to meet new people and enjoy a great time in a friendly and welcoming atmosphere. No dancing experience needed, just a bit of coordination, some energy, and a big sense of fun!

Experienced caller Helen Stonehouse will guide you through all the dances. Our amazing Round The House Céilí Band, featuring Roger Stonehouse, Em Griffiths, and Thomas Stonehouse, will be playing lively jigs and reels and polkas for us.

Handy information:
- Dancing starts at 7:30pm
- Age 10 upwards. Under 16s accompanied by an adult
- Bring a water bottle, hydration is key!
- Parking is limited, we recommend catching the bus. Routes 4, 18, and 21 all stop right next to the hall.
- If you’re unwell then please stay home this time, we’ll see you at the next one

Door sales may be available on the day (cash only): General Admission $30, Unwaged $20.
Pro tip - online tickets will be cheaper!

This event is part of our Set Dance day, and there will be workshops running during the day. Set dances are the traditional social dances of Ireland, and are so much fun to dance. Have a look here to see what it's all about: https://www.facebook.com/reel/1062173322541491.
If you’re interested, head over to Humanitix to get a full day package and fill your weekend with dancing!

events.id: HAWTHORNE HEIGHTS | LONELY WORLD TOUR-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: HAWTHORNE HEIGHTS | LONELY WORLD TOUR
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
HAWTHORNE HEIGHTS  | LONELY WORLD TOUR
Celebrating the 20th Anniversary of If Only You Were Lonely — April 2026

Emo pioneers Hawthorne Heights are set to return to New Zealand in 2026 as part of their global Lonely World Tour, commemorating the 20th anniversary of their influential 2006 album If Only You Were Lonely. The band will perform the album in full, alongside a selection of fan favourites spanning their two-decade career.
Since its release, If Only You Were Lonely has become a defining record of the 2000s emo and post-hardcore movement, featuring anthems such as “Saying Sorry”, “Pens and Needles”, and “This Is Who We Are.” The anniversary tour offers fans the rare chance to experience the album live, in an intimate and high-energy setting.

Hawthorne Heights will play two New Zealand shows only:
Friday 17 April – Auckland | Double Whammy
Saturday 18 April – Wellington | San Fran

Tickets and VIP packages are available now through: waxstartouring.com & hawthorneheights.com

events.id: Mozart Requiem-Michael Fowler Centre-All Events In
events.name: Mozart Requiem
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Mozart Requiem
Experience two masterpieces in one exceptional concert as Orpheus Choir Wellington present Christopher Tin’s To Shiver the Sky alongside Mozart’s Requiem. This concert spans five centuries of imagination and purpose, from the earliest dreams of flight to one of the greatest choral masterpieces ever written.

To Shiver the Sky is an epic choral spectacular from Grammy winning composer Christopher Tin. Drawing on texts from da Vinci, Hildegard von Bingen, Ovid, Jules Verne, Amelia Earhart and John F. Kennedy, it builds a sweeping musical history of human flight from imagined wings to the Moon landing. Born from a record breaking Kickstarter campaign, the work opens with Sogno di Volare, known worldwide as the main theme of Civilization VI, one of the most celebrated strategy games of the decade. Its sound world spans luminous stillness and immense choral climaxes, evoking the exhilaration and peril of human flight.

Mozart’s Requiem stands as one of the greatest choral works ever composed. Written in the final months of his life, it sets the sacred Requiem Mass with blazing dramatic power and a depth of feeling that has captivated audiences for more than two centuries. From the fierce energy of the Dies Irae to the aching beauty of the Lacrimosa, the music moves with a haunting power that remains unmatched in the choral repertoire.

Together these works form an extraordinary musical journey. One celebrates the daring that pushes humanity forward. The other reveals the full dramatic power of the Requiem tradition at its most intense and expressive. Performed by the mighty Orpheus Choir Wellington with Orchestra Wellington, conducted by Brent Stewart and featuring celebrated soprano Emma Pearson, Charlotte Secker alto, Ridge Ponini tenor, and Robert Tucker bass, this is a night of immense musical colour and emotional impact.

Drawing on texts from da Vinci, Hildegard von Bingen, Ovid, Jules Verne, Amelia Earhart and John F. Kennedy, it builds a sweeping musical history of human flight from imagined wings to the Moon landing.
Born from a record breaking Kickstarter campaign, the work opens with Sogno di Volare, known worldwide as the main theme of Civilization VI, one of the most celebrated strategy games of the decade.

Join us for a larger-than-life drag spectacular celebrating the 25th anniversary of the beloved ogre classic, Shrek! For one night only, the swamp is getting a fierce makeover as our cast of dazzling drag royalty transforms your favorite fairy tale misfits into high-glam fantasy icons.

Because this anniversary?

Featuring
Blink Bogan
Bobby Bottle Blonde
Silica Gel

Ace Gray
