events.id: Retirement Reinvented-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Retirement Reinvented
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Retirement Reinvented
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
In your middle years, working, retired or somewhere in between? What’s next? Life is about much more than work or leisure, but most of us have given little thought to what “retirement” might mean.
It’s difficult to speculate what may happen in the future – but we can be proactive and take control.
This course offers insights into how to make the most of our time once we’re less focused on career and/or childrearing. It covers many of the issues people face when considering a move from current employment to a different lifestyle that includes more time to do the things we’ve always promised ourselves we’d do – “when we retire”.
After the workshop, you’ll be able to identify a number of common issues and potential ways to manage them when considering and planning your “rejuvenation”, rather than a retirement. We won’t cover financial planning, but will focus on some of the personal growth opportunities for living and making a conscious contribution to your community.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the day, you’ll have:
Developed your own perspective on ageing and retirement in the 21st Century and identified what’s important to you
Completed a personal life audit exercise and considered your values and your strengths
Considered options to include a sense of purpose and explore choices that can provide you with that sense of purpose
Explored possibilities in the major aspects of your life and identified potential challenges and strategies for overcoming them
Begun to develop your blueprint to maximise the best years of your life and have considered a “future proofing” downsizing plan.
Bring pen and paper.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Valiant Pro: Power Play
events.venue: Lower Hutt Town Hall,32 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Valiant Pro: Power Play
For the first time in over a decade, professional wrestling returns to the iconic Lower Hutt Town Hall, a venue that was the beating heart of Wellington wrestling for more than 20 years.

This is more than a show.
This is a homecoming.

Valiant Pro: Power Play will be the biggest wrestling event Lower Hutt has seen in over 20 years, bringing the past, present, and future together under one roof.

A night where we honour the legends who built the foundations, and watch the new generation carve their own legacy.

Featuring top professional wrestling superstars from all over New Zealand and around the world, Power Play promises high-stakes action, unforgettable moments, and the electric atmosphere only live wrestling can deliver.

Merchandise available
Prizes & surprises on the night
Family-friendly, high-energy entertainment

History lives here.
The future starts now.

Don’t miss Valiant Pro: Power Play, May 9th at Lower Hutt Town Hall.

events.id: Comedy Fest in Kāpiti | NZICF 26-Te Raukura ki Kāpiti,34a Raumati Road, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Comedy Fest in Kāpiti | NZICF 26
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kāpiti,34a Raumati Road, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Comedy Fest in Kāpiti | NZICF 26
The New Zealand Comedy Trust presents – Comedy Fest in Kāpiti.

Kāpiti, we’re back for one night only and we’re bringing the big laughs with us!

Hosted by Sam Smith (as seen on the Best Foods Comedy Gala and The Traitors NZ), this is a punchy, high-energy Festival showcase built like a greatest-hits album: fast, fun, and stacked with comedians you already love, plus a few you’re about to become slightly obsessed with.

Leading the charge is Josh Thomson – one of Aotearoa’s sharpest and most effortlessly funny performers. A mainstay of NZ panel comedy – from 7 Days and 7 Days Live to Have You Been Paying Attention? NZ, Patriot Brains and Taskmaster NZ – Josh brings fast thinking, big presence, and that rare skill of making chaos look completely under control. Expect punchy stand-up, quick-fire riffs, and stories that land hard – the kind of set that has the whole room cackling and wondering how he made it look so easy.

Alongside Sam and Josh, you’ll get three more comedians (still to be announced), bringing stand-up to Te Raukura ki Kāpiti, Coastlands Theatre. This has been a sold-out event for the last few years, and it’s shaping up to sell out again – so get in quick, round up your comedy mates, and lock it in early. World-class stand-up, right in the heart of Paraparaumu.

TICKETS ON SALE WEDNESDAY 3RD DECEMBER

Coming April/May 2026, an unforgettable return of one of rock’s greatest masterpieces.

After nearly three decades of captivating audiences, The Pink Floyd Experience proudly brings back the legendary production of 'The Wall', an epic live reimagining of Pink Floyd’s most powerful concept album.

This breathtaking tour recreates the spectacle of the original, complete with the monumental construction and dramatic destruction of The Wall itself. It’s not just a concert; It’s an immersive journey through sound, light, and emotion — from isolation to transcendence.

Led by the commanding vocals of Stan Gratkowski and the seasoned musicians who have shaped this act for 28 years, this show captures the magic, precision, and raw feeling that
defined Pink Floyd’s legacy.

You’ve heard the songs. You’ve seen the tributes.
But have you felt it — the pulse of the music, the surge of the light, the goosebumps as the first notes hit?

This tour promises all that — and more:

A full-scale visual and sonic experience

Prepare to be immersed, moved, and utterly blown away.

This show is a MUST-SEE for any Floyd fan!

events.id: Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour 2026 - Pōneke Wellington-Embassy Theatre,10 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour 2026 - Pōneke Wellington
events.venue: Embassy Theatre,10 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour 2026 - Pōneke Wellington
Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour presented by the NZ Alpine Club - Wellington Screenings

About this Event

The New Zealand Alpine Club with Presenting Partners, World Expeditions, La Sportiva, NZ Mountain Safety Council and Osprey, bring the Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour back to Pōneke Wellington this May and June!

Banff Centre Mountain Film and Book Festival is one of the largest and most prestigious mountain festivals in the world! Hot on the heels of the Festival that is held every fall in beautiful Banff, Alberta, the Banff Centre Mountain Film Festival World Tour hits the road. With stops planned in over 600 communities and more than 40 countries across the globe, the Banff World Tour celebrates amazing achievements in outdoor storytelling and filmmaking worldwide.

From the over 400 entries submitted to the Festival competition each year, award-winners and audience favourites are among the films that are carefully selected to play in theatres around the world. Travelling to remote vistas, analysing topical environmental issues, and bringing audiences up-close and personal with adrenaline-packed action sports, the 2025/2026 World Tour is an exhilarating and provocative exploration of the mountain world.
Be moved. Be inspired. Don’t miss out. Reserve your tickets today. For more information visit and follow our Facebook page for updates.
This screening is a fundraiser for the New Zealand Alpine Club.


PROGRAMME
We will be screening two programmes, Ruapehu and Tasman. over 4 nights. All screenings start at 5.30pm with doors opening at 5:00pm.


TICKETS
Tickets prices are;
Single Screening General Admission - $30
Single Screening NZAC member / Youth / Student - $25
Double Screening General Admission - $55
Limited stock of select double screening tickets available. Once double screening tickets are sold out, individual tickets to each screening will need to be purchased.
Please make sure you have selected the right dates/locations/quantities to suit your needs. No refunds are available. More details are available here: https://banff.nz/faqs/
On the day please bring your ticket and ID or NZAC membership card to show on entry if applicable. You can show your ticket digitally via your smartphone/device, or print your ticket off from this site. Double Screening Pass tickets will need to be brought along to each screening.
For more information visit https://banff.nz/.

From the over 400 entries submitted to the Festival competition each year, award-winners and audience favourites are among the films that are carefully selected to play in theatres around the world.

events.id: Night Photography - In the Dark-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Night Photography - In the Dark
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Night Photography - In the Dark
This course gives you the opportunity to explore and sharpen both your technical and creative skills, so you have the ability and vision to capture the enchanting world that resides in the darkness.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Identify the equipment needed to take photographs at night
• Learn fundamentals of photography composition and editing of night images
• Identify how to use technical photographic tools in a night time context
• Explore techniques that allow you to develop a more creative approach to picture making including: light painting, intentional camera movement and light trails
• Recognise the importance of light and colour in night photography and develop your ability to use it to make dynamic images
Course includes a couple of evening field trips (tutor will advise of suitable dates once course is under way).
You must bring a tripod and, if you have one, a neutral density filter and cable release remote. You need a fully manual DSLR or mirrorless camera and an intermediate understanding of camera functions. Not suitable for point and shoot cameras.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Bear’s Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Bear’s Den New Zealand 2026 | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
British country and indie-folk band Bear’s Den are coming to play their first ever headline shows in Aotearoa. They’ll perform at Auckland’s Tuning Fork on Tuesday 12 May and San Fran Wellington on Wednesday 13 May. Tickets go on sale this Thursday 26 March at midday

Formed in London 2012, Bear’s Den have carved out a global reputation and devoted following for over a decade. Their debut album Islands was certified gold, with the hit single ‘Above The Clouds of Pompeii’ nominated for the Ivor Novello Award for Best Song musically and lyrically. Since then, they’ve created four critically acclaimed studio albums, a collaborative project with screenwriter Paul Frith and a soundtrack album to Season 3 of Apple TV Series Trying. 
Their emotive story telling and cinematic sound has made up a catalogue that translates into film and onto live stages, taking them to some of the world’s biggest arenas and festivals, including Reading and Leeds, Glastonbury Festival, Pukkelpop, Lowlands and Bonnaroo. 

Consisting of founding member Andrew Davie on vocals and guitar, Christof van der Ven (banjo, guitar, vocals), Marcus Hamblett (synths, horns), and Jools Owen (drums, horns), the band have been on the road celebrating the 10 year anniversary of Islands for the past year and they’re now set to bring it all to New Zealand for the first time. 


Tues 12 May 
Tuning Fork | Auckland 

Wednesday 13 May 
San Fran | Wellington 

Follow Bear’s Den 

Website | Instagram | Facebook | Spotify | Apple Music | TikTok

British country and indie-folk band Bear’s Den are coming to play their first ever headline shows in Aotearoa.
They’ll perform at Auckland’s Tuning Fork on Tuesday 12 May and San Fran Wellington on Wednesday 13 May.
Tickets go on sale this Thursday 26 March at midday Formed in London 2012, Bear’s Den have carved out a global reputation and devoted following for over a decade.
Their debut album Islands was certified gold, with the hit single ‘Above The Clouds of Pompeii’ nominated for the Ivor Novello Award for Best Song musically and lyrically.
Since then, they’ve created four critically acclaimed studio albums, a collaborative project with screenwriter Paul Frith and a soundtrack album to Season 3 of Apple TV Series Trying.

events.long_description:
Hutt City Night Market - 14 May 2026
Experience the sights, smells, and flavors of a traditional night market right in the heart of Lower Hutt this autumn. 🍂

The Hutt City Night Market is an international celebration of food, bringing together a delicious mix of cuisines from around the world.

Stroll through bustling stalls, grab a cozy bite (or three), bring your friends and family, stay for a picnic, and enjoy the crisp autumn air in a vibrant community setting.

🕓 4pm – 8pm
🌧️ Every Thursday night, rain or shine (unless there is a thunderstorm or severe weather, in which case the market will be cancelled)
📍 Dowse Square, 45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt (opposite HCC building and next to Dowse Museum)

The food trucks attending each week will be announced by Tuesday, so keep an eye out to see if your favorite is rolling in!

👉 Like and follow Wellington Street Eats on Facebook & Instagram for all updates:
@wellingtonstreeteats

See you there under the autumn skies! 🍁

Pink Shirt Day Morning Tea at Q Space
Join us for a pink-themed morning tea to celebrate Pink Shirt Day. Wear pink, enjoy a treat, and help us spread kindness around the space.

About this Event

We’re celebrating Pink Shirt Day at Q Space with a fun, pink-themed morning tea 💗
Pink Shirt Day is all about standing together against bullying and promoting kindness, inclusion, and respect — and we’d love for our members to join in.
Wear a pink shirt (or anything pink!) and pop in for a morning treat. We’ll also have a few small engagement activities around the space, including compliment cards and posters to help spread positive vibes throughout the day.
A simple way to show support, connect with others, and brighten the workplace.
What to expect:
💗 Pink-themed morning tea treats
☕ Tea & coffee as always
👚 Wear pink and join in
😊 Compliment cards + small activities to spread kindness

events.long_description:
Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show in Lower Hutt: Fun for All Ages!
Experience the Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show Where You Become the Star of the Show!

About this Event

As seen on Seven Sharp (TVNZ) and going viral on TikTok, you’re in for a treat! Comedy Hypnotist Haiming Jiang is bringing his hit Comedy Hypnosis Show to your area. Get ready to laugh hysterically – and even join in, if you're up for it! It’s a night of unforgettable fun.
Haiming blends hypnosis and comedy to entertain the audience while showcasing the power and benefits of hypnosis through live audience participation. With his unique cultural background and deep understanding of both Western and Oriental cultures, this is a must-see event for anyone curious about hypnosis and keen for a good laugh.
Come and discover how this talented Chinese hypnotist uses hypnosis to both entertain crowds and deeply relax participants.
Experience the hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show – where you could become the star of the night! (Only if you’re keen – no pressure!)

Aussie icons Regurgitator are bringing their JUKEBOXXIN’ 2026 Retrospective Singles Tour to NZ!

A full-throttle dive through 30+ years of genre-bending chaos, nostalgia and fizzers.

Expect a stacked hit parade including Polyester Girl, Black Bugs, The Song Formerly Known As, Kong Foo Sing and more — all delivered with Regurgitator’s trademark absurdist energy and electro-punk punch. A two-hour sugar rush from one of the most inventive live bands around.

12/05 - Auckland – Double Whammy
14/05 - Raglan – Harbour View Hotel
15/05 - Wellington – Meow
16/05 - Christchurch – Loons
17/05 - Dunedin - Pearl Diver

Joined in Auckland by punk mentalists CINDY and everywhere else by industrial punk legends Displeasure.

events.id: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Hayley Sproull — Sproull On the Prowl
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Multi-award-winning Kiwi comic, radio presenter and TV personality Hayley Sproull is back in her hometown. She has a brand-new show that’s not safe for on-air. In Sproull On the Prowl, the self-described walking tornado (over)shares about a wild 12 months. It features stifled farts, hall passes, and an exploration of the Indonesian bush with a cameo from Jesus. 
The Toi Whakaari graduate has come a long way since bursting onto the comedy scene in 2012 when she was named Best Newcomer at that year’s International Comedy Festival. She’s made herself a regular on our airwaves since then through her breakfast radio show and appearances on Taskmaster, 7 Days, Have You Been Paying Attention? and The Great Kiwi Bake Off. Make sure you leave the kids at home for what promises to be a wild night.

events.id: Jewellery Making Intensive - Beginners' Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making Intensive - Beginners' Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jewellery Making Intensive - Beginners' Workshop
Learn the essential skills and techniques needed to make your own creations. The morning will begin with simple design examples and techniques using specialist jewellers’ tools, working with copper or brass. In the afternoon you will form and craft your own ring from sterling silver.
 All materials included in course fee.  Please bring pen and paper and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.  Limited to nine participants. Please note you cannot be late for this one-day course, you must be there from the start of the course when health and safety matters are covered.

By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Create a silver band 
• Demonstrate techniques for working sterling silver 
• Perform metal forming, piercing and texturing skills 
• Use essential jewellery-making tools accurately

Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Your Life’s Purpose – Discover Your Emotional Needs and Core Values
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $49 with ID, all others pay $120).
Put in the hard work on this one day Saturday workshop and experience the benefits of self-knowledge. Let the emotional knowledge you gain inform not only how you live your life but also how you feel about living it. You’ll be asking the big questions about not only how you live your life, but also why. Discover the tools and scientifically based information to inform the exploration of your life’s purpose.
Consider your emotional needs and how they relate to personal meaning. You’ll unwrap the concepts of emotional nutrition and be shown how to be guided by your values and start to think of them as a compass directing the journey of your life. To do this you’ll identify your values hierarchy and learn about how this framework can inform how you live your life. Once you can apply a spirit of consistency to your self-identified larger purpose, as well as your needs, then your life’s purpose, emotional needs and core values, will align. It’s quite a transformational day!
Bring pen and paper.  Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch/snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Develop a Life Purpose Statement
Perform an Emotional Needs Self-Assessment
Elicit Your Core Values
Align your goals with your life purpose, emotional needs and core values
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
The ultimate class in practical cheese making!   Learn all you need to know to replicate making NZ style Brie and Queso Fresco at home.  You will be guided through each step of the cheesemaking process while making your very own round of Brie. Then, while the Brie is ripening and setting (don't worry, all terminology will be explained!), we will make Queso Fresco together.  
All ingredients are provided as are recipes and detailed handouts to take away with you. Bring an apron, a dishcloth, 3 or 4 tea towels, a pen to take notes and a 2 litre container and plastic re-sealable food bags to take your cheese home.
During the workshop, the following topics will also be covered: Milk and where to source it, hygiene and sanitation, equipment required and the origins of the cheeses you are about to make.  You will also receive comprehensive instruction on how to mature your Brie cheese over the next few weeks.
A small break will be provided and you will have the opportunity to purchase equipment and cheesemaking ingredients after the workshop should you wish to replicate the experience at home.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Make Brie and Queso Fresco
- Recall the best milk to use and where to source it from
- Name and identify what equipment and ingredients you need to make Brie and Queso Fresco
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Singapore Hawker Food
Love Singapore Hawker food? Come and discover how to recreate some of these dishes from local ingredients with Mei-Fen, who is passionate about Asian food. Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed.   NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Prepare a range of Singapore “hawker” style dishes which may Kueh Dadar, Mee Goreng and Laksa
• Follow the recipes provided and prepare them with supervision
• Prepare the recipes from local ingredients
• Identify retail outlets which supply the specialty ingredients required 
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique and Preparation
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Meet the demand for excellence in the job market head on. Learn how to utilise the STARR technique in the way that recruiters use it. That is, to define and seek out the skills and qualities of the best candidate. Master the STARR skills and feel confident in your next job interview.

Familiarise yourself with the skills needed to ace your next interview. Learn the STARR method as an approach to responding to behavioural based questions. Understand how to use concrete examples from your life as proof that you possess the experience and skills for the job you are applying for. You’ll develop the key interview skill of being able to share your accomplishments through real-life examples.

In this course, you are taught how to identify specific example situations that are unique to you. You’ll learn how to confidently talk about the tasks involved and describe how you completed the key task and met its challenges. We’ll also consider the context of jobs you are interviewing for and how to choose examples that best show the desired skills interviewers are seeking.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Develop a higher level of confidence in approaching job interviews.
- Identify the different components of the STARR acronym and apply them to the job interview context.
- Identify what behavioural based questions are and why recruiters use them.
- Successfully answer behavioural based interview questions by applying the STARR technique.
- Develop skills in using real life examples to show transferable skills in the workplace.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting the Most from ChatGPT
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $29 with ID, all others pays $120).
AI tools like ChatGPT are everywhere, but how do you get the best out of them? In this hands-on, phone-based workshop, Jon Bell will guide you through practical techniques to make ChatGPT a more useful, reliable partner in your work and life. Whether you’ve tried it a few times or use it regularly, you’ll learn how to write better prompts, refine responses, and avoid common pitfalls without needing any technical background.
We’ll cover real examples, answer your questions as they come up, and spend plenty of time experimenting together so you leave feeling confident and ready to put your new skills to use.
Please bring: A phone with the ChatGPT app installed, and enough battery to last the day.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
Write effective prompts that get clearer, more useful responses
Use ChatGPT for research, brainstorming, and creative work
Recognise the tool’s limitations (and how to work around them)
Apply practical strategies for using AI in your personal or professional projects
Confidently troubleshoot when ChatGPT gives unexpected or unhelpful answers
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Are you tired of staring at the same old, boring lamps in your home? Do you wish you could add a touch of personality or a burst of colour to your living space? Come along and learn how to make your very own drum-shaped lampshade – a classic style very much on trend at the moment

 Power BI is a powerful and accessible tool for data analysis and dashboarding, widely used across many industries. This beginner friendly course is designed to be practical and hands on, covering subjects such as:

events.id: Zine Making - Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Zine Making - Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zine Making - Introduction
In this workshop you will be guided in making a classic cut and fold 8-page zine from a standard A4 sheet of paper - this is a clever design you can easily photocopy and reproduce later! You will be introduced to a range of techniques and approaches such as cut and paste photos and text, collage and drawing on paper.
If you’re keen to make more zines and share them with the world, there's a broad and growing community of Zine-makers and traders, as well as the well-attended annual Wellington Zinefest!
Paper, newspapers, magazines and basic drawing materials will be provided. We recommend you bring along a sketch book and any favourite drawing materials, newspapers, magazines or other interesting paper material to cut up and collage with in class.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Cut and fold a classic 8-page zine format from a single sheet of paper
Brainstorm content ideas for your personalised zine publications
Arrange & compose text and images to fit a classic 8-page zine layout
Combine cut & paste magazine content with hand drawn/ written content
Create a draft zine of your personal interests
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Broken Rice with Grilled Pork Chop, Shredded Pork Skin & Egg Meatloaf-27 Hillary Court, Naenae, Lower Hutt 5011,27 Hillary Ct, Naenae, Lower Hutt 5011, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Broken Rice with Grilled Pork Chop, Shredded Pork Skin & Egg Meatloaf
events.venue: 27 Hillary Court, Naenae, Lower Hutt 5011,27 Hillary Ct, Naenae, Lower Hutt 5011, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Broken Rice with Grilled Pork Chop, Shredded Pork Skin & Egg Meatloaf
Join VHNZ Cuisine for a hands-on cooking workshop featuring Broken Rice with Grilled Pork Chop, Shredded Pork Skin & Egg Meatloaf (Cơm tấm sườn, bì, chả trứng) — one of southern Vietnam’s most loved comfort dishes.



In this session, you’ll learn how to prepare each key component: marinated grilled pork chop, fragrant shredded pork skin, soft savoury egg meatloaf, and perfectly cooked broken rice. We’ll also share the cultural story behind cơm tấm and why this humble dish holds such a special place in Vietnamese everyday life.



This is a relaxed, community-led workshop where you’ll cook together, learn traditional techniques, and enjoy a shared meal at the end. No prior cooking experience needed — just curiosity and an appetite.



To help keep these classes sustainable and support ongoing community activities, there will be an additional $10 contribution per class.

This helps cover:

• Facilitator effort and preparation

• Admin and coordination work

• The ability to continue running and expanding community workshops



This class is delivered in collaboration with I Love Viet Food, and made possible thanks to the generous support and sponsorship from:



* Hutt City Council

* E Tū Whānau

* Pomare Taita Trust

* Te Mako Community Centre

* Newlands Community Centre

In this session, you’ll learn how to prepare each key component: marinated grilled pork chop, fragrant shredded pork skin, soft savoury egg meatloaf, and perfectly cooked broken rice.

events.id: Social Sewing Saturday - May-Karori Arts & Crafts,7 Beauchamp Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Social Sewing Saturday - May
events.venue: Karori Arts & Crafts,7 Beauchamp Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Social Sewing Saturday - May
Come join us for a fun-filled day of sewing and socializing at Karori Arts & Crafts!

About this Event

Come join us for a fun-filled day of sewing and socializing at Karori Arts & Crafts!
Come join us for a fun-filled day of sewing and socializing at Karori Arts & Crafts! Whether you're a seasoned pro or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone who loves to sew. Bring your latest project or start something new - either way, you'll have a blast chatting with fellow sewing enthusiasts. Bring your current project or get inspired by others' creations at our friendly gathering. Share tips, tricks, and stories while working on our projects together.
Don't forget to pack your sewing machine, fabric, and any other supplies you'll need. We can't wait to see what you create! You will need to bring your own machine and materials. Please bring an extension lead and multipoint box if you have one. (Don't worry, though—we have some in the centre, too!) We'll supply an iron and ironing board to share, as well as a table and relatively comfy chair each. There are also spare cutting-out tables at a taller height. You are welcome to come along and work on hand-sewing projects.
A kitchen is available on site and supermarkets as well as cafes are very close by.Parking is free and there are 3 dedicated spaces for the centre as well as others closeby.
Cost: Free for members of the Karori Arts Centre or $15 per person payable directly to KACC. (Membership of the centre is $105 annually and also gives you reductions on classes and access to other groups)
Please do NOT attend if you are sick (even just a little bit!)

cbf with the apps but could be keen to meet new people?
CBK Social Club is for those who want to get out of the house, meet others, and have plans to look forward to. Join us for a afternoon of speed dating with relaxed short chats, genuine company, and a few laughs along the way. Your first drink’s on us.

events.long_description:
LIVE MUSIC - Samuel Cooper
Samuel Cooper is a Wellington-based solo pianist with a versatile repertoire spanning timeless classics from the 1950s through to today’s hits. Blending everything from Johnny Cash, Elton John, Oasis, and Coldplay to Ed Sheeran, alongside soothing jazz, blues, and original compositions, Samuel creates an elegant and memorable atmosphere for any occasion. A regular performer across Wellington’s hospitality and live music scene, he has brought his music to hotels, bars, retirement communities, and corporate events, always tailoring his sets to suit the mood and audience.

events.id: Kajun Brooking Me Being Me | Wellington | NZICF 26-Cavern Club,18 Allen Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Kajun Brooking Me Being Me | Wellington | NZICF 26
events.venue: Cavern Club,18 Allen Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kajun Brooking Me Being Me | Wellington | NZICF 26
ME BEING ME is a show from Māori comedian Kajun Brooking about what happens when you stop trying to please everyone and start being yourself.

He’s a husband, a father, a retail manager so he’s spent years arresting his thoughts. This show is what happens when your thoughts resist arrest.

Inspired by the humour of Billy, the charisma of Winston, and the hustle of Susan Paul, Kajun talks whanau, mahi, social media and the trouble you get into when you speak your mind in the comments section of the nzherald.

He used to play it safe.
Then he thought, “Fuck it.”

This is Me Being Me.

As seen on TikTok, Te Karere and The Hui. Kajun is a 7x award winning comedian that you need to look out for but not in a Police Ten 7 kind of way.

events.id: NZICF Presents STAMPTOWN (Wellington) | NZICF 26-Hannah Playhouse,12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: NZICF Presents STAMPTOWN (Wellington) | NZICF 26
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse,12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
NZICF Presents STAMPTOWN (Wellington) | NZICF 26
The New Zealand International Comedy Festival presents – STAMPTOWN

This is not a “sit nicely and chuckle” comedy show. Stamptown is a raunchy, chaotic, 90 minute spectacle featuring the most exciting alternative performance from around the world.

Hosted by bad boy of clown, Zach Zucker, Stamptown is a five-time New York Times Top Comedy Pick (2021-2025) and has packed out rooms at Netflix Is A Joke, Just For Laughs, Edinburgh Fringe, Glastonbury, SXSW, Lincoln Center, Soho Theatre, MICF, and Off-Broadway – basically, anywhere people like their comedy sweaty, surprising, and slightly unhinged.

You’re not just watching a show, you’re getting swept up in it. Think big laughs, big swings, and the kind of show you have to bring friends to.

★★★★★ “The past, present and future of live entertainment rolled into one magnificently anarchic package” – Chortle

★★★★★ "An absolutely insane night of comedy that is impossible to replicate, which is what makes it such an appealing show to come back to” – Broadway World

★★★★ "Winningly helter-skelter late-night entertainment" – Guardian

★★★★ "An atmosphere of barely controlled chaos... this is the late night cabaret to see" – Telegraph

★ “Run for your life if anyone suggests going to see this irredeemably unfunny show.” – Dick Bath, Scottish Field

You’re not just watching a show, you’re getting swept up in it.
Think big laughs, big swings, and the kind of show you have to bring friends to.

events.id: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: The Waterboys New Zealand Tour tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Destroy All Lines
THE WATERBOYS make their long-awaited return to New Zealand in May 2026 their first tour in over a decade!
Critically acclaimed as one of the best live bands on the planet, the 2025 band features double keyboard players Brother Paul from Memphis and James Hallawell from Cornwall and is grounded by killer Irish rhythm section Aongus Ralston (bass) and Eamon Ferris (drums).
THE WATERBOYS will be performing tracks from their audacious new album, Life, Death and Dennis Hopper: a bold, cinematic tribute to the maverick American actor and cultural icon, along with material across their storied catalogue. From the early "big music" of The Whole Of The Moon and classic album This Is The Sea through to the roots-inspired Fisherman's Blues era and music from their ambitious post-2015 output.
Mike Scott comments: "The Waterboys live for the experience of playing, of feeling our music evolve and grow with each consecutive concert. Nothing makes us happier than seeing a long list of dates like these. How will the music change? What will happen that's never happened before? How much fun and thrills can we and the audiences have? Australia and New Zealand come and join us!"

events.id: Mo Munn & Q Potts in DRIVING ME CRAZY || NZ Intl Comedy Festival-BATS Theatre,1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Mo Munn & Q Potts in DRIVING ME CRAZY || NZ Intl Comedy Festival
events.venue: BATS Theatre,1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mo Munn & Q Potts in DRIVING ME CRAZY || NZ Intl Comedy Festival
Driving Me Crazy is a no-brakes comedy collision starring real-life married comedy couple Mo Munn and Q Potts. This fast-paced, unfiltered blend of stand-up and sketch dives head-on into the chaos of co-piloting a life - and a car - with your partner. Expect rapid-fire banter, brutally relatable stories, and sketches that hit a little too close to home. Mo and Q are living proof that marriage is messy, weird, and completely worth mocking on stage.

Mo Munn is a multi-award-winning comedian, recently winning Best Newcomer Wellington at the 2025 NZ International Comedy Festival and Best Joke at the 2025 Wellington Comedy Awards. Q Potts is a rising star on the scene, and a 2025 Wellington Raw Comedy Quest Finalist.

"Driving Me Crazy takes us on a hilarious and hair-raising ride and has the Fringe Bar buzzing." - Theatreview

"Driving Me Crazy is a wonderful mix of sketch and stand-up that has the most competent execution of marriage comedy I’ve ever seen." - Wellyott

Hutt City Night Market - 21 May 2026
Experience the sights, smells, and flavors of a traditional night market right in the heart of Lower Hutt this autumn. 🍂

The Hutt City Night Market is an international celebration of food, bringing together a delicious mix of cuisines from around the world.

Stroll through bustling stalls, grab a cozy bite (or three), bring your friends and family, stay for a picnic, and enjoy the crisp autumn air in a vibrant community setting.

🕓 4pm – 8pm
🌧️ Every Thursday night, rain or shine (unless there is a thunderstorm or severe weather, in which case the market will be cancelled)
📍 Dowse Square, 45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt (opposite HCC building and next to Dowse Museum)

The food trucks attending each week will be announced by Tuesday, so keep an eye out to see if your favorite is rolling in!

👉 Like and follow Wellington Street Eats on Facebook & Instagram for all updates:
@wellingtonstreeteats

See you there under the autumn skies! 🍁

events.id: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Tommy Emmanuel CGP — Living In The Light Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He’s a world-renowned guitarist and master entertainer. Tommy Emmanuel, CGP, is bringing his beloved Maton guitar to The Opera House stage in Wellington. He is considered a living legend in the acoustic guitar world. The Australian musical legend has enthralled audiences around the globe with his dazzling technique. The concert promises to be an inspiring journey as Tommy strums and plucks his way through jazz, rock, country and pop music. Fans will be captivated by the diverse musical landscape he creates. 
Since his career began in the 1960s, Tommy has toured with luminaries like Eric Clapton and John Denver. He has won a Grammy Award and performed for a televised audience of more than two billion at the 2000 Sydney Olympics. Collaborations include guitar greats like Les Paul, Mark Knopfler, Joe Walsh, and Richard Thompson. 
Tommy’s hero, legendary guitarist Chet Atkins, bestowed the title of Certified Guitar Player (CGP) on him. He is one of only four players in the world with the title. Tommy Emmanuel CGP continues to push the boundaries of what is possible on the acoustic guitar.

events.id: Tommy Emmanuel CGP - Living In The Light Tour | Opera House, Wellington-Opera House-All Events In
events.name: Tommy Emmanuel CGP - Living In The Light Tour | Opera House, Wellington
events.venue: Opera House
events.long_description:
Tommy Emmanuel CGP - Living In The Light Tour | Opera House, Wellington
Tommy Emmanuel CGP - Living In The Light Tour
NEW ZEALAND 2026

Special guest: Mike Dawes

World-renowned guitarist and master entertainer TOMMY EMMANUEL, CGP is thrilled to announce what is sure to be an unforgettable tour of New Zealand in May 2026.

The five-date tour takes in Auckland, Hamilton, Palmerston North, Wellington and Christchurch.

TOMMY EMMANUEL, CGP is a living legend in the world of acoustic guitar, celebrated for his jaw- dropping technique, innovation, and boundless musicality. He has enthralled audiences around the globe with his dazzling fingerstyle performances, and now, New Zealand will have the privilege of witnessing his mastery up close and personal.

“I feel like the Indiana Jones of the guitar world,” says the globe-trotting fingerpicker. “I’m on this wild journey, just making it up as I go, and I’m thrilled that so many people are having fun joining along with me.”

TOMMY EMMANUEL Hailed as “one of the best acoustic guitarists in the world” by NPR’s World Cafe, Tommy Emmanuel got his start at the age of six, when he first began touring his native Australia with his family’s band. As a teenager, he earned a reputation as a highly sought after sideman and session player, and by his early twenties, Emmanuel was playing on chart-topping hits and performing with acts like Air Supply and Men at Work.

TOMMY EMMANUEL, CGP, Living in the Light Tour

Sunday 17 May - Town Hall, Auckland
Tuesday 19 May - BNZ Theatre, Hamilton
Thursday 21 May - Regent Theatre, Palmerston North
Friday 22 May - Opera House, Wellington
Saturday 23 May - Isaac Theatre, Christchurch

events.id: Wednesday 'Bleeds' NZ Tour - Wellington-San Fran,Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Wednesday 'Bleeds' NZ Tour - Wellington
events.venue: San Fran,Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wednesday 'Bleeds' NZ Tour - Wellington
BANISHED MUSIC & STRANGE NEWS PRESENT
WEDNESDAY ‘BLEEDS’ NZ TOUR

FRIDAY 22 MAY - SAN FRAN, WELLINGTON
SATURDAY 23 MAY - LOONS, LYTTELTON
SUNDAY 24 MAY - HOLLYWOOD AVONDALE, AUCKLAND

When North Carolina’s noise makers Wednesday first visited Aotearoa off the back of their album RAT SAW GOD, audiences were taken by surprise in the best possible way. A 95bFM review called the band’s Whammy show “a sold out countrygaze extravaganza”, and everyone who came left with big hearts and even bigger smiles.

Now, with the band’s unbelievable sixth album ‘Bleeds’ out in the world, it’s high time that Banished Music and Strange News had their fun pals from Asheville down for a repeat visit, with three shows across Wellington, Lyttelton, and Auckland happening this May.

Bleeds is the album the band are calling “not only the best Wednesday record—it’s also the most Wednesday record”, with all the elements that make the Southern rock magic of this rare group so special. NME put it beautifully in their five-star review of the album, heaping well deserved praise on Wednesday’s “blown-out distortion, weirdo-country slide guitar and serrated melodies”.

Fronted and founded by the fierce and fun Karly Hartzman, whose songs are at once confessional and crack-up, Wednesday have built a reputation for being one of the best - and most dynamic - live bands on the circuit. Joining Karly will be the inimitable and animated Xandy Chelmis on lap steel and pedal steel, beat-keeper extraordinaire Alan Miller on drums, brilliant bassist Ethan Baechtold, and the band’s scorching touring guitarist Jake Pugh.

If you caught them last time, there’s no way you’re missing this. If you missed them the first time, it’s not too late to join the cult. Good times guaranteed.

“There's an ever-present joy, even during the most harrowing songs… this is catharsis personified” - Exclaim!

events.id: STAMPTOWN Presents Jack Tucker (Wellington) | NZICF 26-Hannah Playhouse,12 Cambridge Ter, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: STAMPTOWN Presents Jack Tucker (Wellington) | NZICF 26
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse,12 Cambridge Ter, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
STAMPTOWN Presents Jack Tucker (Wellington) | NZICF 26
The New Zealand International Comedy Festival & STAMPTOWN presents – Jack Tucker: Comedy Standup Hour

After sold-out shows in London, Edinburgh, Australia, LA, Mumbai, and a 10 week Off-Broadway run in New York, the bad boy of clown, Zach Zucker, is bringing his award-winning high-energy, absurdist, standup alter-ego: Jack Tucker to Aotearoa for the first time ever.

Is it stand-up? Kind of. Is it going well? Absolutely not. Is that the point? Only one way to find out.

This show has sold out at The Kennedy Center, Soho Theatre, Just For Laughs, Netflix Is A Joke, Moontower Festival, SF Sketchfest, Melbourne International Comedy Festival and many more.
Come see what happens when unstoppable confidence meets questionable material, technical chaos, and a performer who learned how to be funny by dropping rocks on his nuts for a laugh.

Created and Performed by Zach Zucker
Directed by Jonny Woolley
Written by Zach Zucker, Jonny Woolley, Dylan Woodley

NY Times Critic’s Pick (2024)

Vulture’s Top Comics You Should.& Will Know (2023)

★★★★★ "A rip-roaring hour of top-notch clowning" - Edinburgh Festivals
★★★★1⁄2 “A raucously funny 60 minute” - Steve Bennett, Chortle
★★★★ “The character is not remotely believable” - Beyond the Joke
★★★ “Washed up” - Guardian
★★ “Terrible comic” - The Times
★ “A fraud completely out of his depth” - Skinny
“Raucously funny… A triumph” - Jason Zinoman, New York Times

events.id: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders — Tā te Manawa Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Michael Fowler Centre is set for two visits by the alt-country/folk musician and his band, The Yarra Benders. The tour is called Tā te Manawa — it literally means ‘heart at rest’. This references Marlon’s wish to take a break after a particularly busy and productive few years. 
Fans will get two sets of songs across the evening. The first comprises songs from Marlon’s latest album ‘Te Whare Tīwekaweka’. The second delves into repertoire from his first album and bits of everything in between. Marlon explains the reason for the break, “For nearly 20 years I’ve explored both the physical and musical world in the company of incredible musicians, songwriters and friends. The catch with it is — it can be easy to overlook the toll it can take over time, on body and soul.” Fair not though, this is only a hiatus. Marlon describes it as a cheerful hurrah, “a see you soon of sorts.”

events.id: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Rock Tenors — Anthems Reloaded
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for some of the biggest anthems in the rock world. Rock Tenors roar into The Opera House in Wellington for a night of big tunes and big voices. The Anthems Reloaded Tour celebrates key moments in the Kiwi group’s musical journey. 
The five members of Rock Tenors have wide experience. They cross multiple music genres from opera and musical theatre to contemporary rock and thrash metal. They’ve reinvented the standard rock vocal arrangements with harmonic complexity, so audiences will get a dose of skilled musicality as well as nostalgia. You’ll hear The Rock Tenors’ signature renditions of legendary anthems from bands stretching back to the 60s. Hear Queen, The Animals, Bon Jovi, Aerosmith, AC/DC, Black Sabbath, Iron Maiden, White Snake, KISS, Guns N’ Roses, Red Hot Chilli Peppers, U2, and more. 
The touring ensemble of James Adams, Luke Butson, Ben Hayward, Steve Jones, and Justin Wilson are some of New Zealand’s premier vocalists. Since forming in 2022, they have taken the South Island by storm. They have become the must-see act for their vocal mastery and high-voltage rock singing.

events.id: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop
If you don’t already know who Bob Ross is then Google him, and you will be amazed at how he creates fantastic paintings using simple tools.  You can too! 
Please wear old clothes and bring your lunch.  You'll go home with an oil painting completed 100% by yourself.
All materials are included in course fee –  a 16x20 inch professional canvas, Bob Ross oil paints, brushes, and all equipment, as seen on NZ TV and YouTube.
This is a unique technique of wet-into-wet oil painting, which is ideal for those who have never painted before, as well as more experienced artists.
Please make sure you have a method to transport your wet oil painting home at the end of day.  Each workshop focuses on doing a specific set landscape painting - the whole class group does the same painting.
(Please note there is no wheelchair access to this venue - held at NZ Art School studio at 61 Seaview Road, Lower Hutt).
Painting for 17 May
Painting for 30 August
Painting for 15 November
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Microsoft Excel - Beginners
Learn how to create and use spreadsheets, as well as identify elements of a work book, enter and edit data, generate basic calculations and use built-in spreadsheet functions.
Windows skills essential - this is not a course for absolute beginners to computing.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course, you'll have had a basic introduction to using MS Excel, including how to:
• Identify elements of a workbook
• Page setup and printing
• Enter and edit data and basic formatting
• Generate basic calculations
• Use cell references and built-in functions 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).
By the end of the course, you'll have had a basic introduction to using MS Excel, including how to: • Identify elements of a workbook • Page setup and printing • Enter and edit data and basic formatting • Generate basic calculations • Use cell references and built-in functions Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel.
We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly.

events.id: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: Strategy, Skills and Self-Awareness
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Equip yourself with the tools and strategies to thrive in today’s competitive job market. This course is designed to help job seekers at all stages across any industry. Whether you're applying for your first job, changing careers, or re-entering the workforce, this course is relevant for everyone because it requires you to self-reflect on your own journey. You’ll learn to reflect deeply on your goals and strengths while building practical skills to stand out from the crowd.
 You’ll leave with a set of practical tools, clear strategies, and a better understanding of what employers want, so you can step into your next opportunity with confidence.
 You’ll develop a tailored job search strategy that works for you. One that has purpose, highlights your transferable skills, sharpens your professional image, and helps you connect authentically with hiring managers. From crafting a digital-ready CV and setting job alerts, to working with recruiters and networking effectively, you’ll gain tools and insights to streamline the process.
 The course also addresses the emotional journey of job hunting. Learn to manage time and energy, stay adaptable, and build resilience as you face the highs and lows of searching for the right opportunity. You’ll explore how emotional intelligence, digital literacy, and critical thinking play essential roles in first impressions, interviews, and long-term career decisions.
 By the end of this course, you’ll be able to:
Identify and articulate your transferable skills.
Apply both emotional and intellectual strategies to mentor and coach yourself from within.
Develop a job search strategy that aligns with your goals.
Build confidence in presenting yourself professionally and authentically.
Develop a confident, authentic approach to your personal branding and professional networking.
Create and manage a job search plan using shortcuts like job alerts, recruitment agents, and focussed applications.
Maintain a polished digital presence and decide whether the use of professional platforms are for you (LinkedIn).
Understand how to navigate the emotional demands of job hunting with self-compassion, motivation, and adaptability.
Build and maintain a network of trusted referees who reflect your work ethic and potential.
 Whether you're starting from scratch or just need a refresh, this course offers a structured, encouraging path forward to help you land your next opportunity.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
Join us for a fun day of creature making! Design and sculpt a one-of-a-kind character in your own style from polymer clays in a full spectrum of colours!

Working with oven-cured clays and wrapped wire armatures, this workshop offers a variety of modelling methods and approaches - a great opportunity to turn sketches & ideas into tangible 3D forms. Modern clay inventions mean we can create translucent, glittery, semi-flexible, UV reactive or even glow-in-the-dark elements to add more realism or a touch of the surreal into each creation!

Students will be coached through designing and creating a small-scale creature sculpture, beginning simple, and building to a more complex final form as we get familiar with materials.

The class offers beginner students a fun introduction to sculpting organic forms from scratch, and for those with some hands-on creative experience, it offers a great opportunity to experiment, hone and advance your making skills to new levels.

If you can, bring along some design ideas, sketches or reference images to help kick start the sculpting process on the day.

Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if you have them.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Women's Health and Aging Workshop - Johnsonville Community Hall
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
An interactive workshop presented by experienced physiotherapist Sara Dore, focusing on how movement and function can be challenging for women as they age. Particularly for women, there is more to the core than abdominal muscles and strength and we explore these broader aspects.  Most importantly, you will come to understand that movement is medicine and we can make a difference.
You will be able to make a positive difference for yourself! The objective of this workshop is to give you knowledge and understanding about how you can manage the effects on your health and wellbeing as we age as women.  Knowledge is power!
In this one-day workshop you will discover:
- The base of the pelvic floor. Anatomy of the pelvic floor, and the common conditions urinary incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse will be discussed. Education on basic pelvic floor advice and management as well as appropriate exercises will also be covered.
- The middle deep abdominal muscles. Trunk stability versus core strength as we age and the impact on our other core muscles and pelvic floor will be discussed. The principles of Clinical Pilates versus traditional Pilates will be presented together with the key principles of movement is medicine.
-The importance of the diaphragm and breathing well will also be discussed to enhance optimal movement and function for women.
Please wear comfortable clothes and socks as this is an interactive workshop, and bring a yoga mat or beach towel.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Writing Short Stories
In this one-day course, we’ll explore the elements that make short stories shine, from creating compelling character dynamics, to choosing the point of view that best serves your story.
Through a mix of discussions and practical writing exercises, participants will leave the course with all the tools they need to write engaging short stories.
Please bring pen and paper, and the name of a short story you like.  Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring your lunch and snacks.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Identify elements that make short stories memorable
- Create compelling characters and relationship dynamics
- Write using different points of view
- Explore using symbolism in short fiction
- Use detail to develop setting and character traits
- Identify places to send your completed short stories
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Marlon Williams Tā te Manawa Tour | Wellington (SOLD OUT)
Marlon Williams has today announced a very significant tour. It’s one that is truly for fans, and one that also marks a temporary departure from the stage for Marlon himself.

After an intensive time making latest album Te Whare Tīwekaweka and over two years filming the Ngā Ao E Rua - Two Worlds (2025) documentary Marlon has decided to take a sabbatical from the spotlight.

Rest and rejuvenation are on the horizon, but not before kiwi fans get to see him live for a cheerful hurrah, a see you soon of sorts…

Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders are warming up to bring it all home.

This is a heartfelt coming together of fans and whanau for Marlon. He invites you to travel through his musical repertoire to date and be in the room before he takes a step back from the stage…. for now.

Tickets go on sale Tuesday 24 Feb, midday.

events.id: Eddie Izzard — The Remix: The First 35 Years-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Eddie Izzard — The Remix: The First 35 Years
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Eddie’s 35 years of sell-out comedy shows stretch from ‘The Ambassadors’ in 1993 to ‘Wunderbar’ in 2019.
Ever wondered what became of Darth Vader and Mr Stevens or the monkey in the tree, did the pears ever ripen… and will God ever appear? It’s a glorious chance to ‘maybe’ find out the answers or discover more questions to those Izzard conundrums. It’s highly likely that no night will be exactly the same, so as ever with Eddie, expect the unexpected.
“In the first 35 years of my stand-up career, I came up with many weird and crazy comedy stories. The ones I like the best will be in my 2025 live Remix Tour,“ says Eddie.
This show promises to be a beautiful, hilarious journey spanning a 35-year career of thought-provoking, intelligent and surreal ideas. Who could resist?
This show may contain strong and imaginative language.

events.id: Eddie Izzard | Wellington-Michael Fowler Centre-All Events In
events.name: Eddie Izzard | Wellington
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Eddie Izzard | Wellington
Eddie Izzard –The Remix Tour Live is a chance for Eddie, inspired by her ever loyal
audiences to remix and re-imagine some of her own favourite, personal comedy
highlights. Eddie’s 35 years of sell- out comedy shows stretch from “The Ambassadors” in
1993 to “Wunderbar” in 2019. Ever wondered what became of Darth Vader and Mr Stevens or the monkey in the tree, did the pears ever ripen… and will God ever appear? It’s a glorious chance to ‘maybe’ find out the answers or discover more questions to those Izzard
conundrums. It’s highly likely that no night will be exactly the same so as ever with
Eddie, expect the unexpected.

“In the first 35 years of my standup career I came up with many weird and crazy
comedy stories. The ones I like the best will be in my 2025 live Remix Tour. “
Eddie Izzard

This show promises to be a beautiful, hilarious journey spanning a 35-year career of
thought provoking, intelligent and surreal ideas. Who could resist?

events.id: Contract Law Fundamentals for Non Lawyers Masterclass Wellington-CBD Wellington,Level 28/100 Willis Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Contract Law Fundamentals for Non Lawyers Masterclass Wellington
events.venue: CBD Wellington,Level 28/100 Willis Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Contract Law Fundamentals for Non Lawyers Masterclass Wellington
Register Today by contacting Crown Leadership International Group: Website: www.crownleadership.com.au Phone: +64 9 889 7217

About this Event

Contract Law Fundamentals for Non Lawyers Masterclass is an intensely practical guide through the essentials of contract law, exploring such details as when a contract will be required, when you have a contract (and don’t necessarily know it), what types of contract exist and the effect of legislation on your contractual arrangements.
This masterclass also scrutinises what happens when things go wrong, including how you can get out of a contract, options for assessing damages and remedies for breach and looking at the contractual implications of working in an e-commerce environment. You will grasp the crucial ingredients required for a good contract, and gain tips to better understand and manage your contracts.

events.id: Calligratherapy - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Calligratherapy - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Calligratherapy - Beginners
Many of us live sedentary lifestyles, sitting for up to eight hours or more a day hunched over our keyboard - all the while rarely taking a proper breath! Even if we wanted to take a deep enriching breath into the lower lobes of our lungs, we are limited due to our posture. Often due to the stresses and strains of a busy work and life balance, our unconscious brain can develop a pattern of holding, irregular and shadow breaths.
Does this sound like your experience? 
Not being able to breathe properly is the key cause of many chronic respiratory diseases (Nestor, 2020)
Calligratherapy combines science journalist James Nestor's theory on proper breathing with the philosophy of ancient Chinese calligraphy to promote a state of optimal wellbeing.
Calligratherapy has been developed specifically for those who have never practiced calligraphy before and who are interested in deriving the benefits without having to learn the language or all the characters. It integrates the simple and intuitive act of inhaling and exhaling into calligraphy practice with Eastern philosophy behind it.
A range of different breathing exercises are embedded into each 90 minute session focusing on different calligraphy scripts.  Over the five weeks, you will develop and practice the awareness of healthy breathing, while learning new techniques of Chinese soft brush calligraphy covering five different scripts. 
All materials included in course fee.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Start a new creative practice in Chinese soft brush calligraphy
Develop your own mindfulness strategy
Better manage everyday stress levels and access a calm, empowered and positive state of mind
Find ways to explore eastern philosophy and develop deeper connections with your spiritual self. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Heathers the Musical-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Heathers the Musical
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s Westerberg High, where popularity is a matter of life and death. Welcome to “Heathers the Musical”. Based on a movie with the same name, the show is a coming-of-age story dealing with love, heartache, defying expectations, and being true to yourself. It originally premiered in Los Angeles in 2013. Since then, the musical has had record-breaking seasons across the globe. It now makes its professional New Zealand debut in 2026, and Wellington is in line to join the school assembly. Described as a high-octane, black comedy rock musical, Heathers is packed with a mix of ballads and showstoppers. The catchy tunes are written by Kevin Murphy (Desperate Housewives) and Laurence O’Keefe (Legally Blonde: The Musical). 
The 1989 film starred Winona Ryder and Christian Slater. It quickly achieved cult status among audiences. Both the movie and musical follow high school student Veronica Sawyer. Veronica is just another nobody until she’s unexpectedly taken under the wings of the beautiful and cruel Heathers. When mysterious teen rebel J.D. arrives, Veronica learns that it might kill to be a nobody, but it’s murder being a somebody. 
This special production will feature a stellar Australasian cast. So grab your scrunchies, break out the Corn Nuts and wait for the bell to ring.

events.id: Ireland – The Voyage-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Ireland – The Voyage
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Breathtaking visuals and voices, cinematic soundscapes, and the fiery passion of dance dominate the St James Theatre stage in May. Ireland – The Voyage is a powerful new theatrical experience that invites you to feel the pulse of a nation. Ireland’s history, heart, and heritage is brought to life through authentic voices, live music, and world-class Irish dance.
The shows spans centuries of storytelling. Take in the mysticism of the Celts, the thunder of Viking invasions, the arrival of the Normans, and the spiritual transformation shaped by St Patrick. Leading the vocal performances is Giselle O’Meara, whose soaring, soulful voice carries the emotion of Ireland’s story from ancient times to today. She is joined on stage by musicians performing on traditional instruments. Together their sound forms the beating heart of the show. It’s raw, real, and unmistakably Irish.

events.id: Wainuiomata Rugby Football Club 80th Jubilee-Wainuiomata Rugby Football Club Inc,2 Moohan Street, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Wainuiomata Rugby Football Club 80th Jubilee
events.venue: Wainuiomata Rugby Football Club Inc,2 Moohan Street, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wainuiomata Rugby Football Club 80th Jubilee
Celebrate 80 years of Wainuiomata Rugby with mates, memories, and good times on the field!

About this Event


Wainuiomata Rugby Football Club 80th Jubilee
Join us in person to celebrate the Wainuiomata Rugby Football Club's 80th Jubilee! It’s going to be a fantastic weekend full of memories, mates, and rugby. Whether you’re a past player, a supporter, or part of our wider Wainui Rugby Community, come along and be part of this special milestone. Don’t miss out on the fun and camaraderie – let’s make it a weekend to remember!


Agenda


🕑: 09:00 AM - 02:30 PM
Friday: Ambrose Golf Tournament
Info: Join us at the Wainuiomata Golf Club, 334 Coast Road Wainuiomata, on Friday 29 May 2026 for a round of golf as we open our 80th Jubilee celebration weekend.


🕑: 11:00 AM - 02:00 PM
Friday: High Tea
Info: Join us at the Wainuiomata Rugby Club, 2 Moohan Street Wainuiomata, on Friday 29 May 2026 for a high tea as we open our 80th Jubilee celebration weekend. Light refreshments provided.


🕑: 03:30 PM - 10:30 PM
Friday: Meet and Greet
Info: Join us on Friday 29 May 2026 for our official opening event with a meet and greet at the Wainuiomata Rugby Football Clubrooms, 2 Moohan Street Wainuiomata. Light refreshments provided by Fine Touch Catering.


🕑: 09:00 AM - 11:00 PM
Saturday: Club Day
Info: Join us at William Jones park on Saturday 30 May 2026 for a day of rugby. We will kick off the day with our junior club from 9:00am and conclude with our Premier team taking on Tawa from 2:45pm. Post match formalities and entertainment at the clubrooms from 5:30pm.


🕑: 05:00 PM - 10:00 PM
Sunday: 80th Jubilee Dinner
Info: Join us for our formal 80th Jubilee Dinner on Sunday 31 May 2026 at the Wainuiomata Rugby Football Clubrooms. We will conclude our 80th Jubilee Celebration weekend with a rugby themed Q&A hosted by Fauono Ken Laban, Mayor of Lower Hutt City and special guests. Dinner provided by Fine Touch Catering.

events.id: Marlon Williams Tā Te Manawa Tour | Wellington (Second Show)-Michael Fowler Centre-All Events In
events.name: Marlon Williams Tā Te Manawa Tour | Wellington (Second Show)
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Marlon Williams Tā Te Manawa Tour | Wellington (Second Show)
Marlon Williams has today announced a very significant tour. It’s one that is truly for fans, and one that also marks a temporary departure from the stage for Marlon himself.

After an intensive time making latest album Te Whare Tīwekaweka and over two years filming the Ngā Ao E Rua - Two Worlds (2025) documentary Marlon has decided to take a sabbatical from the spotlight.

Rest and rejuvenation are on the horizon, but not before kiwi fans get to see him live for a cheerful hurrah, a see you soon of sorts…

Marlon Williams and The Yarra Benders are warming up to bring it all home.

This is a heartfelt coming together of fans and whanau for Marlon. He invites you to travel through his musical repertoire to date and be in the room before he takes a step back from the stage…. for now.

Tickets go on sale Tuesday 24 Feb, 2pm.
