With the overarching theme of ‘collaborations’, Orchestra Wellington is looking forward to bringing people together with their 2026 concerts. A dazzling array of musicians, all at the top of their game, will collaborate with the local orchestra. These include the New Zealand String Quartet, NZTrio, Ballet Collective Aotearoa, four brass bands, and many choirs and soloists. 
The orchestra is not just going big with the number of performers, but with the sheer scale of the works presented. Wellington audiences will get to hear Leonard Bernstein’s Chichester Psalms, Hector Berlioz’s Requiem Grand Messe des Morts, and Carl Nielsen’s Symphony 4.

events.long_description:
Palmerston North Brick Show 2026
Get ready for the ultimate LEGO® extravaganza at the 2026 Palmy Brick Show – it's going to be HUGE!!!

The Wellington LEGO® User Group (Well-LUG) is excited to announce that the Palmerston North Brick Show is back for 2026!

Once again, we are holding the show over King's Birthday weekend so be sure to BLOCK OUT YOUR WEEKEND and mark the 30th and 31st May in your calendar and come along to see the amazing LEGO® creations from some of NZ’s best builders.

This year the show is being held at Arena 3, and we will have amazing LEGO® displays, interactive activities, brick pits to get creative, raffles and much more!

We also have some amazing colab builds, including the return of our amazing "Glo-lab".

The show will be open to the public on:
- Saturday 30th May - 9am to 4:30pm
- Sunday 31st May - 9am to 4:30pm

Tickets will be available soon and will be valid for either day of the show, with pass-outs available for the same day.

We will be sharing more information over the coming weeks so please like and follow the Palmerston Brick Show on Facebook and Instagram - @PalmyBrickShow @well.lug

events.name: The ABBA Day Party: Wellington 💃🕺
events.venue: Eva Pub - Wellington
events.long_description:
The ABBA Day Party: Wellington 💃🕺
Calling all Dancing Queens and Super Troupers! 🎉
Get ready for a glitter-filled afternoon of pure pop perfection as we celebrate the iconic music of ABBA! Whether you're a lifelong fan or just love a bit of feel-good disco, this is your chance to sing, dance, and party like it's Eurovision '74!
What to Expect:
🎶 Wall-to-Wall ABBA AnthemsFrom “Dancing Queen” to “Gimme! Gimme! Gimme!”, enjoy all the greatest hits and hidden gems that made ABBA a global sensation.
✨ Disco, Funk & 70s ClassicsWhile ABBA takes center stage, we'll also be spinning a fabulous mix of other dancefloor-fillers from the disco era—think funky grooves, 70s gems, and all the classics that’ll keep you moving from start to finish.
💫 ABBA-Inspired AtmosphereThink shimmering lights, tinsel wigs and more sequins than you can shake a tambourine at! Step into your own mini-Mamma Mia moment!
🌈 Feel-Good Vibes All Day LongCelebrate friendship, freedom, and unforgettable tunes with an afternoon of singing, dancing, and joyous nostalgia.
Whether you’re a Fernando or a Chiquitita, this is your Waterloo! don’t miss it! 🎤✨FAQ’S🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etcYes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.🗣️ Is the venue accessible?Our events take place at a variety of locations around the world. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.🗣️ Are your events 18+?All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.🗣️ What do I do if I cant find my tickets or confirmation email?We use Fatsoma to sell tickets for our events. If you’re struggling to find your ticket, you can access their support channels via https://support.fatsoma.com/support/home🗣️ Are there tickets available on the door?We always recommend booking in advance as we cannot guarantee on the door entry. However, in certain instances there will be a limited number of tickets on a first come first served basis for sale on the door.🗣️ I have more questions?If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)Refund Policy:In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

Disclaimer:
The event listed here is purely for informational purposes. All the details such as venue, date/time, artist information, etc., are subject to change.
This event is not associated with or sponsored by AllEvents, but it may be of interest to you. You can explore more such events with us at AllEvents.

events.name: Getting Started in Standup - Wellington
events.venue: Cavern Club,18 Allen St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting Started in Standup - Wellington
Have you always wanted to try stand-up comedy but don’t know how to start?

Comedian and writer Brendon Green brings you a fun low-pressure comedy workshop.

Sign up here: https://www.nzcomedyschool.com/enroll

Suggested koha: $20

Everything you need to know to get a mic in your face:
-generating ideas
-joke writing
-effective delivery
-where and how to get practice
-how to avoid many common mistakes new performers make.

Brendon Green is a professional comedian and writer with over 15 years of experience. He has written and performed 10 NZ Comedy Festival shows, receiving the Best Newcomer Award in 2012, a Billy T Nomination in 2014, and a FRED Award Nomination for Best Show in 2023.

------
This class is also an intro to our upcoming "Complete Course" in Wellington

Wellington May/June 2026 (With Brendon Green)

Getting Started (open to all): Sunday 24 May 5:00pm (Cavern Club)
Class 1: Tuesday, 26 May 6:00-9:00pm
Class 2: Tuesday, 2 June 6:00-9:00pm
Class 3: Tuesday, 9 June 6:00-9:00pm
Class 4: Tuesday, 16 June 6:00-9:00pm
Graduation Show: Friday 19 June 8:00pm (Fringe Bar)

Price $450+gst - Includes all classes and materials, two private writing/coaching sessions, and your graduation video

More info: https://www.nzcomedyschool.com/

Brendon Green is a professional comedian and writer with over 15 years of experience. He has written and performed 10 NZ Comedy Festival shows, receiving the Best Newcomer Award in 2012, a Billy T Nomination in 2014, and a FRED Award Nomination for Best Show in 2023. A prolific MC and line-up comedian, he is known for his friendly and consistent stage presence. His comedy is thoughtful, kind, and emotionally playful - allowing him to play to audiences of all sizes and backgrounds. He's been teaching for the NZ Comedy School in Auckland for several years, with graduates who made the finals of the Raw Quest!

events.name: Debaucherous Dinner
events.venue: 213 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6011,213 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Debaucherous Dinner
� DEBAUCHEROUS DINNER



Part of KiwiKinksters Capital Weekend — a curated series of markets, workshops, and celebrations exploring connection, consent, and creative self-expression.




A PLAYFUL CONSENT CABARET

What happens when a dinner party gets a little mischievous?

Welcome to Debaucherous Dinner: A Playful Consent Cabaret — an interactive evening of curiosity, connection, and playful debauchery, all unfolding over a shared meal.

This isn’t a workshop and it’s not a play party.
It’s a social playground for practicing the art of asking for what you want.

Through playful prompts, cabaret-style games, and a touch of theatrical service, guests are invited to explore the delicious tension between curiosity, boundaries, and connection.

Hosted by kink-fluent MC the evening gently transforms from a polite dinner party into a playful salon where asking, negotiating, serving, receiving, and flirting are all part of the fun.

Expect:

� playful consent games
� cheeky social challenges
� flirtation and new connections
� a theatrical service dynamic
� a mischievous dessert ritual to close the evening

Participation is always optional - watching the room unfold can be just as entertaining.

The atmosphere is welcoming, respectful, and just a little bit wicked.

----------------------------------------


� WHO THIS IS FOR

Perfect for:

• curious newcomers exploring kink culture
• experienced players who enjoy social play
• couples wanting to meet like-minded people
• anyone interested in practicing communication and consent in a fun setting

No prior kink experience is required - just curiosity and respect for others.

----------------------------------------


� IMPORTANT NOTES

• Participation is always optional
• Clear consent and respectful behaviour are expected
• This is a social interactive dinner, not a play party
• Dress with flair if you wish - playful elegance encouraged

----------------------------------------

� Seats are limited for this intimate and deliciously debaucherous experience.

events.long_description:
KT Tunstall // Wellington // Eye To The Telescope "20th Anniversary Tour" // Meow Nui // 18+
𝑫𝒆𝒔𝒕𝒓𝒐𝒚 𝑨𝒍𝒍 𝑳𝒊𝒏𝒆𝒔 𝑷𝒓𝒆𝒔𝒆𝒏𝒕𝒔

𝐊𝐓 𝐓𝐔𝐍𝐒𝐓𝐀𝐋𝐋
𝐄𝐘𝐄 𝐓𝐎 𝐓𝐇𝐄 𝐓𝐄𝐋𝐄𝐒𝐂𝐎𝐏𝐄 "𝟐𝟎𝐓𝐇 𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐈𝐕𝐄𝐑𝐒𝐀𝐑𝐘 𝐓𝐎𝐔𝐑"

Sᴀᴛᴜʀᴅᴀʏ 30 Mᴀʏ 2026
Mᴇᴏᴡ Nᴜɪ, Wᴇʟʟɪɴɢᴛᴏɴ 18+
Dᴏᴏʀs 8ᴘᴍ

𝗧𝗶𝗰𝗸𝗲𝘁𝘀:
➟ https://daltours.cc/26KTT-well

KT Tunstall will return to Australia and New Zealand in May 2026 to celebrate the 20th anniversary of her groundbreaking multi-million-selling debut album, '𝐸𝑦𝑒 𝑇𝑜 𝑇ℎ𝑒 𝑇𝑒𝑙𝑒𝑠𝑐𝑜𝑝𝑒’.

These exclusive shows will see KT perform the 5 x times platinum album in its entirety, offering fans a once in a lifetime experience to relive the magic of a defining era.

KT recently supported Train, in a stripped back duo mode, on their huge Australian tour in May of this year and blew everyone away, paving the way for her own headline tour in full band mode next year. The Rock Pit’s review says it all "every song in its own right deserves to be on household playlists. The happiness and joy Tunstall radiates while playing is captivating, there’s is a true art in her craft of songwriting. Tunstall’s energy is simply infectious.”

'𝐸𝑦𝑒 𝑇𝑜 𝑇ℎ𝑒 𝑇𝑒𝑙𝑒𝑠𝑐𝑜𝑝𝑒’ remains an iconic and beloved record that has become a landmark moment of British music in the 2000's. Released in December 2004, it took over the world with hits like “Black Horse and the Cherry Tree” and “Suddenly I See”.

“Suddenly I See” became the song of a generation when it was featured during the opening scene of The Devil Wears Prada and led her to win the iconic Ivor Novello Award.

The tracks, “Universe & U” and “Other Side of the World” also found their way into the public consciousness as part of the hugely popular TV series Grey’s Anatomy and helped the album to reach and incredible 4 million sales around the world.

KT Tunstall live on stage is a force. Her shows are electric; loop pedals, powerful vocals and an engaging stage presence that makes everyone in the crowd feel like she is singing directly to them. Her raw, real energy and humour consistently see her receive rave reviews. With universal critical acclaim and a slew of awards, KT has become one of the most respected singer-songwriters of the modern era.

Do not miss these special shows!

events.long_description:
4th Annual Record Convention
Come immerse yourself in the captivating world of vinyl records at our 4th Annual Record Convention - set to become world-famous in New Zealand. 

This is the perfect way to celebrate the culmination of NZ Music Month with like-minded individuals.

At this event, you'll experience a mind-blowing convergence of vendors from up and down the motu, which promises to be bigger than the both of us, with 10000s of records for sale. 

Don't miss this unparalleled opportunity to connect with fellow music enthusiasts, discover hidden gems, and vibe with the beats that define our culture.

events.venue: Eva Wellington,31-35 Dixon Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Capital Fetish Ball 2026
Capital Fetish Ball returns for an immersive evening of theatrical fetish performance, refined decadence, and ritual spectacle for 2026!

About this Event

Capital Fetish Ball 2026 returns for an immersive evening of theatrical fetish performance, refined decadence, and ritual spectacle.
Curated and community-held by KiwiKinksters, this event is grounded in consent culture, empowered self-expression, and psychological safety.

Featuring performances by Michelle Kasey, Bonita Danger Doll, Tommy Midnight, Miss Hellblazer, and dramatic shibari duo Jerry & -SisterRoxx-.
Expect cinematic fetish performance, dark cabaret, and high-stakes rope ritual exploring beauty, pain, and trust.
Beyond the stage, guests will experience:
• A dedicated Dark Room
• St Andrew’s Cross play space
• Live body painting
• Immersive atmosphere and elevated fetish fashion

Dress to be seen.
Elevated fetish, formal, latex, leather, avant-garde glamour encouraged.
18+ event.
Respect, consent, and community care are paramount.
Community guidelines are available at kiwikinksters.co.nz

About the performers
Michelle Kasey
Michelle Kasey is a powerhouse burlesque performer and multidisciplinary movement artist with over a decade of professional stage experience. Of Samoan and Swiss heritage and born and raised in Aotearoa, she brings soulful sensuality, commanding presence, and technical precision to every performance. A two-time national pole dance champion whose career began in circus, Michelle has also shared stages alongside international icons including Snoop Dogg. She is co-owner of The Velvet Haven burlesque school and, off stage, a twice-voted “New Zealand’s Best Sex Therapist,” supporting thousands worldwide in intimacy and connection.
Bonita Danger Doll
Bonita Danger Doll is one of New Zealand’s most accomplished burlesque entertainers, celebrated for her dynamic stage presence and classic showgirl glamour. A multi-award-winning performer, she has captivated audiences since 2010 with acts ranging from elegant tease to bold, high-impact spectacle. Based in Christchurch, Bonita blends theatrical polish, playful confidence, and strong audience connection in every performance. Beyond the stage, she is a singer, educator, and mentor, co-creating burlesque fitness and performance training spaces that nurture emerging talent and celebrate empowered self-expression.
Tommy Midnight
Tommy Midnight is a live musician whose dark, evocative sound captivated audiences at SPANK and now returns for Capital Fetish Ball. Blending brooding atmosphere with sensual intensity, Tommy creates immersive sonic experiences that deepen the energy of the room. His live performances are both intimate and theatrical, offering a soundtrack that moves between mood, ritual, and presence. Expect sound that seduces, lingers, and sets the pulse for the night.
Miss Hellblazer (Blaze Akira)
Miss Hellblazer, also known as Blaze Akira, is a dynamic performance artist whose work intersects dance, theatricality, and expressive movement. Known for her bold stage presence and genre-defying aesthetic, she brings visceral energy and immersive intensity to every act. Her performances blend dramatic styling, movement, and atmosphere, drawing audiences into moments that feel both powerful and emotionally charged.
Jerry & -SisterRoxx-
Jerry and -SisterRoxx- are dramatic shibari performers known for their captivating, suspenseful scenes that transform rope into living ritual. Through the interplay of light, sound, movement, and emotional vulnerability, they create a sensory journey exploring beauty, pain, and trust. Their work elevates the Japanese art of rope bondage into choreographed, high-stakes performance art — intimate, theatrical, and breathtaking.

💎 THE MOST EXCLUSIVE, NAUGHTY & OVER-THE-TOP VIP EXPERIENCE OF THE YEAR! 💎
VIP ticket grants you access to an unforgettable cocktail-fueled, pleasure-soaked adventure that will leave you gasping, laughing, and begging for more!

🍸 THE SEDUCTIVE COCKTAIL MASTERCLASS 🍸
Ever wanted to shake, stir, and seduce like a true cocktail dominatrix? Now’s your chance.
Learn the art of crafting sensual cocktails with our top-tier mixologists.
Indulge in liquid aphrodisiacs designed to tease the taste buds and ignite the senses.

Top it off with a decadent garnish (chocolate shavings, edible gold, or something more… suggestive 👀).

💃 Several complimentary cocktail are included to sip while you create your masterpiece!

🖤 THE SIN-FUL SHOWDOWN – VIBRATOR RACING! 🖤

Yes, you read that right. Vibrators. A racetrack. Absolute carnage.
Grab your weapon of choice and line up for the Capital Fetish Ball’s most hilarious, competitive, and outrageous race!
Bet on your champion or compete for the ultimate prize – will your vibrator speed its way to victory, or sputter out in disgrace?
Our spectacular host will keep the energy high with cheeky commentary, unexpected challenges, and audience dares.
The winner receives a VERY special prize that will make their night (and maybe their week 😉).

🍾 DECADENT NIBBLES & INDULGENCES 🍾
A true VIP experience wouldn’t be complete without delicious bites to nibble on…

events.id: Dry Cleaning tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Dry Cleaning tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: MG Live & I OH YOU
Florence Shaw talk-sings about rage bait, domestic labour, retractable legs, and scabs on her head. Stories revolve around an obstinate love of carcinogenically scorched food, and the escape of a pet tortoise who loves to kick tinfoil ball. Each verse is a diorama, or a seared-in sense-memory, soundtracked by groove-conscious post-punk that sidewinds alongside her.
Dry Cleaning’s latest album, Secret Love, expands on the new sounds they played with on their stunning sophomore Stumpwork. Disjointed funk and atmospheric synths splice deeper into their art-rock romping while Shaw’s ennui and overload have never felt so vivid.

events.id: Cooking: Vietnamese Street Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Vietnamese Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Vietnamese Street Food
Join our experienced Vietnamese cook and find out how to create a range of traditional dishes from different parts of Vietnam that you can eat for breakfast, lunch and dinner or at any time of day. Recipes include delicious wraps, pancakes, beef noodle soup, fried rolls with rice noodles and much more.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Define what characterises Vietnamese cooking and key ingredients
• Recognise and make popular street food from North to South of Vietnam
• Identify retail outlets which supply Vietnamese ingredients
• More confidently follow recipes provided and prepare those recipes with supervision
• Prepare dishes for home eating or entertaining
All ingredients provided but bring an apron, dish cloth, tea towels and containers to take food home. 
Note:  
Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getdown Services - Australia & New Zealand 2026-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Getdown Services - Australia & New Zealand 2026
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Meet Getdown Services – your new favourite band and one of the UK’s hottest word-of-mouth acts! The brilliantly offbeat cult British duo has been turning heads across Britain and Europe with their balls-to-the-walls live shows, tongue-in-cheek wit, razor-sharp lyrics and infectious disco-fuelled grooves, teetering between chaos and genius. And now, after selling out tours across the UK and Europe, they’re bringing their riotous show down under for the first time in May/June 2026 – with shows in Brisbane, Sydney, Melbourne, Auckland and Wellington, presented by I OH YOU and MG Live. 
Tickets go on sale Friday 14 November at 10.00am local time, with MG Live presale commencing Thursday 13 November at 10.00am local time. Sign up for early access to the MG Live presale here.               
“I reckon we get people asking us to come to Australia probably every day, and everyone in England is moving there, so will be good to see what all the fuss is about.

“Always been one of those places where you’re like, ‘Fair enough, but I can’t be arsed with that. It looks mad – there’s snakes and also it takes 40 hours to get there.’ Will be good fun, though! Went to New Zealand to see a mate of ours get married a few years ago and thought it was a lovely country and I really liked those pies they have. It felt quite similar to the UK in a lot of ways, so playing gigs there should be fun!”
Getdown Services

For those in the know, Bristol-based duo Josh Law and Ben Sadler – aka Getdown Services – are THE must-see new act of the year. And if you don’t know yet, you will soon … and you’ll wish you got in early! Named as “one to watch” at this year’s Glastonbury Festival and dubbed (by the band themselves) “Britain’s Best Band”, their live shows are equal parts smart, sweaty and gloriously unhinged. Their sound is a wild blend of scrappy indie disco, twisted funk and post-punk energy, turning the absurdity of modern Britain into dancefloor gold.        
 
The pair met at school in Minehead, Somerset and now make music together at home in Bristol – churning out what Rolling Stone called “power disco with unrepentant silliness” with a healthy dose of satire and swagger. Their highly lauded debut album Crisps (Breakfast Records, 2023) showcased their eclectic danceable grooves and biting social commentary, and a mix of sharp, funny social observations wrapped in irresistible, funk-fuelled rhythms. Follow-up EPs Your Medal’s In The Post, Crumbs and latest, Primordial Slot Machine, cemented their cult status, alongside an arsenal of red-hot singles – including James Bay’s Hat, Dog Dribble, I Got Views and Eat Quiche, Sleep, Repeat.   
 
 “Getdown Services are unrepentantly silly and constantly deliver one of the most joyously deranged live shows – just one of the best live bands out there!”
 Rolling Stone
“Their live show is as daft and as exhilarating as they come. Five stars.”
 Buzz Magazine
 
 “Genuine contenders to being the most exciting and interesting band in the UK right now.”
 CLASH

 Getdown Services look set to gobble up the world, one crumb at a time. Whether you’re into danceable chaos, satirical genius or just want to be ahead of the curve, Getdown Services are the band you didn’t know you needed – until now! Britain’s favourite new band are about to become yours too! 

MG LIVE TERMS OF SALE

https://mg.live/termsofsale

events.long_description:
Cooking: Indian Vegan
Indian cooking expert, Manjula Patel, has assembled a range of vegan Indian dishes for you to learn and enjoy making and eating. In this practical cooking course, you’ll observe dishes being made by Manjula and then work in pairs to create most of these dishes yourselves.
Please note this is a practical cooking class that will not cover vegan philosophy.  Not suitable for people allergic to nuts.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Discuss the flavours and spices used in Indian cuisine
• Know where to purchase ingredients for a selection of vegan dishes and finger food
• Read and follow a range of recipes step-by-step
• Create some classic dishes to try again at home
All ingredients included in course fee. Bring an apron, dishcloth, and a couple of tea towels and containers to take any leftovers home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Social Media Marketing Secrets for Small Enterprises: Visual Impact-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Social Media Marketing Secrets for Small Enterprises: Visual Impact
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course helps small business owners and entrepreneurs unlock the full potential of social media. Each week we’ll cover practical steps to review your current channels, set up strong profiles, and create a tailored content plan that works for your business. You’ll learn how to choose the right platforms, use hashtags effectively, create engaging posts, select visuals that connect with your audience, and understand the role of AI tools. By the end, you’ll walk away with a clear and actionable plan to grow your social media channels and attract the right customers.
Whether you’re starting out or already running a business, a strong social media presence is no longer optional. The challenge is knowing where to focus your efforts when every platform seems to demand something different.
This course is designed to cut through the noise and give you a roadmap that fits your business. Together, we’ll:
Review the main platforms and choose the right ones for your goals
Tailor your profiles so they look professional and trustworthy
Build a realistic content plan you can actually stick to
Learn how to use hashtags and trends to boost reach
Explore how AI can help (and where it can’t)
Understand what visuals work best and how to avoid common pitfalls
Discuss tips for creating engagement and growing a loyal following
The focus is practical and hands-on. Each week you’ll apply the ideas directly to your own business, so by the end of the course you’ll have a solid social media strategy ready to put into action.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Come Together 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Come Together 2026
events.long_description:
New Zealand’s most successful rock series ever returns in 2026, focusing on an electrifying trio of landmark albums. Come Together is bringing three iconic albums to Wellington. This year, they take on Bruce Springsteen’s Born to Run, Radiohead’s OK Computer and Talking Heads’ Stop Making Sense. Each concert will faithfully recreate an album and follow up with a set of classics and deep cuts that span each artist’s catalogue. 
The group is made up of a rotating roster of Aotearoa’s most acclaimed rock musicians. What began as a bold celebration of legendary rock albums has since exploded into one of New Zealand’s most electrifying live concert experiences. The collective includes Jon Toogood, Tami Neilson, Liam Finn, Delaney Davidson, Reb Fountain, Julia Deans, Dianne Swann, Mel Parsons, Ed Knowles, Arahi, Jazmine Mary, Deva Mahal, and many more. Their endeavours have drawn thousands of fans, cementing Come Together as a must-see event on the music calendar.

events.long_description:
Presented by: Lōemis & Suzie Says
Lōemis Presents in partnership with Susie Says! are elated to announce the Wellington return of heralded artist and producer extraordinaire, Cate Le Bon on Saturday 6 June 2026.
“She’s my favorite modern songwriter, period,”—Annie Clark, aka St Vincent.
Cate’s genre-defying songwriting breaks away from sonic norms, intuitively guided by innovation and intrigue. The result is a breathtaking ride along work so revered that artists as varied as Wilco, Dry Cleaning and St Vincent have called on her to bestow upon their songs the je ne sais quoi that only Cate can deliver.
"It’s hard not to compare her to iconoclasts who seem like aliens, such as David Bowie or Björk."—The New Yorker
Cate Le Bon possess an indubitable magnetism. Her enchanting vocals and swirling guitars envelop listeners while the band’s laser focus beguilingly commands attention. She is master of the craft and a force unto herself.
Don’t miss your opportunity to experience Cate Le Bon live at Meow Nui. Get tickets quick!

events.id: Professor Brian Cox: Emergence-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Professor Brian Cox: Emergence
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s a stage show that covers vast rivers of galaxies in the cosmos, the ecosystems of Earth, and the structure of the human brain. Emergence is the latest show by English physicist, presenter, and personality Professor Brian Cox. He takes to the stage at TSB Arena to contemplate the dazzling complexity of the universe and how it’s built on profoundly simple principles. 
Emergence is also a scientific history of 400 years of human discovery. How did we come to understand so much about the cosmos? Guided by mathematics, curiosity, and an aesthetic sense of symmetry, our species has begun to read the story of the Universe. We’ve started to carry our spacecraft to the edge of the solar system and outwards to the stars. Brian’s engaging style makes these questions come to life. The audience will be left awestruck and thrilled as the show covers vast topics in an accessible way. 
Brian has appeared in many landmark science programs for BBC radio and television over the last 15 years, from the Peabody Award-winning Wonders of the Solar System to the worldwide hit series The Planets. His previous touring show, Horizons, was performed to nearly half a million people across the world.
events.description:
It’s a stage show that covers vast rivers of galaxies in the cosmos, the ecosystems of Earth, and the structure of the human brain.
Emergence is also a scientific history of 400 years of human discovery.
How did we come to understand so much about the cosmos?
Guided by mathematics, curiosity, and an aesthetic sense of symmetry, our species has begun to read the story of the Universe.
We’ve started to carry our spacecraft to the edge of the solar system and outwards to the stars.

events.id: Financial Planning for Retirement-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Financial Planning for Retirement
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Financial Planning for Retirement
Massey University's annual Retirement Expenditure Guidelines study (2019) found:
Just 40% of pre-retirees believed they were well-prepared for retirement, and 57% considered retirement to be a concern.  They also confirmed, New Zealand Superannuation is not sufficient to fund the retirement most people want.
Ministry of Social Development (2020):
22% of all people receiving New Zealand Superannuation also receive additional support payments, with over 44,000 superannuants receiving additional accommodation support,
And if you needed to obtain Government assisted accommodation, at the end of March 2020, more than 16,300 households were waiting for state or social housing.
Living in a motel or boarding house with strangers (who may have other vulnerable backgrounds) is not an ideal or safe location to spend your retirement years.   But for many, it is the only option they can afford.
If you have not planned for your retirement, then this course is you! 
This course has been developed for people in their 50’s or 60’s that are looking at retirement and have no idea if they can financially survive it: what to do, what $’s they need, how or where they can generate additional income, or even where to go to get help. 
It is targeted towards those that still have some time (for some - not much) to make adjustments, learn some basic financial skills, make some big decisions, and positively influence the quality of their pending retirement. 
You will be introduced to a simple step by step guide to developing a financial plan for retirement. 
You will work though a case study in class, and can then go away and repeat the process with your own data/details.  You will be provided with comprehensive course notes to enable you to update the case study information with your own information. 
At each step, key considerations and financial concepts will be defined, explained and discussed to help you understand the financial requirements of retirement.  
Are you ready for the retirement lifestyle you hoped or dreamed of?  If not, then this course is for you.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Disclaimer:  The tutors are not financial advisors.  No investments, products nor specific recommendations will be provided.  A step by step planning process will be taught by working through a case study.  Key considerations that should be used by the participants when looking at their own options to bridge the financial gap between what is available through superannuation and what is required to meet lifestyle goals will be provided and discussed.  Product names may be used in examples and explanations to help participants understand the financial concepts and the planning process.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
While many strive to avoid it, suppressing thoughts, feelings, and values to sidestep disagreements can lead to unresolved issues. Learning to manage and resolve conflicts equips you with the tools to navigate these challenges effectively, fostering understanding and collaboration.
 Aim of this workshop:  To empower you with the skills and confidence needed to effectively manage and resolve conflicts.
 Learning Outcomes:
By the end of the workshop, you will:
Recognise how your actions and behaviors influence conflict.
Develop your ability to clarify and manage expectations.
Acquire practical tools for effective conflict management.
Create a sustainable action plan to continuously apply conflict resolution strategies.

events.id: From Rags to Reuse – Upcycling and Sustainability Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: From Rags to Reuse – Upcycling and Sustainability Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Stella Carruthers, textile artist and eco lifestylist, will guide you on this one-day workshop to learn about sustainable living from a reduce and reuse perspective. Learn about textile waste as a global issue and explore rag rug making techniques - you will leave the workshop with the skills to finish a project at home. Investigate the 8 pillars of the zero-waste movement and how they can inform low waste living. Identify the key components of greenwashing so you can make informed purchases as a conscious consumer. Finally, leave the workshop feeling empowered to explore wider social and political actions as they apply to waste issues in contemporary society.
Most materials provided but please bring a pair of small sewing scissors and sewing shears if you have them, pen and paper, and your lunch.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- talk confidently about textile waste as a global issue and be empowered with the skills and ideas to reduce your personal textile waste footprint through recycling and upcycling practices
- have the beginnings of a rag rug made using the plait and stitch technique and gain the skills and knowledge to finish the project at home.
-Identify and describe the 8 pillars of the zero-waste movement and how they apply to recycling practices in contemporary Aotearoa New Zealand society.
- Understand what greenwashing is and have debunked some common greenwashing myths prevalent in society.
- Have learned some practical tips for living low waste in the city.
- Feel empowered to explore wider social and political action as it applies to waste issues in the modern world.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sola Rosa - In The Mids Tour - Wellington-San Fran,171 Cuba Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Sola Rosa - In The Mids Tour - Wellington
events.venue: San Fran,171 Cuba Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sola Rosa - In The Mids Tour - Wellington
Celebrating 25 years of making music as Sola Rosa, Andrew Spraggon, the force behind one of Aotearoa’s most enduring musical projects, returns with his eighth studio album In The Mids, due for release on Friday 10 April. To mark the upcoming release, Sola Rosa returns to the touring circuit with the In The Mids Album Release Tour, taking place throughout New Zealand in May and June.

Presenting both new material and longstanding favourites, the live show has evolved alongside the music itself, incorporating a newly developed visual and lighting design that reshapes how the songs are experienced. It’s a presentation that marks a clear shift for Sola Rosa, offering a live context unlike anything the project has explored previously.

In The Mids is a ten-track body of work featuring seven collaborations with vocalists from Australia and Aotearoa. The album marks a creative shift for Sola Rosa, reflecting a renewed joy in studio-based production and an evolving set of influences.

Sola Rosa
In The Mids Album Release Tour
New Zealand May-Jun 26’
Funded through NZ On Air and supported by the NZ Music Commission

FRI 22 MAY
Double Whammy | Auckland

SAT 23 MAY
Common Room | Hastings

FRI 29 MAY
Deville | Nelson

SAT 30 MAY
Hide | Christchurch

FRI 5 JUN
The Mayfair | New Plymouth

SAT 6 JUN
San Fran | Wellington

All Tickets are on sale now at solarosa.com

events.id: Rum Jungle ‘Marginalia’ AU/NZ Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Rum Jungle ‘Marginalia’ AU/NZ Tour
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Australian indie-rock outfit Rum Jungle return to Australia and New Zealand following a breakout few years that has seen the band sell out shows across the UK, Europe and North America while quietly building a global fanbase. Known for their infectious guitar hooks, restless energy and emotionally honest songwriting, Rum Jungle have become one of Australia’s most exciting live bands.
The tour arrives alongside new music from their forthcoming sophomore album Marginalia, including recent singles “Coal Dust”, “What’s Next” and the introspective new track “Lowlife”. Written during a period of reflection, the new material sees the band expanding their sound while retaining the sharp melodies and raw immediacy that first drew fans in.
Renowned for their electric live shows, Rum Jungle’s performances blur the line between indie rock urgency and sing-along pop instinct, the kind of gig where the room moves as one. With momentum building internationally and a new chapter of music underway, these shows mark the band’s biggest headline run across Australia and New Zealand to date.
San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

events.id: Acing The Interview-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Acing The Interview
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Acing The Interview
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
People have skills which they want to offer to employers, however, low confidence and anxiety combined with a lack of interview experience results in them not gaining employment.
'Acing The Interview' is a 3-module one day workshop that provides practical, cutting-edge tools and techniques to prepare participants to 'Ace the Interview'.
The 3 modules are:
1. Resourceful You - Think
2. Managing You - Feel
3. Acing the Interview - Do
Explore how to manage your thoughts under pressure and control your critical voice. Understand how to manage anxiety and stress. Demonstrate your interview skills and use body language and well-formed responses to create a powerful and positive first impression.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Manage your thoughts and critical voice under pressure
-  Visualise and mentally rehearse for success
-  Recognise your emotional reactions and manage your emotions under pressure
-  Utilise techniques to manage anxiety
-  Demonstrate interview skills and deliver well-formed responses to questions
-  Understand the STAR model and other communication techniques and how and when to use them
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Bike Maintenance for Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bike Maintenance for Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bicycle Maintenance for Beginners
Have you recently started riding a bicycle and want to make sure you can take care of it properly? Or have you been riding for ages, but have always been scared to fix anything yourself?
Are you unsure of how to pump your tyres, or how much oil your chain needs? Find yourself fumbling for words when trying to describe that clunking sound to the guy in the bike shop?
This one day course lays bare the nuts and bolts of bicycle mechanics for absolute beginners, as well as those with a little experience. Through practical experience working with your own bicycle, you will find out what needs to be done to keep it running smoothly. We’ll cover what to look out for when things start to wear out, and how to check for problems, so you can be confident your bicycle is safe to ride. You will also learn how to fix some of the most common mechanical problems, and learn about the correct terminology and tools to use.
Those who feel intimidated in bike shops are especially welcome in this course. No question is a stupid question!
Bringing a bike to work on is essential, preferably one that you are riding regularly, because the best way to learn is by building familiarity with your own ride. E-bikes are welcome, although the extra weight can mean they are more challenging to work on. Regardless of whether your bike is in pristine condition, or desperately in need of TLC, it is welcome on the course – bikes showing a bit more wear can be useful for the learning of the whole group.
Bring your pushbike along on the day.  If you have the following, please bring them as well:  A multi-tool and pump; chain oil and rag; lightweight close-fitting gardening or rubber gloves.
Note: A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
(Please note this course is not exhaustive and is not a professional training course).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Name most parts of a bicycle correctly
- Perform a quick safety check on any bicycle
- Fix a flat tyre
- Adjust your bike to fit your body
- Oil and clean your chain
- Identify and fix some common brake problems
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Communicate Across Generations: Bridge the Divide-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Communicate Across Generations: Bridge the Divide
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Communicate Across Generations: Bridge the Divide
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
 Discover why different generations prioritise different things in their interactions, from family group chats and face-to-face conversations through to workplace attitudes. Learn to decode the nuances, bridge the divides, and foster more effective communication in your daily life.
Learning Outcomes
Deconstruct Communication Styles: You will be able to identify and describe the typical communication styles, preferences, and values of different generations, including Baby Boomers, Generation X, Millennials, and Generation Z.
Mitigate Conflict: You will learn to recognise and resolve common intergenerational communication conflicts by understanding their root causes and applying effective conflict resolution strategies.
Improve Collaboration: You will develop practical skills to adapt your communication approach to better engage with people from different generations, leading to enhanced understanding and connection.
Foster Inclusivity: You will be able to appreciate and leverage the unique perspectives and strengths that each generation brings to the table, creating more inclusive environments in your relationships.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks, and pen and paper to take notes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Take a day and create a range of delicious and different Indian masalas, using both Manjula’s tried-and-true recipes but also some of the new spice mixes now available in Indian shops. You’ll also learn how to make a variety of breads (naan, roti and parathas) to serve with the curries.

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
WOMP 3D Digital Design - An Introduction
WOMP is user-friendly design-software. Saving you both time and unnecessary stress, WOMP allows your creativity to shine as you create 3D designs.
Whether you want to design toys, figures, logos, product mock-ups, jewelry or other 3D things - then WOMP makes this much more accessible.
Held in a computer lab, this one-day course will explore WOMP and you will be able to use it to create your own 3D designs. You will also get information on where you can send your designs to have them 3D printed.
Please bring an idea of what you would like to work on during the day, and pen and paper to take notes.
Note –  You will be using the free version of WOMP for this workshop - it also has affordable subscription options if you choose to move beyond the free version.
By the end of this workshops you will:
Discovered how WOMP simplifies the 3D Design process
Be able to describe why WOMP is a good software choice
Be able to use WOMP for making designs
Know where you can send your designs to be manufactured
Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network.

events.id: 2026 NZ Card Collectors Show-Te Rauparaha Arena,Trust Porirua Aquatic Recreation Centre, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: 2026 NZ Card Collectors Show
events.venue: Te Rauparaha Arena,Trust Porirua Aquatic Recreation Centre, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
2026 NZ Card Collectors Show
The 5th edition of the New Zealand Card Collectors Show is back in 2026 and will take place in the massive Te Rauparaha Arena in Porirua on Sunday 14 June.

It is the most comprehensive card collecting show in the country – over 60 vendors and 150+ tables of cards covering all interests and periods (ultra-modern through to vintage).

All cards and collectors catered for, including sports cards (rugby, basketball, cricket, league, football, boxing, ice hockey, UFC etc), TCG (Pokémon, MTG, Yu-gi-oh, Dragonball Z, One Piece, Lorcana etc), trading cards, gum cards, trade cards, cigarette cards, stickers, postcards, as well as associated sports memorabilia, card accessories and card grading services.

Public admission fee:
$5 Adults
Under 18 free

Raffles - Prizes - Games - Live Breaks - Trading Space for Adults and Kids - Food Vendors - Free Parking - Free Valuations

Vendor tables (all 1.8m in length) range in price from $30 for the hobbiest/beginner through to $60 for the business professional. There are also sponsor supporter packages available for businesses who want to take their involvement in the Show to the next level. These range in price from $100 through to $500. All info for these can be found on the 2026 NZ Card Collectors Show Facebook page.

For more table information/bookings please contact Dan or Steve ()

events.name: Descendents tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: SBM Presents
Celebrating 30 Years of Everything Sucks – June 2026
So Cal punk royalty Descendents return to Australia and New Zealand this June for the Run Down Under tour, celebrating 30 years of Everything Sucks.
Originally released in 1996, Everything Sucks remains a pivotal release not only in the band’s catalogue, but in the genre they helped define. The album captured the urgency and directness that has underpinned Descendents’ influence since their beginning way back in 1978.
While the tour marks the anniversary of Everything Sucks and will focus heavily on tracks from the album, fans can of course expect a career-spanning set, including live staples that have cemented Descendents as one of punk rock’s most consistent and respected acts.

events.id: The Marriage of Figaro-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Marriage of Figaro
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Written when Mozart was operating at the top of his powers, The Marriage of Figaro is considered one of the great operas. New Zealand Opera’s production in 2026 promises to bring the 240-year-old masterpiece to life in a sumptuous setting. 
Picking up narratively where The Barber of Seville left off, The Marriage of Figaro is renowned for its wit and musical sophistication. It follows Figaro and his bride-to-be, Susannah, on their wedding day. Figaro’s employer, Count Almaviva, tries to seduce Susanna, so the engaged couple seek revenge. The cast boasts an unbelievable wealth of talent from Aotearoa and Australia, including runner-up at the 2022 Lexus Song Quest Felicity Tompkins, Julien Van Mellaerts, and James Clayton. 
Trans-Tasman creative duo Lindy Hume and Tracy Grant Lord take the helm with direction and design while Orchestra Wellington and conductor James Judd lead from the pit. This season offers opera at its most gorgeous, its most vivid, its most unforgettable.

events.id: Melanie Bracewell — Dilly Dallying-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Melanie Bracewell — Dilly Dallying
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Melanie Bracewell is back with a brand-new hour-long show. It took a long time to write because she got distracted by a Sudoku puzzle. Melanie Bracewell — Dilly Dallying is about procrastination. Come along (don’t be late) to hear the Kiwi comic’s hilarious observations about avoidance. The UK comedy guide Chortle described Melanie as a “phenomenal storyteller”. Expect some wild stories as she dissects why she ‘puts things off’ for another day. 
Stand-up comedy, radio announcing, radio producer, script writing — Melanie Bracewell has found success in multiple disciplines. Her TV appearances include ‘The Cheap Seats,’ ‘Taskmaster’ (NZ), ‘Have You Been Paying Attention,’ and ‘Thank God You’re Here.’ She has won multiple awards as well, including the Billy T Award for the country’s best emerging comedian in 2018. The same year, she won Breakthrough Comedian of the Year at the NZ Comedy Guild Awards.

events.long_description:
Microsoft Excel - Advanced
For those who have completed the Intermediate course or equivalent - you must be competent using Excel formulas and formatting.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course, you'll have had an introduction to the more advanced features of MS Excel, including how to:
• Manage lists: sort and filter
• Create a pivot table from an existing set of data
• Create custom date formats
• Analyse data: goal seek, data tables and scenario manager

events.id: Cooking: Thai Home-Style-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Thai Home-Style
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Thai Home-Style
Thai cuisine and cooking has a key secret - the secret is that authentic Thai food balances key flavour elements. Combining sweet, salty, bitter, sour and spicy, Thai cuisine creates sumptuous and exciting flavour combinations. Come along to this half-day cooking course and learn to make some home-style Thai dishes.
Not suitable for vegetarians. All ingredients included in course fee, you just need to bring your apron, a couple of tea towels, a dishcloth and containers to take leftovers home in.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Make some  Thai dishes at home with more confidence
Use ingredients/spices/herbs to match with dishes
Apply some aspects of the Thai eating style at home or when visiting Thailand
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Neurodiversity Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Neurodiversity Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Neurodiversity Workshop
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
This one-day workshop introduces you to the topic of neurodiversity and considers some of the key conditions that people live with. We look at:
•ADHD – Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder
•Autism
•The “Dys family” – dyslexia, dysgraphia, dyscalculia, dyspraxia (Development Coordination Disorder)
•OCD – Obsessive Compulsive Disorder
•Tourette’s Syndrome
Tik Tok creator and public speaker on neurodiversity, Nigel Sutton, will introduce the subject and facilitate you in discovering the definitions and impacts of each of the key kinds of neurodiversity. Working together with other participants, you will consider many key commonalities that those living with various neurodivergent conditions  experience. We will expose the realities of being neurodivergent in a “neurotypical world” and this will allow you to develop empathy and practical strategies to support neurodivergent people to be their best. 
New Zealand’s leading dyslexia expert Mike Styles, will provide an interactive and participatory session to assist you to understand and support a family member or friend who has dyslexia or a similar learning difference. 
You will be able to ask anything you want with our neurodivergent hot seat session.
Find out from those offering “lived experience” what it is really like to have a different kind of brain. This one day workshop is: ”Neurodiversity in a Nutshell” – whether you know a little or a lot, whether you are a relative, employer, partner, friend, or just interested to know more about neurodiversity – this workshop is for you! 
You must be a NZ citizen or resident to register in this course for free and you must provide photo ID when you attend. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Speaking to an Audience
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Build your skills and confidence in speaking to an audience, whether it’s for a work presentation, a more
formal speech, or part of a performance. In this experiential course, we’ll explore and practise skills to
be better prepared to speak, build rapport with your audience, and deliver content effectively.
By the end of the day’s session, you’ll be given:
Essential elements of a great talk—from impromptu talks to formal presentations.
Tools to prepare, organise, practice, and deliver a meaningful, persuasive, and effective
presentation.
Actions that build rapport with your audience.
Strategies to maximise the impact of your words, voice, and body language.
Mindsets and actions to reduce stage-fright and appear more confident.
Memory techniques and strategies for impromptu speaking.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.

events.name: International Yoga Day - Community Event
events.venue: Lotus Yoga Centre & Retreat,91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
International Yoga Day - Community Event
A Kundalini Yoga and Gong Bath workshop to celebrate International Yoga Day

About this Event

To celebrate International Yoga Day, I’m offering an annual community class — a welcoming space for anyone curious about Kundalini Yoga, or simply wanting to move, breathe and reset.
This special 1.5 hour session includes:
• A beginner-friendly Kundalini Yoga class
• Breathwork to energise and centre
• Deep relaxation
• A short, soothing gong bath to integrate

Whether you’re completely new to yoga, returning after a break, or already practising, this class is designed to be accessible and supportive.


💛 Community Ticket — $15 per person
This discounted price (normally $35) is my way of giving back and making the practice accessible for anyone who has been curious to try.

🎟 Bookings essential

Bring a friend.
Come on your own.
All are welcome.

💫No prior experience needed. Just come as you are.
💫Bring an open heart, a yoga mat, blanket, water bottle and anything you need to feel cozy + supported.

If you want to pay by cash on the day or by bank transfer, please send an email to to book your place.

events.venue: The Crooked Elm,25 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
CBK Social Club Beer Bong Tournament!
Keen for a good time? Grab a mate and join our Beer Pong Tournament at the Crooked Elm

About this Event

Keen for a good time and a bit of friendly competition? 🍻
CBK Social Club is all about getting people out, meeting others, and actually doing something fun, not just talking about it.
Join us at the Crooked Elm for our Beer Pong Tournament, where you and your teammate will go head-to-head with other teams for the chance to win a bar tab.
Expect:
🍻 Good vibes + great people
🏓 Competitive (but friendly) beer pong
💧 Hydration support from Daeli to keep you going
🎉 A fun, social atmosphere whether you’re playing or just there for the vibes
Teams of 2 required - grab your partner and get ready to compete.
Come for the tournament, stay for the drinks, and leave with a few new connections.
Want to come solo?
If you don’t have a teammate, email us or DM us on insta cbksocialclub and we’ll do our best to match you with another player.
Once we’ve found you a partner, we’ll send you a link to book your team entry ticket ($50 for 2 players).
👉 Matching is not guaranteed, but we’ll try our best!!
CBK Social Club. Plans, people, and a place to belong.

Refund & Transfer Policy
Tickets are eligible for a full refund if requested at least 14 days before the event date.
Refund requests made within 14 days of the event are not eligible for a refund, however tickets can be transferred to another person.
If the event is cancelled by CBK Social Club, a full refund will be issued.
To request a refund or transfer, please contact:

events.id: Stereolab tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Stereolab tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Banished Music & Strange News
The panic is over! Anglo-French avant-pop group Stereolab are indeed returning to Aotearoa in June, hot on the mechanical heels of last year’s brilliant album Instant Holograms on Metal Film. This was the band’s first studio album in fifteen years, and followed their celebrated reunion in 2019. The Guardian hailed the album as “a radiant return”, and Rolling Stone fizzed over the band’s “gliding grooves, wowing-and-fluttering synthesizers, and lyrics that elegantly pine for more”. They’ve got a cult following for a very good reason, and we’re beyond astonished to have them as part of our winter line-up!

events.id: Engelbert Humperdinck — The Celebration Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Engelbert Humperdinck — The Celebration Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He has a unique voice that has charmed millions of fans around the world. Engelbert Humperdinck returns to Wellington in June 2026 for The Celebration Tour. After 60 extraordinary years, the legend continues to woo with crowd favourites. Audiences can expect hits like Release Me, The Last Waltz, A Man Without Love, Can’t Take My Eyes Off of You, and Quando, Quando, Quando. 
Over the past six decades, Engelbert has sold over 140 million records. His achievements include 64 gold albums, 35 platinum albums, four Grammy nominations, a Golden Globe, and stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, Las Vegas Walk of Fame, and Leicester Walk of Fame. Recordings span everything from romantic ballads to movie theme songs, disco, rock, and even gospel.

This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
What’s the Goal?
To arm you with the strategies, skills, and mindset to walk into any negotiation and leave with the outcome you desire.
What You'll Discover:
Crack the Code on People: Spot different types of negotiators and tailor your approach using the DISC methodology.
Gear Up for Success: Build a killer prep list with intel on your opponent and a clear inventory of your strengths.
Own the Room: Master the art of negotiation strategy, use space to your advantage, and know the unspoken rules.
Discover Your Edge: Tap into your natural negotiation style and make it work for you.
Learn from the Masters: Unlock game-changing tips and theories from the biggest “gurus” in the field.
Practice Makes Perfect: Get hands-on with exercises that turn theory into action.
Ready to negotiate like a pro? This workshop will show you how to make your case, win people over, and seal the deal.

events.long_description:
80s Day Disco: Wellington 🪩
Get Ready to Groove at the Ultimate 80s Day Disco! 🎉
Step into a time machine and relive the magic of the 80s with a day filled with the biggest disco anthems and synth-powered hits of the decade. Whether you’re a true 80s fanatic or just here for the nostalgia, this party is all about electric vibes, funky beats, and non-stop dancing! 🎧🕺✨
What to Expect:
🎶 Nonstop 80s Classics: Dance to the timeless hits that defined the decade—from disco grooves to rock anthems and synth-pop bangers.

events.long_description:
80's Day Disco: Wellington 🪩
Tickets selling fast → https://www.fatsoma.com/e/vb6j5pqr/la/qwmu

Get Ready to Groove at the Ultimate 80s Day Disco! 🎉

Step into a time machine and relive the magic of the 80s with a day filled with the biggest disco anthems and synth-powered hits of the decade. Whether you’re a true 80s fanatic or just here for the nostalgia, this party is all about electric vibes, funky beats, and non-stop dancing! 🎧🕺✨

What to Expect:

🎶 Nonstop 80s Classics: Dance to the timeless hits that defined the decade—from disco grooves to rock anthems and synth-pop bangers.

🕺 Retro Dance Floor Energy: Show off your best moves under the dazzling glow of neon lights and shimmering disco balls.

📸 Totally Rad Photo Ops: Capture the moment with throwback props, vintage backdrops, and all the 80s glam.

🌟 Feel-Good Vibes All Day Long: Celebrate the music, the energy, and the nostalgia with great people and iconic tunes.

Let’s make it a day to remember—80s style! 🎶💿✨

Expect to hear music from:

Michael Jackson ⭐ Madonna ⭐ Prince ⭐ Whitney Houston ⭐ Queen ⭐ George Michael ⭐ David Bowie ⭐ Tina Turner ⭐ Elton John ⭐ Phil Collins ⭐ Cyndi Lauper ⭐ Bon Jovi ⭐ The Police ⭐ Bruce Springsteen ⭐ Duran Duran ⭐ Journey ⭐ Billy Joel ⭐ Lionel Richie ⭐ Eurythmics ⭐ Depeche Mode ⭐ Aerosmith ⭐ U2 ⭐ The Cure ⭐ Van Halen ⭐ Hall & Oates ⭐ AC/DC ⭐ The Smiths ⭐ New Order ⭐ Fleetwood Mac ⭐ The Bangles ⭐ Rick Astley ⭐ Spandau Ballet ⭐ Roxette ⭐ Simple Minds ⭐ Guns N’ Roses ⭐ Billy Idol ⭐ Tears for Fears ⭐ Bryan Adams ⭐ A-ha

FAQ’S

🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?

Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.

🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etc

Yes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.

🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?

Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.

🗣️ Is the venue accessible?

Our events take place at a variety of locations across the UK and Ireland. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.

🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?

Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.

🗣️ Are your events 18+?

All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.

🗣️ I have more questions?

If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)

Refund Policy:
In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

Gazley Volkswagen Wellington Marathon - 2026
The Gazley Volkswagen Wellington Marathon has been the Capital City’s favourite footrace for almost 40 years.

Based at the famous Sky Stadium, the flat, scenic and fast route around Wellington's spectacular waterfront creates an occasion for all age and ability.

Whether in the Full Marathon, Half Marathon, 10k or Kids’ Magic Mile, more than 4000 runners and walkers from more than a dozen countries line up for this great annual occasion, which is also benefits the Heart Foundation.

But don't take our word for it. Find out for yourself... Enter Now for June 28th at www.wellingtonmarathon.co.nz !

events.id: Deafheaven & Nothing-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Deafheaven & Nothing
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Deafhaven return to NZ with special guests Nothing
American black-metal trailblazers Deafhaven announce their return to New Zealand this July, presented by Banished Music. The band will perform two exclusive headline shows in Auckland and Wellington, joined by special guests Nothing.
Formed in the San Francisco Bay Area in 2010, Deafhaven have spent the past decade and a half pushing at the emotional and sonic boundaries of modern metal. From their debut Roads to Judah, to the genre-defining breakthrough Sunbather, and subsequent records like New Bermuda, Ordinary Corrupt Human Love, and Infinite Granite, Deafhaven have continually redefined the limits of heavy music while confronting themes of alienation, addiction, family history, and self-perseverance.
The tour follows the release of their 2025 album Lonely People With Power. Their sixth album and first for Roadrunner Records arrives as both a culmination and a reckoning , an album that embraces the band’s full identity after years of evolution. Produced by Justin Meldal-Johnsen (Beck collaborator and producer of Infinite Granite), the record captures Deafhaven’s innate ability to merge melody with brutality, and pain with poetry. Through reflections on masculinity, inherited trauma, family, and self-perception, the album grapples with the idea that while we can’t fully escape our past or our nature, we can choose how we move forward. Ultimately, Lonely People With Power stands as one of Deafhaven’s most expansive and human statements.
The album has drawn praise from Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, The Line Of Best Fit, Kerrang and more, and features collaborations with Interpol’s Paul Banks and Boy Harsher’s Jae Matthews.
Revered for their transcendental live shows, Deafhaven have toured the world over, performing at festivals such as Coachella and Primavera Sound, and sharing stages with artists ranging from Slipknot and Knocked Loose to Chelsea Wolfe and Mono. They’ve received a Grammy Award nomination for Best Metal Performance and continue to command a reputation as one of the most compelling live acts in heavy music.
Joining them are American shoegaze outfit Nothing, whose latest release A Short History of Decay has been described by Pitchfork as “fresh and exciting.” Their fifth album and first for Run For Cover Records further expands the band’s sonic scope, delivering the most refined and immersive iteration of Nothing to date.

San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

events.name: The Veils tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Banished Music
Following the critically acclaimed release of Asphodels in 2025, local heroes The Veils return with a bold and invigorated new album, Fragile World. Released June 19 on V2 Records, the album marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band, with fearless leader Finn Andrews reflecting on his tendency to make each album as a rebellion against his last. Recorded live to tape at Roundhead by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills), Fragile World captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode. With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, Fragile World stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.

events.id: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
NZ Citizens and residents pay $219 with ID.  f you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $499 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register..
Mental Health First Aid is an evidence-based workshop which gives people the skills and confidence to have supportive conversations with their whānau, co-workers and community and help guide them to professional help if needed.
You will learn to recognise common mental health challenges, how to provide initial help to someone experiencing a mental health challenge using a practical, evidence-based action plan, and how to respond in a crisis situation. During the workshop you will have the opportunity to practice new skills in a safe environment.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Mental health challenges covered in the course include:
Depression
Anxiety
Psychosis
Substance use problems
Mental Health Crisis Covered:
Suicidal thoughts and behaviours
Non- Suicidal self-injury
Panic attack
Traumatic events
Severe effects from Alcohol or other substance use
Aggressive behaviours
People who attend Mental Health First Aid training can complete a short online assessment to be accredited as a Mental Health First Aider. You must attend the full two days to be able to do the online assessment for accreditation. The accreditation is valid for three years.
If you choose not to be accredited, you will receive a certificate of participation if you attend the full course. You will also receive a workbook as part of your course fee.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Get ready as South African sensation Jeremy Loops returns to bring the vibes to Wellington’s Meow Nui this July!
After a massive 2025, including a standout set on the Acoustic Stage at Glastonbury, the world’s favourite high-energy-folk powerhouse is back for a strictly limited run of headline shows.
Known for his legendary live performances that blend acoustic folk, alt pop and urban grit with loop-pedal wizardry, Jeremy Loops delivers a show that is part intimate storytelling, and part stadium-sized party.
From massive breakout anthems like Down South and Waves to the soaring new melodies of his latest record Feathers And Stone, expect an infectious atmosphere and world-class musicianship. Every room becomes a massive communal celebration. Every night is a pure explosion of energy.
Catch his exclusive Wellington performance. One night only, at Meow Nui, supported by Tom River.
